verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच अथ ब्रह्मात्म-जैः देवैः प्रजेशैर् आवृतो ऽभ्यगात् । भवश् च भूत-भव्येशो ययौ भूत-गणैर् वृतः
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca atha brahmātma-jaiḥ devaiḥ prajeśair āvṛto 'bhyagāt / bhavaś ca bhūta-bhavyeśo yayau bhūta-gaṇair vṛtaḥ
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Thereafter Lord Brahm arrived [in Dvrak] being surrounded by his sons, the gods and the lords of man. Lord Bhava [S'iva] also came, the for all living beings favorable controller, who was accompanied by a host of ghostly beings.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रो मरुद्भिर् भगवान् आदित्या वसवो ऽश्विनौ । ऋभवो ऽङ्गिरसो रुद्रा विश्वे साध्याश् च देवताः
verses.transliteration: indro marudbhir bhagavān ādityā vasavo 'śvinau / ṛbhavo 'ṅgiraso rudrā viśve sādhyāś ca devatāḥ
verses.translation: Also Indra, the powerful controller, and his gods [the Maruts] came, the sons of Aditi, the good souls of clarity [the Vasus], the protectors of health [the As'vins], the artists [the Ribhus], the descendants of Angir, the expansions of S'iva [the Rudras], the demigods of the intellect [the Vis'vedevas], of commerce [the Sdhyas], and other demigods, the singers and dancing girls of heaven [Gandharvas and Apsaras], the souls of excel [the Ngas], the perfected souls [Siddhas] and the venerable souls [Cranas], the treasure keepers [Guhyakas], the seers [the Rishis], the forefathers [Pitas] and the scientists [Vidydharas], as also the souls of special talents [the Kinnaras], all came together in Dvrak eager to see Krishna, the Supreme Lord who removes the impurities everywhere in the universe, who with His transcendental form enchants the entire human society, and spreads His fame in all the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: गन्धर्वाप्सरसो नागाः सिद्ध-चारण-गुह्यकाः । ऋषयः पितरश् चैव स-विद्याधर-किन्नराः
verses.transliteration: gandharvāpsaraso nāgāḥ siddha-cāraṇa-guhyakāḥ / ṛṣayaḥ pitaraś caiva sa-vidyādhara-kinnarāḥ
verses.translation: Also Indra, the powerful controller, and his gods [the Maruts] came, the sons of Aditi, the good souls of clarity [the Vasus], the protectors of health [the As'vins], the artists [the Ribhus], the descendants of Angir, the expansions of S'iva [the Rudras], the demigods of the intellect [the Vis'vedevas], of commerce [the Sdhyas], and other demigods, the singers and dancing girls of heaven [Gandharvas and Apsaras], the souls of excel [the Ngas], the perfected souls [Siddhas] and the venerable souls [Cranas], the treasure keepers [Guhyakas], the seers [the Rishis], the forefathers [Pitas] and the scientists [Vidydharas], as also the souls of special talents [the Kinnaras], all came together in Dvrak eager to see Krishna, the Supreme Lord who removes the impurities everywhere in the universe, who with His transcendental form enchants the entire human society, and spreads His fame in all the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: द्वारकाम् उपसञ्जग्मुः सर्वे कृष्ण-दिदृक्षवः । वपुषा येन भगवान् नर-लोक-मनोरमः । यशो वितेने लोकेषु सर्व-लोक-मलापहम्
verses.transliteration: dvārakām upasañjagmuḥ sarve kṛṣṇa-didṛkṣavaḥ / vapuṣā yena bhagavān nara-loka-manoramaḥ / yaśo vitene lokeṣu sarva-loka-malāpaham
verses.translation: Also Indra, the powerful controller, and his gods [the Maruts] came, the sons of Aditi, the good souls of clarity [the Vasus], the protectors of health [the As'vins], the artists [the Ribhus], the descendants of Angir, the expansions of S'iva [the Rudras], the demigods of the intellect [the Vis'vedevas], of commerce [the Sdhyas], and other demigods, the singers and dancing girls of heaven [Gandharvas and Apsaras], the souls of excel [the Ngas], the perfected souls [Siddhas] and the venerable souls [Cranas], the treasure keepers [Guhyakas], the seers [the Rishis], the forefathers [Pitas] and the scientists [Vidydharas], as also the souls of special talents [the Kinnaras], all came together in Dvrak eager to see Krishna, the Supreme Lord who removes the impurities everywhere in the universe, who with His transcendental form enchants the entire human society, and spreads His fame in all the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यां विभ्राजमानायां समृद्धायां महर्द्धिभिः । व्यचक्षतावितृप्ताक्षाः कृष्णम् अद्भुत-दर्शनम्
verses.transliteration: tasyāṃ vibhrājamānāyāṃ samṛddhāyāṃ maharddhibhiḥ / vyacakṣatāvitṛptākṣāḥ kṛṣṇam adbhuta-darśanam
verses.translation: In that splendid city rich with a great abundance, they with their hungry eyes saw Lord Krishna who is so wonderful to behold.

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गोद्यानोपगैर् माल्यैश् छादयन्तो युदूत्तमम् । गीर्भिश् चित्र-पदार्थाभिस् तुष्टुवुर् जगद्-ईश्वरम्
verses.transliteration: svargodyānopagair mālyaiś chādayanto yudūttamam / gīrbhiś citra-padārthābhis tuṣṭuvur jagad-īśvaram
verses.translation: After having covered the best of the Yadus with flower garlands brought from the gardens of heaven, they praised Him, the Lord of the Living Being, and expressed themselves in all kinds of amazing ideas and words.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-देवा ऊचुः नताः स्म ते नाथ पदारविन्दं बुद्धीन्द्रिय-प्राण-मनो-वचोभिः । यच् चिन्त्यते ऽन्तर् हृदि भाव-युक्तैर् मुमुक्षुभिः कर्म-मयोरु-पाशात्
verses.transliteration: śrī-devā ūcuḥ natāḥ sma te nātha padāravindaṃ buddhīndriya-prāṇa-mano-vacobhiḥ / yac cintyate 'ntar hṛdi bhāva-yuktair mumukṣubhiḥ karma-mayoru-pāśāt
verses.translation: The gods said: 'We with all our intelligence, senses, vital air, mind and words, bow down at Your lotus feet, oh Lord, the feet upon which they meditate in their heart who are connected in the love of striving for liberation from the powerful grip of karmic consequences.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं मायया त्रि-गुणयात्मनि दुर्विभाव्यं व्यक्तं सृजस्य् अवसि लुम्पसि तद्-गुण-स्थः नैतैर् भवान् अजित कर्मभिर् अज्यते वै यत् स्वे सुखे ऽव्यवहिते ऽभिरतो ऽनवद्यः
verses.transliteration: tvaṃ māyayā tri-guṇayātmani durvibhāvyaṃ $ vyaktaṃ sṛjasy avasi lumpasi tad-guṇa-sthaḥ &naitair bhavān ajita karmabhir ajyate vai % yat sve sukhe 'vyavahite 'bhirato 'navadyaḥ
verses.translation: You, by [engaging] the material energy consisting of the three basic qualities, create, protect and destroy the inconceivable cosmic manifestation within Yourself while being situated within those modes. [Nevertheless] You are by them not entangled in karmic activities at all, oh Unconquerable One, because You, the irreproachable Lord, are always absorbed in Your unrestricted happiness [see also B.G. 3: 22].

verses.sanskrit: शुद्धिर् नृणां न तु तथेड्य दुराशयानां विद्या-श्रुताध्ययन-दान-तपः-क्रियाभिः शुद्धिर् नृणां न तु तथेड्य दुराशयानां विद्या-श्रुताध्ययन-दान-तपः-क्रियाभिः सत्त्वात्मनाम् ऋषभ ते यशसि प्रवृद्ध- सच्-छ्रद्धया श्रवण-सम्भृतया यथा स्यात्
verses.transliteration: śuddhir nṛṇāṃ na tu tatheḍya durāśayānāṃ $ vidyā-śrutādhyayana-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyābhiḥ &śuddhir nṛṇāṃ na tu tatheḍya durāśayānāṃ $ vidyā-śrutādhyayana-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyābhiḥ &sattvātmanām ṛṣabha te yaśasi pravṛddha- % sac-chraddhayā śravaṇa-sambhṛtayā yathā syāt
verses.translation: Oh Worshipable One, the purification of those persons who have a contaminated consciousness is not as much brought about by incantations, respecting injunctions, study of the scriptures, charity, penances and rituals, oh Greatest of All, as it is caused by listening to those souls who are situated in pure goodness and have a fully matured and transcendental faith in Your glories [see also 4.29: 36-38].

verses.sanskrit: स्यान् नस् तवाङ्घ्रिर् अशुभाशय-धूमकेतुः क्षेमाय यो मुनिभिर् आर्द्र-हृदोह्यमानः स्यान् नस् तवाङ्घ्रिर् अशुभाशय-धूमकेतुः क्षेमाय यो मुनिभिर् आर्द्र-हृदोह्यमानः यः सात्वतैः सम-विभूतय आत्मवद्भिर् व्यूहे ऽर्चितः सवनशः स्वर्-अतिक्रमाय
verses.transliteration: syān nas tavāṅghrir aśubhāśaya-dhūmaketuḥ $ kṣemāya yo munibhir ārdra-hṛdohyamānaḥ &syān nas tavāṅghrir aśubhāśaya-dhūmaketuḥ $ kṣemāya yo munibhir ārdra-hṛdohyamānaḥ &yaḥ sātvataiḥ sama-vibhūtaya ātmavadbhir % vyūhe 'rcitaḥ savanaśaḥ svar-atikramāya
verses.translation: May there for us be Your lotus feet, the fire that annihilates our inauspicious mentality and that by sages desiring the real benefit is carried in their appeased hearts, the fire of Your four forms [of soul, ego, mind and intelligence, the catur-vyha] that three times a day is worshiped by the self-controlled devotees so as to reach beyond the [material] heavens and acquire an opulence [bhaga] like Yours.

verses.sanskrit: यस् चिन्त्यते प्रयत-पाणिभिर् अध्वराग्नौ त्रय्या निरुक्त-विधिनेश हविर् गृहीत्वा यस् चिन्त्यते प्रयत-पाणिभिर् अध्वराग्नौ त्रय्या निरुक्त-विधिनेश हविर् गृहीत्वा अध्यात्म-योग उत योगिभिर् आत्म-मायां जिज्ञासुभिः परम-भागवतैः परीष्टः
verses.transliteration: yas cintyate prayata-pāṇibhir adhvarāgnau $ trayyā nirukta-vidhineśa havir gṛhītvā &yas cintyate prayata-pāṇibhir adhvarāgnau $ trayyā nirukta-vidhineśa havir gṛhītvā &adhyātma-yoga uta yogibhir ātma-māyāṃ % jijñāsubhiḥ parama-bhāgavataiḥ parīṣṭaḥ
verses.translation: On them [Your feet] they are meditating, oh Lord, who, having folded their hands, pour the ghee they took into the fire of sacrifice in the [nirukta] process of understanding the three Vedas. On them the yoga practitioners are meditating who, inquisitive about Your [yoga-]my mystic potency, are united in the realization of the True Self. [But] they are fully attained [only] by the most elevated devotees [see uttama and 11.2: 45-47].

verses.sanskrit: पर्युष्टया तव विभो वन-मालयेयं संस्पार्धिनी भगवती प्रतिपत्नी-वच् छ्रीः पर्युष्टया तव विभो वन-मालयेयं संस्पार्धिनी भगवती प्रतिपत्नी-वच् छ्रीः यः सु-प्रणीतम् अमुयार्हणम् आददन् नो भूयात् सदाङ्घ्रिर् अशुभाशय-धूमकेतुः
verses.transliteration: paryuṣṭayā tava vibho vana-mālayeyaṃ $ saṃspārdhinī bhagavatī pratipatnī-vac chrīḥ &paryuṣṭayā tava vibho vana-mālayeyaṃ $ saṃspārdhinī bhagavatī pratipatnī-vac chrīḥ &yaḥ su-praṇītam amuyārhaṇam ādadan no % bhūyāt sadāṅghrir aśubhāśaya-dhūmaketuḥ
verses.translation: With the withered flower garland that we offer You, Your Lordship's consort S'r [dwelling there], oh Almighty One, feels challenged like a jealous co-wife, for You accept this offering as being properly performed [see also B.G. 9: 26]. May there always be Your lotus feet, the fire that consumes our impure desires!

verses.sanskrit: केतुस् त्रि-विक्रम-युतस् त्रि-पतत्-पताको यस् ते भयाभय-करो ऽसुर-देव-चम्वोः केतुस् त्रि-विक्रम-युतस् त्रि-पतत्-पताको यस् ते भयाभय-करो ऽसुर-देव-चम्वोः स्वर्गाय साधुषु खलेष्व् इतराय भूमन् पदः पुनातु भगवन् भजताम् अघं नः
verses.transliteration: ketus tri-vikrama-yutas tri-patat-patāko $ yas te bhayābhaya-karo 'sura-deva-camvoḥ &ketus tri-vikrama-yutas tri-patat-patāko $ yas te bhayābhaya-karo 'sura-deva-camvoḥ &svargāya sādhuṣu khaleṣv itarāya bhūman % padaḥ punātu bhagavan bhajatām aghaṃ naḥ
verses.translation: Your feet that like flags decorating flagpoles with three mighty steps [defeat the possessiveness and bring down the water of the Ganges] in each of the three worlds [see 8.20], create fear among the armies of the demons and fearlessness among the troops of the God-fearing souls. For the virtuous souls they are there for the attainment of heaven and for the mischievous ones they are there for exactly the opposite, oh Most Powerful One. May these feet, oh Supreme Lord, free us, Your worshipers, from our sins.

verses.sanskrit: नस्य् ओत-गाव इव यस्य वशे भवन्ति ब्रह्मादयस् तनु-भृतो मिथुर् अर्द्यमानाः नस्य् ओत-गाव इव यस्य वशे भवन्ति ब्रह्मादयस् तनु-भृतो मिथुर् अर्द्यमानाः कालस्य ते प्रकृति-पूरुषयोः परस्य शं नस् तनोतु चरणः पुरुषोत्तमस्य
verses.transliteration: nasy ota-gāva iva yasya vaśe bhavanti $ brahmādayas tanu-bhṛto mithur ardyamānāḥ &nasy ota-gāva iva yasya vaśe bhavanti $ brahmādayas tanu-bhṛto mithur ardyamānāḥ &kālasya te prakṛti-pūruṣayoḥ parasya % śaṃ nas tanotu caraṇaḥ puruṣottamasya
verses.translation: Brahm and all the other embodied beings have their existence like oxen bound by a rope through their nose. Being controlled by Time they trouble each other. May the lotus feet of You, the Supreme Personality transcendental to both material nature and the individual person, bring us transcendental happiness [compare 1.13: 42, 6.3: 12].

verses.sanskrit: अस्यासि हेतुर् उदय-स्थिति-संयमानाम् अव्यक्त-जीव-महताम् अपि कालम् आहुः अस्यासि हेतुर् उदय-स्थिति-संयमानाम् अव्यक्त-जीव-महताम् अपि कालम् आहुः सो ऽयं त्रि-णाभिर् अखिलापचये प्रवृत्तः कालो गभीर-रय उत्तम-पूरुषस् त्वम्
verses.transliteration: asyāsi hetur udaya-sthiti-saṃyamānām $ avyakta-jīva-mahatām api kālam āhuḥ &asyāsi hetur udaya-sthiti-saṃyamānām $ avyakta-jīva-mahatām api kālam āhuḥ &so 'yaṃ tri-ṇābhir akhilāpacaye pravṛttaḥ % kālo gabhīra-raya uttama-pūruṣas tvam
verses.translation: You are the cause of the creation, maintenance and annihilation of this [universe]. You are the cause of the unseen, of the individual soul and of the complete whole of the manifest reality. They say that You, this very same personality, are the time factor controlling all, who appears as a wheel divided in three [summer, winter and spring/autumn]. One says that You are the Supreme Personality who, in the form of Time, uninterrupted in Your flow effects the decay of everything [*].

verses.sanskrit: त्वत्तः पुमान् समधिगम्य ययास्य वीर्यं धत्ते महान्तम् इव गर्भम् अमोघ-वीर्यः त्वत्तः पुमान् समधिगम्य ययास्य वीर्यं धत्ते महान्तम् इव गर्भम् अमोघ-वीर्यः सो ऽयं तयानुगत आत्मन आण्ड-कोशं हैमं ससर्ज बहिर् आवरणैर् उपेतम्
verses.transliteration: tvattaḥ pumān samadhigamya yayāsya vīryaṃ $ dhatte mahāntam iva garbham amogha-vīryaḥ &tvattaḥ pumān samadhigamya yayāsya vīryaṃ $ dhatte mahāntam iva garbham amogha-vīryaḥ &so 'yaṃ tayānugata ātmana āṇḍa-kośaṃ % haimaṃ sasarja bahir āvaraṇair upetam
verses.translation: The living being [beginning with Mah-Vishnu] acquires its power [potency] from that [time aspect] of Yours. You establish the vastness of matter with it [mahat-tattva]. United with that same nature You therefrom generate - the way an ordinary fetus is produced - the golden primeval egg of the universe endowed with its [seven] outer layers [see kos'a].

verses.sanskrit: तत् तस्थूषश् च जगतश् च भवान् अधीशो यन् माययोत्थ-गुण-विक्रिययोपनीतान् तत् तस्थूषश् च जगतश् च भवान् अधीशो यन् माययोत्थ-गुण-विक्रिययोपनीतान् अर्थाञ् जुषन्न् अपि हृषीक-पते न लिप्तो ये ऽन्ये स्वतः परिहृताद् अपि बिभ्यति स्म
verses.transliteration: tat tasthūṣaś ca jagataś ca bhavān adhīśo $ yan māyayottha-guṇa-vikriyayopanītān &tat tasthūṣaś ca jagataś ca bhavān adhīśo $ yan māyayottha-guṇa-vikriyayopanītān &arthāñ juṣann api hṛṣīka-pate na lipto % ye 'nye svataḥ parihṛtād api bibhyati sma
verses.translation: You are therewith the original Controller of everything that moves and keeps its place. You after all, are in Your activities, oh Master of the Senses, never affected Yourself by the change of the objects of the senses that presents itself because of the operating modes of nature. Others by contrast, engaged of their own accord [in austerities], having turned away live in fear [see also B.G. 16: 23-24].

verses.sanskrit: स्मायावलोक-लव-दर्शित-भाव-हारि- भ्रू-मण्डल-प्रहित-सौरत-मन्त्र-शौण्डैः स्मायावलोक-लव-दर्शित-भाव-हारि- भ्रू-मण्डल-प्रहित-सौरत-मन्त्र-शौण्डैः पत्न्यस् तु षोडश-सहस्रम् अनङ्ग-बाणैर् यस्येन्द्रियं विमथितुं करणैर् न विभ्व्यः
verses.transliteration: smāyāvaloka-lava-darśita-bhāva-hāri- $ bhrū-maṇḍala-prahita-saurata-mantra-śauṇḍaiḥ &smāyāvaloka-lava-darśita-bhāva-hāri- $ bhrū-maṇḍala-prahita-saurata-mantra-śauṇḍaiḥ &patnyas tu ṣoḍaśa-sahasram anaṅga-bāṇair % yasyendriyaṃ vimathituṃ karaṇair na vibhvyaḥ
verses.translation: Your sixteen thousand wives were enchanting every time they launched the arrows of Cupid by showing their feelings with their eyebrows, smiles and glances. But they were not able to perturb Your senses by all the methods of their messages and advances of conjugal love [see also 1.11: 36].

verses.sanskrit: विभ्व्यस् तवामृत-कथोद-वहास् त्रि-लोक्याः पादावने-ज-सरितः शमलानि हन्तुम् विभ्व्यस् तवामृत-कथोद-वहास् त्रि-लोक्याः पादावने-ज-सरितः शमलानि हन्तुम् आनुश्रवं श्रुतिभिर् अङ्घ्रि-जम् अङ्ग-सङ्गैस् तीर्थ-द्वयं शुचि-षदस् त उपस्पृशन्ति
verses.transliteration: vibhvyas tavāmṛta-kathoda-vahās tri-lokyāḥ $ pādāvane-ja-saritaḥ śamalāni hantum &vibhvyas tavāmṛta-kathoda-vahās tri-lokyāḥ $ pādāvane-ja-saritaḥ śamalāni hantum &ānuśravaṃ śrutibhir aṅghri-jam aṅga-saṅgais % tīrtha-dvayaṃ śuci-ṣadas ta upaspṛśanti
verses.translation: The rivers of nectar of Your stories and the rivers resulting from the bathing of Your feet, can put an end to all the impurities of the three worlds. Those who strive for purification and seek association, approach [You therefore] in two places: by making their ears listen to the stories the tradition offers [in the temples] and by bringing their bodies [in the rivers] in contact with the water that flows from Your feet.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-बादरायणिर् उवाच इत्य् अभिष्टूय विबुधैः सेशः शत-धृतिर् हरिम् । अभ्यभाषत गोविन्दं प्रणम्याम्बरम् आश्रितः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvāca ity abhiṣṭūya vibudhaiḥ seśaḥ śata-dhṛtir harim / abhyabhāṣata govindaṃ praṇamyāmbaram āśritaḥ
verses.translation: The honorable son of Vysa [S'uka] said: 'After he who commands hundreds [of sages, Brahm] together with S'iva and the demigods thus had praised Govinda, the Supreme Lord, he addressed Him offering his obeisances from his position in the sky.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-ब्रह्मोवाच भूमेर् भारावताराय पुरा विज्ञापितः प्रभो । त्वम् अस्माभिर् अशेषात्मन् तत् तथैवोपपादितम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-brahmovāca bhūmer bhārāvatārāya purā vijñāpitaḥ prabho / tvam asmābhir aśeṣātman tat tathaivopapāditam
verses.translation: S'r Brahm said: 'Oh Lord, we requested You previously to diminish the burden of the earth. Oh Unlimited Soul, You have fulfilled that request the way we wanted it.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मश् च स्थापितः सत्सु सत्य-सन्धेषु वै त्वया । कीर्तिश् च दिक्षु विक्षिप्ता सर्व-लोक-मलापहा
verses.transliteration: dharmaś ca sthāpitaḥ satsu satya-sandheṣu vai tvayā / kīrtiś ca dikṣu vikṣiptā sarva-loka-malāpahā
verses.translation: Having established the principles of dharma among both the pious souls and the seekers of truth, it is indeed Your glory, spread by You in all directions, that removes the impurity of all the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: अवतीर्य यदोर् वंशे बिभ्रद् रूपम् अनुत्तमम् । कर्माण्य् उद्दाम-वृत्तानि हिताय जगतो ऽकृथाः
verses.transliteration: avatīrya yador vaṃśe bibhrad rūpam anuttamam / karmāṇy uddāma-vṛttāni hitāya jagato 'kṛthāḥ
verses.translation: For the benefit of the universe assuming a form and descending in the Yadu dynasty, You with magnanimous deeds have performed incomparable activities.

verses.sanskrit: यानि ते चरितानीश मनुष्याः साधवः कलौ । शृण्वन्तः कीर्तयन्तश् च तरिष्यन्त्य् अञ्जसा तमः
verses.transliteration: yāni te caritānīśa manuṣyāḥ sādhavaḥ kalau / śṛṇvantaḥ kīrtayantaś ca tariṣyanty añjasā tamaḥ
verses.translation: Oh Lord, those saintly souls who in the age of Kali hear and chant about Your activities, will easily overcome the darkness [see also 10.14].

verses.sanskrit: यदु-वंशे ऽवतीर्णस्य भवतः पुरुषोत्तम । शरच्-छतं व्यतीयाय पञ्च-विंशाधिकं प्रभो
verses.transliteration: yadu-vaṃśe 'vatīrṇasya bhavataḥ puruṣottama / śarac-chataṃ vyatīyāya pañca-viṃśādhikaṃ prabho
verses.translation: Oh Supreme Personality, since Your descent in the Yadu-vams'a one hundred and twenty-five autumns have passed, oh Master.

verses.sanskrit: नाधुना ते ऽखिलाधार देव-कार्यावशेषितम् । कुलं च विप्र-शापेन नष्ट-प्रायम् अभूद् इदम्
verses.transliteration: nādhunā te 'khilādhāra deva-kāryāvaśeṣitam / kulaṃ ca vipra-śāpena naṣṭa-prāyam abhūd idam
verses.translation: Oh You, Foundation of Everything, for You there is no longer any obligation to the God-fearing souls, and the remaining part of the dynasty has factually been annihilated by this curse of the brahmins [see 11.1]. Therefore we ask You whether You intend to leave for Your Supreme Abode and if You please want to protect us, the servants of Vaikunthha and the guardians of all worlds and their inhabitants.'

verses.sanskrit: ततः स्व-धाम परमं विशस्व यदि मन्यसे । स-लोकाल् लोक-पालान् नः पाहि वैकुण्ठ-किङ्करान्
verses.transliteration: tataḥ sva-dhāma paramaṃ viśasva yadi manyase / sa-lokāl loka-pālān naḥ pāhi vaikuṇṭha-kiṅkarān
verses.translation: Oh You, Foundation of Everything, for You there is no longer any obligation to the God-fearing souls, and the remaining part of the dynasty has factually been annihilated by this curse of the brahmins [see 11.1]. Therefore we ask You whether You intend to leave for Your Supreme Abode and if You please want to protect us, the servants of Vaikunthha and the guardians of all worlds and their inhabitants.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच अवधारितम् एतन् मे यद् आत्थ विबुधेश्वर । कृतं वः कार्यम् अखिलं भूमेर् भारो ऽवतारितः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca avadhāritam etan me yad āttha vibudheśvara / kṛtaṃ vaḥ kāryam akhilaṃ bhūmer bhāro 'vatāritaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'I have understood what you said, oh controller of the demigods. All the work has been accomplished for your sake; the burden of the earth has been removed.

verses.sanskrit: तद् इदं यादव-कुलं वीर्य-शौर्य-श्रियोद्धतम् । लोकं जिघृक्षद् रुद्धं मे वेलयेव महार्णवः
verses.transliteration: tad idaṃ yādava-kulaṃ vīrya-śaurya-śriyoddhatam / lokaṃ jighṛkṣad ruddhaṃ me velayeva mahārṇavaḥ
verses.translation: This same Yadu family that [with My appearance has] expanded its power, courage and opulence, threatened to devour the entire world and has therefore been checked by Me, just like an ocean is checked by its shore.

verses.sanskrit: यद्य् असंहृत्य दृप्तानां यदूनां विपुलं कुलम् । गन्तास्म्य् अनेन लोको ऽयम् उद्वेलेन विनङ्क्ष्यति
verses.transliteration: yady asaṃhṛtya dṛptānāṃ yadūnāṃ vipulaṃ kulam / gantāsmy anena loko 'yam udvelena vinaṅkṣyati
verses.translation: If I would not withdraw the vast dynasty of overly proud Yadus, this world would be destroyed by that flood.

verses.sanskrit: इदानीं नाश आरब्धः कुलस्य द्विज-शाप-जः । यास्यामि भवनं ब्रह्मन्न् एतद्-अन्ते तवानघ
verses.transliteration: idānīṃ nāśa ārabdhaḥ kulasya dvija-śāpa-jaḥ / yāsyāmi bhavanaṃ brahmann etad-ante tavānagha
verses.translation: Because of the brahmins' curse, right now the annihilation of the family has begun. After that has taken place, oh sinless Brahm, I will pay a visit to your abode.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच इत्य् उक्तो लोक-नाथेन स्वयम्-भूः प्रणिपत्य तम् । सह देव-गणैर् देवः स्व-धाम समपद्यत
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca ity ukto loka-nāthena svayam-bhūḥ praṇipatya tam / saha deva-gaṇair devaḥ sva-dhāma samapadyata
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Thus being addressed by the Lord of the World, the self-born one fell down at His feet to offer Him his obeisances. The godhead then returned to his abode together with the different gods.

verses.sanskrit: अथ तस्यां महोत्पातान् द्वारवत्यां समुत्थितान् । विलोक्य भगवान् आह यदु-वृद्धान् समागतान्
verses.transliteration: atha tasyāṃ mahotpātān dvāravatyāṃ samutthitān / vilokya bhagavān āha yadu-vṛddhān samāgatān
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord thereafter observed the development of serious portents in the city of Dvrak. He spoke to the assembled Yadu elders.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच एते वै सु-महोत्पाता व्युत्तिष्ठन्तीह सर्वतः । शापश् च नः कुलस्यासीद् ब्राह्मणेभ्यो दुरत्ययः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca ete vai su-mahotpātā vyuttiṣṭhantīha sarvataḥ / śāpaś ca naḥ kulasyāsīd brāhmaṇebhyo duratyayaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'These very serious disturbances appearing everywhere are a consequence of the curse the brahmins pronounced against our family, it is impossible to counteract.

verses.sanskrit: न वस्तव्यम् इहास्माभिर् जिजीविषुभिर् आर्यकाः । प्रभासं सु-महत्-पुण्यं यास्यामो ऽद्यैव मा चिरम्
verses.transliteration: na vastavyam ihāsmābhir jijīviṣubhir āryakāḥ / prabhāsaṃ su-mahat-puṇyaṃ yāsyāmo 'dyaiva mā ciram
verses.translation: We should not stay here if we want to continue with our lives. Let us not delay, oh venerable souls, and this very day go to Prabhsa, that so very pious place [**].

verses.sanskrit: यत्र स्नात्वा दक्ष-शापाद् गृहीतो यक्ष्मणोदु-राट् । विमुक्तः किल्बिषात् सद्यो भेजे भूयः कलोदयम्
verses.transliteration: yatra snātvā dakṣa-śāpād gṛhīto yakṣmaṇodu-rāṭ / vimuktaḥ kilbiṣāt sadyo bheje bhūyaḥ kalodayam
verses.translation: The king of the stars [the moon god], who was seized by consumption because of a curse of Daksha, once took a bath there, was immediately freed from his offense and resumed the waxing of his phases.

verses.sanskrit: वयं च तस्मिन्न् आप्लुत्य तर्पयित्वा पितॄन् सुरान् । भोजयित्वोषिजो विप्रान् नाना-गुणवतान्धसा
verses.transliteration: vayaṃ ca tasminn āplutya tarpayitvā pitṝn surān / bhojayitvoṣijo viprān nānā-guṇavatāndhasā
verses.translation: When we also bathe there to the satisfaction of the forefathers, offer different kinds of foodstuff to the demigods and the venerable scholars, and also distribute gifts with faith in them as being worth the charity, we will overcome the terrible danger, just as one with boats crosses the ocean.'

verses.sanskrit: तेषु दानानि पात्रेषु श्रद्धयोप्त्वा महान्ति वै । वृजिनानि तरिष्यामो दानैर् नौभिर् इवार्णवम्
verses.transliteration: teṣu dānāni pātreṣu śraddhayoptvā mahānti vai / vṛjināni tariṣyāmo dānair naubhir ivārṇavam
verses.translation: When we also bathe there to the satisfaction of the forefathers, offer different kinds of foodstuff to the demigods and the venerable scholars, and also distribute gifts with faith in them as being worth the charity, we will overcome the terrible danger, just as one with boats crosses the ocean.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच एवं भगवतादिष्टा यादवाः कुरु-नन्दन । गन्तुं कृत-धियस् तीर्थं स्यन्दनान् समयूयुजन्
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca evaṃ bhagavatādiṣṭā yādavāḥ kuru-nandana / gantuṃ kṛta-dhiyas tīrthaṃ syandanān samayūyujan
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Oh child of the Kurus, the Ydavas who were thus instructed by the Fortunate One, decided, after due consideration, to go to the holy site and yoked their horses to their chariots.

verses.sanskrit: तन् निरीक्ष्योद्धवो राजन् श्रुत्वा भगवतोदितम् । दृष्ट्वारिष्टानि घोराणि नित्यं कृष्णम् अनुव्रतः
verses.transliteration: tan nirīkṣyoddhavo rājan śrutvā bhagavatoditam / dṛṣṭvāriṣṭāni ghorāṇi nityaṃ kṛṣṇam anuvrataḥ
verses.translation: Oh King, Uddhava [see also 3.2 and 10.46 & 47], as an ever faithful follower of Krishna, happened to hear what the Lord had said. Facing the fearful evil omens [see also 1.14: 2-5] he approached the Lord of the lords of the universe in private and addressed Him, with folded hands bowing down his head at His feet.

verses.sanskrit: विविक्त उपसङ्गम्य जगताम् ईश्वरेश्वरम् । प्रणम्य शिरिसा पादौ प्राञ्जलिस् तम् अभाषत
verses.transliteration: vivikta upasaṅgamya jagatām īśvareśvaram / praṇamya śirisā pādau prāñjalis tam abhāṣata
verses.translation: Oh King, Uddhava [see also 3.2 and 10.46 & 47], as an ever faithful follower of Krishna, happened to hear what the Lord had said. Facing the fearful evil omens [see also 1.14: 2-5] he approached the Lord of the lords of the universe in private and addressed Him, with folded hands bowing down his head at His feet.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच देव-देवेश योगेश पुण्य-श्रवण-कीर्तन । संहृत्यैतत् कुलं नूनं लोकं सन्त्यक्ष्यते भवान् । विप्र-शापं समर्थो ऽपि प्रत्यहन् न यद् ईश्वरः
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca deva-deveśa yogeśa puṇya-śravaṇa-kīrtana / saṃhṛtyaitat kulaṃ nūnaṃ lokaṃ santyakṣyate bhavān / vipra-śāpaṃ samartho 'pi pratyahan na yad īśvaraḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lord and God of Gods, oh Master of Yoga, oh Piety of Hearing and Singing, You are about to withdraw this family now and give up this world. Even though You as the Supreme Controller are very well capable of it, You are not willing to counteract the curse of the brahmins!

verses.sanskrit: नाहं तवाङ्घ्रि-कमलं क्षणार्धम् अपि केशव । त्यक्तुं समुत्सहे नाथ स्व-धाम नय माम् अपि
verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ tavāṅghri-kamalaṃ kṣaṇārdham api keśava / tyaktuṃ samutsahe nātha sva-dhāma naya mām api
verses.translation: Oh Kes'ava, I cannot even for half a moment tolerate to give up Your lotus feet, please take me with You to Your abode [see also 3.29: 13]!

verses.sanskrit: तव विक्रीडितं कृष्ण नृनां परम-मङ्गलम् । कर्ण-पीयूषम् आसाद्य त्यजन्त्य् अन्य-स्पृहां जनाः
verses.transliteration: tava vikrīḍitaṃ kṛṣṇa nṛnāṃ parama-maṅgalam / karṇa-pīyūṣam āsādya tyajanty anya-spṛhāṃ janāḥ
verses.translation: Your pastimes are supremely auspicious, oh Krishna, they are nectar to the human ear. Once people have the taste of them, they forsake all desire for other things.

verses.sanskrit: शय्यासनाटन-स्थान- स्नान-क्रीडाशनादिषु । कथं त्वां प्रियम् आत्मानं वयं भक्तास् त्यजेम हि
verses.transliteration: śayyāsanāṭana-sthāna- snāna-krīḍāśanādiṣu / kathaṃ tvāṃ priyam ātmānaṃ vayaṃ bhaktās tyajema hi
verses.translation: How can we, who were always devoted to You when we were lying down, sitting, walking, standing, bathing, recreating and eating, and such, ever abandon You, our dear most Self?

verses.sanskrit: त्वयोपभुक्त-स्रग्-गन्ध- वासो-ऽलङ्कार-चर्चिताः । उच्छिष्ट-भोजिनो दासास् तव मायां जयेम हि
verses.transliteration: tvayopabhukta-srag-gandha- vāso-'laṅkāra-carcitāḥ / ucchiṣṭa-bhojino dāsās tava māyāṃ jayema hi
verses.translation: Eating the food remnants and adorned with the garlands, fragrances, garments and ornaments that You enjoyed, we, Your servants, will conquer the illusory energy for certain.

verses.sanskrit: वात-वसना य ऋषयः श्रमणा ऊर्ध्र-मन्थिनः । ब्रह्माख्यं धाम ते यान्ति शान्ताः सन्न्यासीनो ऽमलाः
verses.transliteration: vāta-vasanā ya ṛṣayaḥ śramaṇā ūrdhra-manthinaḥ / brahmākhyaṃ dhāma te yānti śāntāḥ sannyāsīno 'malāḥ
verses.translation: The but in air clad sinless, peaceful members of the renounced order, who as sages of strict observance always send their seed upwards, go to the abode known as Brahman [see rdhva retah and also 10.2: 32].

verses.sanskrit: वयं त्व् इह महा-योगिन् भ्रमन्तः कर्म-वर्त्मसु । त्वद्-वार्तया तरिष्यामस् तावकैर् दुस्तरं तमः
verses.transliteration: vayaṃ tv iha mahā-yogin bhramantaḥ karma-vartmasu / tvad-vārtayā tariṣyāmas tāvakair dustaraṃ tamaḥ
verses.translation: We on the other hand, oh Greatest of All Yogis, who in this world wander on the paths of fruitive labor, will together with Your devotees pass beyond the hard to conquer darkness, by discussing the topics that make us remember and glorify Your deeds, words, movements, broad smiles, glances and Your amorous sports after the example of the human world.'

verses.sanskrit: स्मरन्तः कीर्तयन्तस् ते कृतानि गदितानि च । गत्य्-उत्स्मितेक्षण-क्ष्वेलि यन् नृ-लोक-विडम्बनम्
verses.transliteration: smarantaḥ kīrtayantas te kṛtāni gaditāni ca / gaty-utsmitekṣaṇa-kṣveli yan nṛ-loka-viḍambanam
verses.translation: We on the other hand, oh Greatest of All Yogis, who in this world wander on the paths of fruitive labor, will together with Your devotees pass beyond the hard to conquer darkness, by discussing the topics that make us remember and glorify Your deeds, words, movements, broad smiles, glances and Your amorous sports after the example of the human world.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच एवं विज्ञापितो राजन् भगवान् देवकी-सुतः । एकान्तिनं प्रियं भृत्यम् उद्धवं समभाषत
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca evaṃ vijñāpito rājan bhagavān devakī-sutaḥ / ekāntinaṃ priyaṃ bhṛtyam uddhavaṃ samabhāṣata
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Thus being informed, oh King, the Supreme Lord, the son of Devak, for a long time spoke in private with His dear servant Uddhava.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच यद् आत्थ मां महा-भाग तच्-चिकीर्षितम् एव मे । ब्रह्मा भवो लोक-पालाः स्वर्-वासं मे ऽभिकाङ्क्षिणः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yad āttha māṃ mahā-bhāga tac-cikīrṣitam eva me / brahmā bhavo loka-pālāḥ svar-vāsaṃ me 'bhikāṅkṣiṇaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'That what you said to Me, oh greatly fortunate one [Uddhava], is indeed My plan [to withdraw the dynasty]. Brahm, Bhava and the leaders of the worlds, are looking forward to see me back in My abode [see 11.6: 26-27].

verses.sanskrit: मया निष्पादितं ह्य् अत्र देव-कार्यम् अशेषतः । यद्-अर्थम् अवतीर्णो ऽहम् अंशेन ब्रह्मणार्थितः
verses.transliteration: mayā niṣpāditaṃ hy atra deva-kāryam aśeṣataḥ / yad-artham avatīrṇo 'ham aṃśena brahmaṇārthitaḥ
verses.translation: I have completed My task here for the sake of the God-conscious souls [to diminish the burden of the earth], for which I, upon the prayers of Lord Brahm, have descended together with My partial expansion [Balarma].

verses.sanskrit: कुलं वै शाप-निर्दग्धं नङ्क्ष्यत्य् अन्योन्य-विग्रहात् । समुद्रः सप्तमे ह्य् एनां पुरीं च प्लावयिष्यति
verses.transliteration: kulaṃ vai śāpa-nirdagdhaṃ naṅkṣyaty anyonya-vigrahāt / samudraḥ saptame hy enāṃ purīṃ ca plāvayiṣyati
verses.translation: Because of the curse [of the brahmins] this family will certainly find its end. It will be destroyed in a mutual quarrel, and on the seventh day [from now] the ocean will inundate this city [Dvrak].

verses.sanskrit: यर्ह्य् एवायं मया त्यक्तो लोको ऽयं नष्ट-मङ्गलः । भविष्यत्य् अचिरात् साधो कलिनापि निराकृतः
verses.transliteration: yarhy evāyaṃ mayā tyakto loko 'yaṃ naṣṭa-maṅgalaḥ / bhaviṣyaty acirāt sādho kalināpi nirākṛtaḥ
verses.translation: Oh man of virtue, when I have abandoned this world, it will soon fall victim of Kali and be bereft of all piety [see also 1.16 & 17].

verses.sanskrit: न वस्तव्यं त्वयैवेह मया त्यक्ते मही-तले । जनो ऽभद्र-रुचिर् भद्र भविष्यति कलौ युगे
verses.transliteration: na vastavyaṃ tvayaiveha mayā tyakte mahī-tale / jano 'bhadra-rucir bhadra bhaviṣyati kalau yuge
verses.translation: After I have left, you certainly should not stay here, oh gentle soul, for in Kali's time the people on earth will take pleasure in misconduct.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु सर्वं परित्यज्य स्नेहं स्व-जन-बन्धुषु । मय्य् आवेश्य मनः संयक् सम-दृग् विचरस्व गाम्
verses.transliteration: tvaṃ tu sarvaṃ parityajya snehaṃ sva-jana-bandhuṣu / mayy āveśya manaḥ saṃyak sama-dṛg vicarasva gām
verses.translation: With your mind fully fixed on Me, you should in fact forsake all emotional ties with your family and friends, and being equal towards all wander around in this world [see B.G. 6: 9, 6: 29, 14: 22-25].

verses.sanskrit: यद् इदं मनसा वाचा चक्षुर्भ्यां श्रवणादिभिः । नश्वरं गृह्यमाणं च विद्धि माया-मनो-मयम्
verses.transliteration: yad idaṃ manasā vācā cakṣurbhyāṃ śravaṇādibhiḥ / naśvaraṃ gṛhyamāṇaṃ ca viddhi māyā-mano-mayam
verses.translation: This world you think of, talk about, look at, listen to and such, you should consider a transitory presentation of matters, a game of shadows that captures your imagination [see also 10.40: 25].

verses.sanskrit: पुंसो ऽयुक्तस्य नानार्थो भ्रमः स गुण-दोष-भाक् । कर्माकर्म-विकर्मेति गुण-दोष-धियो भिदा
verses.transliteration: puṃso 'yuktasya nānārtho bhramaḥ sa guṇa-doṣa-bhāk / karmākarma-vikarmeti guṇa-doṣa-dhiyo bhidā
verses.translation: Someone not [spiritually] connected, is confused about many values and assumes things to be right or wrong. Thus considering good and evil he makes a difference between right action, no action and wrong action [he judges, see further B.G. 4: 16].

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् युक्तेन्द्रिय-ग्रामो युक्त-चित्त इदम् जगत् । आत्मनीक्षस्व विततम् आत्मानं मय्य् अधीश्वरे
verses.transliteration: tasmād yuktendriya-grāmo yukta-citta idam jagat / ātmanīkṣasva vitatam ātmānaṃ mayy adhīśvare
verses.translation: Consider therefore, with your senses under control and your mind connected, this world as situated within the Self that expanded everywhere, and that Self as being situated in Me, the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञान-विज्ञान-संयुक्त आत्म-भूतः शरीरिणाम् । अत्मानुभव-तुष्टात्मा नान्तरायैर् विहन्यसे
verses.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-saṃyukta ātma-bhūtaḥ śarīriṇām / atmānubhava-tuṣṭātmā nāntarāyair vihanyase
verses.translation: Fully endowed with knowledge and wisdom, being satisfied in one's mind and of understanding with the Self that for every embodied soul constitutes the object of affection, one is never discouraged by hindrances.

verses.sanskrit: दोष-बुद्ध्योभयातीतो निषेधान् न निवर्तते । गुण-बुद्ध्या च विहितं न करोति यथार्भकः
verses.transliteration: doṣa-buddhyobhayātīto niṣedhān na nivartate / guṇa-buddhyā ca vihitaṃ na karoti yathārbhakaḥ
verses.translation: Having risen above the two of [right and] wrong, he does not turn away from what is forbidden, thinking it is bad, nor does he engage in what is enjoined because of considering it good - like a young child he does not judge.

verses.sanskrit: सर्व-भूत-सुहृच् छान्तो ज्ञान-विज्ञान-निश्चयः । पश्यन् मद्-आत्मकं विश्वं न विपद्येत वै पुनः
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-suhṛc chānto jñāna-vijñāna-niścayaḥ / paśyan mad-ātmakaṃ viśvaṃ na vipadyeta vai punaḥ
verses.translation: When one, firmly being fixed in knowledge and wisdom, sees the universe as being pervaded by Me and peacefully, as a well-wisher, acts towards all living beings, one will never again fall into [the] misfortune [of repeated births].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच इत्य् आदिष्टो भगवता महा-भागवतो नृप । उद्धवः प्रणिपत्याह तत्त्वं जिज्ञासुर् अच्युतम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca ity ādiṣṭo bhagavatā mahā-bhāgavato nṛpa / uddhavaḥ praṇipatyāha tattvaṃ jijñāsur acyutam
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Oh King, after thus by the Supreme Lord having been instructed, the exalted and fortunate Uddhava, eager to learn about the supreme principle, bowed down to the Infallible Lord to offer his obeisances and spoke.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच योगेश योग-विन्यास योगात्मन् योग-सम्भव । निःश्रेयसाय मे प्रोक्तस् त्यागः सन्न्यास-लक्षणः
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yogeśa yoga-vinyāsa yogātman yoga-sambhava / niḥśreyasāya me proktas tyāgaḥ sannyāsa-lakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lord of Yoga, oh Unity keeping us together, oh Essence of uniting in consciousness and Source of mystical power, You spoke to my advantage about the forsaking as is known in the renounced order [sannysa].

verses.sanskrit: त्यागो ऽयं दुष्करो भूमन् कामानां विषयात्मभिः । सुतरां त्वयि सर्वात्मन्न् अभक्तैर् इति मे मतिः
verses.transliteration: tyāgo 'yaṃ duṣkaro bhūman kāmānāṃ viṣayātmabhiḥ / sutarāṃ tvayi sarvātmann abhaktair iti me matiḥ
verses.translation: This renunciation is difficult to perform, my Lord, when one is dedicated to [the not regulated love of one's] lust and sense gratification, in particular when one is not devoted to You, I think [compare B.G. 6: 33-34].

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं ममाहम् इति मूढ-मतिर् विगाढस् त्वन्-मायया विरचितात्मनि सानुबन्धे तत् त्व् अञ्जसा निगदितं भवता यथाहं संसाधयामि भगवन्न् अनुशाधि भृत्यम्
verses.transliteration: so 'haṃ mamāham iti mūḍha-matir vigāḍhas $ tvan-māyayā viracitātmani sānubandhe &tat tv añjasā nigaditaṃ bhavatā yathāhaṃ % saṃsādhayāmi bhagavann anuśādhi bhṛtyam
verses.translation: With my consciousness merged with the body and its relations, as arranged by Your my, I am thus foolish [being caught in the notion] of 'I' and 'mine'. Teach me therefore, so that Your servant may easily perform according to the process You teach.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यस्य ते स्व-दृश आत्मन आत्मनो ऽन्यं वक्तारम् ईश विबुधेष्व् अपि नानुचक्षे सत्यस्य ते स्व-दृश आत्मन आत्मनो ऽन्यं वक्तारम् ईश विबुधेष्व् अपि नानुचक्षे सर्वे विमोहित-धियस् तव माययेमे ब्रह्मादयस् तनु-भृतो बहिर्-अर्थ-भावाः
verses.transliteration: satyasya te sva-dṛśa ātmana ātmano 'nyaṃ $ vaktāram īśa vibudheṣv api nānucakṣe &satyasya te sva-dṛśa ātmana ātmano 'nyaṃ $ vaktāram īśa vibudheṣv api nānucakṣe &sarve vimohita-dhiyas tava māyayeme % brahmādayas tanu-bhṛto bahir-artha-bhāvāḥ
verses.translation: Who else is there but You who are of the Truth and personally reveal Yourself to me? Who else but my Lord, the Supreme Soul, does actually qualify for this? Not even among the awakened souls I find such a one. Everyone up to the one led by Brahm, is in his consciousness an embodied soul who, when he takes the externa c l world for substantial, is bewildered by Your my.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् भवन्तम् अनवद्यम् अनन्त-पारं सर्व-ज्ञम् ईश्वरम् अकुण्ठ-विकुण्ठ-धिष्ण्यम् तस्माद् भवन्तम् अनवद्यम् अनन्त-पारं सर्व-ज्ञम् ईश्वरम् अकुण्ठ-विकुण्ठ-धिष्ण्यम् निर्विण्ण-धीर् अहम् उ हे वृजिनाभितप्तो नारायणं नर-सखं शरणं प्रपद्ये
verses.transliteration: tasmād bhavantam anavadyam ananta-pāraṃ $ sarva-jñam īśvaram akuṇṭha-vikuṇṭha-dhiṣṇyam &tasmād bhavantam anavadyam ananta-pāraṃ $ sarva-jñam īśvaram akuṇṭha-vikuṇṭha-dhiṣṇyam &nirviṇṇa-dhīr aham u he vṛjinābhitapto % nārāyaṇaṃ nara-sakhaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadye
verses.translation: I who with my mind, in renunciation am tormented by distress, approach You therefore for shelter Nryana, oh Friend of Man, oh You perfect, unlimited and omniscient Lord ever fresh in Your abode of Vaikunthha.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच प्रायेण मनुजा लोके लोक-तत्त्व-विचक्षणाः । समुद्धरन्ति ह्य् आत्मानम् आत्मनैवाशुभाशयात्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca prāyeṇa manujā loke loka-tattva-vicakṣaṇāḥ / samuddharanti hy ātmānam ātmanaivāśubhāśayāt
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Human beings well acquainted with the state of affairs in this world, generally deliver themselves, with the help of their own intelligence, from the inauspicious disposition [of the 'I' and 'mine' perspective].

verses.sanskrit: आत्मनो गुरुर् आत्मैव पुरुषस्य विशेषतः । यत् प्रत्यक्षानुमानाभ्यां श्रेयो ऽसाव् अनुविन्दते
verses.transliteration: ātmano gurur ātmaiva puruṣasya viśeṣataḥ / yat pratyakṣānumānābhyāṃ śreyo 'sāv anuvindate
verses.translation: A person in a way constitutes his own guru because he, with the help of his reasoning and direct perception [his self-instruction], may find his [real] benefit.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषत्वे च मां धीराः साङ्ख्य-योग-विशारदाः । आविस्तरां प्रपश्यन्ति सर्व-शक्त्य्-उपबृंहितम्
verses.transliteration: puruṣatve ca māṃ dhīrāḥ sāṅkhya-yoga-viśāradāḥ / āvistarāṃ prapaśyanti sarva-śakty-upabṛṃhitam
verses.translation: They who are wise and experienced with the order of [sankhya or analytic] yoga, can see Me in their human existence, clearly manifested in My full glory, with all My energies [see also Kapila].

verses.sanskrit: एक-द्वि-त्रि-चतुस्-पादो बहु-पादस् तथापदः । बह्व्यः सन्ति पुरः सृष्टास् तासां मे पौरुषी प्रिया
verses.transliteration: eka-dvi-tri-catus-pādo bahu-pādas tathāpadaḥ / bahvyaḥ santi puraḥ sṛṣṭās tāsāṃ me pauruṣī priyā
verses.translation: Many types of bodies have evolved with one, two, three, four or more legs, or with none at all. The human form among these is the one most dear to Me [see also 3.29: 30, 6.4: 9].

verses.sanskrit: अत्र मां मृगयन्त्य् अद्धा युक्ता हेतुभिर् ईश्वरम् । गृह्यमाणैर् गुणैर् लिङ्गैर् अग्राह्यम् अनुमानतः
verses.transliteration: atra māṃ mṛgayanty addhā yuktā hetubhir īśvaram / gṛhyamāṇair guṇair liṅgair agrāhyam anumānataḥ
verses.translation: In this world being situated in such a body, one may look for Me, the Supreme Controller, by following direct signs [in bhakti listening and meditating] with the help of one's qualities of perception [intelligence, mind and senses]. [But in mere jna] by logical reasoning following indirect symptoms [- the ones of My creation -], I cannot be perceived [as a person and am even rejected, see also 2.2: 35, 2.9: 36].

verses.sanskrit: अत्राप्य् उदाहरन्तीमम् इतिहासं पुरातनम् । अवधूतस्य संवादं यदोर् अमित-तेजसः
verses.transliteration: atrāpy udāharantīmam itihāsaṃ purātanam / avadhūtasya saṃvādaṃ yador amita-tejasaḥ
verses.translation: Concerning this one cites the following ancient story of a conversation between the oh so mighty king Yadu and an avadhta.

verses.sanskrit: अवधूतं द्वियं कञ्चिच् चरन्तम् अकुतो-भयम् । कविं निरीक्ष्य तरुणं यदुः पप्रच्छ धर्म-वित्
verses.transliteration: avadhūtaṃ dviyaṃ kañcic carantam akuto-bhayam / kaviṃ nirīkṣya taruṇaṃ yaduḥ papraccha dharma-vit
verses.translation: Yadu, who was well versed in the dharma, once saw a young brahmin mendicant wandering around unafraid of anything and took the opportunity to ask him questions [see also 7.13].

verses.sanskrit: श्री-यदुर् उवाच कुतो बुद्धिर् इयं ब्रह्मन्न् अकर्तुः सु-विशारदा । याम् आसाद्य भवाल् लोकं विद्वांश् चरति बाल-वत्
verses.transliteration: śrī-yadur uvāca kuto buddhir iyaṃ brahmann akartuḥ su-viśāradā / yām āsādya bhavāl lokaṃ vidvāṃś carati bāla-vat
verses.translation: S'r Yadu said: 'How did you acquire this extraordinary intelligence, oh brahmin? How can you, fully cognizant not being engaged in any work, travel the world with the confidence of a child?

verses.sanskrit: प्रायो धर्मार्थ-कामेषु विवित्सायां च मानवाः । हेतुनैव समीहन्त आयुषो यशसः श्रियः
verses.transliteration: prāyo dharmārtha-kāmeṣu vivitsāyāṃ ca mānavāḥ / hetunaiva samīhanta āyuṣo yaśasaḥ śriyaḥ
verses.translation: People who are religious, work for an income, gratify their senses and pursue knowledge, are normally endeavoring for the purpose of opulence, a good name and a long life.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु कल्पः कविर् दक्षः सु-भगो ऽमृत-भाषणः । न कर्ता नेहसे किञ्चिज् जडोन्मत्त-पिशाच-वत्
verses.transliteration: tvaṃ tu kalpaḥ kavir dakṣaḥ su-bhago 'mṛta-bhāṣaṇaḥ / na kartā nehase kiñcij jaḍonmatta-piśāca-vat
verses.translation: You however, capable, learned, experienced, handsome and eloquent as you are, are not a doer and do not desire a thing, like a stupefied, maddened, ghostly creature.

verses.sanskrit: जनेषु दह्यमानेषु काम-लोभ-दवाग्निना । न तप्यसे ऽग्निना मुक्तो गङ्गाम्भः-स्थ इव द्विपः
verses.transliteration: janeṣu dahyamāneṣu kāma-lobha-davāgninā / na tapyase 'gninā mukto gaṅgāmbhaḥ-stha iva dvipaḥ
verses.translation: Everyone burns in the forest fire of lust and greed, but you stand, to be free from the fire, like an elephant in the Ganges and are not burned.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं हि नः पृच्छतां ब्रह्मन्न् आत्मन्य् आनन्द-कारणम् । ब्रूहि स्पर्श-विहीनस्य भवतः केवलात्मनः
verses.transliteration: tvaṃ hi naḥ pṛcchatāṃ brahmann ātmany ānanda-kāraṇam / brūhi sparśa-vihīnasya bhavataḥ kevalātmanaḥ
verses.translation: We ask you, oh brahmin, to please tell us what the cause is of the inner happiness that you, living all by yourself, experience without any form of material enjoyment.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच यदुनैवं महा-भागो ब्रह्मण्येन सु-मेधसा । पृष्टः सभाजितः प्राह प्रश्रयावनतं द्विजः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yadunaivaṃ mahā-bhāgo brahmaṇyena su-medhasā / pṛṣṭaḥ sabhājitaḥ prāha praśrayāvanataṃ dvijaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The brahmin thus being asked and honored by the greatly fortunate and intelligent Yadu, who out of his respect for brahmins humbly bowed his head, then spoke.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-ब्राह्मण उवाच सन्ति मे गुरवो राजन् बहवो बुद्ध्य्-उपश्रिताः । यतो बुद्धिम् उपादाय मुक्तो ऽटामीह तान् शृणु
verses.transliteration: śrī-brāhmaṇa uvāca santi me guravo rājan bahavo buddhy-upaśritāḥ / yato buddhim upādāya mukto 'ṭāmīha tān śṛṇu
verses.translation: The honorable brahmin said: 'There are many spiritual masters I took shelter of by my intelligence, oh King. Having learned to understand through them I now, being freed, wander around in this world. Please listen to their description.

verses.sanskrit: पृथिवी वायुर् आकाशम् आपो ऽग्निश् चन्द्रमा रविः । कपोतो ऽजगरः सिन्धुः पतङ्गो मधुकृद् गजः
verses.transliteration: pṛthivī vāyur ākāśam āpo 'gniś candramā raviḥ / kapoto 'jagaraḥ sindhuḥ pataṅgo madhukṛd gajaḥ
verses.translation: The earth, the air, the sky, the water, the fire, the moon and the sun, the pigeon, the python, the sea, the moth, the honeybee and the elephant, the honey thief, the deer, the fish, the prostitute [Pingal], the osprey and the child, the girl, the arrow maker, the serpent, the spider and the wasp, are my twenty-four spiritual masters, oh King. From studying their actions I in this life have learned everything about the Self.

verses.sanskrit: मधु-हा हरिणो मीनः पिङ्गला कुररो ऽर्भकः । कुमारी शर-कृत् सर्प ऊर्णनाभिः सुपेशकृत्
verses.transliteration: madhu-hā hariṇo mīnaḥ piṅgalā kuraro 'rbhakaḥ / kumārī śara-kṛt sarpa ūrṇanābhiḥ supeśakṛt
verses.translation: The earth, the air, the sky, the water, the fire, the moon and the sun, the pigeon, the python, the sea, the moth, the honeybee and the elephant, the honey thief, the deer, the fish, the prostitute [Pingal], the osprey and the child, the girl, the arrow maker, the serpent, the spider and the wasp, are my twenty-four spiritual masters, oh King. From studying their actions I in this life have learned everything about the Self.

verses.sanskrit: एते मे गुरवो राजन् चतुर्-विंशतिर् आश्रिताः । शिक्षा वृत्तिभिर् एतेषाम् अन्वशिक्षम् इहात्मनः
verses.transliteration: ete me guravo rājan catur-viṃśatir āśritāḥ / śikṣā vṛttibhir eteṣām anvaśikṣam ihātmanaḥ
verses.translation: The earth, the air, the sky, the water, the fire, the moon and the sun, the pigeon, the python, the sea, the moth, the honeybee and the elephant, the honey thief, the deer, the fish, the prostitute [Pingal], the osprey and the child, the girl, the arrow maker, the serpent, the spider and the wasp, are my twenty-four spiritual masters, oh King. From studying their actions I in this life have learned everything about the Self.

verses.sanskrit: यतो यद् अनुशिक्षामि यथा वा नाहुषात्मज । तत् तथा पुरुष-व्याघ्र निबोध कथयामि ते
verses.transliteration: yato yad anuśikṣāmi yathā vā nāhuṣātmaja / tat tathā puruṣa-vyāghra nibodha kathayāmi te
verses.translation: Listen, oh son of Nhusha [or Yayti], oh tiger among men, I shall tell you what I have learned from each of them separately.

verses.sanskrit: भूतैर् आक्रम्यमाणो ऽपि धीरो दैव-वशानुगैः । तद् विद्वान् न चलेन् मार्गाद् अन्वशिक्षं क्षितेर् व्रतम्
verses.transliteration: bhūtair ākramyamāṇo 'pi dhīro daiva-vaśānugaiḥ / tad vidvān na calen mārgād anvaśikṣaṃ kṣiter vratam
verses.translation: From the earth I learned the rule that a learned person should not deviate from the path and keep steady, however much he is harassed by his fellow living beings who simply follow what is arranged by fate.

verses.sanskrit: शश्वत् परार्थ-सर्वेहः परार्थैकान्त-सम्भवः । साधुः शिक्षेत भू-भृत्तो नग-शिष्यः परात्मताम्
verses.transliteration: śaśvat parārtha-sarvehaḥ parārthaikānta-sambhavaḥ / sādhuḥ śikṣeta bhū-bhṛtto naga-śiṣyaḥ parātmatām
verses.translation: From the mountain one must learn to be always there for others, that one must devote all one's actions to the welfare of others. In following the example of a tree, for a pious person to be dedicated to others [see S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka-3] constitutes the sole reason for his existence [see also 10.22: 31-35 and B.G. 17: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: प्राण-वृत्त्यैव सन्तुष्येन् मुनिर् नैवेन्द्रिय-प्रियैः । ज्ञानं यथा न नश्येत नावकीर्येत वाङ्-मनः
verses.transliteration: prāṇa-vṛttyaiva santuṣyen munir naivendriya-priyaiḥ / jñānaṃ yathā na naśyeta nāvakīryeta vāṅ-manaḥ
verses.translation: A sage should be happy with the mere movement of his vital air and not seek his satisfaction in sense gratification. His spiritual knowing will thus not be lost and his mind and speech will not be distracted.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेष्व् आविशन् योगी नाना-धर्मेषु सर्वतः । गुण-दोष-व्यपेतात्मा न विषज्जेत वायु-वत्
verses.transliteration: viṣayeṣv āviśan yogī nānā-dharmeṣu sarvataḥ / guṇa-doṣa-vyapetātmā na viṣajjeta vāyu-vat
verses.translation: A yogi free from selfhood should, just like the wind, never get entangled in relating to the objects of the senses and all their different favorable and unfavorable qualities.

verses.sanskrit: पार्थिवेष्व् इह देहेषु प्रविष्टस् तद्-गुणाश्रयः । गुणैर् न युज्यते योगी गन्धैर् वायुर् इवात्म-दृक्
verses.transliteration: pārthiveṣv iha deheṣu praviṣṭas tad-guṇāśrayaḥ / guṇair na yujyate yogī gandhair vāyur ivātma-dṛk
verses.translation: When a self-realized soul has entered different bodies made of earth [elements] in this world and is endowed with their different qualities, he, well aware of himself, will not connect himself with these qualities, just like the wind does not with different odors.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तर्हितश् च स्थिर-जङ्गमेषु ब्रह्मात्म-भावेन समन्वयेन । व्याप्त्याव्यवच्छेदम् असङ्गम् आत्मनो मुनिर् नभस्त्वं विततस्य भावयेत्
verses.transliteration: antarhitaś ca sthira-jaṅgameṣu brahmātma-bhāvena samanvayena / vyāptyāvyavacchedam asaṅgam ātmano munir nabhastvaṃ vitatasya bhāvayet
verses.translation: A sage should meditate on the soul stretched out in all moving and nonmoving living beings, and thereby, with his different contacts [embodiments], consider himself a pure spirit, equal to the ether that expands everywhere [see also B.G. 2: 24, 3: 15, 6: 29-30, 9: 6, 11: 17, 12: 3-4 and 13: 14].

verses.sanskrit: तेजो-ऽब्-अन्न-मयैर् भावैर् मेघाद्यैर् वायुनेरितैः । न स्पृश्यते नभस् तद्वत् काल-सृष्टैर् गुणैः पुमान्
verses.transliteration: tejo-'b-anna-mayair bhāvair meghādyair vāyuneritaiḥ / na spṛśyate nabhas tadvat kāla-sṛṣṭair guṇaiḥ pumān
verses.translation: Just as the realm of the ether is not touched by the winds that blow the clouds, a person [in his real self] is not affected by his physical bodies consisting of fire, water and earth, that are moved by the basic qualities of nature created by Time.

verses.sanskrit: स्वच्छः प्रकृतितः स्निग्धो माधुर्यस् तीर्थ-भूर् नृणाम् । मुनिः पुनात्य् अपां मित्रम् ईक्षोपस्पर्श-कीर्तनैः
verses.transliteration: svacchaḥ prakṛtitaḥ snigdho mādhuryas tīrtha-bhūr nṛṇām / muniḥ punāty apāṃ mitram īkṣopasparśa-kīrtanaiḥ
verses.translation: A sage who by nature is a pure, softhearted, sweet and gentle place of pilgrimage for human beings, sanctifies, just like water, the souls who gather [the friends], by being seen by them, touched and honored [see also skhya].

verses.sanskrit: तेजस्वी तपसा दीप्तो दुर्धर्षोदर-भाजनः । सर्व-भक्ष्यो ऽपि युक्तात्मा नादत्ते मलम् अग्नि-वत्
verses.transliteration: tejasvī tapasā dīpto durdharṣodara-bhājanaḥ / sarva-bhakṣyo 'pi yuktātmā nādatte malam agni-vat
verses.translation: Brilliant, glowing and immovable because of his austerity, he who only eats when it is necessary is connected in the soul. Even when he eats everything [and thus goes beyond necessity], he does not lose his purity, just like a fire does not [irrespective of what it consumes].

verses.sanskrit: क्वचिच् छन्नः क्वचित् स्पष्ट उपास्यः श्रेय इच्छताम् । भुङ्क्ते सर्वत्र दातृणां दहन् प्राग्-उत्तराशुभम्
verses.transliteration: kvacic channaḥ kvacit spaṣṭa upāsyaḥ śreya icchatām / bhuṅkte sarvatra dātṛṇāṃ dahan prāg-uttarāśubham
verses.translation: Sometimes [like a fire under ashes being] concealed, sometimes being manifested and being worshipable to those who desire the real benefit, he [the sage, when he serves as their guru] always enjoys their offerings and burns both their past and subsequent [present] misfortune [see also 10.81: 4 and B.G. 3: 14].

verses.sanskrit: स्व-मायया सृष्टम् इदं सद्-असल्-लक्षणं विभुः । प्रविष्ट ईयते तत्-तत्- स्वरूपो ऽग्निर् इवैधसि
verses.transliteration: sva-māyayā sṛṣṭam idaṃ sad-asal-lakṣaṇaṃ vibhuḥ / praviṣṭa īyate tat-tat- svarūpo 'gnir ivaidhasi
verses.translation: The Almighty One assumes the identity of each after, just like fire appearing in firewood, having entered the different types of bodies of the higher and lower life forms He created by His potency ['true' and 'untrue' ones, god or animal].

verses.sanskrit: विसर्गाद्याः श्मशानान्ता भावा देहस्य नात्मनः । कलानाम् इव चन्द्रस्य कालेनाव्यक्त-वर्त्मना
verses.transliteration: visargādyāḥ śmaśānāntā bhāvā dehasya nātmanaḥ / kalānām iva candrasya kālenāvyakta-vartmanā
verses.translation: The state of the body [one undergoes] from one's birth until one's death, changes by the course of Time that itself cannot be seen; it is the body that changes, not the soul, just as the phases of the moon [change, but not the moon itself, B.G. 2: 13, 2: 20].

verses.sanskrit: कालेन ह्य् ओघ-वेगेन भूतानां प्रभवाप्ययौ । नित्याव् अपि न दृश्येते आत्मनो ऽग्नेर् यथार्चिषाम्
verses.transliteration: kālena hy ogha-vegena bhūtānāṃ prabhavāpyayau / nityāv api na dṛśyete ātmano 'gner yathārciṣām
verses.translation: Just as with flames [which one cannot see apart] from a fire individual souls cannot be seen separately from the bodies that constantly die and are born again, also the [absolute of] Time itself cannot be seen, despite [the relativity of] its speeding, compelling stream [*].

verses.sanskrit: गुणैर् गुणान् उपादत्ते यथा-कालं विमुञ्चति । न तेषु युज्यते योगी गोभिर् गा इव गो-पतिः
verses.transliteration: guṇair guṇān upādatte yathā-kālaṃ vimuñcati / na teṣu yujyate yogī gobhir gā iva go-patiḥ
verses.translation: A yogi with his senses accepts and forsakes sense objects depending the moment [according to the cakra order] and does not attach to them, just as the sun, with its rays engaged in [evaporating and returning] bodies of water, is not ruled by them.

verses.sanskrit: बुध्यते स्वे न भेदेन व्यक्ति-स्थ इव तद्-गतः । लक्ष्यते स्थूल-मतिभिर् आत्मा चावस्थितो ऽर्क-वत्
verses.transliteration: budhyate sve na bhedena vyakti-stha iva tad-gataḥ / lakṣyate sthūla-matibhir ātmā cāvasthito 'rka-vat
verses.translation: When the sun seems to have fallen apart in its reflections one, unless one is dull-witted, does not consider its original form as being different. Similarly the soul, despite of having entered in reflections [of different selves], is not seen as different.

verses.sanskrit: नाति-स्नेहः प्रसङ्गो वा कर्तव्यः क्वापि केनचित् । कुर्वन् विन्देत सन्तापं कपोत इव दीन-धीः
verses.transliteration: nāti-snehaḥ prasaṅgo vā kartavyaḥ kvāpi kenacit / kurvan vindeta santāpaṃ kapota iva dīna-dhīḥ
verses.translation: One should never lose oneself in too much affection or close association with anyone, because thus indulging one will suffer great distress, just like a foolish pigeon [see also 7.2: 50-56].

verses.sanskrit: कपोतः कश्चनारण्ये कृत-नीडो वनस्पतौ । कपोत्या भार्यया सार्धम् उवास कतिचित् समाः
verses.transliteration: kapotaḥ kaścanāraṇye kṛta-nīḍo vanaspatau / kapotyā bhāryayā sārdham uvāsa katicit samāḥ
verses.translation: A certain pigeon once in the forest built its nest in a tree and dwelt there for some years with a female companion.

verses.sanskrit: कपोतौ स्नेह-गुणित- हृदयौ गृह-धर्मिणौ । दृष्टिं दृष्ट्याङ्गम् अङ्गेन बुद्धिं बुद्ध्या बबन्धतुः
verses.transliteration: kapotau sneha-guṇita- hṛdayau gṛha-dharmiṇau / dṛṣṭiṃ dṛṣṭyāṅgam aṅgena buddhiṃ buddhyā babandhatuḥ
verses.translation: The pigeons, with their hearts full of love, lived a householder's life whereby their glances, bodies and minds were tied to each other [like with ropes].

verses.sanskrit: शय्यासनाटन-स्थान वार्ता-क्रीडाशनादिकम् । मिथुनी-भूय विश्रब्धौ चेरतुर् वन-राजिषु
verses.transliteration: śayyāsanāṭana-sthāna vārtā-krīḍāśanādikam / mithunī-bhūya viśrabdhau ceratur vana-rājiṣu
verses.translation: Trusting each other making love, they in the trees of the forest were engaged in resting, sitting, walking, standing, communicating, playing, eating, and so on.

verses.sanskrit: यं यं वाञ्छति सा राजन् तर्पयन्त्य् अनुकम्पिता । तं तं समनयत् कामं कृच्छ्रेणाप्य् अजितेन्द्रियः
verses.transliteration: yaṃ yaṃ vāñchati sā rājan tarpayanty anukampitā / taṃ taṃ samanayat kāmaṃ kṛcchreṇāpy ajitendriyaḥ
verses.translation: Whatever she would like, oh King, was what he, desirous to please her, did. He mercifully catered to all her desires, even when it was difficult, and had no control over his senses.

verses.sanskrit: कपोती प्रथमं गर्भं गृह्णन्ती काल आगते । अण्डानि सुषुवे नीडे स्त-पत्युः सन्निधौ सती
verses.transliteration: kapotī prathamaṃ garbhaṃ gṛhṇantī kāla āgate / aṇḍāni suṣuve nīḍe sta-patyuḥ sannidhau satī
verses.translation: The chaste female pigeon got pregnant for the first time and delivered, in due course, in the nest the eggs in the presence of her husband.

verses.sanskrit: तेषु काले व्यजायन्त रचितावयवा हरेः । शक्तिभिर् दुर्विभाव्याभिः कोमलाङ्ग-तनूरुहाः
verses.transliteration: teṣu kāle vyajāyanta racitāvayavā hareḥ / śaktibhir durvibhāvyābhiḥ komalāṅga-tanūruhāḥ
verses.translation: From them at the appropriate time the little ones hatched with the tender limbs and feathers that were created by the inconceivable potencies of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: प्रजाः पुपुषतुः प्रीतौ दम्पती पुत्र-वत्सलौ । शृण्वन्तौ कूजितं तासां निर्वृतौ कल-भाषितैः
verses.transliteration: prajāḥ pupuṣatuḥ prītau dampatī putra-vatsalau / śṛṇvantau kūjitaṃ tāsāṃ nirvṛtau kala-bhāṣitaiḥ
verses.translation: The couple then, most pleased, nourished their progeny, to which they compassionately listened to the awkward sounds of the chirping children that surrounded them.

verses.sanskrit: तासां पतत्रैः सु-स्पर्शैः कूजितैर् मुग्ध-चेष्टितैः । प्रत्युद्गमैर् अदीनानां पितरौ मुदम् आपतुः
verses.transliteration: tāsāṃ patatraiḥ su-sparśaiḥ kūjitair mugdha-ceṣṭitaiḥ / pratyudgamair adīnānāṃ pitarau mudam āpatuḥ
verses.translation: To see the little ones, happy with their fluffy wings, their endearing chirping and their activities of jumping up to fly, filled the parents with joy.

verses.sanskrit: स्नेहानुबद्ध-हृदयाव् अन्योन्यं विष्णु-मायया । विमोहितौ दीन-धियौ शिशून् पुपुषतुः प्रजाः
verses.transliteration: snehānubaddha-hṛdayāv anyonyaṃ viṣṇu-māyayā / vimohitau dīna-dhiyau śiśūn pupuṣatuḥ prajāḥ
verses.translation: With their hearts bound together by their affection, they, not giving it any further thought, completely being bewildered by the illusory potency of Vishnu, fed their children, their offspring.

verses.sanskrit: एकदा जग्मतुस् तासाम् अन्नार्थं तौ कुटुम्बिनौ । परितः कानने तस्मिन्न् अर्थिनौ चेरतुश् चिरम्
verses.transliteration: ekadā jagmatus tāsām annārthaṃ tau kuṭumbinau / paritaḥ kānane tasminn arthinau ceratuś ciram
verses.translation: One day the two heads of the family left for finding food for the children and wandered far away, most anxiously searching all around in the forest.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तान् लुब्धकः कश्चिद् यदृच्छातो वने-चरः । जगृहे जालम् आतत्य चरतः स्वालयान्तिके
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tān lubdhakaḥ kaścid yadṛcchāto vane-caraḥ / jagṛhe jālam ātatya carataḥ svālayāntike
verses.translation: Some hunter, who happened to pass through the forest, saw the young birds moving near their nest and caught them with a net he had spread.

verses.sanskrit: कपोतश् च कपोती च प्रजा-पोषे सदोत्सुकौ । गतौ पोषणम् आदाय स्व-नीडम् उपजग्मतुः
verses.transliteration: kapotaś ca kapotī ca prajā-poṣe sadotsukau / gatau poṣaṇam ādāya sva-nīḍam upajagmatuḥ
verses.translation: The male and female pigeon, who were always eagerly engaged in taking care of their children, thereupon returned to the nest to bring them food.

verses.sanskrit: कपोती स्वात्मजान् वीक्ष्य बालकान् जाल-सम्वृतान् । तान् अभ्यधावत् क्रोशन्ती क्रोशतो भृश-दुःखिता
verses.transliteration: kapotī svātmajān vīkṣya bālakān jāla-samvṛtān / tān abhyadhāvat krośantī krośato bhṛśa-duḥkhitā
verses.translation: The female pigeon saw that the little ones born from her, her children, were trapped in the net and rushed forward, in utter distress crying out to them who were also crying.

verses.sanskrit: सासकृत् स्नेह-गुणिता दीन-चित्ताज-मायया । स्वयं चाबध्यत शिचा बद्धान् पश्यन्त्य् अपस्मृतिः
verses.transliteration: sāsakṛt sneha-guṇitā dīna-cittāja-māyayā / svayaṃ cābadhyata śicā baddhān paśyanty apasmṛtiḥ
verses.translation: Bound to her love constantly she had looked after her children without a thought for herself, and so she, overwhelmed by the my of the Unborn One, forgot about herself and was also trapped in the net.

verses.sanskrit: कपोतः स्वात्मजान् बद्धान् आत्मनो ऽप्य् अधिकान् प्रियान् । भार्यां चात्म-समां दीनो विललापाति-दुःखितः
verses.transliteration: kapotaḥ svātmajān baddhān ātmano 'py adhikān priyān / bhāryāṃ cātma-samāṃ dīno vilalāpāti-duḥkhitaḥ
verses.translation: The unfortunate male pigeon most miserably lamented that his children and his wife, so much alike him, had been caught. They were more dear to him than his life:

verses.sanskrit: अहो मे पश्यतापायम् अल्प-पुण्यस्य दुर्मतेः । अतृप्तस्याकृतार्थस्य गृहस् त्रै-वर्गिको हतः
verses.transliteration: aho me paśyatāpāyam alpa-puṇyasya durmateḥ / atṛptasyākṛtārthasya gṛhas trai-vargiko hataḥ
verses.translation: 'Alas, just see how I, so unintelligent and of little merit, find my destruction. Unfulfilled I failed in my life's purpose and have ruined my family life, the threefold path [of the purushrthas]!

verses.sanskrit: अनुरूपानुकूला च यस्य मे पति-देवता । शून्ये गृहे मां सन्त्यज्य पुत्रैः स्वर् याति साधुभिः
verses.transliteration: anurūpānukūlā ca yasya me pati-devatā / śūnye gṛhe māṃ santyajya putraiḥ svar yāti sādhubhiḥ
verses.translation: She who suitable and faithful accepted me as her husband, as her god, has gone to heaven with her saintly children, leaving me behind in an empty house.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं शून्ये गृहे दीनो मृत-दारो मृत-प्रजः । जिजीविषे किम् अर्थं वा विधुरो दुःख-जीवितः
verses.transliteration: so 'haṃ śūnye gṛhe dīno mṛta-dāro mṛta-prajaḥ / jijīviṣe kim arthaṃ vā vidhuro duḥkha-jīvitaḥ
verses.translation: What now is the purpose of my life with my wife and children dead? What should I live for, miserable and wretched living in an empty nest?'

verses.sanskrit: तांस् तथैवावृतान् शिग्भिर् मृत्यु-ग्रस्तान् विचेष्टतः । स्वयं च कृपणः शिक्षु पश्यन्न् अप्य् अबुधो ऽपतत्
verses.transliteration: tāṃs tathaivāvṛtān śigbhir mṛtyu-grastān viceṣṭataḥ / svayaṃ ca kṛpaṇaḥ śikṣu paśyann apy abudho 'patat
verses.translation: Seeing them caught within the net, still in the grip of death, he in misery sat motionless, and empty minded also landed in the net.

verses.sanskrit: तं लब्ध्वा लुब्धकः क्रूरः कपोतं गृह-मेधिनम् । कपोतकान् कपोतीं च सिद्धार्थः प्रययौ गृहम्
verses.transliteration: taṃ labdhvā lubdhakaḥ krūraḥ kapotaṃ gṛha-medhinam / kapotakān kapotīṃ ca siddhārthaḥ prayayau gṛham
verses.translation: The ruthless hunter, who had achieved his purpose, took the householder, his children and his pigeon wife and headed for his home.

verses.sanskrit: एवं कुटुम्ब्य् अशान्तात्मा द्वन्द्वारामः पतत्रि-वत् । पुष्णन् कुटुम्बं कृपणः सानुबन्धो ऽवसीदति
verses.transliteration: evaṃ kuṭumby aśāntātmā dvandvārāmaḥ patatri-vat / puṣṇan kuṭumbaṃ kṛpaṇaḥ sānubandho 'vasīdati
verses.translation: A family man who dissatisfied with the soul takes pleasure in material opposites [like that of man and wife], will [unmeditated] suffer greatly with his relatives, just like this bird so miserable in maintaining its family.

verses.sanskrit: यः प्राप्य मानुषं लोकं मुक्ति-द्वारम् अपावृतम् । गृहेषु खग-वत् सक्तस् तम् आरूढ-च्युतं विदुः
verses.transliteration: yaḥ prāpya mānuṣaṃ lokaṃ mukti-dvāram apāvṛtam / gṛheṣu khaga-vat saktas tam ārūḍha-cyutaṃ viduḥ
verses.translation: A person who achieved the human position, but with the door of liberation wide open, is attached to family affairs like this bird, may, to whatever height he might have reached, be considered fallen [see also 3.30, 3.32: 1-3, 4.28: 17, 5.26: 35, 7.14, 7.15: 38-39, 7.15: 67, 8.16: 9 and 10.69: 40].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-ब्राह्मण उवाच सुखम् ऐन्द्रियकं राजन् स्वर्गे नरक एव च । देहिनां यद् यथा दुःखं तस्मान् नेच्छेत तद्-बुधः
verses.transliteration: śrī-brāhmaṇa uvāca sukham aindriyakaṃ rājan svarge naraka eva ca / dehināṃ yad yathā duḥkhaṃ tasmān neccheta tad-budhaḥ
verses.translation: The honorable brahmin said: 'Since there is sensual happiness, oh King, in both heaven and hell, and also feelings of unhappiness exist for all embodied beings [as their counterpart], an intelligent person should not desire such happiness [see B.G. 2: 14].

verses.sanskrit: ग्रासं सु-मृष्टं विरसं महान्तं स्तोकम् एव वा । यदृच्छयैवापतितं ग्रसेद् आजगरो ऽक्रियः
verses.transliteration: grāsaṃ su-mṛṣṭaṃ virasaṃ mahāntaṃ stokam eva vā / yadṛcchayaivāpatitaṃ grased ājagaro 'kriyaḥ
verses.translation: He should eat, as passive as a python, what is acquired accidentally, whether it is much or little, tasteless or pure and delicious food [7.13: 37-38].

verses.sanskrit: शयीताहानि भूरीणि निराहारो ऽनुपक्रमः । यदि नोपनयेद् ग्रासो महाहिर् इव दिष्ट-भुक्
verses.transliteration: śayītāhāni bhūrīṇi nirāhāro 'nupakramaḥ / yadi nopanayed grāso mahāhir iva diṣṭa-bhuk
verses.translation: When no food is coming he, just like a big python that eats whatever providence provides, should lie down and fast for many days [7.15: 15].

verses.sanskrit: ओजः-सहो-बल-युतं बिभ्रद् देहम् अकर्मकम् । शयानो वीत-निद्रश् च नेहेतेन्द्रियवान् अपि
verses.transliteration: ojaḥ-saho-bala-yutaṃ bibhrad deham akarmakam / śayāno vīta-nidraś ca nehetendriyavān api
verses.translation: Both physically and mentally being strong, he, even though endowed with senses, should stay free from desire and, resting [but] clear-minded, carry his body without engaging in action.

verses.sanskrit: मुनिः प्रसन्न-गम्भीरो दुर्विगाह्यो दुरत्ययः । अनन्त-पारो ह्य् अक्षोभ्यः स्तिमितोद इवार्णवः
verses.transliteration: muniḥ prasanna-gambhīro durvigāhyo duratyayaḥ / ananta-pāro hy akṣobhyaḥ stimitoda ivārṇavaḥ
verses.translation: A sage is pleasing, grave, unfathomable, unlimited, unsurpassable [in his knowing] and never disturbed, just like the calm waters of the ocean [see also B.G. 12: 15].

verses.sanskrit: समृद्ध-कामो हीनो वा नारायण-परो मुनिः । नोत्सर्पेत न शुष्येत सरिद्भिर् इव सागरः
verses.transliteration: samṛddha-kāmo hīno vā nārāyaṇa-paro muniḥ / notsarpeta na śuṣyeta saridbhir iva sāgaraḥ
verses.translation: Someone wise who accepts Nryana as the One Supreme, does not dry up or swell, just like the ocean with its rivers, whether he flourishes to his liking or is penniless [B.G. 2: 70].

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा स्त्रियं देव-मायां तद्-भावैर् अजितेन्द्रियः । प्रलोभितः पतत्य् अन्धे तमस्य् अग्नौ पतङ्ग-वत्
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā striyaṃ deva-māyāṃ tad-bhāvair ajitendriyaḥ / pralobhitaḥ pataty andhe tamasy agnau pataṅga-vat
verses.translation: When someone who did not conquer his senses, sees a woman, he is tempted by that seductive illusory energy of the Lord and lands in darkness, just like a moth lands in the fire.

verses.sanskrit: योषिद्-धिरण्याभरणाम्बरादि- द्रव्येषु माया-रचितेषु मूढः । प्रलोभितात्मा ह्य् उपभोग-बुद्ध्या पतङ्ग-वन् नश्यति नष्ट-दृष्टिः
verses.transliteration: yoṣid-dhiraṇyābharaṇāmbarādi- dravyeṣu māyā-raciteṣu mūḍhaḥ / pralobhitātmā hy upabhoga-buddhyā pataṅga-van naśyati naṣṭa-dṛṣṭiḥ
verses.translation: Upon seeing the by my produced clothing, golden ornaments and so on of the women, a person lacking in discrimination will, with his desire for sense-gratification, feel aroused by lusty desires and no doubt lose his spiritual bearings, just like a moth is destroyed [B.G. 2: 62-63].

verses.sanskrit: स्तोकं स्तोकं ग्रसेद् ग्रासं देहो वर्तेत यावता । गृहान् अहिंसन्न् आतिष्ठेद् वृत्तिं माधुकरीं मुनिः
verses.transliteration: stokaṃ stokaṃ grased grāsaṃ deho varteta yāvatā / gṛhān ahiṃsann ātiṣṭhed vṛttiṃ mādhukarīṃ muniḥ
verses.translation: A sage has to practice the occupation of a honeybee by going door to door, without giving trouble, and only eat little bits of food, just enough to keep the body alive [5.5: 3, 7.2: 11-13, 7.12: 6. 7.14: 5, 7.15: 15 and B.G. 4: 21].

verses.sanskrit: अणुभ्यश् च महद्भ्यश् च शास्त्रेभ्यः कुशलो नरः । सर्वतः सारम् आदद्यात् पुष्पेभ्य इव षट्पदः
verses.transliteration: aṇubhyaś ca mahadbhyaś ca śāstrebhyaḥ kuśalo naraḥ / sarvataḥ sāram ādadyāt puṣpebhya iva ṣaṭpadaḥ
verses.translation: Just like a honeybee obtains its nectar from small and large flowers, a well versed man must extract the [Vedic] essence from both the smallest and the largest scriptures [11.7: 23, B.G. 15: 15].

verses.sanskrit: सायन्तनं श्वस्तनं वा न सङ्गृह्णीत भिक्षितम् । पाणि-पात्रोदरामत्रो मक्षिकेव न सङ्ग्रही
verses.transliteration: sāyantanaṃ śvastanaṃ vā na saṅgṛhṇīta bhikṣitam / pāṇi-pātrodarāmatro makṣikeva na saṅgrahī
verses.translation: With the belly as his container and the hand as his plate he should, not being a collector like a honeybee, accept food in charity and not keep it for the night or the next day.

verses.sanskrit: सायन्तनं श्वस्तनं वा न सङ्गृह्णीत भिक्षुकः । मक्षिका इव सङ्गृह्णन् सह तेन विनश्यति
verses.transliteration: sāyantanaṃ śvastanaṃ vā na saṅgṛhṇīta bhikṣukaḥ / makṣikā iva saṅgṛhṇan saha tena vinaśyati
verses.translation: A mendicant should not collect for the night or the following day, because he, just like a honeybee [collecting more and more], will lose himself thereby [in excess].

verses.sanskrit: पदापि युवतीं भिक्षुर् न स्पृशेद् दारवीम् अपि । स्पृशन् करीव बध्येत करिण्या अङ्ग-सङ्गतः
verses.transliteration: padāpi yuvatīṃ bhikṣur na spṛśed dāravīm api / spṛśan karīva badhyeta kariṇyā aṅga-saṅgataḥ
verses.translation: A mendicant must not touch a girl, not even with his foot, nor one of wood, because he otherwise will be captured by the physical contact, just like an elephant in the grip of a she-elephant.

verses.sanskrit: नाधिगच्छेत् स्त्रियं प्राज्ञः कर्हिचिन् मृत्युम् आत्मनः । बलाधिकैः स हन्येत गजैर् अन्यैर् गजो यथा
verses.transliteration: nādhigacchet striyaṃ prājñaḥ karhicin mṛtyum ātmanaḥ / balādhikaiḥ sa hanyeta gajair anyair gajo yathā
verses.translation: A man of intelligence should never try to get a woman, because he otherwise may find himself killed [because of a rival], the way an elephant will be destroyed by other elephants superior in strength.

verses.sanskrit: न देयं नोपभोग्यं च लुब्धैर् यद् दुःख-सञ्चितम् । भुङ्क्ते तद् अपि तच् चान्यो मधु-हेवार्थविन् मधु
verses.transliteration: na deyaṃ nopabhogyaṃ ca lubdhair yad duḥkha-sañcitam / bhuṅkte tad api tac cānyo madhu-hevārthavin madhu
verses.translation: Riches that with great difficulty are accumulated by a greedy person who neither enjoys them himself nor shares them with others, are rather enjoyed by someone else who steals them away, just like the honey collector does upon discovering honey [see also 5.13: 10].

verses.sanskrit: सु-दुःखोपार्जितैर् वित्तैर् आशासानां गृहाशिषः । मधु-हेवाग्रतो भुङ्क्ते यतिर् वै गृह-मेधिनाम्
verses.transliteration: su-duḥkhopārjitair vittair āśāsānāṃ gṛhāśiṣaḥ / madhu-hevāgrato bhuṅkte yatir vai gṛha-medhinām
verses.translation: The way a honey thief is the first one to enjoy the honey that was painstakingly collected, also the ascetic is the first one to enjoy the eagerly desired blessings of the wealth that with a lot of trouble was acquired by householders [see e.g. 1.19: 39 and 7.14: 17].

verses.sanskrit: ग्राम्य-गीतं न शृणुयाद् यतिर् वन-चरः क्वचित् । शिक्षेत हरिणाद् बद्धान् मृगयोर् गीत-मोहितात्
verses.transliteration: grāmya-gītaṃ na śṛṇuyād yatir vana-caraḥ kvacit / śikṣeta hariṇād baddhān mṛgayor gīta-mohitāt
verses.translation: An ascetic moving through the forest should not listen to worldly songs; he should learn from the deer that was captured for being fooled by the hunter's call [see the bhajans].

verses.sanskrit: नृत्य-वादित्र-गीतानि जुषन् ग्राम्याणि योषिताम् । आसां क्रीडनको वश्य ऋष्यशृङ्गो मृगी-सुतः
verses.transliteration: nṛtya-vāditra-gītāni juṣan grāmyāṇi yoṣitām / āsāṃ krīḍanako vaśya ṛṣyaśṛṅgo mṛgī-sutaḥ
verses.translation: Taking pleasure in common dancing, musical entertainment and songs, Rishyas'ringa, the son of Mrig ['deer'], was subdued by women, like he was a plaything [see *, 5.8 and 5.25: 11].

verses.sanskrit: जिह्वयाति-प्रमाथिन्या जनो रस-विमोहितः । मृत्युम् ऋच्छत्य् असद्-बुद्धिर् मीनस् तु बडिशैर् यथा
verses.transliteration: jihvayāti-pramāthinyā jano rasa-vimohitaḥ / mṛtyum ṛcchaty asad-buddhir mīnas tu baḍiśair yathā
verses.translation: The way a fish that follows its taste with no intelligence is hooked and finds its death, also a person, most harassed being fooled by what the tongue dictates, may against his better judgement waste his life.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि जयन्त्य् आशु निराहारा मनीषिणः । वर्जयित्वा तु रसनं तन् निरन्नस्य वर्धते
verses.transliteration: indriyāṇi jayanty āśu nirāhārā manīṣiṇaḥ / varjayitvā tu rasanaṃ tan nirannasya vardhate
verses.translation: Sages [even] who are of self-restraint quickly conquer the material senses, except however for the tongue, the desire of which increases with the fasting [see prasdam prayer].

verses.sanskrit: तावज् जितेन्द्रियो न स्याद् विजितान्येन्द्रियः पुमान् । न जयेद् रसनं यावज् जितं सर्वं जिते रसे
verses.transliteration: tāvaj jitendriyo na syād vijitānyendriyaḥ pumān / na jayed rasanaṃ yāvaj jitaṃ sarvaṃ jite rase
verses.translation: A human being not in control of his tongue but in control of his other senses, is not of self-control. But, when someone has conquered his tongue, he has mastered them all [see also 8: 16 and B.G. 2: 59].

verses.sanskrit: पिङ्गला नाम वेश्यासीद् विदेह-नगरे पुरा । तस्या मे शिक्षितं किञ्चिन् निबोध नृप-नन्दन
verses.transliteration: piṅgalā nāma veśyāsīd videha-nagare purā / tasyā me śikṣitaṃ kiñcin nibodha nṛpa-nandana
verses.translation: In the city of Videha there used to be a prostitute called Pingal. Oh son of kings, learn now from me something I learned from her.

verses.sanskrit: सा स्वैरिण्य् एकदा कान्तं सङ्केत उपनेष्यती । अभूत् काले बहिर् द्वारे बिभ्रती रूपम् उत्तमम्
verses.transliteration: sā svairiṇy ekadā kāntaṃ saṅketa upaneṣyatī / abhūt kāle bahir dvāre bibhratī rūpam uttamam
verses.translation: She one night as a prostitute stood outside her door showing off her beautiful figure to get a customer into her house.

verses.sanskrit: मार्ग आगच्छतो वीक्ष्य पुरुषान् पुरुषर्षभ । तान् शुल्क-दान् वित्तवतः कान्तान् मेने ऽर्थ-कामुकी
verses.transliteration: mārga āgacchato vīkṣya puruṣān puruṣarṣabha / tān śulka-dān vittavataḥ kāntān mene 'rtha-kāmukī
verses.translation: Oh best among men, desiring money she looked at all the men passing in the street and thought: 'Oh this lover can pay the price, that one is wealthy enough.'

verses.sanskrit: आगतेष्व् अपयातेषु सा सङ्केतोपजीविनी । अप्य् अन्यो वित्तवान् को ऽपि माम् उपैष्यति भूरि-दः
verses.transliteration: āgateṣv apayāteṣu sā saṅketopajīvinī / apy anyo vittavān ko 'pi mām upaiṣyati bhūri-daḥ
verses.translation: With them coming and going thus subsisting on selling her love, she thought: 'Maybe some guy carrying plenty will approach me for love and give me a bundle...' Giving thought to this vain hope, while standing in the doorway and spoiling her sleep, walking down the street and returning to the house, it became midnight.

verses.sanskrit: एवं दुराशया ध्वस्त- निद्रा द्वार्य् अवलम्बती । निर्गच्छन्ती प्रविशती निशीथं समपद्यत
verses.transliteration: evaṃ durāśayā dhvasta- nidrā dvāry avalambatī / nirgacchantī praviśatī niśīthaṃ samapadyata
verses.translation: With them coming and going thus subsisting on selling her love, she thought: 'Maybe some guy carrying plenty will approach me for love and give me a bundle...' Giving thought to this vain hope, while standing in the doorway and spoiling her sleep, walking down the street and returning to the house, it became midnight.

verses.sanskrit: तस्या वित्ताशया शुष्यद्- वक्त्राया दीन-चेतसः । निर्वेदः परमो जज्ञे चिन्ता-हेतुः सुखावहः
verses.transliteration: tasyā vittāśayā śuṣyad- vaktrāyā dīna-cetasaḥ / nirvedaḥ paramo jajñe cintā-hetuḥ sukhāvahaḥ
verses.translation: As she sadly dropped her face in her desire for money, her anxiety started to give way to a supreme detachment that brought her happiness.

verses.sanskrit: तस्या निर्विण्ण-चित्ताया गीतं शृणु यथा मम । निर्वेद आशा-पाशानां पुरुषस्य यथा ह्य् असिः
verses.transliteration: tasyā nirviṇṇa-cittāyā gītaṃ śṛṇu yathā mama / nirveda āśā-pāśānāṃ puruṣasya yathā hy asiḥ
verses.translation: Please hear from me the song she sang after this disgust of her mind, a detachment that is like a sword to the ties of someone's hopes and desires.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्य् अङ्गाजात-निर्वेदो देह-बन्धं जिहासति । यथा विज्ञान-रहितो मनुजो ममतां नृप
verses.transliteration: na hy aṅgājāta-nirvedo deha-bandhaṃ jihāsati / yathā vijñāna-rahito manujo mamatāṃ nṛpa
verses.translation: Dear King, a person who has not developed detachment is not willing to give up his physical ties, just as a human being lacking in wisdom is not willing to give up his [claims of] ownership.

verses.sanskrit: पिङ्गलोवाच अहो मे मोह-विततिं पश्यताविजितात्मनः । या कान्ताद् असतः कामं कामये येन बालिशा
verses.transliteration: piṅgalovāca aho me moha-vitatiṃ paśyatāvijitātmanaḥ / yā kāntād asataḥ kāmaṃ kāmaye yena bāliśā
verses.translation: Pingal said: 'See how greatly illusioned I am! I must be out of my mind, making a fool of my self in my lust to desire useless pleasures from a lover.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तं समीपे रमणं रति-प्रदं वित्त-प्रदं नित्यम् इमं विहाय । अकाम-दं दुःख-भयाधि-शोक- मोह-प्रदं तुच्छम् अहं भजे ऽज्ञा
