verses.transliteration: santaṃ samīpe ramaṇaṃ rati-pradaṃ vitta-pradaṃ nityam imaṃ vihāya / akāma-daṃ duḥkha-bhayādhi-śoka- moha-pradaṃ tuccham ahaṃ bhaje 'jñā
verses.translation: Being ignorantly devoted to a most insignificant and unsatisfactory lust that only leads to illusion, grief, distress, misery and fear, I have refrained from the love of Him, the Eternal One bringing welfare, most dear and close to me.

verses.sanskrit: अहो मयात्मा परितापितो वृथा साङ्केत्य-वृत्त्याति-विगर्ह्य-वार्तया । स्त्रैणान् नराद् यार्थ-तृषो ऽनुशोच्यात् क्रीतेन वित्तं रतिम् आत्मनेच्छती
verses.transliteration: aho mayātmā paritāpito vṛthā sāṅketya-vṛttyāti-vigarhya-vārtayā / straiṇān narād yārtha-tṛṣo 'nuśocyāt krītena vittaṃ ratim ātmanecchatī
verses.translation: Oh, uselessly subjecting my soul to torture, I engaged as a prostitute, the most reprehensible of all occupations! Desiring money and sexual pleasure, I sold my body to greedy, lusty, pitiable womanizers.

verses.sanskrit: यद् अस्थिभिर् निर्मित-वंश-वंस्य- स्थूणं त्वचा रोम-नखैः पिनद्धम् क्षरन्-नव-द्वारम् अगारम् एतद् विण्-मूत्र-पूर्णं मद् उपैति कान्या
verses.transliteration: yad asthibhir nirmita-vaṃśa-vaṃsya- $ sthūṇaṃ tvacā roma-nakhaiḥ pinaddham &kṣaran-nava-dvāram agāram etad % viṇ-mūtra-pūrṇaṃ mad upaiti kānyā
verses.translation: What other woman would devote herself [so] to this house with nine doors, full of stool and dripping urine, that is constructed with the bones of a spine, the ribs, hands and legs, and is covered by a skin, hair and nails [compare B.G. 5: 13 and 4.25-28]?

verses.sanskrit: विदेहानां पुरे ह्य् अस्मिन्न् अहम् एकैव मूढ-धीः । यान्यम् इच्छन्त्य् असत्य् अस्माद् आत्म-दात् कामम् अच्युतात्
verses.transliteration: videhānāṃ pure hy asminn aham ekaiva mūḍha-dhīḥ / yānyam icchanty asaty asmād ātma-dāt kāmam acyutāt
verses.translation: Among the residents of Videha I am the one as foolish of intelligence to desire, most unfaithful lusting, another man than Him who gives us the Soul, Acyuta.

verses.sanskrit: सुहृत् प्रेष्ठतमो नाथ आत्मा चायं शरीरिणाम् । तं विक्रीयात्मनैवाहं रमे ऽनेन यथा रमा
verses.transliteration: suhṛt preṣṭhatamo nātha ātmā cāyaṃ śarīriṇām / taṃ vikrīyātmanaivāhaṃ rame 'nena yathā ramā
verses.translation: When I pay the price of giving myself to Him, the well-wisher who is the one most dear, the Lord and Soul of all embodied beings, I will enjoy with Him, just like [the goddess] Ram.

verses.sanskrit: कियत् प्रियं ते व्यभजन् कामा ये काम-दा नराः । आद्य्-अन्तवन्तो भार्याया देवा वा काल-विद्रुताः
verses.transliteration: kiyat priyaṃ te vyabhajan kāmā ye kāma-dā narāḥ / ādy-antavanto bhāryāyā devā vā kāla-vidrutāḥ
verses.translation: How little happiness did the sensual pleasure give me and the men who pleased my senses! The enjoyment of having a man or [even the grace of] the gods has, being spread in time, all its beginning and its end.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं मे भगवान् प्रीतो विष्णुः केनापि कर्मणा । निर्वेदो ऽयं दुराशाया यन् मे जातः सुखावहः
verses.transliteration: nūnaṃ me bhagavān prīto viṣṇuḥ kenāpi karmaṇā / nirvedo 'yaṃ durāśāyā yan me jātaḥ sukhāvahaḥ
verses.translation: I who so stubbornly went for pleasure, therefore with my disgust somehow must have pleased the Supreme One, Lord Vishnu, who brings the happiness I now experience!

verses.sanskrit: मैवं स्युर् मन्द-भाग्यायाः क्लेशा निर्वेद-हेतवः । येनानुबन्धं निर्हृत्य पुरुषः शमम् ऋच्छति
verses.transliteration: maivaṃ syur manda-bhāgyāyāḥ kleśā nirveda-hetavaḥ / yenānubandhaṃ nirhṛtya puruṣaḥ śamam ṛcchati
verses.translation: Had I been unlucky, there would not have been this misery leading to disgust, this loathing that makes someone relinquish his bondage and find [real] peace [compare 1.8: 25].

verses.sanskrit: तेनोपकृतम् आदाय शिरसा ग्राम्य-सङ्गताः । त्यक्त्वा दुराशाः शरणं व्रजामि तम् अधीश्वरम्
verses.transliteration: tenopakṛtam ādāya śirasā grāmya-saṅgatāḥ / tyaktvā durāśāḥ śaraṇaṃ vrajāmi tam adhīśvaram
verses.translation: Having refrained from cherishing vain hope in my addiction to sensual pleasures, I now approach Him for shelter and accept devotedly the great help that He, the Original Lord, offers me.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तुष्टा श्रद्दधत्य् एतद् यथा-लाभेन जीवती । विहराम्य् अमुनैवाहम् आत्मना रमणेन वै
verses.transliteration: santuṣṭā śraddadhaty etad yathā-lābhena jīvatī / viharāmy amunaivāham ātmanā ramaṇena vai
verses.translation: Fully satisfied being convinced that I thus can handle whatever comes my way, I will succeed in living and enjoying just with Him, the Self of love and the Happiness that is certain.

verses.sanskrit: संसार-कूपे पतितं विषयैर् मुषितेक्षणम् । ग्रस्तं कालाहिनात्मानं को ऽन्यस् त्रातुम् अधीश्वरः
verses.transliteration: saṃsāra-kūpe patitaṃ viṣayair muṣitekṣaṇam / grastaṃ kālāhinātmānaṃ ko 'nyas trātum adhīśvaraḥ
verses.translation: When one has fallen in the well of a material existence, by sensual pleasures has been robbed of one's insight and is caught in the grip of the snake of Time, who else but the Original Lord, would deliver one's soul [see also 10.34]?

verses.sanskrit: आत्मैव ह्य् आत्मनो गोप्ता निर्विद्येत यदाखिलात् । अप्रमत्त इदं पश्येद् ग्रस्तं कालाहिना जगत्
verses.transliteration: ātmaiva hy ātmano goptā nirvidyeta yadākhilāt / apramatta idaṃ paśyed grastaṃ kālāhinā jagat
verses.translation: The moment a soul attentively sees the universe as being seized by the snake of Time, he, being sober, will detach from everything material and be fit to serve as his own protector.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-ब्राह्मण उवाच एवं व्यवसित-मतिर् दुराशां कान्त-तर्ष-जाम् । छित्त्वोपशमम् आस्थाय शय्याम् उपविवेश सा
verses.transliteration: śrī-brāhmaṇa uvāca evaṃ vyavasita-matir durāśāṃ kānta-tarṣa-jām / chittvopaśamam āsthāya śayyām upaviveśa sā
verses.translation: The honorable brahmin said: 'Thus being determined to put an end to the desperation caused by her desire for lovers, she sat down on her bed, having found inner peace.

verses.sanskrit: आशा हि परमं दुःखं नैराश्यं परमं सुखम् । यथा सञ्छिद्य कान्ताशां सुखं सुष्वाप पिङ्गला
verses.transliteration: āśā hi paramaṃ duḥkhaṃ nairāśyaṃ paramaṃ sukham / yathā sañchidya kāntāśāṃ sukhaṃ suṣvāpa piṅgalā
verses.translation: The greatest unhappiness results from [material] desires and the greatest happiness from the absence of them. Therewith putting an end to her hope for a lover, Pingal [finally] slept happily.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-ब्राह्मण उवाच परिग्रहो हि दुःखाय यद् यत् प्रियतमं नृणाम् । अनन्तं सुखम् आप्नोति तद् विद्वान् यस् त्व् अकिञ्चनः
verses.transliteration: śrī-brāhmaṇa uvāca parigraho hi duḥkhāya yad yat priyatamaṃ nṛṇām / anantaṃ sukham āpnoti tad vidvān yas tv akiñcanaḥ
verses.translation: The honorable brahmin said: 'When one strives to own all the things held dear by man [a house, a wife, goods etc.], that will be a source of unhappiness. Anyone who knows this and is of detachment, achieves unlimited happiness.

verses.sanskrit: सामिषं कुररं जघ्नुर् बलिनो ऽन्ये निरामिषाः । तदामिषं परित्यज्य स सुखं समविन्दत
verses.transliteration: sāmiṣaṃ kuraraṃ jaghnur balino 'nye nirāmiṣāḥ / tadāmiṣaṃ parityajya sa sukhaṃ samavindata
verses.translation: A large hawk [the osprey] who had a prey, was attacked by others who were very strong and without meat. The moment he gave up his [desire for] prey, he achieved happiness.

verses.sanskrit: न मे मानापमानौ स्तो न चिन्ता गेह-पुत्रिणाम् । आत्म-क्रीड आत्म-रतिर् विचरामीह बाल-वत्
verses.transliteration: na me mānāpamānau sto na cintā geha-putriṇām / ātma-krīḍa ātma-ratir vicarāmīha bāla-vat
verses.translation: I am not concerned with honor or dishonor, nor do I know the worries of people with a house and children. I wander around in the world like a child, sporting and enjoying the soul only.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाव् एव चिन्तया मुक्तौ परमानन्द आप्लुतौ । यो विमुग्धो जडो बालो यो गुणेभ्यः परं गतः
verses.transliteration: dvāv eva cintayā muktau paramānanda āplutau / yo vimugdho jaḍo bālo yo guṇebhyaḥ paraṃ gataḥ
verses.translation: There are two types of people free from anxiety and merged in great happiness: those who are childish and foolish, and those who reached [the Lord] beyond the natural modes.

verses.sanskrit: क्वचित् कुमारी त्व् आत्मानं वृणानान् गृहम् आगतान् । स्वयं तान् अर्हयाम् आस क्वापि यातेषु बन्धुषु
verses.transliteration: kvacit kumārī tv ātmānaṃ vṛṇānān gṛham āgatān / svayaṃ tān arhayām āsa kvāpi yāteṣu bandhuṣu
verses.translation: Once, at the house of a young girl all of whose relatives [that day] had gone to another place, a couple of men arrived who wanted to marry her. She received them with great hospitality.

verses.sanskrit: तेषाम् अभ्यवहारार्थं शालीन् रहसि पार्थिव । अवघ्नन्त्याः प्रकोष्ठ-स्थाश् चक्रुः शङ्खाः स्वनं महत्
verses.transliteration: teṣām abhyavahārārthaṃ śālīn rahasi pārthiva / avaghnantyāḥ prakoṣṭha-sthāś cakruḥ śaṅkhāḥ svanaṃ mahat
verses.translation: Being alone she beat the rice so that her guests could eat, and doing so the conch shell bracelets on her arms made a lot of noise.

verses.sanskrit: सा तज् जुगुप्सितं मत्वा महती वृईडिता ततः । बभञ्जैकैकशः शङ्खान् द्वौ द्वौ पाण्योर् अशेषयत्
verses.transliteration: sā taj jugupsitaṃ matvā mahatī vṛīḍitā tataḥ / babhañjaikaikaśaḥ śaṅkhān dvau dvau pāṇyor aśeṣayat
verses.translation: Shy she, filled with shame, thought about that [servant] noise and then intelligently one by one broke the shell bracelets from her arms, leaving but two on each wrist.

verses.sanskrit: उभयोर् अप्य् अभूद् घोषो ह्य् अवघ्नन्त्याः स्व-शङ्खयोः । तत्राप्य् एकं निरभिदद् एकस्मान् नाभवद् ध्वनिः
verses.transliteration: ubhayor apy abhūd ghoṣo hy avaghnantyāḥ sva-śaṅkhayoḥ / tatrāpy ekaṃ nirabhidad ekasmān nābhavad dhvaniḥ
verses.translation: From those two there was still the noise of course as she was husking the rice, but after she further removed one from each pair of shell ornaments, only one remained and no sound could be heard anymore.

verses.sanskrit: अन्वशिक्षम् इमं तस्या उपदेशम् अरिन्दम । लोकान् अनुचरन्न् एतान् लोक-तत्त्व-विवित्सया
verses.transliteration: anvaśikṣam imaṃ tasyā upadeśam arindama / lokān anucarann etān loka-tattva-vivitsayā
verses.translation: Oh subduer of the enemy, I, wandering around in all regions searching for the truth about the world, personally witnessed the lesson taught by this girl.

verses.sanskrit: वासे बहूनां कलहो भवेद् वार्ता द्वयोर् अपि । एक एव वसेत् तस्मात् कुमार्या इव कङ्कणः
verses.transliteration: vāse bahūnāṃ kalaho bhaved vārtā dvayor api / eka eva vaset tasmāt kumāryā iva kaṅkaṇaḥ
verses.translation: When there are many people in one place there will be discord; even among two people there will be arguing. Therefore one should live alone, just like the bracelet of the girl.

verses.sanskrit: मन एकत्र संयुञ्ज्याज् जित-श्वासो जितासनः । वैराग्याभ्यास-योगेन ध्रियमाणम् अतन्द्रितः
verses.transliteration: mana ekatra saṃyuñjyāj jita-śvāso jitāsanaḥ / vairāgyābhyāsa-yogena dhriyamāṇam atandritaḥ
verses.translation: The mind should be controlled by detachment and a regulated yoga practice [vairgya and abhysa], in which one conquers one's breathing in sitting postures and one carefully focusses on one point [the true self, see also B.G. 6: 10-15 and 6: 46-47].

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन् मनो लब्ध-पदं यद् एतच् छनैः शनैर् मुञ्चति कर्म-रेणून् । सत्त्वेन वृद्धेन रजस् तमश् च विधूय निर्वाणम् उपैत्य् अनिन्धनम्
verses.transliteration: yasmin mano labdha-padaṃ yad etac chanaiḥ śanair muñcati karma-reṇūn / sattvena vṛddhena rajas tamaś ca vidhūya nirvāṇam upaity anindhanam
verses.translation: When the mind thus has been stabilized one, step by step, is freed from its contamination of karma and sees the quality of goodness increase, while passion and ignorance recede. Free from fuel [for one's karma] one attains beatitude [nirvna, see also B.G. 6: 26 and 14: 6-8].

verses.sanskrit: तदैवम् आत्मन्य् अवरुद्ध-चित्तो न वेद किञ्चिद् बहिर् अन्तरं वा । यथेषु-कारो नृपतिं व्रजन्तम् इषौ गतात्मा न ददर्श पार्श्वे
verses.transliteration: tadaivam ātmany avaruddha-citto na veda kiñcid bahir antaraṃ vā / yatheṣu-kāro nṛpatiṃ vrajantam iṣau gatātmā na dadarśa pārśve
verses.translation: When one is thus being fixed in the soul, one does not know anything about what is outside or inside, just like the arrow maker who absorbed in his arrow did not notice the king standing at his side [see B.G. 7: 27-28].

verses.sanskrit: एक-चार्य् अनिकेतः स्याद् अप्रमत्तो गुहाशयः । अलक्ष्यमाण आचारैर् मुनिर् एको ऽल्प-भाषणः
verses.transliteration: eka-cāry aniketaḥ syād apramatto guhāśayaḥ / alakṣyamāṇa ācārair munir eko 'lpa-bhāṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: A sage must walk alone without a fixed residence [or temple], must very alert exercise restraint and should not be recognized in his actions. Without companions he speaks only little.

verses.sanskrit: गृहारम्भो हि दुःखाय विफलश् चाध्रुवात्मनः । सर्पः पर-कृतं वेश्म प्रविश्य सुखम् एधते
verses.transliteration: gṛhārambho hi duḥkhāya viphalaś cādhruvātmanaḥ / sarpaḥ para-kṛtaṃ veśma praviśya sukham edhate
verses.translation: Building a home for one's temporary self is a vain and troublesome endeavor. A serpent happily prospers having entered a home built by others [see also B.G. 4: 18].

verses.sanskrit: एको नारायणो देवः पूर्व-सृष्टं स्व-मायया । संहृत्य काल-कलया कल्पान्त इदम् ईश्वरः । एक एवाद्वितीयो ऽभूद् आत्माधारो ऽखिलाश्रयः
verses.transliteration: eko nārāyaṇo devaḥ pūrva-sṛṣṭaṃ sva-māyayā / saṃhṛtya kāla-kalayā kalpānta idam īśvaraḥ / eka evādvitīyo 'bhūd ātmādhāro 'khilāśrayaḥ
verses.translation: The one Self, the One Lord without a second who became the Foundation and Reservoir of All, is Nryana, the Godhead who by His own potency created the universe in the beginning and by His potency of Time withdraws His creation within Hi mself at the end of the kalpa.

verses.sanskrit: कालेनात्मानुभावेन साम्यं नीतासु शक्तिषु । सत्त्वादिष्व् आदि-पुरुषः प्रधान-पुरुषेश्वरः
verses.transliteration: kālenātmānubhāvena sāmyaṃ nītāsu śaktiṣu / sattvādiṣv ādi-puruṣaḥ pradhāna-puruṣeśvaraḥ
verses.translation: When the material powers of sattva and so on, are balanced by the time factor that is the potency of the True Self [the Soul, the Lord], the Original Personality, the purusha is found as the Supreme Controller, the Lord of both the primary nature [pradhna] and the person. He, the worshipable object of all conditioned and transcendental souls, has His existence in the purest experience that one describes as kaivalya [or beatitude], the fulness of the blissful state without [guna] attributes [see also B.G. 7: 5 and *].

verses.sanskrit: परावराणां परम आस्ते कैवल्य-संज्ञितः । केवलानुभवानन्द- सन्दोहो निरुपाधिकः
verses.transliteration: parāvarāṇāṃ parama āste kaivalya-saṃjñitaḥ / kevalānubhavānanda- sandoho nirupādhikaḥ
verses.translation: When the material powers of sattva and so on, are balanced by the time factor that is the potency of the True Self [the Soul, the Lord], the Original Personality, the purusha is found as the Supreme Controller, the Lord of both the primary nature [pradhna] and the person. He, the worshipable object of all conditioned and transcendental souls, has His existence in the purest experience that one describes as kaivalya [or beatitude], the fulness of the blissful state without [guna] attributes [see also B.G. 7: 5 and *].

verses.sanskrit: केवलात्मानुभावेन स्व-मायां त्रि-गुणात्मिकाम् । सङ्क्षोभयन् सृजत्य् आदौ तया सूत्रम् अरिन्दम
verses.transliteration: kevalātmānubhāvena sva-māyāṃ tri-guṇātmikām / saṅkṣobhayan sṛjaty ādau tayā sūtram arindama
verses.translation: By means of the pure potency of His Self, His own bewildering energy composed of the three basic qualities, He, oh subduer of the enemies, at the onset of creation agitating [in the form of Time], manifests the plan of matter [the stra, the thread, the rule or direction of the mahat-tattva, see also 3.26: 19].

verses.sanskrit: ताम् आहुस् त्रि-गुण-व्यक्तिं सृजन्तीं विश्वतो-मुखम् । यस्मिन् प्रोतम् इदं विश्वं येन संसरते पुमान्
verses.transliteration: tām āhus tri-guṇa-vyaktiṃ sṛjantīṃ viśvato-mukham / yasmin protam idaṃ viśvaṃ yena saṃsarate pumān
verses.translation: This universe, in which the living being finds its existence [of repeated births], is strung and bound to that [thread, that plan], that manifested itself as the cause of the three modes, that [in their turn] bring about the different categories of the manifestation, so one says [see also B.G. 7: 7].

verses.sanskrit: यथोर्णनाभिर् हृदयाद् ऊर्णां सन्तत्य वक्त्रतः । तया विहृत्य भूयस् तां ग्रसत्य् एवं महेश्वरः
verses.transliteration: yathorṇanābhir hṛdayād ūrṇāṃ santatya vaktrataḥ / tayā vihṛtya bhūyas tāṃ grasaty evaṃ maheśvaraḥ
verses.translation: The way a spider expands its thread from itself, with that thread by its mouth enjoys [its meal] and swallows that thread again, the Supreme Lord also operates.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र यत्र मनो देही धारयेत् सकलं धिया । स्नेहाद् द्वेषाद् भयाद् वापि याति तत्-तत्-स्वरूपताम्
verses.transliteration: yatra yatra mano dehī dhārayet sakalaṃ dhiyā / snehād dveṣād bhayād vāpi yāti tat-tat-svarūpatām
verses.translation: Whether a conditioned soul out of love, hate or fear, with intelligence fixes his mind on a particular form of existence, he will attain that existence [see B.G. 8: 6].

verses.sanskrit: कीटः पेशस्कृतं ध्यायन् कुड्यां तेन प्रवेशितः । याति तत्-सात्मतां राजन् पूर्व-रूपम् असन्त्यजन्
verses.transliteration: kīṭaḥ peśaskṛtaṃ dhyāyan kuḍyāṃ tena praveśitaḥ / yāti tat-sātmatāṃ rājan pūrva-rūpam asantyajan
verses.translation: Oh King, a wasp larva, meditating on the fully grown wasp that put him in the hive, reaches without leaving his previous body [by transformation] the same state of being when fully grown.

verses.sanskrit: एवं गुरुभ्य एतेभ्य एषा मे शिक्षिता मतिः । स्वात्मोपशिक्षितां बुद्धिं शृणु मे वदतः प्रभो
verses.transliteration: evaṃ gurubhya etebhya eṣā me śikṣitā matiḥ / svātmopaśikṣitāṃ buddhiṃ śṛṇu me vadataḥ prabho
verses.translation: This is what I know from taking instruction from all these gurus. Now please, oh King, hear from me what I have to say about the knowledge I acquired by learning from my own body.

verses.sanskrit: देहो गुरुर् मम विरक्ति-विवेक-हेतुर् बिभ्रत् स्म सत्त्व-निधनं सततार्त्य्-उदर्कम् तत्त्वान्य् अनेन विमृशामि यथा तथापि पारक्यम् इत्य् अवसितो विचराम्य् असङ्गः
verses.transliteration: deho gurur mama virakti-viveka-hetur $ bibhrat sma sattva-nidhanaṃ satatārty-udarkam &tattvāny anena vimṛśāmi yathā tathāpi % pārakyam ity avasito vicarāmy asaṅgaḥ
verses.translation: With one's body one always has to suffer because of the inevitable burden of its maintenance and future destruction. I contemplate the truths of the world with it and the body is therefore, despite being there for the service of others, to me a teacher of renunciation and discrimination who convinces me to wander about in detachment.

verses.sanskrit: जायात्मजार्थ-पशु-भृत्य-गृहाप्त-वर्गान् पुष्नाति यत्-प्रिय-चिकीर्षया वितन्वन् जायात्मजार्थ-पशु-भृत्य-गृहाप्त-वर्गान् पुष्नाति यत्-प्रिय-चिकीर्षया वितन्वन् स्वान्ते स-कृच्छ्रम् अवरुद्ध-धनः स देहः सृष्ट्वास्य बीजम् अवसीदति वृक्ष-धर्मः
verses.transliteration: jāyātmajārtha-paśu-bhṛtya-gṛhāpta-vargān $ puṣnāti yat-priya-cikīrṣayā vitanvan &jāyātmajārtha-paśu-bhṛtya-gṛhāpta-vargān $ puṣnāti yat-priya-cikīrṣayā vitanvan &svānte sa-kṛcchram avaruddha-dhanaḥ sa dehaḥ % sṛṣṭvāsya bījam avasīdati vṛkṣa-dharmaḥ
verses.translation: Wishing to give it pleasure, one has to divide one's care over the different departments of the wife, the children, the animals, the servants, the home and the relatives. Just as in nature a tree drops the seed that was produced and dies, the body at the time of death must give up the wealth it with great struggle accumulated.

verses.sanskrit: जिह्वैकतो ऽमुम् अपकर्षति कर्हि तर्षा शिश्नो ऽन्यतस् त्वग् उदरं श्रवणं कुतश्चित् जिह्वैकतो ऽमुम् अपकर्षति कर्हि तर्षा शिश्नो ऽन्यतस् त्वग् उदरं श्रवणं कुतश्चित् घ्राणो ऽन्यतश् चपल-दृक् क्व च कर्म-शक्तिर् बह्व्यः सपत्न्य इव गेह-पतिं लुनन्ति
verses.transliteration: jihvaikato 'mum apakarṣati karhi tarṣā $ śiśno 'nyatas tvag udaraṃ śravaṇaṃ kutaścit &jihvaikato 'mum apakarṣati karhi tarṣā $ śiśno 'nyatas tvag udaraṃ śravaṇaṃ kutaścit &ghrāṇo 'nyataś capala-dṛk kva ca karma-śaktir % bahvyaḥ sapatnya iva geha-patiṃ lunanti
verses.translation: One moment the tongue distracts the cherished body and sometimes thirst is doing this, the other moment the genitals distract and then the sense of touch is pressing, the belly demands attention, the ears lead elsewhere, the smell points in a direction or the fickle eyes are leading astray. The operating forces of the body are thus pulled in many directions, just like the head of a household is, being led by many co-wives.

verses.sanskrit: सृष्ट्वा पुराणि विविधान्य् अजयात्म-शक्त्या वृक्षान् सरीसृप-पशून् खग-दन्दशूकान् सृष्ट्वा पुराणि विविधान्य् अजयात्म-शक्त्या वृक्षान् सरीसृप-पशून् खग-दन्दशूकान् तैस् तैर् अतुष्ट-हृदयः पुरुषं विधाय ब्रह्मावलोक-धिषणं मुदम् आप देवः
verses.transliteration: sṛṣṭvā purāṇi vividhāny ajayātma-śaktyā $ vṛkṣān sarīsṛpa-paśūn khaga-dandaśūkān &sṛṣṭvā purāṇi vividhāny ajayātma-śaktyā $ vṛkṣān sarīsṛpa-paśūn khaga-dandaśūkān &tais tair atuṣṭa-hṛdayaḥ puruṣaṃ vidhāya % brahmāvaloka-dhiṣaṇaṃ mudam āpa devaḥ
verses.translation: After He from His powers had created the many different physical forms of the crawling creatures [the insects], the mammals, birds, snakes and so on, the Lord, in His heart not satisfied with it, created the human life form that He endowed with an intelligence fit for envisioning the Absolute Truth, and that brought Him gladness.

verses.sanskrit: लब्ध्वा सु-दुर्लभम् इदं बहु-सम्भवान्ते मानुष्यम् अर्थ-दम् अनित्यम् अपीह धीरः लब्ध्वा सु-दुर्लभम् इदं बहु-सम्भवान्ते मानुष्यम् अर्थ-दम् अनित्यम् अपीह धीरः तूर्णं यतेत न पतेद् अनु-मृत्यु यावन् निःश्रेयसाय विषयः खलु सर्वतः स्यात्
verses.transliteration: labdhvā su-durlabham idaṃ bahu-sambhavānte $ mānuṣyam artha-dam anityam apīha dhīraḥ &labdhvā su-durlabham idaṃ bahu-sambhavānte $ mānuṣyam artha-dam anityam apīha dhīraḥ &tūrṇaṃ yateta na pated anu-mṛtyu yāvan % niḥśreyasāya viṣayaḥ khalu sarvataḥ syāt
verses.translation: After many births having attained this human form that is so difficult to attain and, though not eternal, has a great value, someone in control of himself but doomed to die, must as long as he has not ended, without delay in this world endeavor for the ultimate liberation, [the way out]; sense-gratification after all is available in all forms of life.

verses.sanskrit: एवं सञ्जात-वैराग्यो विज्ञानालोक आत्मनि । विचरामि महीम् एतां मुक्त-सङ्गो ऽनहङ्कृतः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ sañjāta-vairāgyo vijñānāloka ātmani / vicarāmi mahīm etāṃ mukta-saṅgo 'nahaṅkṛtaḥ
verses.translation: Thus [with all these twenty-four plus one masters] without ties having awakened and with wisdom looking [at the world] from within the soul, I wander the earth free from attachment and false ego.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्य् एकस्माद् गुरोर् ज्ञानं सु-स्थिरं स्यात् सु-पुष्कलम् । ब्रह्मैतद् अद्वितीयं वै गीयते बहुधर्षिभिः
verses.transliteration: na hy ekasmād guror jñānaṃ su-sthiraṃ syāt su-puṣkalam / brahmaitad advitīyaṃ vai gīyate bahudharṣibhiḥ
verses.translation: The knowledge acquired from a single teacher cannot be very solid or complete [see 11.3: 21]. The Absolute Truth without a second, is by the sages sung in many ways.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच इत्य् उक्त्वा स यदुं विप्रस् तम् आमन्त्र्य गभीर-धीः । वन्दितः स्व्-अर्चितो राज्ञा ययौ प्रीतो यथागतम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca ity uktvā sa yaduṃ vipras tam āmantrya gabhīra-dhīḥ / vanditaḥ sv-arcito rājñā yayau prīto yathāgatam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'After the so very intelligent brahmin [who in fact was Lord Datttreya, see 2.7: 4 and **] thus had spoken to king Yadu and properly was honored by the king offering his obeisances, he thereupon bid farewell and went away, just as contented as he had come.

verses.sanskrit: अवधूत-वचः श्रुत्वा पूर्वेषां नः स पूर्व-जः । सर्व-सङ्ग-विनिर्मुक्तः सम-चित्तो बभूव ह
verses.transliteration: avadhūta-vacaḥ śrutvā pūrveṣāṃ naḥ sa pūrva-jaḥ / sarva-saṅga-vinirmuktaḥ sama-citto babhūva ha
verses.translation: Yadu, the forefather of our forefathers, having heard the words of the avadhta, became liberated from all his attachment in a consciousness equal toward all.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच मयोदितेष्व् अवहितः स्व-धर्मेषु मद्-आश्रयः । वर्णाश्रम-कुलाचारम् अकामात्मा समाचरेत्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca mayoditeṣv avahitaḥ sva-dharmeṣu mad-āśrayaḥ / varṇāśrama-kulācāram akāmātmā samācaret
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'A soul free from desire who accepts My shelter, should, conscientiously performing the personal duties I spoke about [see also in e.g. 10.60: 52 and B.G. 3: 35], practice the varns'rama system of behaving oneself in society [B.G. 4: 13].

verses.sanskrit: अन्वीक्षेत विशुद्धात्मा देहिनां विषयात्मनाम् । गुणेषु तत्त्व-ध्यानेन सर्वारम्भ-विपर्ययम्
verses.transliteration: anvīkṣeta viśuddhātmā dehināṃ viṣayātmanām / guṇeṣu tattva-dhyānena sarvārambha-viparyayam
verses.translation: A purified soul should understand that all endeavors of embodied souls who, focussed on sense gratification, take the material qualities for true, are doomed to fail [see also B.G. 13: 32].

verses.sanskrit: सुप्तस्य विषयालोको ध्यायतो वा मनोरथः । नानात्मकत्वाद् विफलस् तथा भेदात्म-धीर् गुणैः
verses.transliteration: suptasya viṣayāloko dhyāyato vā manorathaḥ / nānātmakatvād viphalas tathā bhedātma-dhīr guṇaiḥ
verses.translation: Just as the sensual matters, seen by the meditator in the realm of sleep or in his fantasy, do not help him because of their great variety, also the intelligence is ineffectual when being lead by the senses directed at a diversity of outer appearances [B.G. 2: 41 & 9: 15].

verses.sanskrit: निवृत्तं कर्म सेवेत प्रवृत्तं मत्-परस् त्यजेत् । जिज्ञासायां सम्प्रवृत्तो नाद्रियेत् कर्म-चोदनाम्
verses.transliteration: nivṛttaṃ karma seveta pravṛttaṃ mat-paras tyajet / jijñāsāyāṃ sampravṛtto nādriyet karma-codanām
verses.translation: Devoted to Me one gives up one's active [material] life [pravritti] and engages in a contemplative life [nivritti]; when one is fully absorbed in [self] inquiry one is no longer bound to the injunctions for performing material labor [karma, see 7.15: 47].

verses.sanskrit: यमान् अभीक्ष्णं सेवेत नियमान् मत्-परः क्वचित् । मद्-अभिज्ञं गुरुं शान्तम् उपासीत मद्-आत्मकम्
verses.transliteration: yamān abhīkṣṇaṃ seveta niyamān mat-paraḥ kvacit / mad-abhijñaṃ guruṃ śāntam upāsīta mad-ātmakam
verses.translation: Devoted to Me one should at all times respect the vow of yoga [yama, the vidhi] and its regulated practice [niyama] as good as one can, as also respect a guru who knows My form and is peaceful under My control [see also 7.14: 41-42].

verses.sanskrit: अमान्य् अमत्सरो दक्षो निर्ममो दृढ-सौहृदः । असत्वरो ऽर्थ-जिज्ञासुर् अनसूयुर् अमोघ-वाक्
verses.transliteration: amāny amatsaro dakṣo nirmamo dṛḍha-sauhṛdaḥ / asatvaro 'rtha-jijñāsur anasūyur amogha-vāk
verses.translation: Humble without false pride and jealousy, be industrious [in devotion], non-possessive, fixed in friendship, without haste, interested in inquiring and free from spite and idle talk.

verses.sanskrit: जायापत्य-गृह-क्षेत्र- स्वजन-द्रविणादिषु । उदासीनः समं पश्यन् सर्वेष्व् अर्थम् इवात्मनः
verses.transliteration: jāyāpatya-gṛha-kṣetra- svajana-draviṇādiṣu / udāsīnaḥ samaṃ paśyan sarveṣv artham ivātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Remaining neutral with an equal vision towards one's wife, children, home, land, relatives and bank account and such, one should recognize one's self-interest in that of others [see B.G. 5: 18].

verses.sanskrit: विलक्षणः स्थूल-सूक्ष्माद् देहाद् आत्मेक्षिता स्व-दृक् । यथाग्निर् दारुणो दाह्याद् दाहको ऽन्यः प्रकाशकः
verses.transliteration: vilakṣaṇaḥ sthūla-sūkṣmād dehād ātmekṣitā sva-dṛk / yathāgnir dāruṇo dāhyād dāhako 'nyaḥ prakāśakaḥ
verses.translation: The soul is the self-enlightened seer different from the gross and subtle body, just as fire, emitting light as it burns, differs from the firewood [see also B.G. 2: 16-24].

verses.sanskrit: निरोधोत्पत्त्य्-अणु-बृहन्- नानात्वं तत्-कृतान् गुणान् । अन्तः प्रविष्ट आधत्त एवं देह-गुणान् परः
verses.transliteration: nirodhotpatty-aṇu-bṛhan- nānātvaṃ tat-kṛtān guṇān / antaḥ praviṣṭa ādhatta evaṃ deha-guṇān paraḥ
verses.translation: Just like the different qualities of being dormant, manifest, being large or small are there [of the fire], there are also the qualities of the material body the higher self accepts that has entered it [see also 3.24: 6, 4.9: 7, 10.37: 10-11, 10.46: 36].

verses.sanskrit: यो ऽसौ गुणैर् विरचितो देहो ऽयं पुरुषस्य हि । संसारस् तन्-निबन्धो ऽयं पुंसो विद्या च्छिद् आत्मनः
verses.transliteration: yo 'sau guṇair viracito deho 'yaṃ puruṣasya hi / saṃsāras tan-nibandho 'yaṃ puṃso vidyā cchid ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: This body, which was formed by the basic qualities of nature [expanding] from the Original Person [see B.G. 8: 4], ties the living entity to a material existence [to samsra], from which it breaks away by the knowledge of the Soul.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माज् जिज्ञासयात्मानम् आत्म-स्थं केवलं परम् । सङ्गम्य निरसेद् एतद् वस्तु-बुद्धिं यथा-क्रमम्
verses.transliteration: tasmāj jijñāsayātmānam ātma-sthaṃ kevalaṃ param / saṅgamya nirased etad vastu-buddhiṃ yathā-kramam
verses.translation: One should therefore, by cultivating knowledge, get in touch with the pure, transcendental [Supreme] Soul situated within oneself [2.2 and B.G. 9: 5] and gradually give up one's minding about the material affair.

verses.sanskrit: आचार्यो ऽरणिर् आद्यः स्याद् अन्ते-वास्य् उत्तरारणिः । तत्-सन्धानं प्रवचनं विद्या-सन्धिः सुखावहः
verses.transliteration: ācāryo 'raṇir ādyaḥ syād ante-vāsy uttarāraṇiḥ / tat-sandhānaṃ pravacanaṃ vidyā-sandhiḥ sukhāvahaḥ
verses.translation: The crya can be compared to the lower part of [a pile of] kindling wood, the disciple to the top part and the instructions to the [kindling] stick used in between, while the knowledge that brings happiness is there like the fire [that brings light, compare 9.14: 44-46].

verses.sanskrit: वैशारदी साति-विशुद्ध-बुद्धिर् धुनोति मायां गुण-सम्प्रसूताम् । गुनांश् च सन्दह्य यद्-आत्मम् एतत् स्वयं च शांयत्य् असमिद् यथाग्निः
verses.transliteration: vaiśāradī sāti-viśuddha-buddhir dhunoti māyāṃ guṇa-samprasūtām / gunāṃś ca sandahya yad-ātmam etat svayaṃ ca śāṃyaty asamid yathāgniḥ
verses.translation: This purest intelligence of the souls of experience [the cryas], drives away the illusion originating from the gunas. Thereupon that intelligence, which rose because of those modes, finds peace itself, the way a fire burns up when it runs out of fuel [see also 11.3: 12].

verses.sanskrit: अथैषाम् कर्म-कर्तॄणां भोक्तॄणां सुख-दुःखयोः । नानात्वम् अथ नित्यत्वं लोक-कालागमात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: athaiṣām karma-kartṝṇāṃ bhoktṝṇāṃ sukha-duḥkhayoḥ / nānātvam atha nityatvaṃ loka-kālāgamātmanām
verses.translation: For the controllers and enjoyers of karma, fruitful labor, there is of course the eternal duality of happiness and grief, time and place, to have and to be. When you take all that is matter for eternal and complete, your intelligence is ruled by all the different forms and changes belonging to it. All living beings thinking thus, oh Uddhava, time and again will find themselves being born [falling ill and dying] and so on [see 3.11]; after all, being united with a form one is bound to the [conditioning] limbs of time [of sun and moon, day and night etc.].

verses.sanskrit: मन्यसे सर्व-भावानां संस्था ह्य् औत्पत्तिकी यथा । तत्-तद्-आकृति-भेदेन जायते भिद्यते च धीः
verses.transliteration: manyase sarva-bhāvānāṃ saṃsthā hy autpattikī yathā / tat-tad-ākṛti-bhedena jāyate bhidyate ca dhīḥ
verses.translation: For the controllers and enjoyers of karma, fruitful labor, there is of course the eternal duality of happiness and grief, time and place, to have and to be. When you take all that is matter for eternal and complete, your intelligence is ruled by all the different forms and changes belonging to it. All living beings thinking thus, oh Uddhava, time and again will find themselves being born [falling ill and dying] and so on [see 3.11]; after all, being united with a form one is bound to the [conditioning] limbs of time [of sun and moon, day and night etc.].

verses.sanskrit: एवम् अप्य् अङ्ग सर्वेषां देहिनां देह-योगतः । कालावयवतः सन्ति भावा जन्मादयो ऽसकृत्
verses.transliteration: evam apy aṅga sarveṣāṃ dehināṃ deha-yogataḥ / kālāvayavataḥ santi bhāvā janmādayo 'sakṛt
verses.translation: For the controllers and enjoyers of karma, fruitful labor, there is of course the eternal duality of happiness and grief, time and place, to have and to be. When you take all that is matter for eternal and complete, your intelligence is ruled by all the different forms and changes belonging to it. All living beings thinking thus, oh Uddhava, time and again will find themselves being born [falling ill and dying] and so on [see 3.11]; after all, being united with a form one is bound to the [conditioning] limbs of time [of sun and moon, day and night etc.].

verses.sanskrit: तत्रापि कर्मणां कर्तुर् अस्वातन्त्र्यं च लक्ष्यते । भोक्तुश् च दुःख-सुखयोः को न्व् अर्थो विवशं भजेत्
verses.transliteration: tatrāpi karmaṇāṃ kartur asvātantryaṃ ca lakṣyate / bhoktuś ca duḥkha-sukhayoḥ ko nv artho vivaśaṃ bhajet
verses.translation: In this, one can clearly see the lack of independence of the karmic controller, as also the happiness and unhappiness of such an enjoyer. What purpose is served by someone [that] helpless [see B.G. 9: 3 and 11.9: 1]?

verses.sanskrit: न देहिनां सुखं किञ्चिद् विद्यते विदुषाम् अपि । तथा च दुःखं मूढानां वृथाहङ्करणं परम्
verses.transliteration: na dehināṃ sukhaṃ kiñcid vidyate viduṣām api / tathā ca duḥkhaṃ mūḍhānāṃ vṛthāhaṅkaraṇaṃ param
verses.translation: Among the embodied beings the fool is sometimes unhappy and even the sage is neither always happy. Wishing to be happy always is a useless endeavor and in fact something most egotistical [see also B.G. 2: 15 and 11.9: 4].

verses.sanskrit: यदि प्राप्तिं विघातं च जानन्ति सुख-दुःखयोः । ते ऽप्य् अद्धा न विदुर् योगं मृत्युर् न प्रभवेद् यथा
verses.transliteration: yadi prāptiṃ vighātaṃ ca jānanti sukha-duḥkhayoḥ / te 'py addhā na vidur yogaṃ mṛtyur na prabhaved yathā
verses.translation: Even if one manages to achieve happiness and escape distress, one still has no direct knowledge of the [yoga] process by which death loses its power [compare B.G. 10: 34].

verses.sanskrit: को ऽन्व् अर्थः सुखयत्य् एनं कामो वा मृत्युर् अन्तिके । आघातं नीयमानस्य वध्यस्येव न तुष्टि-दः
verses.transliteration: ko 'nv arthaḥ sukhayaty enaṃ kāmo vā mṛtyur antike / āghātaṃ nīyamānasya vadhyasyeva na tuṣṭi-daḥ
verses.translation: What guarantee of material happiness would a material object or lust be able to provide the person? With death never pleasing close at hand such a thing gives no satisfaction, like with a person condemned who is led to his place of execution.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतं च दृष्ट-वद् दुष्टं स्पर्धासूयात्यय-व्ययैः । बह्व्-अन्तराय-कामत्वात् कृषि-वच् चापि निष्फलम्
verses.transliteration: śrutaṃ ca dṛṣṭa-vad duṣṭaṃ spardhāsūyātyaya-vyayaiḥ / bahv-antarāya-kāmatvāt kṛṣi-vac cāpi niṣphalam
verses.translation: What we heard about [heaven] as also what we know from our own experience [on earth], is spoilt by rivalry, fault-finding, lapse and decay. Just like with agriculture where many obstacles are in the way [of a happy result], it is useless to desire for [perfect] material happiness [see also 11.3: 20].

verses.sanskrit: अन्तरायैर् अविहितो यदि धर्मः स्व्-अनुष्ठितः । तेनापि निर्जितं स्थानं यथा गच्छति तच् छृणु
verses.transliteration: antarāyair avihito yadi dharmaḥ sv-anuṣṭhitaḥ / tenāpi nirjitaṃ sthānaṃ yathā gacchati tac chṛṇu
verses.translation: When one manages to perfectly practice one's righteousness [dharma] without being troubled by hindrances, even the status one thus acquires will not last forever. Please, listen therefore to the following [see also B.G. 2: 14].

verses.sanskrit: इष्ट्वेह देवता यज्ञैः स्वर्-लोकं याति याज्ञिकः । भुञ्जीत देव-वत् तत्र भोगान् दिव्यान् निजार्जितान्
verses.transliteration: iṣṭveha devatā yajñaiḥ svar-lokaṃ yāti yājñikaḥ / bhuñjīta deva-vat tatra bhogān divyān nijārjitān
verses.translation: On earth having worshiped the gods with sacrifices, the performer goes to the heavenly worlds where he may enjoy like a god the celestial pleasures he achieved [see B.G. 3: 11 and 4: 12].

verses.sanskrit: स्व-पुण्योपचिते शुभ्रे विमान उपगीयते । गन्धर्वैर् विहरन् मध्ये देवीनां हृद्य-वेष-धृक्
verses.transliteration: sva-puṇyopacite śubhre vimāna upagīyate / gandharvair viharan madhye devīnāṃ hṛdya-veṣa-dhṛk
verses.translation: Shining in the temple [the 'vimna'] because of his accumulated merit he enjoys, being surrounded by goddesses wearing charming clothes, while he [as he leaves this earth] is glorified with songs by the singers of heaven.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीभिः कामग-यानेन किङ्किनी-जाल-मालिना । क्रीडन् न वेदात्म-पातं सुराक्रीडेषु निर्वृतः
verses.transliteration: strībhiḥ kāmaga-yānena kiṅkinī-jāla-mālinā / krīḍan na vedātma-pātaṃ surākrīḍeṣu nirvṛtaḥ
verses.translation: Surrounded by the sounds of bells he fares to his desire with heavenly women, but while relaxing comfortably in the pleasure gardens of the gods, he in his delight has no awareness of his downfall [see e.g. 7.15: 69-73].

verses.sanskrit: तावत् स मोदते स्वर्गे यावत् पुण्यं समाप्यते । क्षीण-पुन्यः पतत्य् अर्वाग् अनिच्छन् काल-चालितः
verses.transliteration: tāvat sa modate svarge yāvat puṇyaṃ samāpyate / kṣīṇa-punyaḥ pataty arvāg anicchan kāla-cālitaḥ
verses.translation: After for long having enjoyed the heavens until his pious credits were used up, having exhausted his merits he against his will falls down from heaven, [not properly fixed] being forced from his course by time [compare B.G. 9: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: यद्य् अधर्म-रतः सङ्गाद् असतां वाजितेन्द्रियः । कामात्मा कृपणो लुब्धः स्त्रैणो भूत-विहिंसकः
verses.transliteration: yady adharma-rataḥ saṅgād asatāṃ vājitendriyaḥ / kāmātmā kṛpaṇo lubdhaḥ straiṇo bhūta-vihiṃsakaḥ
verses.translation: When he, due to his material involvement, is engaged in actions against the dharma or, not having conquered his senses, lives wantonly as a miserable, greedy philanderer, is of violence against other living beings, kills animals against the rules and worships hordes of ghosts and spirits [compare 7.12: 12], a living being will, once he passed away, thereupon helplessly land in the deepest darkness of the hellish worlds. He will, because of what he did, again have to accept a material body to perform therewith activities that [again] cause him great grief in the future. What happiness would someone find being sworn to activities that invariably lead to death [see also 5.26: 37 and B.G. 16: 19-21]?

verses.sanskrit: पशून् अविधिनालभ्य प्रेत-भूत-गणान् यजन् । नरकान् अवशो जन्तुर् गत्वा यात्य् उल्बणं तमः
verses.transliteration: paśūn avidhinālabhya preta-bhūta-gaṇān yajan / narakān avaśo jantur gatvā yāty ulbaṇaṃ tamaḥ
verses.translation: When he, due to his material involvement, is engaged in actions against the dharma or, not having conquered his senses, lives wantonly as a miserable, greedy philanderer, is of violence against other living beings, kills animals against the rules and worships hordes of ghosts and spirits [compare 7.12: 12], a living being will, once he passed away, thereupon helplessly land in the deepest darkness of the hellish worlds. He will, because of what he did, again have to accept a material body to perform therewith activities that [again] cause him great grief in the future. What happiness would someone find being sworn to activities that invariably lead to death [see also 5.26: 37 and B.G. 16: 19-21]?

verses.sanskrit: कर्माणि दुःखोदर्काणि कुर्वन् देहेन तैः पुनः । देहम् आभजते तत्र किं सुखं मर्त्य-धर्मिणः
verses.transliteration: karmāṇi duḥkhodarkāṇi kurvan dehena taiḥ punaḥ / deham ābhajate tatra kiṃ sukhaṃ martya-dharmiṇaḥ
verses.translation: When he, due to his material involvement, is engaged in actions against the dharma or, not having conquered his senses, lives wantonly as a miserable, greedy philanderer, is of violence against other living beings, kills animals against the rules and worships hordes of ghosts and spirits [compare 7.12: 12], a living being will, once he passed away, thereupon helplessly land in the deepest darkness of the hellish worlds. He will, because of what he did, again have to accept a material body to perform therewith activities that [again] cause him great grief in the future. What happiness would someone find being sworn to activities that invariably lead to death [see also 5.26: 37 and B.G. 16: 19-21]?

verses.sanskrit: लोकानां लोक-पालानां मद् भयं कल्प-जीविनाम् । ब्रह्मणो ऽपि भयं मत्तो द्वि-परार्ध-परायुषः
verses.transliteration: lokānāṃ loka-pālānāṃ mad bhayaṃ kalpa-jīvinām / brahmaṇo 'pi bhayaṃ matto dvi-parārdha-parāyuṣaḥ
verses.translation: In all the worlds among all their leaders there exists fear of Me [in the form of Time]; the individual souls living as long as a kalpa fear Me, and even the one supreme, Brahm, who lives for two parrdhas, fears Me [see also 1.13: 17-20, 3.8: 20, 3.11: 33, 3.25: 42, 3.26: 16, 3.29: 37, 3.29: 40-45, 5.24: 15, 5.24: 28].

verses.sanskrit: गुणाः सृजन्ति कर्माणि गुणो ऽनुसृजते गुणान् । जीवस् तु गुण-संयुक्तो भुङ्क्ते कर्म-फलान्य् असौ
verses.transliteration: guṇāḥ sṛjanti karmāṇi guṇo 'nusṛjate guṇān / jīvas tu guṇa-saṃyukto bhuṅkte karma-phalāny asau
verses.translation: The material senses, stimulated by the qualities of nature, give rise to activities, and the individual soul, the jva, who is fully engaged by the materially oriented senses and the gunas, undergoes the various karmic consequences [see also 3.32 and B.G. 3: 27].

verses.sanskrit: यावत् स्याद् गुण-वैषम्यं तावन् नानात्वम् आत्मनः । नानात्वम् आत्मनो यावत् पारतन्त्र्यं तदैव हि
verses.transliteration: yāvat syād guṇa-vaiṣamyaṃ tāvan nānātvam ātmanaḥ / nānātvam ātmano yāvat pāratantryaṃ tadaiva hi
verses.translation: As long as there is the differentiation of the modes of nature, there will be the different forms of existence of the soul, and as long as there are these different states of the soul, there will be [karmic] dependence [see also B.G. 17: 2].

verses.sanskrit: यावद् अस्यास्वतन्त्रत्वं तावद् ईश्वरतो भयम् । य एतत् समुपासीरंस् ते मुह्यन्ति शुचार्पिताः
verses.transliteration: yāvad asyāsvatantratvaṃ tāvad īśvarato bhayam / ya etat samupāsīraṃs te muhyanti śucārpitāḥ
verses.translation: As long as one is not free from this dependence there will be fear for the Lord and Controller [who is the Time]. They [then] who enjoy this [karmic bond] will become bewildered and always be full of sorrow.

verses.sanskrit: काल आत्मागमो लोकः स्वभावो धर्म एव च । इति मां बहुधा प्राहुर् गुण-व्यतिकरे सति
verses.transliteration: kāla ātmāgamo lokaḥ svabhāvo dharma eva ca / iti māṃ bahudhā prāhur guṇa-vyatikare sati
verses.translation: With the given reciprocal action [or the operation] of the basic qualities of nature, one calls Me variously the Time, the Soul, the Vedic Knowledge, the World, [the original nature or] Nature [at large], as also the Dharma.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच गुणेषु वर्तमानो ऽपि देह-जेष्व् अनपावृतः । गुणैर् न बध्यते देही बध्यते वा कथं विभो
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca guṇeṣu vartamāno 'pi deha-jeṣv anapāvṛtaḥ / guṇair na badhyate dehī badhyate vā kathaṃ vibho
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Even though the one embodied is present in the midst of the natural modes, he is not necessarily bound to what incessantly operates upon him from the material body [like happiness and distress]. In other words, how can it happen that someone, as a free soul, is caught in the grip of those basic qualities, oh Almighty One?

verses.sanskrit: कथं वर्तेत विहरेत् कैर् वा ज्ञायेत लक्षणैः । किं भुञ्जीतोत विसृजेच् छयीतासीत याति वा
verses.transliteration: kathaṃ varteta viharet kair vā jñāyeta lakṣaṇaiḥ / kiṃ bhuñjītota visṛjec chayītāsīta yāti vā
verses.translation: How is he situated, how does he enjoy, or by what symptoms can he be known? What would he eat or how would he evacuate, lie down or sit [compare B.G. 14: 21]? Please explain to me what I ask You, oh Infallible One, oh Best Answerer of Questions. This at the same time being eternally bound and being eternally liberated is something confusing me.'

verses.sanskrit: एतद् अच्युत मे ब्रूहि प्रश्नं प्रश्न-विदां वर । नित्य-बद्धो नित्य-मुक्त एक एवेति मे भ्रमः
verses.transliteration: etad acyuta me brūhi praśnaṃ praśna-vidāṃ vara / nitya-baddho nitya-mukta eka eveti me bhramaḥ
verses.translation: How is he situated, how does he enjoy, or by what symptoms can he be known? What would he eat or how would he evacuate, lie down or sit [compare B.G. 14: 21]? Please explain to me what I ask You, oh Infallible One, oh Best Answerer of Questions. This at the same time being eternally bound and being eternally liberated is something confusing me.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच बद्धो मुक्त इति व्याख्या गुणतो मे न वस्तुतः । गुणस्य माया-मूलत्वान् न मे मोक्षो न बन्धनम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca baddho mukta iti vyākhyā guṇato me na vastutaḥ / guṇasya māyā-mūlatvān na me mokṣo na bandhanam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'One cannot really say that one is bound or liberated here due to My modes. To be in the grip of the natural qualities or to be free from them is not caused by My illusory energy [*].

verses.sanskrit: शोक-मोहौ सुखं दुःखं देहापत्तिश् च मायया । स्वप्नो यथात्मनः ख्यातिः संसृतिर् न तु वास्तवी
verses.transliteration: śoka-mohau sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ dehāpattiś ca māyayā / svapno yathātmanaḥ khyātiḥ saṃsṛtir na tu vāstavī
verses.translation: There is lamentation, bewilderment, happiness, distress and the acceptance of a material body because of illusion. Material life with its successive states is [merely] an idea of the soul just as unreal as a dream is.

verses.sanskrit: विद्याविद्ये मम तनू विद्ध्य् उद्धव शरीरिणाम् । मोक्ष-बन्ध-करी आद्ये मायया मे विनिर्मिते
verses.transliteration: vidyāvidye mama tanū viddhy uddhava śarīriṇām / mokṣa-bandha-karī ādye māyayā me vinirmite
verses.translation: Please Uddhava, understand that knowledge and ignorance are two forms of Myself that, as a result of My original potency, give rise to the liberation and bondage of the embodied beings.

verses.sanskrit: एकस्यैव ममांशस्य जीवस्यैव महा-मते । बन्धो ऽस्याविद्ययानादिर् विद्यया च तथेतरः
verses.transliteration: ekasyaiva mamāṃśasya jīvasyaiva mahā-mate / bandho 'syāvidyayānādir vidyayā ca tathetaraḥ
verses.translation: The bondage of the living entity, which is part and parcel of My Oneness, oh great intelligence, is there since time immemorial because of ignorance, and the opposite [of liberation] is there because of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: अथ बद्धस्य मुक्तस्य वैलक्षण्यं वदामि ते । विरुद्ध-धर्मिणोस् तात स्थितयोर् एक-धर्मिणि
verses.transliteration: atha baddhasya muktasya vailakṣaṇyaṃ vadāmi te / viruddha-dharmiṇos tāta sthitayor eka-dharmiṇi
verses.translation: Let Me now, My dearest, dilate on the different characteristics of the opposing nature of being conditioned and being liberated, an opposition that is found in one and the same practice.

verses.sanskrit: सुपर्णाव् एतौ सदृशौ सखायौ यदृच्छयैतौ कृत-नीडौ च वृक्षे । एकस् तयोः खादति पिप्पलान्नम् अन्यो निरन्नो ऽपि बलेन भूयान्
verses.transliteration: suparṇāv etau sadṛśau sakhāyau yadṛcchayaitau kṛta-nīḍau ca vṛkṣe / ekas tayoḥ khādati pippalānnam anyo niranno 'pi balena bhūyān
verses.translation: Two birds of a similar nature and friends of each other, one day make a nest in a tree. One of them eats from the fruits of the tree, while the other does not eat, even though he is the strongest of the two [see also 6.4: 24].

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानम् अन्यं च स वेद विद्वान् अपिप्पलादो न तु पिप्पलादः । यो ऽविद्यया युक् स तु नित्य-बद्धो विद्या-मयो यः स तु नित्य-मुक्तः
verses.transliteration: ātmānam anyaṃ ca sa veda vidvān apippalādo na tu pippalādaḥ / yo 'vidyayā yuk sa tu nitya-baddho vidyā-mayo yaḥ sa tu nitya-muktaḥ
verses.translation: The one not eating the fruits of the tree, is conscious and knows both himself and the other one, while the one that eats does not have a clue. The latter is always bound, while the one full of knowledge is eternally liberated [see also B.G. 4: 5].

verses.sanskrit: देह-स्थो ऽपि न देह-स्थो विद्वान् स्वप्नाद् यथोत्थितः । अदेह-स्थो ऽपि देह-स्थः कुमतिः स्वप्न-दृग् यथा
verses.transliteration: deha-stho 'pi na deha-stho vidvān svapnād yathotthitaḥ / adeha-stho 'pi deha-sthaḥ kumatiḥ svapna-dṛg yathā
verses.translation: Although engaged in a body an enlightened person does not abide by the body, like having risen from a dream. A foolish person abides by the body even though he is not the body itself, like seeing a dream [see B.G. 16: 18].

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियैर् इन्द्रियार्थेषु गुणैर् अपि गुणेषु च । गृह्यमाणेष्व् अहं कुर्यान् न विद्वान् यस् त्व् अविक्रियः
verses.transliteration: indriyair indriyārtheṣu guṇair api guṇeṣu ca / gṛhyamāṇeṣv ahaṃ kuryān na vidvān yas tv avikriyaḥ
verses.translation: Someone not enlightened is invariably obliged to the false ego, that thinks in accord with the senses directed by the gunas and the sense objects generated by the modes [see B.G. 3: 28].

verses.sanskrit: दैवाधीने शरीरे ऽस्मिन् गुण-भाव्येन कर्मणा । वर्तमानो ऽबुधस् तत्र कर्तास्मीति निबध्यते
verses.transliteration: daivādhīne śarīre 'smin guṇa-bhāvyena karmaṇā / vartamāno 'budhas tatra kartāsmīti nibadhyate
verses.translation: The ignorant soul who thus, by fate ordained, was caught here in this body because of his by the gunas generated karmic activities, thinks: 'I am the doer' [see also B.G. 3: 27].

verses.sanskrit: एवं विरक्तः शयन आसनाटन-मज्जने । दर्शन-स्पर्शन-घ्राण- भोजन-श्रवणादिषु । न तथा बध्यते विद्वान् तत्र तत्रादयन् गुणान्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ viraktaḥ śayana āsanāṭana-majjane / darśana-sparśana-ghrāṇa- bhojana-śravaṇādiṣu / na tathā badhyate vidvān tatra tatrādayan guṇān
verses.translation: An intelligent person is not bound that way. Wherever he goes, rests, sits, walks, bathes, sees, touches, smells, eats, hears and so on, he remains detached from the qualities he experiences.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृति-स्थो ऽप्य् असंसक्तो यथा खं सवितानिलः । वैशारद्येक्षयासङ्ग- शितया छिन्न-संशयः । प्रतिबुद्ध इव स्वप्नान् नानात्वाद् विनिवर्तते
verses.transliteration: prakṛti-stho 'py asaṃsakto yathā khaṃ savitānilaḥ / vaiśāradyekṣayāsaṅga- śitayā chinna-saṃśayaḥ / pratibuddha iva svapnān nānātvād vinivartate
verses.translation: Though being situated in the material world, he, turned away from its ruling powers and assisted by the most expert and in detachment sharpened vision, cuts with all doubts. Just as the sky, the sun and the wind do not attach themselves, he neither attaches to the variety of matters [the duality of the world], like he has awakened from a dream.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य स्युर् वीत-सङ्कल्पाः प्राणेन्द्रिय-र्ननो-धियाम् । वृत्तयः स विनिर्मुक्तो देह-स्थो ऽपि हि तद्-गुणैः
verses.transliteration: yasya syur vīta-saṅkalpāḥ prāṇendriya-rnano-dhiyām / vṛttayaḥ sa vinirmukto deha-stho 'pi hi tad-guṇaiḥ
verses.translation: The person whose functions of the life breath, senses, mind and intelligence are free from desires, is liberated, despite being situated in a body [ruled] by the natural modes.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यात्मा हिंस्यते हिंस्रैर् येन किञ्चिद् यदृच्छया । अर्च्यते वा क्वचित् तत्र न व्यतिक्रियते बुधः
verses.transliteration: yasyātmā hiṃsyate hiṃsrair yena kiñcid yadṛcchayā / arcyate vā kvacit tatra na vyatikriyate budhaḥ
verses.translation: One's body is sometimes for some reason attacked by animals or destructive people, and sometimes one is worshiped [by a lover or a follower]. An intelligent person is never seriously moved when that happens [see B.G. 14: 22-25].

verses.sanskrit: न स्तुवीत न निन्देत कुर्वतः साध्व् असाधु वा । वदतो गुण-दोषाभ्यां वर्जितः सम-दृङ् मुनिः
verses.transliteration: na stuvīta na nindeta kurvataḥ sādhv asādhu vā / vadato guṇa-doṣābhyāṃ varjitaḥ sama-dṛṅ muniḥ
verses.translation: With an equal vision having moved beyond the notion of good and bad, a wise man will neither praise those who are behaving very well or speaking excellently, nor criticize others who are acting or expressing themselves poorly [see also B.G. 5: 18].

verses.sanskrit: न कुर्यान् न वदेत् किञ्चिन् न ध्यायेत् साध्व् असाधु वा । आत्मारामो ऽनया वृत्त्या विचरेज् जड-वन् मुनिः
verses.transliteration: na kuryān na vadet kiñcin na dhyāyet sādhv asādhu vā / ātmārāmo 'nayā vṛttyā vicarej jaḍa-van muniḥ
verses.translation: Someone satisfied within should live free from acting, without saying a word and not contemplating good or evil, and that way wander around like being dumb [see also 5.9].

verses.sanskrit: शब्द-ब्रह्मणि निष्णातो न निष्णायात् परे यदि । श्रमस् तस्य श्रम-फलो ह्य् अधेनुम् इव रक्षतः
verses.transliteration: śabda-brahmaṇi niṣṇāto na niṣṇāyāt pare yadi / śramas tasya śrama-phalo hy adhenum iva rakṣataḥ
verses.translation: When someone well versed in the Vedic literatures is not absorbed in the Supreme [does not meditate or perform devotional service], the fruit of his effort, his work, will be like that of someone taking care of a cow that gives no milk.

verses.sanskrit: गां दुग्ध-दोहाम् असतीं च भार्यां देहं पराधीनम् असत्-प्रजां च । वित्तं त्व् अतीर्थी-कृतम् अङ्ग वाचं हीनां मया रक्षति दुःख-दुःखी
verses.transliteration: gāṃ dugdha-dohām asatīṃ ca bhāryāṃ dehaṃ parādhīnam asat-prajāṃ ca / vittaṃ tv atīrthī-kṛtam aṅga vācaṃ hīnāṃ mayā rakṣati duḥkha-duḥkhī
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, he who takes care of a cow that gives no milk, an unchaste wife, a body fully dependent upon others, children without service, wealth wrongly spent, and speaks [about Vedic knowledge] without [ever] mentioning Me [see also 10.14: 4 and 5.6: 11], will suffer one misery after another.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यां न मे पावनम् अङ्ग कर्म स्थित्य्-उद्भव-प्राण-निरोधम् अस्य । लीलावतारेप्सित-जन्म वा स्याद् वन्ध्यां गिरं तां बिभृयान् न धीरः
verses.transliteration: yasyāṃ na me pāvanam aṅga karma sthity-udbhava-prāṇa-nirodham asya / līlāvatārepsita-janma vā syād vandhyāṃ giraṃ tāṃ bibhṛyān na dhīraḥ
verses.translation: A wise person My dear Uddhava, will not accept words [or texts] devoid of My purifying activities or desired appearances as ll-avatras for the sake of the maintenance, creation and annihilation of the world.

verses.sanskrit: एवं जिज्ञासयापोह्य नानात्व-भ्रमम् आत्मनि । उपारमेत विरजं मनो मय्य् अर्प्य सर्व-गे
verses.transliteration: evaṃ jijñāsayāpohya nānātva-bhramam ātmani / upārameta virajaṃ mano mayy arpya sarva-ge
verses.translation: When one desirous of knowledge thus gives up the misconception of a [material] diversity as existing [separately] from the soul [**], one should put an end to one's materialistic life and fix one's purified mind upon Me, the All-pervading One [see also B.G. 7: 19, B.G. 18: 55].

verses.sanskrit: यद्य् अनीशो धारयितुं मनो ब्रह्मणि निश्चलम् । मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि निरपेक्षः समाचर
verses.transliteration: yady anīśo dhārayituṃ mano brahmaṇi niścalam / mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi nirapekṣaḥ samācara
verses.translation: In case you are not able to steady your mind on the spiritual platform, then dedicate, not deviating [from the regulative principles], all your actions to Me without expecting anything in return [B.G. 12: 11, 10: 10, 18: 54].

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धालुर् मत्-कथाः शृण्वन् सु-भद्रा लोक-पावनीः । गायन्न् अनुस्मरन् कर्म जन्म चाभिनयन् मुहुः
verses.transliteration: śraddhālur mat-kathāḥ śṛṇvan su-bhadrā loka-pāvanīḥ / gāyann anusmaran karma janma cābhinayan muhuḥ
verses.translation: When one with belief listens to, sings about and constantly remembers the narrations about My birth and activities that purify the entire world, and also enacts them dramatically, and thereto, for My sake, under My protection regulates one's religiosity, sensual pleasure and finances [the purushrtas], one will develop an unflinching devotion unto Me, the One Eternal, oh Uddhava.

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्थे धर्म-कामार्थान् आचरन् मद्-अपाश्रयः । लभते निश्चलां भक्तिं मय्य् उद्धव सनातने
verses.transliteration: mad-arthe dharma-kāmārthān ācaran mad-apāśrayaḥ / labhate niścalāṃ bhaktiṃ mayy uddhava sanātane
verses.translation: When one with belief listens to, sings about and constantly remembers the narrations about My birth and activities that purify the entire world, and also enacts them dramatically, and thereto, for My sake, under My protection regulates one's religiosity, sensual pleasure and finances [the purushrtas], one will develop an unflinching devotion unto Me, the One Eternal, oh Uddhava.

verses.sanskrit: सत्-सङ्ग-लब्धया भक्त्या मयि मां स उपासिता । स वै मे दर्शितं सद्भिर् अञ्जसा विन्दते पदम्
verses.transliteration: sat-saṅga-labdhayā bhaktyā mayi māṃ sa upāsitā / sa vai me darśitaṃ sadbhir añjasā vindate padam
verses.translation: By the devotion as acquired in the [sat-sanga] association with devotees, someone very easily will be able to attain My refuge that those pure souls undoubtedly will show him.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच साधुस् तवोत्तम-श्लोक मतः कीदृग्-विधः प्रभो । भक्तिस् त्वय्य् उपयुज्येत कीदृशी सद्भिर् आदृता
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca sādhus tavottama-śloka mataḥ kīdṛg-vidhaḥ prabho / bhaktis tvayy upayujyeta kīdṛśī sadbhir ādṛtā
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'What kind of person would in Your opinion, oh Uttamas'loka, be a pure soul, and what sort of worship unto You would carry the approval of Your honored faithful followers? Please speak about this to me, Your surrendered devotee who loves You as his only shelter, oh Master of the Universe, Supervisor of the World and Commander of the Person.

verses.sanskrit: एतन् मे पुरुषाध्यक्ष लोकाध्यक्ष जगत्-प्रभो । प्रणतायानुरक्ताय प्रपन्नाय च कथ्यताम्
verses.transliteration: etan me puruṣādhyakṣa lokādhyakṣa jagat-prabho / praṇatāyānuraktāya prapannāya ca kathyatām
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'What kind of person would in Your opinion, oh Uttamas'loka, be a pure soul, and what sort of worship unto You would carry the approval of Your honored faithful followers? Please speak about this to me, Your surrendered devotee who loves You as his only shelter, oh Master of the Universe, Supervisor of the World and Commander of the Person.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं ब्रह्म परमं व्योम पुरुषः प्रकृतेः परः । अवतीर्नो ऽसि भगवन् स्वेच्छोपात्त-पृथग्-वपुः
verses.transliteration: tvaṃ brahma paramaṃ vyoma puruṣaḥ prakṛteḥ paraḥ / avatīrno 'si bhagavan svecchopātta-pṛthag-vapuḥ
verses.translation: You, the Supreme Truth and Spirit as [unattached] as the sky, the Original Person transcendental to material nature, are the Supreme Lord who, descending to the desire of Your devotees, has accepted a diversity of forms.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच कृपालुर् अकृत-द्रोहस् तितिक्षुः सर्व-देहिनाम् । सत्य-सारो ऽनवद्यात्मा समः सर्वोपकारकः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca kṛpālur akṛta-drohas titikṣuḥ sarva-dehinām / satya-sāro 'navadyātmā samaḥ sarvopakārakaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When someone is merciful, causes no harm, is tolerant towards all embodied beings, is firmly anchored in the truth, an irreproachable soul, is equal-minded and helpful towards everyone; when someone is of an intelligence that is not disturbed by material desires, is of restraint, is soft-natured, pure-hearted, non-possessive, not worldly, eats little, is peaceful, steady, has Me for his shelter and is thoughtful; when someone is vigilant, a profound soul, of a firm resolve, has conquered the shath-guna [the six different forms of material misery], offers respect, inspires, is friendly, compassionate and learned; when someone knows the qualities and shortcomings as taught by Me, and, worshiping Me, is even willing to give up all his religious preferences [see also B.G. 18: 66], he belongs to the best of the truthful souls [see also 5.18: 12, B.G. 12: 13-20].

verses.sanskrit: कामैर् अहत-धीर् दान्तो मृदुः शुचिर् अकिञ्चनः । अनीहो मित-भुक् शान्तः स्थिरो मच्-छरणो मुनिः
verses.transliteration: kāmair ahata-dhīr dānto mṛduḥ śucir akiñcanaḥ / anīho mita-bhuk śāntaḥ sthiro mac-charaṇo muniḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When someone is merciful, causes no harm, is tolerant towards all embodied beings, is firmly anchored in the truth, an irreproachable soul, is equal-minded and helpful towards everyone; when someone is of an intelligence that is not disturbed by material desires, is of restraint, is soft-natured, pure-hearted, non-possessive, not worldly, eats little, is peaceful, steady, has Me for his shelter and is thoughtful; when someone is vigilant, a profound soul, of a firm resolve, has conquered the shath-guna [the six different forms of material misery], offers respect, inspires, is friendly, compassionate and learned; when someone knows the qualities and shortcomings as taught by Me, and, worshiping Me, is even willing to give up all his religious preferences [see also B.G. 18: 66], he belongs to the best of the truthful souls [see also 5.18: 12, B.G. 12: 13-20].

verses.sanskrit: अप्रमत्तो गभीरात्मा धृतिमाञ् जित-षड्-गुणः । अमानी मान-दः कल्यो मैत्रः कारुणिकः कविः
verses.transliteration: apramatto gabhīrātmā dhṛtimāñ jita-ṣaḍ-guṇaḥ / amānī māna-daḥ kalyo maitraḥ kāruṇikaḥ kaviḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When someone is merciful, causes no harm, is tolerant towards all embodied beings, is firmly anchored in the truth, an irreproachable soul, is equal-minded and helpful towards everyone; when someone is of an intelligence that is not disturbed by material desires, is of restraint, is soft-natured, pure-hearted, non-possessive, not worldly, eats little, is peaceful, steady, has Me for his shelter and is thoughtful; when someone is vigilant, a profound soul, of a firm resolve, has conquered the shath-guna [the six different forms of material misery], offers respect, inspires, is friendly, compassionate and learned; when someone knows the qualities and shortcomings as taught by Me, and, worshiping Me, is even willing to give up all his religious preferences [see also B.G. 18: 66], he belongs to the best of the truthful souls [see also 5.18: 12, B.G. 12: 13-20].

verses.sanskrit: आज्ञायैवं गुणान् दोषान् मयादिष्टान् अपि स्वकान् । धर्मान् सन्त्यज्य यः सर्वान् मां भजेत स तु सत्तमः
verses.transliteration: ājñāyaivaṃ guṇān doṣān mayādiṣṭān api svakān / dharmān santyajya yaḥ sarvān māṃ bhajeta sa tu sattamaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When someone is merciful, causes no harm, is tolerant towards all embodied beings, is firmly anchored in the truth, an irreproachable soul, is equal-minded and helpful towards everyone; when someone is of an intelligence that is not disturbed by material desires, is of restraint, is soft-natured, pure-hearted, non-possessive, not worldly, eats little, is peaceful, steady, has Me for his shelter and is thoughtful; when someone is vigilant, a profound soul, of a firm resolve, has conquered the shath-guna [the six different forms of material misery], offers respect, inspires, is friendly, compassionate and learned; when someone knows the qualities and shortcomings as taught by Me, and, worshiping Me, is even willing to give up all his religious preferences [see also B.G. 18: 66], he belongs to the best of the truthful souls [see also 5.18: 12, B.G. 12: 13-20].

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञात्वाज्ञात्वाथ ये वै मां यावान् यश् चास्मि यादृशः । भजन्त्य् अनन्य-भावेन ते मे भक्ततमा मताः
verses.transliteration: jñātvājñātvātha ye vai māṃ yāvān yaś cāsmi yādṛśaḥ / bhajanty ananya-bhāvena te me bhaktatamā matāḥ
verses.translation: Whether someone knows or does not know what I am, who I am or how I exist, when he is exclusively devoted to Me, I count him among the best devotees.

verses.sanskrit: मल्-लिङ्ग-मद्-भक्त-जन- दर्शन-स्पर्शनार्चनम् । परिचर्या स्तुतिः प्रह्व- गुण-कर्मानुकीर्तनम्
verses.transliteration: mal-liṅga-mad-bhakta-jana- darśana-sparśanārcanam / paricaryā stutiḥ prahva- guṇa-karmānukīrtanam
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: मत्-कथा-श्रवणे श्रद्धा मद्-अनुध्यानम् उद्धव । सर्व-लाभोपहरणं दास्येनात्म-निवेदनम्
verses.transliteration: mat-kathā-śravaṇe śraddhā mad-anudhyānam uddhava / sarva-lābhopaharaṇaṃ dāsyenātma-nivedanam
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: मज्-जन्म-कर्म-कथनं मम पर्वानुमोदनम् । गीत-ताण्डव-वादित्र- गोष्ठीभिर् मद्-गृहोत्सवः
verses.transliteration: maj-janma-karma-kathanaṃ mama parvānumodanam / gīta-tāṇḍava-vāditra- goṣṭhībhir mad-gṛhotsavaḥ
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: यात्रा बलि-विधानं च सर्व-वार्षिक-पर्वसु । वैदिकी तान्त्रिकी दीक्षा मदीय-व्रत-धारणम्
verses.transliteration: yātrā bali-vidhānaṃ ca sarva-vārṣika-parvasu / vaidikī tāntrikī dīkṣā madīya-vrata-dhāraṇam
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: ममार्चा-स्थापने श्रद्धा स्वतः संहत्य चोद्यमः । उद्यानोपवनाक्रीड- पुर-मन्दिर-कर्मणि
verses.transliteration: mamārcā-sthāpane śraddhā svataḥ saṃhatya codyamaḥ / udyānopavanākrīḍa- pura-mandira-karmaṇi
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: सम्मार्जनोपलेपाभ्यां सेक-मण्डल-वर्तनैः । गृह-शुश्रूषणं मह्यं दास-वद् यद् अमायया
verses.transliteration: sammārjanopalepābhyāṃ seka-maṇḍala-vartanaiḥ / gṛha-śuśrūṣaṇaṃ mahyaṃ dāsa-vad yad amāyayā
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: अमानित्वम् अदम्भित्वं कृतस्यापरिकीर्तनम् । अपि दीपावलोकं मे नोपयुञ्ज्यान् निवेदितम्
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvaṃ kṛtasyāparikīrtanam / api dīpāvalokaṃ me nopayuñjyān niveditam
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: यद् यद् इष्टतमं लोके यच् चाति-प्रियम् आत्मनः । तत् तन् निवेदयेन् मह्यं तद् आनन्त्याय कल्पते
verses.transliteration: yad yad iṣṭatamaṃ loke yac cāti-priyam ātmanaḥ / tat tan nivedayen mahyaṃ tad ānantyāya kalpate
verses.translation: Seeing, touching and worshiping My appearance in this world, My devotees render personal service and offer prayers of glorification and obeisance, to which they regularly sing about My qualities and activities. Hearing the topics about Me they always with faith meditate upon Me, oh Uddhava, and offer as servants in full surrender all that they acquire. Discussing My birth and activities they take great delight in engaging with musical instruments and songs and dance, with discussions and festivals in My sacred houses. Sacrificing at all celebrations and annual festivities as mentioned in the Vedic literatures and their tantras, they observe vows and are of initiation in relation to Me. With faithfully installing My deity they endeavor, for themselves as also for others, by working for flower gardens, orchards, playgrounds, cities and temples. Sincere as servants they serve Me by thoroughly cleaning and dusting the house [the temple], washing it with water [and cow-dung, see also 10.6: 20*], sprinkling it with scented water and making mandalas. Modestly without pride, not advertising their devotional service, they not even use for themselves the light of the lamps presented to Me and offer Me articles most dear to them or most desirable in the material world. With that kind of offering one qualifies for immortality.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्यो ऽग्निर् ब्राह्मणा गावो वैष्णवः खं मरुज् जलम् । भूर् आत्मा सर्व-भूतानि भद्र पूजा-पदानि मे
verses.transliteration: sūryo 'gnir brāhmaṇā gāvo vaiṣṇavaḥ khaṃ maruj jalam / bhūr ātmā sarva-bhūtāni bhadra pūjā-padāni me
verses.translation: The sun, the fire, the brahmins, the cows, the Vaishnavas, the sky, the wind, the water, the earth, the self and all living beings, My dearest, constitute the places of My worship.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्ये तु विद्यया त्रय्या हविषाग्नौ यजेत माम् । आतिथ्येन तु विप्राग्र्ये गोष्व् अङ्ग यवसादिना
verses.transliteration: sūrye tu vidyayā trayyā haviṣāgnau yajeta mām / ātithyena tu viprāgrye goṣv aṅga yavasādinā
verses.translation: In the sun one can find Me by offering selected verses from the three Vedas [see also the Gyatr and the Srya-namaskr]. In fire one finds Me with oblations of ghee. One can find Me as the best among the scholars when one worships Me by offering them hospitality. With the cows, My dear, you reach Me by means of offerings of grass and such. One finds Me in the Vaishnavas by honoring them with loving friendship. I am found in the sky [of one's inner space] when one is fixed in meditation in the heart. In the air one finds Me by considering it the most important [life giving principle of prna, by means of prnyma see B.G. 4: 29]. In water one can find Me by using articles of worship together with water [see B.G. 9: 26]. In the earth [one finds Me] with confidential mantras and within the self one can find Me by offering food to the embodied soul [see e.g. Prasda sevya and Bhoga rati]. One may worship Me as the knower of the field in all living beings [see Paramtm and B.G. 13: 3] by seeing Me as equally present everywhere [see niyama].

verses.sanskrit: वैष्णवे बन्धु-सत्-कृत्या हृदि खे ध्यान-निष्ठया । वायौ मुख्य-धिया तोये द्रव्यैस् तोय-पुरःसरैः
verses.transliteration: vaiṣṇave bandhu-sat-kṛtyā hṛdi khe dhyāna-niṣṭhayā / vāyau mukhya-dhiyā toye dravyais toya-puraḥsaraiḥ
verses.translation: In the sun one can find Me by offering selected verses from the three Vedas [see also the Gyatr and the Srya-namaskr]. In fire one finds Me with oblations of ghee. One can find Me as the best among the scholars when one worships Me by offering them hospitality. With the cows, My dear, you reach Me by means of offerings of grass and such. One finds Me in the Vaishnavas by honoring them with loving friendship. I am found in the sky [of one's inner space] when one is fixed in meditation in the heart. In the air one finds Me by considering it the most important [life giving principle of prna, by means of prnyma see B.G. 4: 29]. In water one can find Me by using articles of worship together with water [see B.G. 9: 26]. In the earth [one finds Me] with confidential mantras and within the self one can find Me by offering food to the embodied soul [see e.g. Prasda sevya and Bhoga rati]. One may worship Me as the knower of the field in all living beings [see Paramtm and B.G. 13: 3] by seeing Me as equally present everywhere [see niyama].

verses.sanskrit: स्थण्डिले मन्त्र-हृदयैर् भोगैर् आत्मानम् आत्मनि । क्षेत्र-ज्ञं सर्व-भूतेषु समत्वेन यजेत माम्
verses.transliteration: sthaṇḍile mantra-hṛdayair bhogair ātmānam ātmani / kṣetra-jñaṃ sarva-bhūteṣu samatvena yajeta mām
verses.translation: In the sun one can find Me by offering selected verses from the three Vedas [see also the Gyatr and the Srya-namaskr]. In fire one finds Me with oblations of ghee. One can find Me as the best among the scholars when one worships Me by offering them hospitality. With the cows, My dear, you reach Me by means of offerings of grass and such.
