One finds Me in the Vaishnavas by honoring them with loving friendship. I am found in the sky [of one's inner space] when one is fixed in meditation in the heart. In the air one finds Me by considering it the most important [life giving principle of prna, by means of prnyma see B.G. 4: 29]. In water one can find Me by using articles of worship together with water [see B.G. 9: 26]. In the earth [one finds Me] with confidential mantras and within the self one can find Me by offering food to the embodied soul [see e.g. Prasda sevya and Bhoga rati]. One may worship Me as the knower of the field in all living beings [see Paramtm and B.G. 13: 3] by seeing Me as equally present everywhere [see niyama].

verses.sanskrit: धिष्ण्येष्व् इत्य् एषु मद्-रूपं शङ्ख-चक्र-गदाम्बुजैः । युक्तं चतुर्-भुजं शान्तं ध्यायन्न् अर्चेत् समाहितः
verses.transliteration: dhiṣṇyeṣv ity eṣu mad-rūpaṃ śaṅkha-cakra-gadāmbujaiḥ / yuktaṃ catur-bhujaṃ śāntaṃ dhyāyann arcet samāhitaḥ
verses.translation: Peacefully being absorbed in Me thus meditating these different places, one should be of worship for My transcendental form that is equipped with the conch shell, the disc, the club and the lotus flower [see picture].

verses.sanskrit: इष्टा-पूर्तेन माम् एवं यो यजेत समाहितः । लभते मयि सद्-भक्तिं मत्-स्मृतिः साधु-सेवया
verses.transliteration: iṣṭā-pūrtena mām evaṃ yo yajeta samāhitaḥ / labhate mayi sad-bhaktiṃ mat-smṛtiḥ sādhu-sevayā
verses.translation: Someone who with his mind fixed on Me thus worships Me with sacrificial performances and pious works [as mentioned], obtains loving service unto Me and has My remembrance [of realized knowledge] because of his fine service [see also B.G. 5: 29].

verses.sanskrit: प्रायेण भक्ति-योगेन सत्-सङ्गेन विनोद्धव । नोपायो विद्यते सम्यक् प्रायणं हि सताम् अहम्
verses.transliteration: prāyeṇa bhakti-yogena sat-saṅgena vinoddhava / nopāyo vidyate samyak prāyaṇaṃ hi satām aham
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, apart from the bhakti-yoga realized through association with My devotees, there are no means that actually work, for I am the path of life, the refuge, for the souls of virtue [see also 4.31: 12].

verses.sanskrit: अथैतत् परमं गुह्यं शृण्वतो यदु-नन्दन । सु-गोप्यम् अपि वक्ष्यामि त्वं मे भृत्यः सुहृत् सखा
verses.transliteration: athaitat paramaṃ guhyaṃ śṛṇvato yadu-nandana / su-gopyam api vakṣyāmi tvaṃ me bhṛtyaḥ suhṛt sakhā
verses.translation: And now, oh child of the Yadus, I will speak to you, who are willing to listen, even about the most confidential supreme secret [of intimate association with Me], for you are My servant, well-wisher and friend [compare B.G. 18: 63 & 68].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच न रोधयति मां योगो न साङ्ख्यं धर्म एव च । न स्वाध्यायस् तपस् त्यागो नेष्टा-पूर्तं न दक्षिणा
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca na rodhayati māṃ yogo na sāṅkhyaṃ dharma eva ca / na svādhyāyas tapas tyāgo neṣṭā-pūrtaṃ na dakṣiṇā
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'One does not ascend to Me by mysticism or analysis, common piety or the study of the scriptures, by penances, renunciation, pious works or charity, by respecting vows, ceremonies, Vedic hymns, pilgrimage, by general discipline or the basic rules. One rather closes Me in one's heart by the sat-sanga with My devotees [see 11.11: 25] that drives away all attachment to sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: व्रतानि यज्ञश् छन्दांसि तीर्थानि नियमा यमाः । यथावरुन्धे सत्-सङ्गः सर्व-सङ्गापहो हि माम्
verses.transliteration: vratāni yajñaś chandāṃsi tīrthāni niyamā yamāḥ / yathāvarundhe sat-saṅgaḥ sarva-saṅgāpaho hi mām
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'One does not ascend to Me by mysticism or analysis, common piety or the study of the scriptures, by penances, renunciation, pious works or charity, by respecting vows, ceremonies, Vedic hymns, pilgrimage, by general discipline or the basic rules. One rather closes Me in one's heart by the sat-sanga with My devotees [see 11.11: 25] that drives away all attachment to sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: सत्-सङ्गेन हि दैतेया यातुधाना मृगाः खगाः । गन्धर्वाप्सरसो नागाः सिद्धाश् चारण-गुह्यकाः
verses.transliteration: sat-saṅgena hi daiteyā yātudhānā mṛgāḥ khagāḥ / gandharvāpsaraso nāgāḥ siddhāś cāraṇa-guhyakāḥ
verses.translation: By associating with My devotees many living beings in every age achieved My refuge, like the sons of Diti, the malicious ones, the animals, the birds, the singers and dancers of heaven, the souls of excellence and of perfection, the venerable souls and the treasure keepers, the scientific ones among the humans and the merchants, laborers and women, the uncivilized ones and those of passion and slowness. So also did Vritrsura, the son of Kaydhu [Prahlda, see 6.18: 12-13] and others like them, Vrishaparv [see 9.18: 26], Bali, Bna, Maya as also Vibhshana [brother of Rvana], Sugrva [leader of the Vnaras] and Hanumn, Jmbavn, Gajendra, Jathyu, Tuldhra, Dharma-vydha, Kubj and the gops in Vraja, the wives of the brahmins [see 10.23] and others.

verses.sanskrit: विद्याधरा मनुष्येषु वैश्याः शूद्राः स्त्रियो ऽन्त्य-जाः । रजस्-तमः-प्रकृतयस् तस्मिंस् तस्मिन् युगे युगे
verses.transliteration: vidyādharā manuṣyeṣu vaiśyāḥ śūdrāḥ striyo 'ntya-jāḥ / rajas-tamaḥ-prakṛtayas tasmiṃs tasmin yuge yuge
verses.translation: By associating with My devotees many living beings in every age achieved My refuge, like the sons of Diti, the malicious ones, the animals, the birds, the singers and dancers of heaven, the souls of excellence and of perfection, the venerable souls and the treasure keepers, the scientific ones among the humans and the merchants, laborers and women, the uncivilized ones and those of passion and slowness. So also did Vritrsura, the son of Kaydhu [Prahlda, see 6.18: 12-13] and others like them, Vrishaparv [see 9.18: 26], Bali, Bna, Maya as also Vibhshana [brother of Rvana], Sugrva [leader of the Vnaras] and Hanumn, Jmbavn, Gajendra, Jathyu, Tuldhra, Dharma-vydha, Kubj and the gops in Vraja, the wives of the brahmins [see 10.23] and others.

verses.sanskrit: बहवो मत्-पदं प्राप्तास् त्वाष्ट्र-कायाधवादयः । वृषपर्वा बलिर् बाणो मयश् चाथ विभीषणः
verses.transliteration: bahavo mat-padaṃ prāptās tvāṣṭra-kāyādhavādayaḥ / vṛṣaparvā balir bāṇo mayaś cātha vibhīṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: By associating with My devotees many living beings in every age achieved My refuge, like the sons of Diti, the malicious ones, the animals, the birds, the singers and dancers of heaven, the souls of excellence and of perfection, the venerable souls and the treasure keepers, the scientific ones among the humans and the merchants, laborers and women, the uncivilized ones and those of passion and slowness. So also did Vritrsura, the son of Kaydhu [Prahlda, see 6.18: 12-13] and others like them, Vrishaparv [see 9.18: 26], Bali, Bna, Maya as also Vibhshana [brother of Rvana], Sugrva [leader of the Vnaras] and Hanumn, Jmbavn, Gajendra, Jathyu, Tuldhra, Dharma-vydha, Kubj and the gops in Vraja, the wives of the brahmins [see 10.23] and others.

verses.sanskrit: सुग्रीवो हनुमान् ऋक्षो गजो गृध्रो वणिक्पथः । व्याधः कुब्जा व्रजे गोप्यो यज्ञ-पत्न्यस् तथापरे
verses.transliteration: sugrīvo hanumān ṛkṣo gajo gṛdhro vaṇikpathaḥ / vyādhaḥ kubjā vraje gopyo yajña-patnyas tathāpare
verses.translation: By associating with My devotees many living beings in every age achieved My refuge, like the sons of Diti, the malicious ones, the animals, the birds, the singers and dancers of heaven, the souls of excellence and of perfection, the venerable souls and the treasure keepers, the scientific ones among the humans and the merchants, laborers and women, the uncivilized ones and those of passion and slowness. So also did Vritrsura, the son of Kaydhu [Prahlda, see 6.18: 12-13] and others like them, Vrishaparv [see 9.18: 26], Bali, Bna, Maya as also Vibhshana [brother of Rvana], Sugrva [leader of the Vnaras] and Hanumn, Jmbavn, Gajendra, Jathyu, Tuldhra, Dharma-vydha, Kubj and the gops in Vraja, the wives of the brahmins [see 10.23] and others.

verses.sanskrit: ते नाधीत-श्रुति-गणा नोपासित-महत्तमाः । अव्रतातप्त-तपसः मत्-सङ्गान् माम् उपागताः
verses.transliteration: te nādhīta-śruti-gaṇā nopāsita-mahattamāḥ / avratātapta-tapasaḥ mat-saṅgān mām upāgatāḥ
verses.translation: Not having studied the sacred scriptures, nor having worshiped the great saints, they all, without vows and not having undergone austerities, attained Me by association with My devotees.

verses.sanskrit: केवलेन हि भावेन गोप्यो गावो नगा मृगाः । ये ऽन्ये मूढ-धियो नागाः सिद्धा माम् ईयुर् अञ्जसा
verses.transliteration: kevalena hi bhāvena gopyo gāvo nagā mṛgāḥ / ye 'nye mūḍha-dhiyo nāgāḥ siddhā mām īyur añjasā
verses.translation: Only by unalloyed love indeed, the gops, just as others of a limited intelligence like the cows, the immobile creatures, the snakes [like Kliya] and more animals, managed to achieve perfection and quite easily attained Me, I who cannot even be reached by greatly endeavoring in yoga, analysis, charity, vows, penances, ritualistic sacrifices, exegesis, personal study or taking to the renounced order.

verses.sanskrit: यं न योगेन साङ्ख्येन दान-व्रत-तपो-ऽध्वरैः । व्याख्या-स्वाध्याय-सन्न्यासैः प्राप्नुयाद् यत्नवान् अपि
verses.transliteration: yaṃ na yogena sāṅkhyena dāna-vrata-tapo-'dhvaraiḥ / vyākhyā-svādhyāya-sannyāsaiḥ prāpnuyād yatnavān api
verses.translation: Only by unalloyed love indeed, the gops, just as others of a limited intelligence like the cows, the immobile creatures, the snakes [like Kliya] and more animals, managed to achieve perfection and quite easily attained Me, I who cannot even be reached by greatly endeavoring in yoga, analysis, charity, vows, penances, ritualistic sacrifices, exegesis, personal study or taking to the renounced order.

verses.sanskrit: रामेण सार्धं मथुरां प्रणीते श्वाफल्किना मय्य् अनुरक्त-चित्ताः । विगाढ-भावेन न मे वियोग- तीव्राधयो ऽन्यं ददृशुः सुखाय
verses.transliteration: rāmeṇa sārdhaṃ mathurāṃ praṇīte śvāphalkinā mayy anurakta-cittāḥ / vigāḍha-bhāvena na me viyoga- tīvrādhayo 'nyaṃ dadṛśuḥ sukhāya
verses.translation: When Akrra took Me and Balarma to Mathur, they, [the inhabitants of Vrindvana] whose hearts were attached [to Me] with the deepest love, suffered greatly because of the separation, for they saw no one else to make them happy [see 10: 39].

verses.sanskrit: तास् ताः क्षपाः प्रेष्ठतमेन नीता मयैव वृन्दावन-गोचरेण । क्षणार्ध-वत् ताः पुनर् अङ्ग तासां हीना मया कल्प-समा बभूवुः
verses.transliteration: tās tāḥ kṣapāḥ preṣṭhatamena nītā mayaiva vṛndāvana-gocareṇa / kṣaṇārdha-vat tāḥ punar aṅga tāsāṃ hīnā mayā kalpa-samā babhūvuḥ
verses.translation: All the nights they in Vrindvana spent with Me, their most dearly Beloved, oh Uddhava, seemed to take but a moment to them, but again bereft of Me they became as long as a kalpa.

verses.sanskrit: ता नाविदन् मय्य् अनुषङ्ग-बद्ध- धियः स्वम् आत्मानम् अदस् तथेदम् । यथा समाधौ मुनयो ऽब्धि-तोये नद्यः प्रविष्टा इव नाम-रूपे
verses.transliteration: tā nāvidan mayy anuṣaṅga-baddha- dhiyaḥ svam ātmānam adas tathedam / yathā samādhau munayo 'bdhi-toye nadyaḥ praviṣṭā iva nāma-rūpe
verses.translation: Just as sages fully absorbed lose the awareness of names and forms - like they are rivers entering the water of the ocean - also they, whose consciousness was intimately bound to Me, had no awareness anymore of their bodies, their present or their future [see also B.G. 2: 70].

verses.sanskrit: मत्-कामा रमणं जारम् अस्वरूप-विदो ऽबलाः । ब्रह्म मां परमं प्रापुः सङ्गाच् छत-सहस्रशः
verses.transliteration: mat-kāmā ramaṇaṃ jāram asvarūpa-vido 'balāḥ / brahma māṃ paramaṃ prāpuḥ saṅgāc chata-sahasraśaḥ
verses.translation: The hundreds and thousands [of women] who desired Me as their friend and lover, had no knowledge of My actual position and attained Me by associating with Me, the Absolute Supreme Truth.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् त्वम् उद्धवोत्सृज्य चोदनां प्रतिचोदनाम् । प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च श्रोतव्यं श्रुतम् एव च
verses.transliteration: tasmāt tvam uddhavotsṛjya codanāṃ praticodanām / pravṛttiṃ ca nivṛttiṃ ca śrotavyaṃ śrutam eva ca
verses.translation: Therefore, oh Uddhava, give up the [religious] precepts and prohibitions, denial and routines, and that what you should listen to and have listened to. Choose for Me alone, the actual shelter of the Soul within all embodied beings. With that exclusive devotion you will enjoy My mercy and have nothing to fear from any side [compare B.g. 18: 66].'

verses.sanskrit: माम् एकम् एव शरणम् आत्मानं सर्व-देहिनाम् । याहि सर्वात्म-भावेन मया स्या ह्य् अकुतो-भयः
verses.transliteration: mām ekam eva śaraṇam ātmānaṃ sarva-dehinām / yāhi sarvātma-bhāvena mayā syā hy akuto-bhayaḥ
verses.translation: Therefore, oh Uddhava, give up the [religious] precepts and prohibitions, denial and routines, and that what you should listen to and have listened to. Choose for Me alone, the actual shelter of the Soul within all embodied beings. With that exclusive devotion you will enjoy My mercy and have nothing to fear from any side [compare B.g. 18: 66].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच संशयः शृण्वतो वाचं तव योगेश्वरेश्वर । न निवर्तत आत्म-स्थो येन भ्राम्यति मे मनः
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca saṃśayaḥ śṛṇvato vācaṃ tava yogeśvareśvara / na nivartata ātma-stho yena bhrāmyati me manaḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Hearing Your words, oh master of all yoga masters, has not dispelled the doubt in my heart that is clouding my mind.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच स एष जीवो विवर-प्रसूतिः प्राणेन घोषेण गुहां प्रविष्टः । मनो-मयं सूक्ष्मम् उपेत्य रूपं मात्रा स्वरो वर्ण इति स्थविष्ठः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca sa eṣa jīvo vivara-prasūtiḥ prāṇena ghoṣeṇa guhāṃ praviṣṭaḥ / mano-mayaṃ sūkṣmam upetya rūpaṃ mātrā svaro varṇa iti sthaviṣṭhaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'He, the living being in person [the Lord], is manifest within along with the prna. He after all entered the heart and has His place in the subtle sound vibration that fills the mind with the grosser form of the [sounds of words, that are] different intonations of short and long vowels and consonants.

verses.sanskrit: यथानलः खे ऽनिल-बन्धुर् उष्मा बलेन दारुण्य् अधिमथ्यमानः । अणुः प्रजातो हविषा समेधते तथैव मे व्यक्तिर् इयं हि वाणी
verses.transliteration: yathānalaḥ khe 'nila-bandhur uṣmā balena dāruṇy adhimathyamānaḥ / aṇuḥ prajāto haviṣā samedhate tathaiva me vyaktir iyaṃ hi vāṇī
verses.translation: Just as fire confined within wood, with the help of air, kindled by friction is born very tiny and increases with ghee, I similarly manifest Myself in this [Vedic, inner] voice.

verses.sanskrit: एवं गदिः कर्म गतिर् विसर्गो घ्राणो रसो दृक् स्पर्शः श्रुतिश् च । सङ्कल्प-विज्ञानम् अथाभिमानः सूत्रं रजः-सत्त्व-तमो-विकारः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ gadiḥ karma gatir visargo ghrāṇo raso dṛk sparśaḥ śrutiś ca / saṅkalpa-vijñānam athābhimānaḥ sūtraṃ rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-vikāraḥ
verses.translation: Speech, the function of the hands and legs, the genitals and the anus [the karmendriyas], smell, taste, sight, touch and hearing [the jnendriyas], and the functions of one's determination, wisdom and self concern [or 'mind, intelligence and false ego'], as also the primary cause of matter [pradhna or the 'thread', see 11.9: 19] and [the gunas] rajas, tamas and sattva, are thus [to be understood as] transformations [of My nature].

verses.sanskrit: अयं हि जीवस् त्रि-वृद् अब्ज-योनिर् अव्यक्त एको वयसा स आद्यः । विश्लिष्ट-शक्तिर् बहुधेव भाति बीजानि योनिं प्रतिपद्य यद्वत्
verses.transliteration: ayaṃ hi jīvas tri-vṛd abja-yonir avyakta eko vayasā sa ādyaḥ / viśliṣṭa-śaktir bahudheva bhāti bījāni yoniṃ pratipadya yadvat
verses.translation: This living entity, one and unmanifest, which is concerned with the threefold [nature of the gunas], constitutes the source of the lotus of creation. He who is eternal, in the course of time divided [therewith] His potencies in many divisions, just like seeds do that fell on fertile soil.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन्न् इदं प्रोतम् अशेषम् ओतं पटो यथा तन्तु-वितान-संस्थः । य एष संसार-तरुः पुराणः कर्मात्मकः पुष्प-फले प्रसूते
verses.transliteration: yasminn idaṃ protam aśeṣam otaṃ paṭo yathā tantu-vitāna-saṃsthaḥ / ya eṣa saṃsāra-taruḥ purāṇaḥ karmātmakaḥ puṣpa-phale prasūte
verses.translation: The complete of t his universe, expanding long and wide like cloth expanding along the threads of its warp and woof, is situated in Him [on His thread, see also 6.3: 12 and B.G. 7: 7]. Since time immemorial there is this tree of material existence [samsra] that, blossoming and producing fruits, is naturally inclined to fruitive action [or karma].

verses.sanskrit: द्वे अस्य बीजे शत-मूलस् त्रि-नालः पञ्च-स्कन्धः पञ्च-रस-प्रसूतिः । दशैक-शाखो द्वि-सुपर्ण-नीडस् त्रि-वल्कलो द्वि-फलो ऽर्कं प्रविष्टः
verses.transliteration: dve asya bīje śata-mūlas tri-nālaḥ pañca-skandhaḥ pañca-rasa-prasūtiḥ / daśaika-śākho dvi-suparṇa-nīḍas tri-valkalo dvi-phalo 'rkaṃ praviṣṭaḥ
verses.translation: Of this tree extending in the sun there are two seeds [sin and piety], hundreds of roots [the living entities], three lower trunks [the modes], five upper trunks [the elements], five saps produced [sound, form, touch, taste and aroma], eleven branches [the mind and the ten indriyas], two birds having a nest [jva and tm], three types of bark [air, bile and mucus] and two fruits [happiness and distress]. Those who fervently desiring live a household life [the 'vultures'], enjoy one fruit of the tree [that of sin], whereas the other fruit [of piety] is enjoyed by the swanlike [sages] who live in the forest. He who, with the help of the worshipable souls, [the devotees, the gurus] knows the Oneness of Him who, by dint of His my, appears in many forms, knows the [actual meaning of the] Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: अदन्ति चैकं फलम् अस्य गृध्रा ग्रामे-चरा एकम् अरण्य-वासाः । हंसा य एकं बहु-रूपम् इज्यैर् माया-मयं वेद स वेद वेदम्
verses.transliteration: adanti caikaṃ phalam asya gṛdhrā grāme-carā ekam araṇya-vāsāḥ / haṃsā ya ekaṃ bahu-rūpam ijyair māyā-mayaṃ veda sa veda vedam
verses.translation: Of this tree extending in the sun there are two seeds [sin and piety], hundreds of roots [the living entities], three lower trunks [the modes], five upper trunks [the elements], five saps produced [sound, form, touch, taste and aroma], eleven branches [the mind and the ten indriyas], two birds having a nest [jva and tm], three types of bark [air, bile and mucus] and two fruits [happiness and distress]. Those who fervently desiring live a household life [the 'vultures'], enjoy one fruit of the tree [that of sin], whereas the other fruit [of piety] is enjoyed by the swanlike [sages] who live in the forest. He who, with the help of the worshipable souls, [the devotees, the gurus] knows the Oneness of Him who, by dint of His my, appears in many forms, knows the [actual meaning of the] Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: एवं गुरूपासनयैक-भक्त्या विद्या-कुठारेण शितेन धीरः । विवृश्च्य जीवाशयम् अप्रमत्तः सम्पद्य चात्मानम् अथ त्यजास्त्रम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ gurūpāsanayaika-bhaktyā vidyā-kuṭhāreṇa śitena dhīraḥ / vivṛścya jīvāśayam apramattaḥ sampadya cātmānam atha tyajāstram
verses.translation: The sober soul who, thus with unalloyed devotion, honors the spiritual master and with the axe of knowledge cuts with the subtle body of attachment entertained by the individual soul, attains with the greatest care [living spiritually] the Supreme Soul, and then abandons the means [by which he attained, see also B.G. 15: 3-4].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच सत्त्वं रजस् तम इति गुणा बुद्धेर् न चात्मनः । सत्त्वेनान्यतमौ हन्यात् सत्त्वं सत्त्वेन चैव हि
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca sattvaṃ rajas tama iti guṇā buddher na cātmanaḥ / sattvenānyatamau hanyāt sattvaṃ sattvena caiva hi
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The goodness, passion and ignorance we know from the gunas are matters of the mind and not of the soul; with goodness the other two can be counteracted while goodness itself is controlled by character and good sense [*].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वाद् धर्मो भवेद् वृद्धात् पुंसो मद्-भक्ति-लक्षणः । सात्त्विकोपासया सत्त्वं ततो धर्मः प्रवर्तते
verses.transliteration: sattvād dharmo bhaved vṛddhāt puṃso mad-bhakti-lakṣaṇaḥ / sāttvikopāsayā sattvaṃ tato dharmaḥ pravartate
verses.translation: The goodness of someone leads to - and strengthens - the dharma that is characterized by devotional service unto Me. That what belongs to the mode of goodness [like nature, consciousness, courage and wisdom] will result in [bhgavata-]dharma when one seriously cultivates [its] inner strength.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मो रजस् तमो हन्यात् सत्त्व-वृद्धिर् अनुत्तमः । आशु नश्यति तन्-मूलो ह्य् अधर्म उभये हते
verses.transliteration: dharmo rajas tamo hanyāt sattva-vṛddhir anuttamaḥ / āśu naśyati tan-mūlo hy adharma ubhaye hate
verses.translation: When goodness increases and dominates, dharma puts an end to passion and ignorance. When they are superseded the godlessness [adharma] that is the root of the two is quickly vanquished.

verses.sanskrit: आगमो ऽपः प्रजा देशः कालः कर्म च जन्म च । ध्यानं मन्त्रो ऽथ संस्कारो दशैते गुण-हेतवः
verses.transliteration: āgamo 'paḥ prajā deśaḥ kālaḥ karma ca janma ca / dhyānaṃ mantro 'tha saṃskāro daśaite guṇa-hetavaḥ
verses.translation: The doctrine [followed], [the way one deals with] water, the people [one associates with], one's surroundings and [the way one behaves with] time, one's [occupational] activities, one's birth [or social background], as also [the type of] meditation, mantras and purificatory rites [one respects], are the ten [factors] determining the [prominence of a particular] mode.

verses.sanskrit: तत् तत् सात्त्विकम् एवैषां यद् यद् वृद्धाः प्रचक्षते । निन्दन्ति तामसं तत् तद् राजसं तद्-उपेक्षितम्
verses.transliteration: tat tat sāttvikam evaiṣāṃ yad yad vṛddhāḥ pracakṣate / nindanti tāmasaṃ tat tad rājasaṃ tad-upekṣitam
verses.translation: That what of these matters belongs to the mode of goodness is appreciated by the classical sages, that what belongs to the mode of ignorance is criticized by them, and that what belongs to the mode of passion they are neutral about.

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विकान्य् एव सेवेत पुमान् सत्त्व-विवृद्धये । ततो धर्मस् ततो ज्ञानं यावत् स्मृतिर् अपोहनम्
verses.transliteration: sāttvikāny eva seveta pumān sattva-vivṛddhaye / tato dharmas tato jñānaṃ yāvat smṛtir apohanam
verses.translation: As long as there is no self-realization [self-remembrance] making amends [for the influence of the basic qualities], a person should cultivate the things belonging to the mode of goodness, so that the character develops from which the religiousness rises that leads to spiritual insight.

verses.sanskrit: वेणु-सङ्घर्ष-जो वह्निर् दग्ध्वा शाम्यति तद्-वनम् । एवं गुण-व्यत्यय-जो देहः शाम्यति तत्-क्रियः
verses.transliteration: veṇu-saṅgharṣa-jo vahnir dagdhvā śāmyati tad-vanam / evaṃ guṇa-vyatyaya-jo dehaḥ śāmyati tat-kriyaḥ
verses.translation: Just as fire, which in a forest of bamboos was generated by friction of the stalks, pacifies after having burned [see also 1.10: 2, 3.1: 21], also the activity of the material body [and the mind] will pacify that was generated by the interaction of the natural modes.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच विदन्ति मर्त्याः प्रायेण विषयान् पदम् आपदाम् । तथापि भुञ्जते कृष्ण तत् कथं श्व-खराज-वत्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vidanti martyāḥ prāyeṇa viṣayān padam āpadām / tathāpi bhuñjate kṛṣṇa tat kathaṃ śva-kharāja-vat
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Mortals generally are very well known with the fact that sense gratification is a source of trouble, but they nevertheless delight in it, oh Krishna. How can it be that they willingly behave like dogs, asses and goats?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच अहम् इत्य् अन्यथा-बुद्धिः प्रमत्तस्य यथा हृदि । उत्सर्पति रजो घोरं ततो वैकारिकं मनः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca aham ity anyathā-buddhiḥ pramattasya yathā hṛdi / utsarpati rajo ghoraṃ tato vaikārikaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'It is because a fool has another intelligence in his I-awareness. Not paying attention [to his dharma] a terrible passion arises in his heart that leads his mind astray. Bound to that passion the mind thus convinced imagines all kinds of things. Focussed on the qualities of nature [the gunas] he is thus beset with desires that make his life unbearable.

verses.sanskrit: रजो-युक्तस्य मनसः सङ्कल्पः स-विकल्पकः । ततः कामो गुण-ध्यानाद् दुःसहः स्याद् धि दुर्मतेः
verses.transliteration: rajo-yuktasya manasaḥ saṅkalpaḥ sa-vikalpakaḥ / tataḥ kāmo guṇa-dhyānād duḥsahaḥ syād dhi durmateḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'It is because a fool has another intelligence in his I-awareness. Not paying attention [to his dharma] a terrible passion arises in his heart that leads his mind astray. Bound to that passion the mind thus convinced imagines all kinds of things. Focussed on the qualities of nature [the gunas] he is thus beset with desires that make his life unbearable.

verses.sanskrit: करोति काम-वश-गः कर्माण्य् अविजितेन्द्रियः । दुःखोदर्काणि सम्पश्यन् रजो-वेग-विमोहितः
verses.transliteration: karoti kāma-vaśa-gaḥ karmāṇy avijitendriyaḥ / duḥkhodarkāṇi sampaśyan rajo-vega-vimohitaḥ
verses.translation: With the senses not under control someone, bewildered by the force of passion and under the sway of desires, engages in fruitful activities, despite being well aware of the resulting unhappiness.

verses.sanskrit: रजस्-तमोभ्यां यद् अपि विद्वान् विक्षिप्त-धीः पुनः । अतन्द्रितो मनो युञ्जन् दोष-दृष्टिर् न सज्जते
verses.transliteration: rajas-tamobhyāṃ yad api vidvān vikṣipta-dhīḥ punaḥ / atandrito mano yuñjan doṣa-dṛṣṭir na sajjate
verses.translation: Even though [also] the intelligence of a learned person gets bewildered by passion and ignorance, no attachment arises in him because he, well aware of the contamination, carefully puts his mind back on the right track.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रमत्तो ऽनुयुञ्जीत मनो मय्य् अर्पयञ् छनैः । अनिर्विण्णो यथा-कालं जित-श्वासो जितासनः
verses.transliteration: apramatto 'nuyuñjīta mano mayy arpayañ chanaiḥ / anirviṇṇo yathā-kālaṃ jita-śvāso jitāsanaḥ
verses.translation: When one has conquered the breathing process [prnyma] and has mastered the sitting postures [sana], one should attentively, step by step, without slackening gather one's mind by concentrating on Me at appointed times [to the positions of the sun and the moon, see B.G. 7: 8 and 5: 26-28].

verses.sanskrit: एतावान् योग आदिष्टो मच्-छिष्यैः सनकादिभिः । सर्वतो मन आकृष्य मय्य् अद्धावेश्यते यथा
verses.transliteration: etāvān yoga ādiṣṭo mac-chiṣyaiḥ sanakādibhiḥ / sarvato mana ākṛṣya mayy addhāveśyate yathā
verses.translation: The yoga system as instructed by My pupils under the lead of Sanaka [the Kumras] boils down to the following: turn the mind away from everything and directly find absorption in Me the way it should [with mantras, see also 8.3: 22-24].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यदा त्वं सनकादिभ्यो येन रूपेण केशव । योगम् आदिष्टवान् एतद् रूपम् इच्छामि वेदितुम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yadā tvaṃ sanakādibhyo yena rūpeṇa keśava / yogam ādiṣṭavān etad rūpam icchāmi veditum
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'When and in what form, dear Kes'ava, have You instructed Sanaka and the others in this yoga? That is what I would like to know.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच पुत्रा हिरण्यगर्भस्य मानसाः सनकादयः । पप्रच्छुः पितरं सूक्ष्मां योगस्यैकान्तिकीम् गतिम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca putrā hiraṇyagarbhasya mānasāḥ sanakādayaḥ / papracchuḥ pitaraṃ sūkṣmāṃ yogasyaikāntikīm gatim
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The sons headed by Sanaka who took their birth from the mind of him who originated from the golden egg [Hiranyagarbha or Brahm], inquired of their father about the so very subtle, supreme goal of the science of yoga.

verses.sanskrit: सनकादय ऊचुः गुणेष्व् आविशते चेतो गुणाश् चेतसि च प्रभो । कथम् अन्योन्य-सन्त्यागो मुमुक्षोर् अतितितीर्षोः
verses.transliteration: sanakādaya ūcuḥ guṇeṣv āviśate ceto guṇāś cetasi ca prabho / katham anyonya-santyāgo mumukṣor atititīrṣoḥ
verses.translation: Sanaka and the others said to him: 'The mind is directed at the gunas, the qualities of nature, and the gunas impose themselves on the mind. Oh Master, what is for someone who desires liberation, for someone who wishes to cross over this material ocean, the process of breaking away from that mutual effect [see also B.G. 2: 62-63]?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच एवं पृष्टो महा-देवः स्वयम्भूर् भूत-भावनः । ध्यायमानः प्रश्न-बीजं नाभ्यपद्यत कर्म-धीः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca evaṃ pṛṣṭo mahā-devaḥ svayambhūr bhūta-bhāvanaḥ / dhyāyamānaḥ praśna-bījaṃ nābhyapadyata karma-dhīḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The great self-born godhead, the creator of all beings, thus being questioned, seriously pondered over what was asked but could not find the words to describe the essential truth, for his mind was bewildered because of his creative labor [see also 2.6: 34, 2.9: 32-37 and 10: 13].

verses.sanskrit: स माम् अचिन्तयद् देवः प्रश्न-पार-तितीर्षया । तस्याहं हंस-रूपेण सकाशम् अगमं तदा
verses.transliteration: sa mām acintayad devaḥ praśna-pāra-titīrṣayā / tasyāhaṃ haṃsa-rūpeṇa sakāśam agamaṃ tadā
verses.translation: With the desire to find closure he remembered Me, the original godhead [he originated from, see 3.8], and at that time I became visible in My Hamsa form [the Swan **].

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा माम् त उपव्रज्य कृत्व पादाभिवन्दनम् । ब्रह्माणम् अग्रतः कृत्वा पप्रच्छुः को भवान् इति
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā mām ta upavrajya kṛtva pādābhivandanam / brahmāṇam agrataḥ kṛtvā papracchuḥ ko bhavān iti
verses.translation: Seeing Me they, headed by Brahm, approached Me, offered their obeisances at My lotus feet and asked: 'Who are You?'

verses.sanskrit: इत्य् अहं मुनिभिः पृष्टस् तत्त्व-जिज्ञासुभिस् तदा । यद् अवोचम् अहं तेभ्यस् तद् उद्धव निबोध मे
verses.transliteration: ity ahaṃ munibhiḥ pṛṣṭas tattva-jijñāsubhis tadā / yad avocam ahaṃ tebhyas tad uddhava nibodha me
verses.translation: Thus being questioned by the sages interested in the ultimate truth, I spoke to them. Please Uddhava, hear now what I thereupon said to them.

verses.sanskrit: वस्तुनो यद्य् अनानात्व आत्मनः प्रश्न ईदृशः । कथं घटेत वो विप्रा वक्तुर् वा मे क आश्रयः
verses.transliteration: vastuno yady anānātva ātmanaḥ praśna īdṛśaḥ / kathaṃ ghaṭeta vo viprā vaktur vā me ka āśrayaḥ
verses.translation: 'Oh brahmins, if you with that question mean to say that, relating to the one true essence, there would be no difference between our individual souls, how then would you be able to pose a question like this, oh sages, or how could I as a speaker then be of any authority [or constitute a refuge]?

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चात्मकेषु भूतेषु समानेषु च वस्तुतः । को भवान् इति वः प्रश्नो वाचारम्भो ह्य् अनर्थकः
verses.transliteration: pañcātmakeṣu bhūteṣu samāneṣu ca vastutaḥ / ko bhavān iti vaḥ praśno vācārambho hy anarthakaḥ
verses.translation: Also if you would refer to the five elements our bodies are equally composed of, your question about who I am would in fact be a meaningless gesture of words.

verses.sanskrit: मनसा वचसा दृष्ट्या गृह्यते ऽन्यैर् अपीन्द्रियैः । अहम् एव न मत्तो ऽन्यद् इति बुध्यध्वम् अञ्जसा
verses.transliteration: manasā vacasā dṛṣṭyā gṛhyate 'nyair apīndriyaiḥ / aham eva na matto 'nyad iti budhyadhvam añjasā
verses.translation: That what by the mind, speech, sight and the other senses is grasped, is what I all am. There is really nothing that exists outside of Me, that is what you have to understand clearly.

verses.sanskrit: गुणेष्व् आविशते चेतो गुणाश् चेतसि च प्रजाः । जीवस्य देह उभयं गुणाश् चेतो मद्-आत्मनः
verses.transliteration: guṇeṣv āviśate ceto guṇāś cetasi ca prajāḥ / jīvasya deha ubhayaṃ guṇāś ceto mad-ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: The mind is directed at the gunas and the gunas impose themselves on the mind, dear men, but to the living entity of which I am the Soul, both the mind and the gunas are outer appearances.

verses.sanskrit: गुणेषु चाविशच् चित्तम् अभीक्ष्णं गुण-सेवया । गुणाश् च चित्त-प्रभवा मद्-रूप उभयं त्यजेत्
verses.transliteration: guṇeṣu cāviśac cittam abhīkṣṇaṃ guṇa-sevayā / guṇāś ca citta-prabhavā mad-rūpa ubhayaṃ tyajet
verses.translation: With the mind directed at the natural qualities and the qualities that, imposing themselves by stimulating the senses, constantly put the mind at work, the one who is of [realization with] My transcendental [Hamsa] form, [by means of meditation] has to give up both the mind and the [operation of the] gunas [see also vritti and neti neti].

verses.sanskrit: जाग्रत् स्वप्नः सुषुप्तं च गुणतो बुद्धि-वृत्तयः । तासां विलक्षणो जीवः साक्षित्वेन विनिश्चितः
verses.transliteration: jāgrat svapnaḥ suṣuptaṃ ca guṇato buddhi-vṛttayaḥ / tāsāṃ vilakṣaṇo jīvaḥ sākṣitvena viniścitaḥ
verses.translation: Wakefulness, dreaming and deep sleep are the transformations of the mind due to the basic qualities of nature. The individual soul is, with characteristics different from them, known to be their witness [see also 7.7: 25 and B.G. 7: 5].

verses.sanskrit: यर्हि संसृति-बन्धो ऽयम् आत्मनो गुण-वृत्ति-दः । मयि तुर्ये स्थितो जह्यात् त्यागस् तद् गुण-चेतसाम्
verses.transliteration: yarhi saṃsṛti-bandho 'yam ātmano guṇa-vṛtti-daḥ / mayi turye sthito jahyāt tyāgas tad guṇa-cetasām
verses.translation: The materially motivated intelligence constitutes the bondage that keeps the soul occupied with the modes of nature, but when one is situated in Me, in the fourth state of consciousness [turya], one at that moment can give up both the mind and the sense objects [see 11.3: 35].

verses.sanskrit: अहङ्कार-कृतं बन्धम् आत्मनो ऽर्थ-विपर्ययम् । विद्वान् निर्विद्य संसार- चिन्तां तुर्ये स्थितस् त्यजेत्
verses.transliteration: ahaṅkāra-kṛtaṃ bandham ātmano 'rtha-viparyayam / vidvān nirvidya saṃsāra- cintāṃ turye sthitas tyajet
verses.translation: The bondage of the soul as a result of identifying oneself with the body [of false ego,] constitutes the opposite purpose. A soul of knowledge who detached in samsra is situated in the fourth state, gives up the anxiety [about those ego-matters].

verses.sanskrit: यावन् नानार्थ-धीः पुंसो न निवर्तेत युक्तिभिः । जागर्त्य् अपि स्वपन्न् अज्ञः स्वप्ने जागरणं यथा
verses.transliteration: yāvan nānārtha-dhīḥ puṃso na nivarteta yuktibhiḥ / jāgarty api svapann ajñaḥ svapne jāgaraṇaṃ yathā
verses.translation: As long as a person is convinced of many different purposes and does not desist [from that engagement] by means of meditation, he, even though awake, will be sleeping with his eyes open, just as unaware as someone who sees something in a dream [see also B.G. 2: 41].

verses.sanskrit: असत्त्वाद् आत्मनो ऽन्येषां भावानां तत्-कृता भिदा । गतयो हेतवश् चास्य मृषा स्वप्न-दृशो यथा
verses.transliteration: asattvād ātmano 'nyeṣāṃ bhāvānāṃ tat-kṛtā bhidā / gatayo hetavaś cāsya mṛṣā svapna-dṛśo yathā
verses.translation: The states of existence apart from the Supreme Soul are inessential because of the separation created by them; to the seer who is filled with motives and objectives they are just as deluding as what one experiences in a dream.

verses.sanskrit: यो जागरे बहिर् अनुक्षण-धर्मिणो ऽर्थान् भुङ्क्ते समस्त-करणैर् हृदि तत्-सदृक्षान् स्वप्ने सुषुप्त उपसंहरते स एकः स्मृत्य्-अन्वयात् त्रि-गुण-वृत्ति-दृग् इन्द्रियेशः
verses.transliteration: yo jāgare bahir anukṣaṇa-dharmiṇo 'rthān $ bhuṅkte samasta-karaṇair hṛdi tat-sadṛkṣān &svapne suṣupta upasaṃharate sa ekaḥ % smṛty-anvayāt tri-guṇa-vṛtti-dṛg indriyeśaḥ
verses.translation: In the waking state he enjoys the qualities of the external matters at the moment. In his dreams he undergoes with all his senses a similar experience in the mind. In deep sleep he withdraws himself completely. But being one in his remembrance [in turya] he, as a witness of the functioning of the three successive states of consciousness, becomes lord and master over his senses [see also 4.29: 60-79 and B.G. 15: 7-8].

verses.sanskrit: एवं विमृश्य गुणतो मनसस् त्र्य्-अवस्था मन्-मायया मयि कृता इति निश्चितार्थाः एवं विमृश्य गुणतो मनसस् त्र्य्-अवस्था मन्-मायया मयि कृता इति निश्चितार्थाः सञ्छिद्य हार्दम् अनुमान-सद्-उक्ति-तीक्ष्ण ज्ञानासिना भजत माखिल-संशयाधिम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ vimṛśya guṇato manasas try-avasthā $ man-māyayā mayi kṛtā iti niścitārthāḥ &evaṃ vimṛśya guṇato manasas try-avasthā $ man-māyayā mayi kṛtā iti niścitārthāḥ &sañchidya hārdam anumāna-sad-ukti-tīkṣṇa % jñānāsinā bhajata mākhila-saṃśayādhim
verses.translation: After considering the three states of consciousness that originate from the modes of My nature, My deluding potency, then be resolute about the purpose [of realizing Me as the fourth state] and cut in your heart with the cause of all doubts [the ahankra] by means of the sword of discrimination sharpened by the logic and instructions concerning the truth.

verses.sanskrit: ईक्षेत विभ्रमम् इदं मनसो विलासं दृष्टं विनष्टम् अति-लोलम् अलात-चक्रम् ईक्षेत विभ्रमम् इदं मनसो विलासं दृष्टं विनष्टम् अति-लोलम् अलात-चक्रम् विज्ञानम् एकम् उरुधेव विभाति माया स्वप्नस् त्रिधा गुण-विसर्ग-कृतो विकल्पः
verses.transliteration: īkṣeta vibhramam idaṃ manaso vilāsaṃ $ dṛṣṭaṃ vinaṣṭam ati-lolam alāta-cakram &īkṣeta vibhramam idaṃ manaso vilāsaṃ $ dṛṣṭaṃ vinaṣṭam ati-lolam alāta-cakram &vijñānam ekam urudheva vibhāti māyā % svapnas tridhā guṇa-visarga-kṛto vikalpaḥ
verses.translation: Regard this delusional state of mind, [with images] popping up today and gone tomorrow, as the most restless sphere of a firebrand. The one spiritual soul deceptively appears in many divisions as an illusion, a threefold dream of variations operated by the guna creation [see also B.G. 9: 15, 15: 16, linga and siddhnta].

verses.sanskrit: दृष्टिम् ततः प्रतिनिवर्त्य निवृत्त-तृष्णस् तूष्णीं भवेन् निज-सुखानुभवो निरीहः दृष्टिम् ततः प्रतिनिवर्त्य निवृत्त-तृष्णस् तूष्णीं भवेन् निज-सुखानुभवो निरीहः सन्दृश्यते क्व च यदीदम् अवस्तु-बुद्ध्या त्यक्तं भ्रमाय न भवेत् स्मृतिर् आ-निपातात्
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭim tataḥ pratinivartya nivṛtta-tṛṣṇas $ tūṣṇīṃ bhaven nija-sukhānubhavo nirīhaḥ &dṛṣṭim tataḥ pratinivartya nivṛtta-tṛṣṇas $ tūṣṇīṃ bhaven nija-sukhānubhavo nirīhaḥ &sandṛśyate kva ca yadīdam avastu-buddhyā % tyaktaṃ bhramāya na bhavet smṛtir ā-nipātāt
verses.translation: When you turn your eye away from that [creation] and become silent with your desires ended, you [in meditation] must arrive at the realization of your [actual] happiness. And in case you happen to be [full of thoughts] about this earth, you have to understand that that is insubstantial. That what you give up you will remember till the end of your life and will not confuse you again.

verses.sanskrit: देहं च नश्वरम् अवस्थितम् उत्थितं वा सिद्धो न पश्यति यतो ऽध्यगमत् स्वरूपम् देहं च नश्वरम् अवस्थितम् उत्थितं वा सिद्धो न पश्यति यतो ऽध्यगमत् स्वरूपम् दैवाद् अपेतम् अथ दैव-वशाद् उपेतं वासो यथा परिकृतं मदिरा-मदान्धः
verses.transliteration: dehaṃ ca naśvaram avasthitam utthitaṃ vā $ siddho na paśyati yato 'dhyagamat svarūpam &dehaṃ ca naśvaram avasthitam utthitaṃ vā $ siddho na paśyati yato 'dhyagamat svarūpam &daivād apetam atha daiva-vaśād upetaṃ % vāso yathā parikṛtaṃ madirā-madāndhaḥ
verses.translation: Just as someone blurred by liquor is indifferent about the clothes he put on, it makes no difference to the one of perfection whether his perishable body sits or stands, or whether he by providence leaves this earth or obtains [a new body], for he reached his original position [of service, his svarpa].

verses.sanskrit: देहो ऽपि दैव-वश-गः खलु कर्म यावत् स्वारम्भकं प्रतिसमीक्षत एव सासुः देहो ऽपि दैव-वश-गः खलु कर्म यावत् स्वारम्भकं प्रतिसमीक्षत एव सासुः तं स-प्रपञ्चम् अधिरूढ-समाधि-योगः स्वाप्नं पुनर् न भजते प्रतिबुद्ध-वस्तुः
verses.transliteration: deho 'pi daiva-vaśa-gaḥ khalu karma yāvat $ svārambhakaṃ pratisamīkṣata eva sāsuḥ &deho 'pi daiva-vaśa-gaḥ khalu karma yāvat $ svārambhakaṃ pratisamīkṣata eva sāsuḥ &taṃ sa-prapañcam adhirūḍha-samādhi-yogaḥ % svāpnaṃ punar na bhajate pratibuddha-vastuḥ
verses.translation: The body will, as long as its karma, its chain of fruitful actions, lasts, continue with the breathing it took up as ordained by fate. But having awakened to his original position someone highly situated in the absorption of yoga will no longer cultivate any such [ego driven] dreaming or manifest as such.

verses.sanskrit: मयैतद् उक्तं वो विप्रा गुह्यं यत् साङ्ख्य-योगयोः । जानीत मागतं यज्ञं युष्मद्-धर्म-विवक्षया
verses.transliteration: mayaitad uktaṃ vo viprā guhyaṃ yat sāṅkhya-yogayoḥ / jānīta māgataṃ yajñaṃ yuṣmad-dharma-vivakṣayā
verses.translation: Oh learned souls, understand that with this explanation about the confidential, analytical knowledge of yoga, the science of uniting one's consciousness, I have come to you as Yaja [Vishnu, the Lord of Sacrifice] with the desire to describe the dharma of persons like you.

verses.sanskrit: अहं योगस्य साङ्ख्यस्य सत्यस्यर्तस्य तेजसः । परायणं द्विज-श्रेष्ठाः श्रियः कीर्तेर् दमस्य च
verses.transliteration: ahaṃ yogasya sāṅkhyasya satyasyartasya tejasaḥ / parāyaṇaṃ dvija-śreṣṭhāḥ śriyaḥ kīrter damasya ca
verses.translation: Oh best of the twice-born souls, I am the Supreme Way of yoga, of analysis, of the truth and the sacred law, as also the [ultimate] path of [all] beauty, fame and self-control.

verses.sanskrit: मां भजन्ति गुणाः सर्वे निर्गुणं निरपेक्षकम् । सुहृदं प्रियम् आत्मानं साम्यासङ्गादयो ऽगुणाः
verses.transliteration: māṃ bhajanti guṇāḥ sarve nirguṇaṃ nirapekṣakam / suhṛdaṃ priyam ātmānaṃ sāmyāsaṅgādayo 'guṇāḥ
verses.translation: For being free from the natural modes, all the excellence, such as being transcendentally situated, being free from expectations, being the Well-wisher, the Dearest One, the True Self, the One Equal, the detachment and so on, belongs to My honor.'

verses.sanskrit: इति मे छिन्न-सन्देहा मुनयः सनकादयः । सभाजयित्वा परया भक्त्यागृणत संस्तवैः
verses.transliteration: iti me chinna-sandehā munayaḥ sanakādayaḥ / sabhājayitvā parayā bhaktyāgṛṇata saṃstavaiḥ
verses.translation: [Krishna said to Uddhava:] I thus put an end to the doubts of the sages headed by Sanaka. Honoring Me with transcendental devotion, they with beautiful hymns chanted My glories.

verses.sanskrit: तैर् अहं पूजितः संयक् संस्तुतः परमर्षिभिः । प्रत्येयाय स्वकं धाम पश्यतः परमेष्ठिनः
verses.transliteration: tair ahaṃ pūjitaḥ saṃyak saṃstutaḥ paramarṣibhiḥ / pratyeyāya svakaṃ dhāma paśyataḥ parameṣṭhinaḥ
verses.translation: Perfectly worshiped and glorified by the greatest among the sages I thereupon, before the eyes of Brahm, returned to My abode.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच वदन्ति कृष्ण श्रेयांसि बहूनि ब्रह्म-वादिनः । तेषां विकल्प-प्राधान्यम् उताहो एक-मुख्यता
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vadanti kṛṣṇa śreyāṃsi bahūni brahma-vādinaḥ / teṣāṃ vikalpa-prādhānyam utāho eka-mukhyatā
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Krishna, the defenders of the Absolute Truth speak of many processes for spiritual advancement, are they supreme in their combination or would one of them be the most important?

verses.sanskrit: भवतोदाहृतः स्वामिन् भक्ति-योगो ऽनपेक्षितः । निरस्य सर्वतः सङ्गं येन त्वय्य् आविशेन् मनः
verses.transliteration: bhavatodāhṛtaḥ svāmin bhakti-yogo 'napekṣitaḥ / nirasya sarvataḥ saṅgaṃ yena tvayy āviśen manaḥ
verses.translation: You stated clearly, oh Master, that the yoga of devotion by which the mind becomes fixed on You, practiced free from desires, removes all material attachment.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच कालेन नष्टा प्रलये वाणीयं वेद-संज्ञिता । मयादौ ब्रह्मणे प्रोक्ता धर्मो यस्यां मद्-आत्मकः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca kālena naṣṭā pralaye vāṇīyaṃ veda-saṃjñitā / mayādau brahmaṇe proktā dharmo yasyāṃ mad-ātmakaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'This message known as the Vedas that concerns the dharma that is founded on Me, was lost at the time of the universal annihilation [pralaya], but was by Me at the beginning [of a new era] spoken to Brahm [see also 3.9: 29-43].

verses.sanskrit: तेन प्रोक्ता स्व-पुत्राय मनवे पूर्व-जाय सा । ततो भृग्व्-आदयो ऽगृह्णन् सप्त ब्रह्म-महर्षयः
verses.transliteration: tena proktā sva-putrāya manave pūrva-jāya sā / tato bhṛgv-ādayo 'gṛhṇan sapta brahma-maharṣayaḥ
verses.translation: Brahm spoke this to his eldest son Manu, and the seven great sages headed by Bhrigu in their turn accepted it from Manu [see 8.1 & 8.13 and B.G. 4: 1-3].

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्यः पितृभ्यस् तत्-पुत्रा देव-दानव-गुह्यकाः । मनुष्याः सिद्ध-गन्धर्वाः स-विद्याधर-चारणाः
verses.transliteration: tebhyaḥ pitṛbhyas tat-putrā deva-dānava-guhyakāḥ / manuṣyāḥ siddha-gandharvāḥ sa-vidyādhara-cāraṇāḥ
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: किन्देवाः किन्नरा नागा रक्षः-किम्पुरुषादयः । बह्व्यस् तेषां प्रकृतयो रजः-सत्त्व-तमो-भुवः
verses.transliteration: kindevāḥ kinnarā nāgā rakṣaḥ-kimpuruṣādayaḥ / bahvyas teṣāṃ prakṛtayo rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-bhuvaḥ
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: याभिर् भूतानि भिद्यन्ते भूतानां पतयस् तथा । यथा-प्रकृति सर्वेषां चित्रा वाचः स्रवन्ति हि
verses.transliteration: yābhir bhūtāni bhidyante bhūtānāṃ patayas tathā / yathā-prakṛti sarveṣāṃ citrā vācaḥ sravanti hi
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: एवं प्रकृति-वैचित्र्याद् भिद्यन्ते मतयो नृणाम् । पारम्पर्येण केषाञ्चित् पाषण्ड-मतयो ऽपरे
verses.transliteration: evaṃ prakṛti-vaicitryād bhidyante matayo nṛṇām / pāramparyeṇa keṣāñcit pāṣaṇḍa-matayo 'pare
verses.translation: Due to this great variety of natures the philosophies of life are thus divided among the human beings. Some of those philosophies in this constitute traditions of disciplic succession while others are heretical [pshanda].

verses.sanskrit: मन्-माया-मोहित-धियः पुरुषाः पुरुषर्षभ । श्रेयो वदन्त्य् अनेकान्तं यथा-कर्म यथा-रुचि
verses.transliteration: man-māyā-mohita-dhiyaḥ puruṣāḥ puruṣarṣabha / śreyo vadanty anekāntaṃ yathā-karma yathā-ruci
verses.translation: The people whose intelligence is bewildered by My illusory power, oh best of all persons, express themselves in countless ways about what according to their karma and taste would be better.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मम् एके यशश् चान्ये कामं सत्यं दमं शमम् । अन्ये वदन्ति स्वार्थं वा ऐश्वर्यं त्याग-भोजनम् । केचिद् यज्ञं तपो दानं व्रतानि नियमान् यमान्
verses.transliteration: dharmam eke yaśaś cānye kāmaṃ satyaṃ damaṃ śamam / anye vadanti svārthaṃ vā aiśvaryaṃ tyāga-bhojanam / kecid yajñaṃ tapo dānaṃ vratāni niyamān yamān
verses.translation: Some people speak in favor of pious activities while others speak of fame, sense gratification, truthfulness, self-control and peacefulness. Some propound self-interest, political influence, renunciation or consumption and others defend sacrifice, penance, charity, vows and arrangements of do's and don'ts [yama-niyama].

verses.sanskrit: आद्य्-अन्त-वन्त एवैषां लोकाः कर्म-विनिर्मिताः । दुःखोदर्कास् तमो-निष्ठाः क्षुद्रा मन्दाः शुचार्पिताः
verses.transliteration: ādy-anta-vanta evaiṣāṃ lokāḥ karma-vinirmitāḥ / duḥkhodarkās tamo-niṣṭhāḥ kṣudrā mandāḥ śucārpitāḥ
verses.translation: With inevitably a beginning and an end to the meager ends gained with one's karma, there is the prospect of misery as a consequence; situated in ignorance one is doomed to a miserable existence full of complaints.

verses.sanskrit: मय्य् अर्पितात्मनः सभ्य निरपेक्षस्य सर्वतः । मयात्मना सुखं यत् तत् कुतः स्याद् विषयात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: mayy arpitātmanaḥ sabhya nirapekṣasya sarvataḥ / mayātmanā sukhaṃ yat tat kutaḥ syād viṣayātmanām
verses.translation: Someone who has fixed his consciousness on Me, oh man of learning, and in every respect is free from material desires, knows the happiness of My spiritual Self. How can such a happiness ever be attained by those who are attached to worldly affairs [see 4.31: 12]?

verses.sanskrit: अकिञ्चनस्य दान्तस्य शान्तस्य सम-चेतसः । मया सन्तुष्ट-मनसः सर्वाः सुख-मया दिशः
verses.transliteration: akiñcanasya dāntasya śāntasya sama-cetasaḥ / mayā santuṣṭa-manasaḥ sarvāḥ sukha-mayā diśaḥ
verses.translation: He who does not desire, who is of peace and controls his senses, whose consciousness is equal whatever the circumstances and who has a mind that is completely satisfied with Me, is filled with happiness wherever he goes.

verses.sanskrit: न पारमेष्ठ्यं न महेन्द्र-धिष्ण्यं न सार्वभौमं न रसाधिपत्यम् न योग-सिद्धीर् अपुनर्-भवं वा मय्य् अर्पितात्मेच्छति मद् विनान्यत्
verses.transliteration: na pārameṣṭhyaṃ na mahendra-dhiṣṇyaṃ $ na sārvabhaumaṃ na rasādhipatyam &na yoga-siddhīr apunar-bhavaṃ vā % mayy arpitātmecchati mad vinānyat
verses.translation: A soul fixed on Me, wants Me and nothing else. He does not desire the position of Brahm, nor the position of Indra. He neither wants an empire on earth nor a sovereign position in the lower worlds, he does not desire the perfections [the siddhis] of yoga or a second birth [see e.g. 5.1: 6].

verses.sanskrit: न तथा मे प्रियतम आत्म-योनिर् न शङ्करः । न च सङ्कर्षणो न श्रीर् नैवात्मा च यथा भवान्
verses.transliteration: na tathā me priyatama ātma-yonir na śaṅkaraḥ / na ca saṅkarṣaṇo na śrīr naivātmā ca yathā bhavān
verses.translation: Neither the one born from My body [Brahm], nor S'ankara [S'iva], Sankarshana [Balarma], the Goddess of Fortune [S'r] or even My own Self, is as dear to Me as you [see also B.G 12: 20].

verses.sanskrit: निरपेक्षं मुनिं शान्तं निर्वैरं सम-दर्शनम् । अनुव्रजाम्य् अहं नित्यं पूयेयेत्य् अङ्घ्रि-रेणुभिः
verses.transliteration: nirapekṣaṃ muniṃ śāntaṃ nirvairaṃ sama-darśanam / anuvrajāmy ahaṃ nityaṃ pūyeyety aṅghri-reṇubhiḥ
verses.translation: The sage [the devotee] who without personal desire is of peace, who is not inimical to anyone and of an equal vision, I always follow closely so that there is purification with the dust of the lotus feet [see also 7.14: 17].

verses.sanskrit: निष्किञ्चना मय्य् अनुरक्त-चेतसः शान्ता महान्तो ऽखिल-जीव-वत्सलाः । कामैर् अनालब्ध-धियो जुषन्ति ते यन् नैरपेक्ष्यं न विदुः सुखं मम
verses.transliteration: niṣkiñcanā mayy anurakta-cetasaḥ śāntā mahānto 'khila-jīva-vatsalāḥ / kāmair anālabdha-dhiyo juṣanti te yan nairapekṣyaṃ na viduḥ sukhaṃ mama
verses.translation: The great souls who, not eager for sense gratification, are of a mind that is constantly attached to Me, who are of inner peace, who care for all individual souls and whose consciousness is not ruled by lusts, experience My happiness that can only be known in complete independence [in detachment].

verses.sanskrit: बाध्यमानो ऽपि मद्-भक्तो विषयैर् अजितेन्द्रियः । प्रायः प्रगल्भया भक्त्या विषयैर् नाभिभूयते
verses.transliteration: bādhyamāno 'pi mad-bhakto viṣayair ajitendriyaḥ / prāyaḥ pragalbhayā bhaktyā viṣayair nābhibhūyate
verses.translation: Despite being harassed by sensual desires, a devotee of Mine who did not conquer his often strong senses, by dint of his devotion will not be defeated by them [see also 1.5: 17, 8.7: 44, 11.13: 12 and B.G. 9: 30, 2: 62 -64].

verses.sanskrit: यथाग्निः सु-समृद्धार्चिः करोत्य् एधांसि भस्मसात् । तथा मद्-विषया भक्तिर् उद्धवैनांसि कृत्स्नशः
verses.transliteration: yathāgniḥ su-samṛddhārciḥ karoty edhāṃsi bhasmasāt / tathā mad-viṣayā bhaktir uddhavaināṃsi kṛtsnaśaḥ
verses.translation: Just as firewood because of the blazing flames of a fire turns into ashes, devotion with Me as the object burns the [consequences of one's] sins completely, oh Uddhava.

verses.sanskrit: न साधयति मां योगो न साङ्ख्यं धर्म उद्धव । न स्वाध्यायस् तपस् त्यागो यथा भक्तिर् ममोर्जिता
verses.transliteration: na sādhayati māṃ yogo na sāṅkhyaṃ dharma uddhava / na svādhyāyas tapas tyāgo yathā bhaktir mamorjitā
verses.translation: The yoga system, analytical philosophy, pious activities, Vedic study, austerity and renunciation, oh Uddhava, do not keep Me as satisfied as a well developed devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्याहम् एकया ग्राह्यः श्रद्धयात्मा प्रियः सताम् । भक्तिः पुनाति मन्-निष्ठा श्व-पाकान् अपि सम्भवात्
verses.transliteration: bhaktyāham ekayā grāhyaḥ śraddhayātmā priyaḥ satām / bhaktiḥ punāti man-niṣṭhā śva-pākān api sambhavāt
verses.translation: One obtains Me by single-minded devotion with faith in the Supreme Soul, the object of love of the devotees. Devotional service directed at Me will even purify someone who has eaten dogs.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मः सत्य-दयोपेतो विद्या वा तपसान्विता । मद्-भक्त्यापेतम् आत्मानं न सम्यक् प्रपुनाति हि
verses.transliteration: dharmaḥ satya-dayopeto vidyā vā tapasānvitā / mad-bhaktyāpetam ātmānaṃ na samyak prapunāti hi
verses.translation: Neither dharma accompanied by truthfulness and mercy, nor knowledge linked to austerity, will purify one's consciousness fully when bereft of devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: कथं विना रोम-हर्षं द्रवता चेतसा विना । विनानन्दाश्रु-कलया शुध्येद् भक्त्या विनाशयः
verses.transliteration: kathaṃ vinā roma-harṣaṃ dravatā cetasā vinā / vinānandāśru-kalayā śudhyed bhaktyā vināśayaḥ
verses.translation: How can without bhakti one's hair stand on end, without loving service the heart melt, without devotion the tears flow, the bliss be and one's heart be purified?

verses.sanskrit: वाग् गद्गदा द्रवते यस्य चित्तं रुदत्य् अभीक्ष्णं हसति क्वचिच् च । विलज्ज उद्गायति नृत्यते च मद्-भक्ति-युक्तो भुवनं पुनाति
verses.transliteration: vāg gadgadā dravate yasya cittaṃ rudaty abhīkṣṇaṃ hasati kvacic ca / vilajja udgāyati nṛtyate ca mad-bhakti-yukto bhuvanaṃ punāti
verses.translation: The universe is purified by the one whose voice chokes up, whose heart melts, who cries again and again, sometimes laughs, is ashamed, loudly sings and dances in the connectedness of My bhakti [see also S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka and 11.2: 40].

verses.sanskrit: यथाग्निना हेम मलं जहाति ध्मातं पुनः स्वं भजते च रूपम् । आत्मा च कर्मानुशयं विधूय मद्-भक्ति-योगेन भजत्य् अथो माम्
verses.transliteration: yathāgninā hema malaṃ jahāti dhmātaṃ punaḥ svaṃ bhajate ca rūpam / ātmā ca karmānuśayaṃ vidhūya mad-bhakti-yogena bhajaty atho mām
verses.translation: Just like gold being molten in fire gives up impurities and returns to its original state, also the contamination of karma is removed from the spirit soul when one worships Me united in My loving service.

verses.sanskrit: यथा यथात्मा परिमृज्यते ऽसौ मत्-पुण्य-गाथा-श्रवणाभिधानैः । तथा तथा पश्यति वस्तु सूक्ष्मं चक्षुर् यथैवाञ्जन-सम्प्रयुक्तम्
verses.transliteration: yathā yathātmā parimṛjyate 'sau mat-puṇya-gāthā-śravaṇābhidhānaiḥ / tathā tathā paśyati vastu sūkṣmaṃ cakṣur yathaivāñjana-samprayuktam
verses.translation: Even as the power to see restores once the eye is treated with ointment, the spirit soul again sees the One Subtle Essence once being cleansed by hearing and chanting My pious narrations.

verses.sanskrit: विषयान् ध्यायतश् चित्तं विषयेषु विषज्जते । माम् अनुस्मरतश् चित्तं मय्य् एव प्रविलीयते
verses.transliteration: viṣayān dhyāyataś cittaṃ viṣayeṣu viṣajjate / mām anusmarataś cittaṃ mayy eva pravilīyate
verses.translation: Just as the intelligence of someone meditating the objects of the senses is entangled in the sense experience [see B.G. 2: 62-63], one's thought becomes dissolved in Me when one keeps thinking of Me.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् असद्-अभिध्यानं यथा स्वप्न-मनोरथम् । हित्वा मयि समाधत्स्व मनो मद्-भाव-भावितम्
verses.transliteration: tasmād asad-abhidhyānaṃ yathā svapna-manoratham / hitvā mayi samādhatsva mano mad-bhāva-bhāvitam
verses.translation: One's material considerations are therefore like the figments of a dream; [having awakened being] absorbed in Me one forsakes them. The mind is purified when one is completely absorbed in My love.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीणां स्त्री-सङ्गिनां सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा दूरत आत्मवान् । क्षेमे विविक्त आसीनश् चिन्तयेन् माम् अतन्द्रितः
verses.transliteration: strīṇāṃ strī-saṅgināṃ saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dūrata ātmavān / kṣeme vivikta āsīnaś cintayen mām atandritaḥ
verses.translation: Giving up being associated [intimately] with women and keeping away from those who chase women, one should [thus free from being physically identified] composed sit at ease in seclusion and with great care concentrate on Me [see also 11.8: 13-14 *].

verses.sanskrit: न तथास्य भवेत् क्लेशो बन्धश् चान्य-प्रसङ्गतः । योषित्-सङ्गाद् यथा पुंसो यथा तत्-सङ्गि-सङ्गतः
verses.transliteration: na tathāsya bhavet kleśo bandhaś cānya-prasaṅgataḥ / yoṣit-saṅgād yathā puṃso yathā tat-saṅgi-saṅgataḥ
verses.translation: No other attachment brings a man as much suffering and bondage as the attachment to women and the association with those who are attached to women [see also 1.4: 25, 5.5: 2, 5.13: 16, 6.9: 9, 7.12: 9, 9.14: 36, 9.19: 17, 10.10: 8, 10.51: 51, 10.60: 44-45 & 48].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यथा त्वाम् अरविन्दाक्ष यादृशं वा यद्-आत्मकम् । ध्यायेन् मुमुक्षुर् एतन् मे ध्यानं त्वं वक्तुम् अर्हसि
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yathā tvām aravindākṣa yādṛśaṃ vā yad-ātmakam / dhyāyen mumukṣur etan me dhyānaṃ tvaṃ vaktum arhasi
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lotus-eyed One, how should one meditate on You when one desires liberation, in what way and on what form? Please, can You speak to me about meditation?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच सम आसन आसीनः सम-कायो यथा-सुखम् । हस्ताव् उत्सङ्ग आधाय स्व-नासाग्र-कृतेक्षणः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca sama āsana āsīnaḥ sama-kāyo yathā-sukham / hastāv utsaṅga ādhāya sva-nāsāgra-kṛtekṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Sitting straight and comfortably on a seat at the level of the floor, one should place one's hands in one's lap and focus one's eyes on the tip of one's nose. Then one should clear the path of one's prna, one's vital air, step by step exercising inhaling, retaining and exhaling and the other way around - while controlling one's senses [**, see prnyma, and B.G. 4: 29].

verses.sanskrit: प्राणस्य शोधयेन् मार्गं पूर-कुम्भक-रेचकैः । विपर्ययेणापि शनैर् अभ्यसेन् निर्जितेन्द्रियः
verses.transliteration: prāṇasya śodhayen mārgaṃ pūra-kumbhaka-recakaiḥ / viparyayeṇāpi śanair abhyasen nirjitendriyaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Sitting straight and comfortably on a seat at the level of the floor, one should place one's hands in one's lap and focus one's eyes on the tip of one's nose. Then one should clear the path of one's prna, one's vital air, step by step exercising inhaling, retaining and exhaling and the other way around - while controlling one's senses [**, see prnyma, and B.G. 4: 29].

verses.sanskrit: हृद्य् अविच्छिनम् ॐकारं घण्टा-नादं बिसोर्ण-वत् । प्राणेनोदीर्य तत्राथ पुनः संवेशयेत् स्वरम्
verses.transliteration: hṛdy avicchinam oṃkāraṃ ghaṇṭā-nādaṃ bisorṇa-vat / prāṇenodīrya tatrātha punaḥ saṃveśayet svaram
verses.translation: With the help of one's life breath [prna] one should, like through the fibre of a lotus stalk, guide the sound AUM upwards to the heart, to vibrate it there continuously like a ringing bell so as to reunite the [15 after] sounds of recitation [in the nose, anusvra ***].

verses.sanskrit: एवं प्रणव-संयुक्तं प्राणम् एव समभ्यसेत् । दश-कृत्वस् त्रि-षवणं मासाद् अर्वाग् जितानिलः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ praṇava-saṃyuktaṃ prāṇam eva samabhyaset / daśa-kṛtvas tri-ṣavaṇaṃ māsād arvāg jitānilaḥ
verses.translation: The life breath thus joined with the Pranava [see also 9.14: 46] must be practiced ten times, at sunrise, noon and sunset. After one month one will then be in control of one's vital air [*4].

verses.sanskrit: हृत्-पुण्डरीकम् अन्तः-स्थम् ऊर्ध्व-नालम् अधो-मुखम् । ध्यात्वोर्ध्व-मुखम् उन्निद्रम् अष्ट-पत्रं स-कर्णिकम्
verses.transliteration: hṛt-puṇḍarīkam antaḥ-stham ūrdhva-nālam adho-mukham / dhyātvordhva-mukham unnidram aṣṭa-patraṃ sa-karṇikam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: कर्णिकायां न्यसेत् सूर्य- सोमाग्नीन् उत्तरोत्तरम् । वह्नि-मध्ये स्मरेद् रूपं ममैतद् ध्यान-मङ्गलम्
verses.transliteration: karṇikāyāṃ nyaset sūrya- somāgnīn uttarottaram / vahni-madhye smared rūpaṃ mamaitad dhyāna-maṅgalam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: समं प्रशान्तं सु-मुखं दीर्घ-चारु-चतुर्-भुजम् । सु-चारु-सुन्दर-ग्रीवं सु-कपोलं शुचि-स्मितम्
verses.transliteration: samaṃ praśāntaṃ su-mukhaṃ dīrgha-cāru-catur-bhujam / su-cāru-sundara-grīvaṃ su-kapolaṃ śuci-smitam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: समान-कर्ण-विन्यस्त- स्फुरन्-मकर-कुण्डलम् । हेमाम्बरं घन-श्यामं श्रीवत्स-श्री-निकेतनम्
verses.transliteration: samāna-karṇa-vinyasta- sphuran-makara-kuṇḍalam / hemāmbaraṃ ghana-śyāmaṃ śrīvatsa-śrī-niketanam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: शङ्ख-चक्र-गदा-पद्म- वनमाला-विभूषितम् । नूपुरैर् विलसत्-पादं कौस्तुभ-प्रभया युतम्
verses.transliteration: śaṅkha-cakra-gadā-padma- vanamālā-vibhūṣitam / nūpurair vilasat-pādaṃ kaustubha-prabhayā yutam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: द्युमत्-किरीट-कटक- कटि-सूत्राङ्गदायुतम् । सर्वाङ्ग-सुन्दरं हृद्यं प्रसाद-सुमुखेक्षनम्
verses.transliteration: dyumat-kirīṭa-kaṭaka- kaṭi-sūtrāṅgadāyutam / sarvāṅga-sundaraṃ hṛdyaṃ prasāda-sumukhekṣanam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: सु-कुमारम् अभिध्यायेत् सर्वाङ्गेषु मनो दधत् । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यो मनसाकृष्य तन् मनः । बुद्ध्या सारथिना धीरः प्रणयेन् मयि सर्वतः
verses.transliteration: su-kumāram abhidhyāyet sarvāṅgeṣu mano dadhat / indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyo manasākṛṣya tan manaḥ / buddhyā sārathinā dhīraḥ praṇayen mayi sarvataḥ
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: तत् सर्व-व्यापकं चित्तम् आकृष्यैकत्र धारयेत् । नान्यानि चिन्तयेद् भूयः सु-स्मितं भावयेन् मुखम्
verses.transliteration: tat sarva-vyāpakaṃ cittam ākṛṣyaikatra dhārayet / nānyāni cintayed bhūyaḥ su-smitaṃ bhāvayen mukham
verses.translation: One's consciousness spread over all of this then must be withdrawn to one point by focussing on the wonderful smile of the face, and by no longer regarding other parts.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र लब्ध-पदं चित्तम् आकृष्य व्योम्नि धारयेत् । तच् च त्यक्त्वा मद्-आरोहो न किञ्चिद् अपि चिन्तयेत्
verses.transliteration: tatra labdha-padaṃ cittam ākṛṣya vyomni dhārayet / tac ca tyaktvā mad-āroho na kiñcid api cintayet
verses.translation: Thus being established, one should withdraw one's consciousness by meditating on the ether. Also forsaking that, one next must ascend to Me and not think of anything else anymore.

verses.sanskrit: एवं समाहित-मतिर् माम् एवात्मानम् आत्मनि । विचष्टे मयि सर्वात्मन् ज्योतिर् ज्योतिषि संयुतम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ samāhita-matir mām evātmānam ātmani / vicaṣṭe mayi sarvātman jyotir jyotiṣi saṃyutam
verses.translation: With one's consciousness thus fully absorbed, the individual soul sees Me in the self and all selves in Me, the same way the rays of the sun are united in the sun [see also B.G. 9: 29].

verses.sanskrit: ध्यानेनेत्थं सु-तीव्रेण युञ्जतो योगिनो मनः । संयास्यत्य् आशु निर्वाणं द्रव्य ज्ञान-क्रिया-भ्रमः
verses.transliteration: dhyānenetthaṃ su-tīvreṇa yuñjato yogino manaḥ / saṃyāsyaty āśu nirvāṇaṃ dravya jñāna-kriyā-bhramaḥ
verses.translation: The deluded state of considering oneself the owner, the knower and the doer, will all together soon find its dissolution in the mind of the yogi who, most concentrated, practices the meditation as mentioned [compare 2.2: 8-14].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच जितेन्द्रियस्य युक्तस्य जित-श्वासस्य योगिनः । मयि धारयतश् चेत उपतिष्ठन्ति सिद्धयः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca jitendriyasya yuktasya jita-śvāsasya yoginaḥ / mayi dhārayataś ceta upatiṣṭhanti siddhayaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When a yogi is connected [in the Absolute Truth], has conquered his senses and breathing, and has fixed his attention on Me, the mystical perfections of yoga are at his disposal.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच कया धारणया का स्वित् कथं वा सिद्धिर् अच्युत । कति वा सिद्धयो ब्रूहि योगिनां सिद्धि-दो भवान्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca kayā dhāraṇayā kā svit kathaṃ vā siddhir acyuta / kati vā siddhayo brūhi yogināṃ siddhi-do bhavān
verses.translation: Uddhava said: 'Oh You who bring all yogis perfection, kindly tell me which method is required for concentration and how these perfections work. And, Acyuta, how many perfections are there?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच सिद्धयो ऽष्टादश प्रोक्ता धारणा योग-पार-गैः । तासाम् अष्टौ मत्-प्रधाना दशैव गुण-हेतवः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca siddhayo 'ṣṭādaśa proktā dhāraṇā yoga-pāra-gaiḥ / tāsām aṣṭau mat-pradhānā daśaiva guṇa-hetavaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The masters of yoga speak of eighteen mystical perfections [siddhis] and meditations [leading to them]. Eight of them are [primary and] prominent in Me and ten of them [are secondary and] originate from the quality [of goodness].

verses.sanskrit: अणिमा महिमा मूर्तेर् लघिमा प्राप्तिर् इन्द्रियैः । प्राकाम्यं श्रुत-दृष्टेषु शक्ति-प्रेरणम् ईशिता
verses.transliteration: aṇimā mahimā mūrter laghimā prāptir indriyaiḥ / prākāmyaṃ śruta-dṛṣṭeṣu śakti-preraṇam īśitā
verses.translation: Oh gentle one, the eight mystical perfections consist of the ability to become, as for the form, the smallest [anim], the biggest [mahim] or the lightest [laghim relative to garim, the heaviest], the ability to acquire whatever material object [prpti], the ability to enjoy sensually whatever can be seen or heard [prkmya], the ability to have the upper hand in employing the forces [s'it or s'itv], the ability to be in control by means of magic unobstructed by the modes [vas'it], and the ability to answer to any desire that seeks [His] favor [kmvasyit]. Know them to be the ones prominent in Me.

verses.sanskrit: गुणेष्व् असङ्गो वशिता यत्-कामस् तद् अवस्यति । एता मे सिद्धयः सौम्य अष्टाव् औत्पत्तिका मताः
verses.transliteration: guṇeṣv asaṅgo vaśitā yat-kāmas tad avasyati / etā me siddhayaḥ saumya aṣṭāv autpattikā matāḥ
verses.translation: Oh gentle one, the eight mystical perfections consist of the ability to become, as for the form, the smallest [anim], the biggest [mahim] or the lightest [laghim relative to garim, the heaviest], the ability to acquire whatever material object [prpti], the ability to enjoy sensually whatever can be seen or heard [prkmya], the ability to have the upper hand in employing the forces [s'it or s'itv], the ability to be in control by means of magic unobstructed by the modes [vas'it], and the ability to answer to any desire that seeks [His] favor [kmvasyit]. Know them to be the ones prominent in Me.

verses.sanskrit: अनूर्मिमत्त्वं देहे ऽस्मिन् दूर-श्रवण-दर्शनम् । मनो-जवः काम-रूपं पर-काय-प्रवेशनम्
verses.transliteration: anūrmimattvaṃ dehe 'smin dūra-śravaṇa-darśanam / mano-javaḥ kāma-rūpaṃ para-kāya-praveśanam
verses.translation: [The ten secondary siddhis consist of] the ability in this body not to be plagued by hunger and thirst and such, the ability to hear from afar, the ability to see things far away, the ability to be transported with the speed of mind, the ability to assume any form at will, the ability to enter into the bodies of others, the ability to die at will, the ability to witness the sporting [of the heavenly girls] with the gods, the ability to be of perfect accomplishment as one likes, and the ability to have one's commands fulfilled unimpeded.

verses.sanskrit: स्वच्छन्द-मृत्युर् देवानां सह-क्रीडानुदर्शनम् । यथा-सङ्कल्प-संसिद्धिर् आज्ञाप्रतिहता गतिः
verses.transliteration: svacchanda-mṛtyur devānāṃ saha-krīḍānudarśanam / yathā-saṅkalpa-saṃsiddhir ājñāpratihatā gatiḥ
verses.translation: [The ten secondary siddhis consist of] the ability in this body not to be plagued by hunger and thirst and such, the ability to hear from afar, the ability to see things far away, the ability to be transported with the speed of mind, the ability to assume any form at will, the ability to enter into the bodies of others, the ability to die at will, the ability to witness the sporting [of the heavenly girls] with the gods, the ability to be of perfect accomplishment as one likes, and the ability to have one's commands fulfilled unimpeded.

verses.sanskrit: त्रि-काल-ज्ञत्वम् अद्वन्द्वं पर-चित्ताद्य्-अभिज्ञता । अग्न्य्-अर्काम्बु-विषादीनां प्रतिष्टम्भो ऽपराजयः
verses.transliteration: tri-kāla-jñatvam advandvaṃ para-cittādy-abhijñatā / agny-arkāmbu-viṣādīnāṃ pratiṣṭambho 'parājayaḥ
verses.translation: To know the past, the present and the future, to be free from the dualities, to know the minds of others, to check the potency of fire, the sun, water, poison and so on, and not to be conquered by others, are examples of the perfections that are described as being the result of concentrating in yoga. Please learn now from Me by means of which type of meditation what perfection occurs.

verses.sanskrit: एताश् चोद्देशतः प्रोक्ता योग-धारण-सिद्धयः । यया धारणया या स्याद् यथा वा स्यान् निबोध मे
verses.transliteration: etāś coddeśataḥ proktā yoga-dhāraṇa-siddhayaḥ / yayā dhāraṇayā yā syād yathā vā syān nibodha me
verses.translation: To know the past, the present and the future, to be free from the dualities, to know the minds of others, to check the potency of fire, the sun, water, poison and so on, and not to be conquered by others, are examples of the perfections that are described as being the result of concentrating in yoga. Please learn now from Me by means of which type of meditation what perfection occurs.

verses.sanskrit: भूत-सूक्ष्मात्मनि मयि तन्-मात्रं धारयेन् मनः । अणिमानम् अवाप्नोति तन्-मात्रोपासको मम
verses.transliteration: bhūta-sūkṣmātmani mayi tan-mātraṃ dhārayen manaḥ / aṇimānam avāpnoti tan-mātropāsako mama
verses.translation: My worshiper who focusses his mind, pertaining to the subtle elements of perception [tanmtra], on Me as the Self of those sense elements and nothing else, obtains the anim perfection [the ability to enter the smallest].

verses.sanskrit: महत्-तत्त्वात्मनि मयि यथा-संस्थं मनो दधत् । महिमानम् अवाप्नोति भूतानां च पृथक् पृथक्
