verses.transliteration: mahat-tattvātmani mayi yathā-saṃsthaṃ mano dadhat / mahimānam avāpnoti bhūtānāṃ ca pṛthak pṛthak
verses.translation: Focussing one's mind on the truth of the complete [the mahat-tattva] with Me as the inhabiting soul, one achieves the perfection of mahim [to enter the greatest] as also grip on each element separately.

verses.sanskrit: परमाणु-मये चित्तं भूतानां मयि रञ्जयन् । काल-सूक्ष्मार्थतां योगी लघिमानम् अवाप्नुयात्
verses.transliteration: paramāṇu-maye cittaṃ bhūtānāṃ mayi rañjayan / kāla-sūkṣmārthatāṃ yogī laghimānam avāpnuyāt
verses.translation: The yogi may obtain laghim [lightness] by attaching to Me as the supreme element of the smallest elements [the atoms], the subtle property of Time [see also cakra].

verses.sanskrit: धारयन् मय्य् अहं-तत्त्वे मनो वैकारिके ऽखिलम् । सर्वेन्द्रियाणाम् आत्मत्वं प्राप्तिं प्राप्नोति मन्-मनाः
verses.transliteration: dhārayan mayy ahaṃ-tattve mano vaikārike 'khilam / sarvendriyāṇām ātmatvaṃ prāptiṃ prāpnoti man-manāḥ
verses.translation: He who with his mind focussed on Me narrows down the mind completely to the emotionality of the I-principle, obtains the siddhi of prpti [mystic acquisition] by which he becomes the proprietor of the senses of all living beings.

verses.sanskrit: महत्य् आत्मनि यः सूत्रे धारयेन् मयि मानसम् । प्राकाम्यं पारमेष्ठ्यं मे विन्दते ऽव्यक्त-जन्मनः
verses.transliteration: mahaty ātmani yaḥ sūtre dhārayen mayi mānasam / prākāmyaṃ pārameṣṭhyaṃ me vindate 'vyakta-janmanaḥ
verses.translation: In order to obtain from Me, whose appearance lies beyond perception, the super excellent siddhi of prkmya [to enjoy whatever whenever], one should fix one's mental activity on Me, the Supersoul that is the thread running through the huge reality of matter [see also stra].

verses.sanskrit: विष्णौ त्र्य्-अधीश्वरे चित्तं धारयेत् काल-विग्रहे । स ईशित्वम् अवाप्नोति क्षेत्रज्ञ-क्षेत्र-चोदनाम्
verses.transliteration: viṣṇau try-adhīśvare cittaṃ dhārayet kāla-vigrahe / sa īśitvam avāpnoti kṣetrajña-kṣetra-codanām
verses.translation: When one focusses one's consciousness on Vishnu, the Original Controller of the Three [gunas, see also B.G. 7: 13] who is the mover in the form of Time, one will obtain the siddhi of s'itv [the supremacy] by means of which the conditioned body [the field] and its knower can be controlled [*].

verses.sanskrit: नारायणे तुरीयाख्ये भगवच्-छब्द-शब्दिते । मनो मय्य् आदधद् योगी मद्-धर्मा वशिताम् इयात्
verses.transliteration: nārāyaṇe turīyākhye bhagavac-chabda-śabdite / mano mayy ādadhad yogī mad-dharmā vaśitām iyāt
verses.translation: The yogi who fixes his mind on Me, Nryana, the fourth state [turya] that is described by the word fortunate [bhagavat **], obtains, being endowed with My nature, the vas'it perfection [the ability to subdue by means of magic].

verses.sanskrit: निर्गुणे ब्रह्मणि मयि धारयन् विशदं मनः । परमानन्दम् आप्नोति यत्र कामो ऽवसीयते
verses.transliteration: nirguṇe brahmaṇi mayi dhārayan viśadaṃ manaḥ / paramānandam āpnoti yatra kāmo 'vasīyate
verses.translation: When one focusses one's mind, which is pure in Me, on the impersonal absolute [brahman] that is free from material qualities [or transcendental], one obtains the supreme of happiness wherein one's desire finds its complete fulfillment [kmvasyit].

verses.sanskrit: श्वेतद्वीप-पतौ चित्तं शुद्धे धर्म-मये मयि । धारयञ् छ्वेततां याति षड्-ऊर्मि-रहितो नरः
verses.transliteration: śvetadvīpa-patau cittaṃ śuddhe dharma-maye mayi / dhārayañ chvetatāṃ yāti ṣaḍ-ūrmi-rahito naraḥ
verses.translation: When a human being concentrates on Me as the Lord of S'vetadvpa, the personification of goodness, the sum total of dharma, he obtains a pure existence free from the six waves [of material disturbance: hunger, thirst, bewilderment, decay, grief and death, anrmi-mattvam, see also shath-rmi].

verses.sanskrit: मय्य् आकाशात्मनि प्राणे मनसा घोषम् उद्वहन् । तत्रोपलब्धा भूतानां हंसो वाचः शृणोत्य् असौ
verses.transliteration: mayy ākāśātmani prāṇe manasā ghoṣam udvahan / tatropalabdhā bhūtānāṃ haṃso vācaḥ śṛṇoty asau
verses.translation: When one in the mind leads home the transcendental sound that is present in the prna [see 11.14: 35], in Me, the personification of the sky, one perceives therein the Swan [Lord Hamsa or the saintly person, see 11.13: 19] and hears the words spoken by all living beings [dra-s'ravana, see also divyam s'rotam].

verses.sanskrit: चक्षुस् त्वष्टरि संयोज्य त्वष्टारम् अपि चक्षुषि । मां तत्र मनसा ध्यायन् विश्वं पश्यति दूरतः
verses.transliteration: cakṣus tvaṣṭari saṃyojya tvaṣṭāram api cakṣuṣi / māṃ tatra manasā dhyāyan viśvaṃ paśyati dūrataḥ
verses.translation: When one merges one's eyes with the sun and the sun with one's eyes [doing so transcendentally and not staring physically into the sun] one can, with one's mind in meditation on Me, therein see anything that is far away [dra-dars'ana, see also 2.1: 30].

verses.sanskrit: मनो मयि सु-संयोज्य देहं तद्-अनुवायुना । मद्-धारणानुभावेन तत्रात्मा यत्र वै मनः
verses.transliteration: mano mayi su-saṃyojya dehaṃ tad-anuvāyunā / mad-dhāraṇānubhāvena tatrātmā yatra vai manaḥ
verses.translation: By virtue of fully uniting in Me one's mind, one's body, and one's breath following the mind, one's self goes wherever the mind goes [manojava].

verses.sanskrit: यदा मन उपादाय यद् यद् रूपं बुभूषति । तत् तद् भवेन् मनो-रूपं मद्-योग-बलम् आश्रयः
verses.transliteration: yadā mana upādāya yad yad rūpaṃ bubhūṣati / tat tad bhaven mano-rūpaṃ mad-yoga-balam āśrayaḥ
verses.translation: Because it lies within the power of My yoga [to appear in different forms], for the mind intent on assuming whatever form, when I am the shelter, the very form will appear that was desired [kmarpa].

verses.sanskrit: पर-कायं विशन् सिद्ध आत्मानं तत्र भावयेत् । पिण्डं हित्वा विशेत् प्राणो वायु-भूतः षडङ्घ्रि-वत्
verses.transliteration: para-kāyaṃ viśan siddha ātmānaṃ tatra bhāvayet / piṇḍaṃ hitvā viśet prāṇo vāyu-bhūtaḥ ṣaḍaṅghri-vat
verses.translation: When a perfected yogi [a siddha] wishes to enter the body of another person, he must give up his own gross body and project himself into that body. That he should accomplish by, like being the wind, entering it through the vital breath, the way a bee switches flowers [para-kya-praves'anam].

verses.sanskrit: पार्ष्ण्यापीड्य गुदं प्राणं हृद्-उरः-कण्ठ-मूर्धसु । आरोप्य ब्रह्म-रन्ध्रेण ब्रह्म नीत्वोत्सृजेत् तनुम्
verses.transliteration: pārṣṇyāpīḍya gudaṃ prāṇaṃ hṛd-uraḥ-kaṇṭha-mūrdhasu / āropya brahma-randhreṇa brahma nītvotsṛjet tanum
verses.translation: When [a yogi is about to die and] wants to give up the material body, he blocks his anus with his heel and carries his prna from the heart up to the chest, and from there to the throat to go to the head, from where he, rising to the spiritual seat at the top of the skull [the brahma-randhrena], leads himself to the spiritual world [svacchandu-mrityu, see also 2.2: 19-21].

verses.sanskrit: विहरिष्यन् सुराक्रीडे मत्-स्थं सत्त्वं विभावयेत् । विमानेनोपतिष्ठन्ति सत्त्व-वृत्तीः सुर-स्त्रियः
verses.transliteration: vihariṣyan surākrīḍe mat-sthaṃ sattvaṃ vibhāvayet / vimānenopatiṣṭhanti sattva-vṛttīḥ sura-striyaḥ
verses.translation: When one desires to enjoy the pleasure gardens of the demigods, one should meditate on the mode of goodness situated in Me, so that one sees the by goodness moved women of the demigods arrive in their vehicles [their vimnas, devnm saha-krdnudars'anam].

verses.sanskrit: यथा सङ्कल्पयेद् बुद्ध्या यदा वा मत्-परः पुमान् । मयि सत्ये मनो युञ्जंस् तथा तत् समुपाश्नुते
verses.transliteration: yathā saṅkalpayed buddhyā yadā vā mat-paraḥ pumān / mayi satye mano yuñjaṃs tathā tat samupāśnute
verses.translation: When someone by means of his reason became convinced of My truth, or else by his devotion unto Me, he with his mind thus absorbed, consequently will achieve his purpose [yath-sankalpa-samsiddhi].

verses.sanskrit: यो वै मद्-भावम् आपन्न ईशितुर् वशितुः पुमान् । कुतश्चिन् न विहन्येत तस्य चाज्ञा यथा मम
verses.transliteration: yo vai mad-bhāvam āpanna īśitur vaśituḥ pumān / kutaścin na vihanyeta tasya cājñā yathā mama
verses.translation: The person who arrived at the realization of My nature, supremacy and dominion, is someone who by no means can be frustrated, for his order and command are as good as Mine [jpratihat gatih, see also B.G. 9: 31].

verses.sanskrit: मद्-भक्त्या शुद्ध-सत्त्वस्य योगिनो धारणा-विदः । तस्य त्रै-कालिकी बुद्धिर् जन्म-मृत्यूपबृंहिता
verses.transliteration: mad-bhaktyā śuddha-sattvasya yogino dhāraṇā-vidaḥ / tasya trai-kālikī buddhir janma-mṛtyūpabṛṃhitā
verses.translation: A yogi, pure of character, who by dint of his devotion unto Me knows how to focus his mind [dhran], acquires insight in the three phases of time [past, present and future] including knowledge of birth and death [see tri-klika].

verses.sanskrit: अग्न्य्-आदिभिर् न हन्येत मुनेर् योग-मयं वपुः । मद्-योग-शान्त-चित्तस्य यादसाम् उदकं यथा
verses.transliteration: agny-ādibhir na hanyeta muner yoga-mayaṃ vapuḥ / mad-yoga-śānta-cittasya yādasām udakaṃ yathā
verses.translation: Of a sage versed in uniting consciousness whose mind was pacified in My yoga, the body cannot be injured by fire and so on, just as aquatics cannot be harmed by water [see also 7.5: 33-50].

verses.sanskrit: मद्-विभूतीर् अभिध्यायन् श्रीवत्सास्त्र-विभूषिताः । ध्वजातपत्र-व्यजनैः स भवेद् अपराजितः
verses.transliteration: mad-vibhūtīr abhidhyāyan śrīvatsāstra-vibhūṣitāḥ / dhvajātapatra-vyajanaiḥ sa bhaved aparājitaḥ
verses.translation: He [my devotee] becomes unconquerable when he meditates on My expansions that are decorated with the S'rvatsa and the weapons, flags, ceremonial umbrellas and different fans [see also B.G. 11: 32].

verses.sanskrit: उपासकस्य माम् एवं योग-धारणया मुनेः । सिद्धयः पूर्व-कथिता उपतिष्ठन्त्य् अशेषतः
verses.transliteration: upāsakasya mām evaṃ yoga-dhāraṇayā muneḥ / siddhayaḥ pūrva-kathitā upatiṣṭhanty aśeṣataḥ
verses.translation: The man of wisdom who worships Me thus by the process of concentrating in yoga, will in every respect be attended by the mystical perfections as described.

verses.sanskrit: जितेन्द्रियस्य दान्तस्य जित-श्वासात्मनो मुनेः । मद्-धारणां धारयतः का सा सिद्धिः सु-दुर्लभा
verses.transliteration: jitendriyasya dāntasya jita-śvāsātmano muneḥ / mad-dhāraṇāṃ dhārayataḥ kā sā siddhiḥ su-durlabhā
verses.translation: What perfection would be difficult to achieve for a sage who, being focussed by meditation on Me, subjugated his senses, his breathing and his mind?

verses.sanskrit: अन्तरायान् वदन्त्य् एता युञ्जतो योगम् उत्तमम् । मया सम्पद्यमानस्य काल-क्षपण-हेतवः
verses.transliteration: antarāyān vadanty etā yuñjato yogam uttamam / mayā sampadyamānasya kāla-kṣapaṇa-hetavaḥ
verses.translation: One says [though] that they [these siddhis], for the one who practices the highest form of yoga - the [bhakti] yoga by means of which one obtains everything thinkable from Me - are a hindrance and a waste of time.

verses.sanskrit: जन्मौषधि-तपो-मन्त्रैर् यावतीर् इह सिद्धयः । योगेनाप्नोति ताः सर्वा नान्यैर् योग-गतिं व्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: janmauṣadhi-tapo-mantrair yāvatīr iha siddhayaḥ / yogenāpnoti tāḥ sarvā nānyair yoga-gatiṃ vrajet
verses.translation: All perfections one in this world may acquire by birth, by herbs, austerities and by mantras, are all obtained by the practice of [bhakti] yoga. Progress in uniting one's consciousness cannot be achieved by any other method [***].

verses.sanskrit: सर्वासाम् अपि सिद्धीनां हेतुः पतिर् अहं प्रभुः । अहं योगस्य साङ्ख्यस्य धर्मस्य ब्रह्म-वादिनाम्
verses.transliteration: sarvāsām api siddhīnāṃ hetuḥ patir ahaṃ prabhuḥ / ahaṃ yogasya sāṅkhyasya dharmasya brahma-vādinām
verses.translation: I am the cause and the protector of all the perfections. I am the Lord of Yoga [the final union], the Lord of analysis, of dharma and of the [Vedic] teachers, propounders and adherents.

verses.sanskrit: अहम् आत्मान्तरो बाह्यो ऽनावृतः सर्व-देहिनाम् । यथा भूतानि भूतेषु बहिर् अन्तः स्वयं तथा
verses.transliteration: aham ātmāntaro bāhyo 'nāvṛtaḥ sarva-dehinām / yathā bhūtāni bhūteṣu bahir antaḥ svayaṃ tathā
verses.translation: The same way material elements have their existence inside and outside the living beings, I Myself, the Soul, who cannot be enclosed, exist both inside and outside of all the embodied beings [see also B.G. 2: 29-30].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच त्वं ब्रह्म परमं साक्षाद् अनाद्य्-अन्तम् अपावृतम् । सर्वेषाम् अपि भावानां त्राण-स्थित्य्-अप्ययोद्भवः
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca tvaṃ brahma paramaṃ sākṣād anādy-antam apāvṛtam / sarveṣām api bhāvānāṃ trāṇa-sthity-apyayodbhavaḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'You are the Supreme Spirit in person, not restrained and without a beginning and an end. You are the [true] protector and the maintenance, destruction and creation of all that exists.

verses.sanskrit: उच्चावचेषु भूतेषु दुर्ज्ञेयम् अकृतात्मभिः । उपासते त्वां भगवन् याथा-तथ्येन ब्राह्मणाः
verses.transliteration: uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu durjñeyam akṛtātmabhiḥ / upāsate tvāṃ bhagavan yāthā-tathyena brāhmaṇāḥ
verses.translation: Oh Supreme Lord, while You for souls lacking in self-control are hard to understand, the brahmin knowers worship Your presence within both the superior and the inferior entities of creation.

verses.sanskrit: येषु येषु च भूतेषु भक्त्या त्वां परमर्षयः । उपासीनाः प्रपद्यन्ते संसिद्धिं तद् वदस्व मे
verses.transliteration: yeṣu yeṣu ca bhūteṣu bhaktyā tvāṃ paramarṣayaḥ / upāsīnāḥ prapadyante saṃsiddhiṃ tad vadasva me
verses.translation: The great sages achieve perfection by worshiping You with devotion for this and that form. Please speak to me about these forms.

verses.sanskrit: गूढश् चरसि भूतात्मा भूतानां भूत-भावन । न त्वां पश्यन्ति भूतानि पश्यन्तं मोहितानि ते
verses.transliteration: gūḍhaś carasi bhūtātmā bhūtānāṃ bhūta-bhāvana / na tvāṃ paśyanti bhūtāni paśyantaṃ mohitāni te
verses.translation: Oh Maintainer of All Beings, not being visible You are engaged as the Very Soul of the living beings. You see them while they, bewildered by You[r external reality], do not see You.

verses.sanskrit: याः काश् च भूमौ दिवि वै रसायां विभूतयो दिक्षु महा-विभूते । ता मह्यम् आख्याह्य् अनुभावितास् ते नमामि ते तीर्थ-पदाङ्घ्रि-पद्मम्
verses.transliteration: yāḥ kāś ca bhūmau divi vai rasāyāṃ vibhūtayo dikṣu mahā-vibhūte / tā mahyam ākhyāhy anubhāvitās te namāmi te tīrtha-padāṅghri-padmam
verses.translation: And, please, oh greatest Might, explain to me what all the potencies are that You manifest in all directions on earth, in heaven and in hell. I offer my obeisances at Your lotus feet, the abode of all holy places.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच एवम् एतद् अहं पृष्टः प्रश्नं प्रश्न-विदां वर । युयुत्सुना विनशने सपत्नैर् अर्जुनेन वै
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca evam etad ahaṃ pṛṣṭaḥ praśnaṃ praśna-vidāṃ vara / yuyutsunā vinaśane sapatnair arjunena vai
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'This question, oh best of all questioners, was at the time of the destruction posed by Arjuna who desired to fight his rivals [at Kurukshetra, see B.G. 2: 54, 13: 1-2, 14: 21, 18: 1].

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञात्वा ज्ञाति-वधं गर्ह्यम् अधर्मं राज्य-हेतुकम् । ततो निवृत्तो हन्ताहं हतो ऽयम् इति लौकिकः
verses.transliteration: jñātvā jñāti-vadhaṃ garhyam adharmaṃ rājya-hetukam / tato nivṛtto hantāhaṃ hato 'yam iti laukikaḥ
verses.translation: Aware of the fact that the killing of his relatives for the sake of sovereignty was an irreligious, abominable act, he desisted and said in a worldly mind: 'Having them killed I would be the killer' [B.G. 1: 37-45, compare 2: 19].

verses.sanskrit: स तदा पुरुष-व्याघ्रो युक्त्या मे प्रतिबोधितः । अभ्यभाषत माम् एवं यथा त्वं रण-मूर्धनि
verses.transliteration: sa tadā puruṣa-vyāghro yuktyā me pratibodhitaḥ / abhyabhāṣata mām evaṃ yathā tvaṃ raṇa-mūrdhani
verses.translation: That tiger among men, asked Me just before the battle took place questions like you did and was then by Me instructed with the needed arguments.

verses.sanskrit: अहम् आत्मोद्धवामीषां भूतानां सुहृद् ईश्वरः । अहं सर्वाणि भूतानि तेषां स्थित्य्-उद्भवाप्ययः
verses.transliteration: aham ātmoddhavāmīṣāṃ bhūtānāṃ suhṛd īśvaraḥ / ahaṃ sarvāṇi bhūtāni teṣāṃ sthity-udbhavāpyayaḥ
verses.translation: I am the Supersoul of these living entities, oh Uddhava, their Well-wisher and Lord and Master; I am the maintenance, creation and annihilation of all living beings.

verses.sanskrit: अहं गतिर् गतिमतां कालः कलयताम् अहम् । गुनाणां चाप्य् अहं साम्यं गुणिन्य् औत्पत्तिको गुणः
verses.transliteration: ahaṃ gatir gatimatāṃ kālaḥ kalayatām aham / gunāṇāṃ cāpy ahaṃ sāmyaṃ guṇiny autpattiko guṇaḥ
verses.translation: I am the goal of those who seek progress, the Time of those who exert control, I am the equilibrium of the basic qualities of nature, as also the virtue of those endowed with good qualities.

verses.sanskrit: गुणिनाम् अप्य् अहं सूत्रं महतां च महान् अहम् । सूक्ष्माणाम् अप्य् अहं जीवो दुर्जयानाम् अहं मनः
verses.transliteration: guṇinām apy ahaṃ sūtraṃ mahatāṃ ca mahān aham / sūkṣmāṇām apy ahaṃ jīvo durjayānām ahaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: I am the thread (the rule, stra) for everything that has quality, I am the totality of all great things, among that what is subtle I am the spirit soul, and of all things that are difficult to conquer I am the mind.

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्यगर्भो वेदानां मन्त्राणां प्रणवस् त्रि-वृत् । अक्षराणाम् अ-कारो ऽस्मि पदानि च्छन्दुसाम् अहम्
verses.transliteration: hiraṇyagarbho vedānāṃ mantrāṇāṃ praṇavas tri-vṛt / akṣarāṇām a-kāro 'smi padāni cchandusām aham
verses.translation: I am Hiranyagarbha [Brahm, the original teacher] of the Vedas, among the mantras I am the three-lettered Omkra, of the letters I am the first one [the 'a'], and among the sacred metres I am the three-footed one [the Gyatr mantra].

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रो ऽहं सर्व-देवानां वसूनाम् अस्मि हव्य-वाट् । आदित्यानाम् अहं विष्णू रुद्राणां नील-लोहितः
verses.transliteration: indro 'haṃ sarva-devānāṃ vasūnām asmi havya-vāṭ / ādityānām ahaṃ viṣṇū rudrāṇāṃ nīla-lohitaḥ
verses.translation: Among the gods I am Indra, among the Vasus I am Agni, among the sons of Aditi I am Vishnu [Vmana], and among the Rudras I am Nla-Lohita [the one colored red-blue, S'iva. See also 3.12: 7].

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मर्षीणां भृगुर् अहं राजर्षीणाम् अहं मनुः । देवर्षीणां नारदो ऽहं हविर्धान्य् अस्मि धेनुषु
verses.transliteration: brahmarṣīṇāṃ bhṛgur ahaṃ rājarṣīṇām ahaṃ manuḥ / devarṣīṇāṃ nārado 'haṃ havirdhāny asmi dhenuṣu
verses.translation: I am Bhrigu among the brahmin sages, I am Manu among the saintly kings, among the demigod sages I am Nrada, and among the cows I am Kmadhenu [the cow of plenty].

verses.sanskrit: सिद्धेश्वराणां कपिलः सुपर्णो ऽहं पतत्रिणाम् । प्रजापतीनां दक्षो ऽहं पितॄणाम् अहम् अर्यमा
verses.transliteration: siddheśvarāṇāṃ kapilaḥ suparṇo 'haṃ patatriṇām / prajāpatīnāṃ dakṣo 'haṃ pitṝṇām aham aryamā
verses.translation: Among those of perfect control I am Kapila, Garuda I am among the birds, Daksha among the founding fathers, and Aryam among the forefathers.

verses.sanskrit: मां विद्ध्य् उद्धव दैत्यानां प्रह्लादम् असुरेश्वरम् । सोमं नक्षत्रौषधीनां धनेशं यक्ष-रक्षसाम्
verses.transliteration: māṃ viddhy uddhava daityānāṃ prahlādam asureśvaram / somaṃ nakṣatrauṣadhīnāṃ dhaneśaṃ yakṣa-rakṣasām
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, know Me among the sons of Diti as Prahlda, the lord of the unenlightened souls, know Me as the [order of the] moon for the stars and the herbs, and as Kuvera, the lord of wealth, among the Yakshas and Rkshasas.

verses.sanskrit: ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां यादसां वरुणं प्रभुम् । तपतां द्युमतां सूर्यं मनुष्याणां च भू-पतिम्
verses.transliteration: airāvataṃ gajendrāṇāṃ yādasāṃ varuṇaṃ prabhum / tapatāṃ dyumatāṃ sūryaṃ manuṣyāṇāṃ ca bhū-patim
verses.translation: Among the lordly elephants I am Airvata, I am Varuna, the master of the aquatics, of everything that heats and illumines I am the sun, and among the human beings I am the master of the realm [the king].

verses.sanskrit: उच्चैःश्रवास् तुरङ्गाणां धातूनाम् अस्मि काञ्चनम् । यमः संयमतां चाहम् सर्पाणाम् अस्मि वासुकिः
verses.transliteration: uccaiḥśravās turaṅgāṇāṃ dhātūnām asmi kāñcanam / yamaḥ saṃyamatāṃ cāham sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ
verses.translation: Ucchais'rav I am among the horses, among the metals I am gold, Yamarja I am among those who exercise control, and among the serpents I am Vsuki.

verses.sanskrit: नागेन्द्राणाम् अनन्तो ऽहं मृगेन्द्रः शृङ्गि-दंष्ट्रिणाम् । आश्रमाणाम् अहं तुर्यो वर्णानां प्रथमो ऽनघ
verses.transliteration: nāgendrāṇām ananto 'haṃ mṛgendraḥ śṛṅgi-daṃṣṭriṇām / āśramāṇām ahaṃ turyo varṇānāṃ prathamo 'nagha
verses.translation: Among the hooded snakes I am Anantadeva, among the beasts with teeth and horns I am the lion, among the social orders [the status-groups, the s'ramas] I am the fourth order [the sannyss] and among the vocations [the varnas] I am the first one [of the brahmins], oh sinless one.

verses.sanskrit: तीर्थानां स्रोतसां गङ्गा समुद्रः सरसाम् अहम् । आयुधानां धनुर् अहं त्रिपुर-घ्नो धनुष्मताम्
verses.transliteration: tīrthānāṃ srotasāṃ gaṅgā samudraḥ sarasām aham / āyudhānāṃ dhanur ahaṃ tripura-ghno dhanuṣmatām
verses.translation: Among the holy rivers I am the Ganges, I am the ocean among the expanses of water, I am the bow among the weapons, and among the wielders of the bow I am the destroyer of Tripura [S'iva].

verses.sanskrit: धिष्ण्यानाम् अस्म्य् अहं मेरुर् गहनानां हिमालयः । वनस्पतीनाम् अश्वत्थ ओषधीनाम् अहं यवः
verses.transliteration: dhiṣṇyānām asmy ahaṃ merur gahanānāṃ himālayaḥ / vanaspatīnām aśvattha oṣadhīnām ahaṃ yavaḥ
verses.translation: I am Meru among the abodes, among the places that are inaccessible I am the Himalayas, among the trees I am the as'vattha, and among the plants I am the ones bearing grains [barley].

verses.sanskrit: पुरोधसां वसिष्ठो ऽहं ब्रह्मिष्ठानां बृहस्पतिः । स्कन्दो ऽहं सर्व-सेनान्याम् अग्रण्यां भगवान् अजः
verses.transliteration: purodhasāṃ vasiṣṭho 'haṃ brahmiṣṭhānāṃ bṛhaspatiḥ / skando 'haṃ sarva-senānyām agraṇyāṃ bhagavān ajaḥ
verses.translation: Among the priests I am Vasishthha, among those vowed to the Veda I am Brihaspati, Krtikeya [Skanda] I am among the military leaders, and among the foremost [of spiritual leadership] I am the unborn, supreme Lord [Brahm, the Creator].

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञानां ब्रह्म-यज्ञो ऽहं व्रतानाम् अविहिंसनम् । वाय्व्-अग्न्य्-अर्काम्बु-वाग्-आत्मा शुचीनाम् अप्य् अहं शुचिः
verses.transliteration: yajñānāṃ brahma-yajño 'haṃ vratānām avihiṃsanam / vāyv-agny-arkāmbu-vāg-ātmā śucīnām apy ahaṃ śuciḥ
verses.translation: Among the sacrifices I am the study of the Veda, among the vows I am the vow of nonviolence [vegetarianism], and among the purifiers fire, wind, sun, water, speech and soul I am the purest [the Supersoul].

verses.sanskrit: योगानाम् आत्म-संरोधो मन्त्रो ऽस्मि विजिगीषताम् । आन्वीक्षिकी कौशलानां विकल्पः ख्याति-वादिनाम्
verses.transliteration: yogānām ātma-saṃrodho mantro 'smi vijigīṣatām / ānvīkṣikī kauśalānāṃ vikalpaḥ khyāti-vādinām
verses.translation: Of the [eight-fold] process of yoga I am the full restraint with the soul [the final stage of samdhi], to those desiring victory I am prudent counsel, I am the [metaphysical] logic of all discrimination [of distinguishing between spirit and matter], and for the speculative [opinionated, reputed] philosophers I am the diversity of views [shad-dars'ana].

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीणां तु शतरूपाहं पुंसां स्वायम्भुवो मनुः । नारायणो मुनीनां च कुमारो ब्रह्मचारिणाम्
verses.transliteration: strīṇāṃ tu śatarūpāhaṃ puṃsāṃ svāyambhuvo manuḥ / nārāyaṇo munīnāṃ ca kumāro brahmacāriṇām
verses.translation: Among the ladies I am S'atarp [wife of Manu, see 3.12: 54] and among the men I am Svyambhuva Manu. Among the sages I am sage Nryana [see 10.87: 4] and among the celibates I am Sanat-kumra.

verses.sanskrit: धर्माणाम् अस्मि सन्न्यासः क्षेमाणाम् अबहिर्-मतिः । गुह्यानां सु-नृतं मौनं मिथुनानाम् अजस् त्व् अहम्
verses.transliteration: dharmāṇām asmi sannyāsaḥ kṣemāṇām abahir-matiḥ / guhyānāṃ su-nṛtaṃ maunaṃ mithunānām ajas tv aham
verses.translation: Among the principles of religion I am renunciation, of all things basic I am the inner awareness, of the secrecy I am pleasant speech and silence, and of the sexual couple I am the unborn one [the original Prajpati who is Brahm, see 3.12: 53-54].

verses.sanskrit: संवत्सरो ऽस्म्य् अनिमिषाम् ऋतूनां मधु-माधवौ । मासानां मार्गशीर्षो ऽहं नक्षत्राणां तथाभिजित्
verses.transliteration: saṃvatsaro 'smy animiṣām ṛtūnāṃ madhu-mādhavau / māsānāṃ mārgaśīrṣo 'haṃ nakṣatrāṇāṃ tathābhijit
verses.translation: Of that what constitutes a stable vision I am the solar year, of the seasons I am spring, among the months I am Mrgas'rsha [November-December] and of the lunar mansions [the twenty-seven nakshatras] I am Abhijit.

verses.sanskrit: अहं युगानां च कृतं धीराणां देवलो ऽसितः । द्वैपायनो ऽस्मि व्यासानां कवीनां काव्य आत्मवान्
verses.transliteration: ahaṃ yugānāṃ ca kṛtaṃ dhīrāṇāṃ devalo 'sitaḥ / dvaipāyano 'smi vyāsānāṃ kavīnāṃ kāvya ātmavān
verses.translation: Among the yugas I am Satya-yuga, among the steady I am Devala and Asita, of the Vedic editors I am Dvaipyana [Vysadeva] and among the scholars learned in spirituality I am S'ukrcrya.

verses.sanskrit: वासुदेवो भगवतां त्वं तु भागवतेष्व् अहम् । किम्पुरुषानां हनुमान् विद्याध्राणां सुदर्शनः
verses.transliteration: vāsudevo bhagavatāṃ tvaṃ tu bhāgavateṣv aham / kimpuruṣānāṃ hanumān vidyādhrāṇāṃ sudarśanaḥ
verses.translation: Among those named Bhagavn [the Supreme Lord] I am Vsudeva, among My devotees I am indeed you [Uddhava], among the ape-like souls I am Hanumn, and among the scientists I am Sudars'ana.

verses.sanskrit: रत्नानां पद्म-रागो ऽस्मि पद्म-कोशः सु-पेशसाम् । कुशो ऽस्मि दर्भ-जातीनां गव्यम् आज्यं हविःष्व् अहम्
verses.transliteration: ratnānāṃ padma-rāgo 'smi padma-kośaḥ su-peśasām / kuśo 'smi darbha-jātīnāṃ gavyam ājyaṃ haviḥṣv aham
verses.translation: I am the ruby among the jewels, of all things beautiful I am the lotus cup, of all types of grass I am the kus'a grass, and of the oblations I am the ghee of the cow.

verses.sanskrit: व्यवसायिनाम् अहं लक्ष्मीः कितवानां छल-ग्रहः । तितिक्षास्मि तितिक्षूणां सत्त्वं सत्त्ववताम् अहम्
verses.transliteration: vyavasāyinām ahaṃ lakṣmīḥ kitavānāṃ chala-grahaḥ / titikṣāsmi titikṣūṇāṃ sattvaṃ sattvavatām aham
verses.translation: I am the wealth of those engaged in business, of the cheaters I am the gambling, I am the forgiveness of the tolerant, and I am the character of those in the mode of goodness.

verses.sanskrit: ओजः सहो बलवतां कर्माहं विद्धि सात्वताम् । सात्वतां नव-मूर्तीनाम् आदि-मूर्तिर् अहं परा
verses.transliteration: ojaḥ saho balavatāṃ karmāhaṃ viddhi sātvatām / sātvatāṃ nava-mūrtīnām ādi-mūrtir ahaṃ parā
verses.translation: I am the mental and physical strength of the strong. Please know that I am the [devotional] work performed by the devotees and that of My nine forms [nava mrti] by which these Stvatas are worshiping Me, I am the Supreme Original Form [Vsudeva].

verses.sanskrit: विश्वावसुः पूर्वचित्तिर् गन्धर्वाप्सरसाम् अहम् । भूधराणाम् अहं स्थैर्यं गन्ध-मात्रम् अहं भुवः
verses.transliteration: viśvāvasuḥ pūrvacittir gandharvāpsarasām aham / bhūdharāṇām ahaṃ sthairyaṃ gandha-mātram ahaṃ bhuvaḥ
verses.translation: Among the singers of heaven I am Vis'vvasu, and among the heavenly dancing girls I am Prvacitti. I am the steadiness of the mountains and the pure aroma of the earth.

verses.sanskrit: अपां रसश् च परमस् तेजिष्ठानां विभावसुः । प्रभा सूर्येन्दु-ताराणां शब्दो ऽहं नभसः परः
verses.transliteration: apāṃ rasaś ca paramas tejiṣṭhānāṃ vibhāvasuḥ / prabhā sūryendu-tārāṇāṃ śabdo 'haṃ nabhasaḥ paraḥ
verses.translation: I am the supreme taste of water and of what is most brilliant I am the sun. I am the effulgence of the moon, the stars and the sun, and I am the transcendental sound vibration in the ether [see also 11.15: 19].

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्यानां बलिर् अहं वीराणाम् अहम् अर्जुनः । भूतानां स्थितिर् उत्पत्तिर् अहं वै प्रतिसङ्क्रमः
verses.transliteration: brahmaṇyānāṃ balir ahaṃ vīrāṇām aham arjunaḥ / bhūtānāṃ sthitir utpattir ahaṃ vai pratisaṅkramaḥ
verses.translation: Among those devoted to the brahminical culture I am Bali, among the heroes I am Arjuna, and I am the generation, stability and dissolution of all living beings.

verses.sanskrit: गत्य्-उक्त्य्-उत्सर्गोपादानम् आनन्द-स्पर्श-लक्षनम् । आस्वाद-श्रुत्य्-अवघ्राणम् अहं सर्वेन्द्रियेन्द्रियम्
verses.transliteration: gaty-ukty-utsargopādānam ānanda-sparśa-lakṣanam / āsvāda-śruty-avaghrāṇam ahaṃ sarvendriyendriyam
verses.translation: Of all the senses I am the potency of walking, speaking, evacuating, handling and enjoying [the karmendriyas], as also the touching, seeing, tasting, hearing and smelling [the jnendriyas].

verses.sanskrit: पृथिवी वायुर् आकाश आपो ज्योतिर् अहं महान् । विकारः पुरुषो ऽव्यक्तं रजः सत्त्वं तमः परम् । अहम् एतत् प्रसङ्ख्यानं ज्ञानं तत्त्व-विनिश्चयः
verses.transliteration: pṛthivī vāyur ākāśa āpo jyotir ahaṃ mahān / vikāraḥ puruṣo 'vyaktaṃ rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamaḥ param / aham etat prasaṅkhyānaṃ jñānaṃ tattva-viniścayaḥ
verses.translation: All this I enumerated - earth, air, sky, water and fire, the ego, the greater [principle, the intellect], the [additional eleven] transformations [the ten indriyas and the mind], the person, that what is unmanifest and the modes of rajas, tamas and sattva - stands, together with the spiritual knowledge and the steady conviction [therewith], for Me, I, the One Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: मयेश्वरेण जीवेन गुणेन गुणिना विना । सर्वात्मनापि सर्वेण न भावो विद्यते क्वचित्
verses.transliteration: mayeśvareṇa jīvena guṇena guṇinā vinā / sarvātmanāpi sarveṇa na bhāvo vidyate kvacit
verses.translation: I, the Supreme Lord, the living entity, the [good] qualities and the possessor of the qualities, am the Soul of all; I am indeed everything, the one outside of whom nothing whatsoever exists.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्ख्यानं परमाणूनां कालेन क्रियते मया । न तथा मे विभूतीनां सृजतो ऽण्डानि कोटिशः
verses.transliteration: saṅkhyānaṃ paramāṇūnāṃ kālena kriyate mayā / na tathā me vibhūtīnāṃ sṛjato 'ṇḍāni koṭiśaḥ
verses.translation: I who create the universes by the millions, may count their atoms after a certain time, but not so My opulences [compare 10.14: 7].

verses.sanskrit: तेजः श्रीः कीर्तिर् ऐश्वर्यं ह्रीस् त्यागः सौभगं भगः । वीर्यं तितिक्षा विज्ञानं यत्र यत्र स मे ऽंशकः
verses.transliteration: tejaḥ śrīḥ kīrtir aiśvaryaṃ hrīs tyāgaḥ saubhagaṃ bhagaḥ / vīryaṃ titikṣā vijñānaṃ yatra yatra sa me 'ṃśakaḥ
verses.translation: Whatever power, beauty, fame, dominion [see 11.15], humility, renunciation, enjoyment, fortune, strength, tolerance or wisdom one may find, all constitutes an integral part of Me.

verses.sanskrit: एतास् ते कीर्तिताः सर्वाः सङ्क्षेपेण विभूतयः । मनो-विकारा एवैते यथा वाचाभिधीयते
verses.transliteration: etās te kīrtitāḥ sarvāḥ saṅkṣepeṇa vibhūtayaḥ / mano-vikārā evaite yathā vācābhidhīyate
verses.translation: All these spiritual potencies I briefly described to you [see also B.G. 7, 9 and 10] are transformations that by the mind are put in corresponding words [in the scriptures, in the teachings].

verses.sanskrit: वाचं यच्छ मनो यच्छ प्राणान् यच्छेद्रियाणि च । आत्मानम् आत्मना यच्छ न भूयः कल्पसे ऽध्वने
verses.transliteration: vācaṃ yaccha mano yaccha prāṇān yacchedriyāṇi ca / ātmānam ātmanā yaccha na bhūyaḥ kalpase 'dhvane
verses.translation: [Therefore] control your mind, control your speech, control your breath and senses. Control yourself [through meditation] from within the soul, so that you will never again stumble on the path of material existence.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै वाङ्-मनसी संयग् असंयच्छन् धिया यतिः । तस्य व्रतं तपो दानं स्रवत्य् आम-घटाम्बु-वत्
verses.transliteration: yo vai vāṅ-manasī saṃyag asaṃyacchan dhiyā yatiḥ / tasya vrataṃ tapo dānaṃ sravaty āma-ghaṭāmbu-vat
verses.translation: Of a transcendentalist who is not by meditation completely in control of his speech and mind, his vows, penance and charity will flow away like water from an unbaked pot.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् वचो मनः प्राणान् नियच्छेन् मत्-परायणः । मद्-भक्ति-युक्तया बुद्ध्या ततः परिसमाप्यते
verses.transliteration: tasmād vaco manaḥ prāṇān niyacchen mat-parāyaṇaḥ / mad-bhakti-yuktayā buddhyā tataḥ parisamāpyate
verses.translation: Someone devoted to Me, should therefore control his words, mind and life air. With that intelligence thus being connected in devotion unto Me, he achieves his life's purpose.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यस् त्वयाभिहितः पूर्वं धर्मस् त्वद्-भक्ति-लक्षणः । वर्णाशमाचारवतां सर्वेषां द्वि-पदाम् अपि
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yas tvayābhihitaḥ pūrvaṃ dharmas tvad-bhakti-lakṣaṇaḥ / varṇāśamācāravatāṃ sarveṣāṃ dvi-padām api
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lotus-eyed One, You previously described the religious principles of being devoted to You that are respected by all varns'rama followers and even by those who do not follow this system. Explain now to me how human beings by mindfully executing their occupational duties may arrive at devotional service unto You.

verses.sanskrit: यथानुष्ठीयमानेन त्वयि भक्तिर् नृणां भवेत् । स्व-धर्मेणारविन्दाक्ष तन् ममाख्यातुम् अर्हसि
verses.transliteration: yathānuṣṭhīyamānena tvayi bhaktir nṛṇāṃ bhavet / sva-dharmeṇāravindākṣa tan mamākhyātum arhasi
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lotus-eyed One, You previously described the religious principles of being devoted to You that are respected by all varns'rama followers and even by those who do not follow this system. Explain now to me how human beings by mindfully executing their occupational duties may arrive at devotional service unto You.

verses.sanskrit: पुरा किल महा-बाहो धर्मं परमकं प्रभो । यत् तेन हंस-रूपेण ब्रह्मणे ऽभ्यात्थ माधव
verses.transliteration: purā kila mahā-bāho dharmaṃ paramakaṃ prabho / yat tena haṃsa-rūpeṇa brahmaṇe 'bhyāttha mādhava
verses.translation: Dear Mdhava, oh Mighty-armed One, in the past You in the form of Lord Hamsa approached Brahm about the religious principles by which one finds supreme happiness [11.13]. After having ruled for such a very long time, oh Subduer of the Enemies, these principles that You have taught today, will no longer be common in human society [see also 5.6: 10 and 11.5: 36 and Kali-yuga].

verses.sanskrit: स इदानीं सु-महता कालेनामित्र-कर्शन । न प्रायो भविता मर्त्य- लोके प्राग् अनुशासितः
verses.transliteration: sa idānīṃ su-mahatā kālenāmitra-karśana / na prāyo bhavitā martya- loke prāg anuśāsitaḥ
verses.translation: Dear Mdhava, oh Mighty-armed One, in the past You in the form of Lord Hamsa approached Brahm about the religious principles by which one finds supreme happiness [11.13]. After having ruled for such a very long time, oh Subduer of the Enemies, these principles that You have taught today, will no longer be common in human society [see also 5.6: 10 and 11.5: 36 and Kali-yuga].

verses.sanskrit: वक्ता कर्ताविता नान्यो धर्मस्याच्युत ते भुवि । सभायाम् अपि वैरिञ्च्यां यत्र मूर्ति-धराः कलाः
verses.transliteration: vaktā kartāvitā nānyo dharmasyācyuta te bhuvi / sabhāyām api vairiñcyāṃ yatra mūrti-dharāḥ kalāḥ
verses.translation: Dear Acyuta, there is no speaker, creator and protector of dharma other than You; not on earth and not even in the assembly of Brahm where You are present in a supporting manifestation of Yours [viz. the Vedas, see also 10.87]. When the earth is abandoned by Your lordship, oh Madhusdana, oh Creator, Protector and Speaker, who then, oh Lord, will speak about the [knowledge that was] lost?

verses.sanskrit: कर्त्रावित्रा प्रवक्त्रा च भवता मधुसूदन । त्यक्ते मही-तले देव विनष्टं कः प्रवक्ष्यति
verses.transliteration: kartrāvitrā pravaktrā ca bhavatā madhusūdana / tyakte mahī-tale deva vinaṣṭaṃ kaḥ pravakṣyati
verses.translation: Dear Acyuta, there is no speaker, creator and protector of dharma other than You; not on earth and not even in the assembly of Brahm where You are present in a supporting manifestation of Yours [viz. the Vedas, see also 10.87]. When the earth is abandoned by Your lordship, oh Madhusdana, oh Creator, Protector and Speaker, who then, oh Lord, will speak about the [knowledge that was] lost?

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वं नः सर्व-धर्म-ज्ञ धर्मस् त्वद्-भक्ति-लक्षणः । यथा यस्य विधीयेत तथा वर्णय मे प्रभो
verses.transliteration: tat tvaṃ naḥ sarva-dharma-jña dharmas tvad-bhakti-lakṣaṇaḥ / yathā yasya vidhīyeta tathā varṇaya me prabho
verses.translation: Please describe therefore to me, oh Master, oh Knower of All Dharma, who would qualify for the duties characterized by devotion unto You, and how one should perform them.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच इत्थं स्व-भृत्य-मुख्येन पृष्टः स भगवान् हरिः । प्रीतः क्षेमाय मर्त्यानां धर्मान् आह सनातनान्
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca itthaṃ sva-bhṛtya-mukhyena pṛṣṭaḥ sa bhagavān hariḥ / prītaḥ kṣemāya martyānāṃ dharmān āha sanātanān
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'He, the Supreme Lord Hari, thus being questioned by the best of His devotees, was pleased and then spoke, for the sake of the welfare of all conditioned souls, about the eternal duties of dharma.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच धर्म्य एष तव प्रश्नो नैःश्रेयस-करो नृणाम् । वर्णाश्रमाचारवतां तम् उद्धव निबोध मे
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca dharmya eṣa tava praśno naiḥśreyasa-karo nṛṇām / varṇāśramācāravatāṃ tam uddhava nibodha me
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'This question of yours is one faithful to the dharma, it leads man to beatitude. Please, Uddhava, learn from Me about these forms of good [varns'rama] conduct concerning the societal status [s'rama] and vocation [varna].

verses.sanskrit: आदौ कृत-युगे वर्णो नृणां हंस इति स्मृतः । कृत-कृत्याः प्रजा जात्या तस्मात् कृत-युगं विदुः
verses.transliteration: ādau kṛta-yuge varṇo nṛṇāṃ haṃsa iti smṛtaḥ / kṛta-kṛtyāḥ prajā jātyā tasmāt kṛta-yugaṃ viduḥ
verses.translation: In the beginning in Satya-yuga there was [only] one class of human beings and they were called hamsa [swan]. The citizens of that age were by birth well known with the duties to be performed - hence the scholars know that age as Krita-yuga, the age of the fulfillment of duty.

verses.sanskrit: वेदः प्रणव एवाग्रे धर्मो ऽहं वृष-रूप-धृक् । उपासते तपो-निष्ठा हंसं मां मुक्त-किल्बिषाः
verses.transliteration: vedaḥ praṇava evāgre dharmo 'haṃ vṛṣa-rūpa-dhṛk / upāsate tapo-niṣṭhā haṃsaṃ māṃ mukta-kilbiṣāḥ
verses.translation: One during that time with the Pranava gave expression to the [not in four divided] Veda, knowing Me to be the duty in the form of the bull of religion [see 1.16: 18 and 1.17: 24]. Fixed in austerity free from sins, one worshiped Me as Lord Hamsa.

verses.sanskrit: त्रेता-मुखे महा-भाग प्राणान् मे हृदयात् त्रयी । विद्या प्रादुरभूत् तस्या अहम् आसं त्रि-वृन् मखः
verses.transliteration: tretā-mukhe mahā-bhāga prāṇān me hṛdayāt trayī / vidyā prādurabhūt tasyā aham āsaṃ tri-vṛn makhaḥ
verses.translation: At the beginning of Tret-yuga, oh greatly fortunate one, in My heart from the prna the threefold of knowing [the three Vedas Rig, Sma and Yajur] originated, whereby I appeared in the three forms of sacrifice [hence the name Tret, see ritvik].

verses.sanskrit: विप्र-क्षत्रिय-विट्-शूद्रा मुख-बाहूरु-पाद-जाः । वैराजात् पुरुषाज् जाता य आत्माचार-लक्षणाः
verses.transliteration: vipra-kṣatriya-viṭ-śūdrā mukha-bāhūru-pāda-jāḥ / vairājāt puruṣāj jātā ya ātmācāra-lakṣaṇāḥ
verses.translation: From the Original Personality the intellectuals, the rulers, the merchants and the laborers [the varnas] generated, whose personal activities can be recognized as [respectively] those from the mouth, the arms, the thighs and feet [legs] of the universal form [compare 2.1: 37].

verses.sanskrit: गृहाश्रमो जघनतो ब्रह्मचर्यं हृदो मम । वक्षः-स्थलाद् वने-वासः सन्न्यासः शिरसि स्थितः
verses.transliteration: gṛhāśramo jaghanato brahmacaryaṃ hṛdo mama / vakṣaḥ-sthalād vane-vāsaḥ sannyāsaḥ śirasi sthitaḥ
verses.translation: The celibate students came from My heart, the householders are from My loins, from My chest those [of retreat] living in the forest found their existence and the renounced order of life is found in My head [see s'ramas].

verses.sanskrit: वर्णानाम् आश्रमाणां च जन्म-भूम्य्-अनुसारिणीः । आसन् प्रकृतयो नॄनां नीचैर् नीचोत्तमोत्तमाः
verses.transliteration: varṇānām āśramāṇāṃ ca janma-bhūmy-anusāriṇīḥ / āsan prakṛtayo nṝnāṃ nīcair nīcottamottamāḥ
verses.translation: The natures of the people of the different societal classes [varnas] and status groups [s'ramas] evolved according to the situation of their birth [in My body]: in a low position inferior natures evolved and in a high position superior natures developed.

verses.sanskrit: शमो दमस् तपः शौचं सन्तोषः क्षान्तिर् आर्जवम् । मद्-भक्तिश् च दया सत्यं ब्रह्म-प्रकृतयस् त्व् इमाः
verses.transliteration: śamo damas tapaḥ śaucaṃ santoṣaḥ kṣāntir ārjavam / mad-bhaktiś ca dayā satyaṃ brahma-prakṛtayas tv imāḥ
verses.translation: Equanimity, sense-control, austerity, cleanliness, contentment, forgiveness, sincerity, devotion to Me, compassion and truthfulness are the natural qualities of the brahmins [compare 7.11: 21 and B.G. 18: 42].

verses.sanskrit: तेजो बलं धृतिः शौर्यं तितिक्षौदार्यम् उद्यमः । स्थैर्यं ब्रह्मन्यम् ऐश्वर्यं क्षत्र-प्रकृतयस् त्व् इमाः
verses.transliteration: tejo balaṃ dhṛtiḥ śauryaṃ titikṣaudāryam udyamaḥ / sthairyaṃ brahmanyam aiśvaryaṃ kṣatra-prakṛtayas tv imāḥ
verses.translation: Ardor, physical strength, determination, heroism, tolerance, generosity, endeavor, steadiness, being mindful of the brahminical culture and leadership are the natural qualities of the kshatriyas [My arms, compare 7.11: 22 and B.G. 18: 43].

verses.sanskrit: आस्तिक्यं दान-निष्ठा च अदम्भो ब्रह्म-सेवनम् । अतुष्टिर् अर्थोपचयैर् वैश्य-प्रकृतयस् त्व् इमाः
verses.transliteration: āstikyaṃ dāna-niṣṭhā ca adambho brahma-sevanam / atuṣṭir arthopacayair vaiśya-prakṛtayas tv imāḥ
verses.translation: Faith, dedication to charity, straightforwardness, service to the brahmins and always engaged in the accumulation of money, constitute the natural qualities of the vais'yas [My thighs, compare 7.11: 23 and B.G. 18: 44].

verses.sanskrit: शुश्रूषणं द्विज-गवां देवानां चाप्य् अमायया । तत्र लब्धेन सन्तोषः शूद्र-प्रकृतयस् त्व् इमाः
verses.transliteration: śuśrūṣaṇaṃ dvija-gavāṃ devānāṃ cāpy amāyayā / tatra labdhena santoṣaḥ śūdra-prakṛtayas tv imāḥ
verses.translation: Free from deceit being obedient towards the twice-born souls [the three higher varnas], of service for both the cows and the divine personalities, as also being perfectly contented with the reward thus acquired, are the natural qualities of the s'dras [His feet or legs, compare 7.11: 24 and B.G. 18: 44].

verses.sanskrit: अशौचम् अनृतं स्तेयं नास्तिक्यं शुष्क-विग्रहः । कामः क्रोधश् च तर्षश् च स भावो ऽन्त्यावसायिनाम्
verses.transliteration: aśaucam anṛtaṃ steyaṃ nāstikyaṃ śuṣka-vigrahaḥ / kāmaḥ krodhaś ca tarṣaś ca sa bhāvo 'ntyāvasāyinām
verses.translation: To be unclean, deceitful, thievish, faithless, quarrelsome, lusty, fiery and of constant hankering, constitutes the nature of those who occupy the lowest position [the outcasts].

verses.sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यम् अस्तेयम् अकाम-क्रोध-लोभता । भूत-प्रिय-हितेहा च धर्मो ऽयं सार्व-वर्णिकः
verses.transliteration: ahiṃsā satyam asteyam akāma-krodha-lobhatā / bhūta-priya-hitehā ca dharmo 'yaṃ sārva-varṇikaḥ
verses.translation: It is the duty of all members of society to be of nonviolence, truthfulness and honesty, to be free from lust, anger and greed and to desire the welfare and happiness of all living entities.

verses.sanskrit: द्वितीयं प्राप्यानुपूर्व्याज् जन्मोपनयनं द्विजः । वसन् गुरु-कुले दान्तो ब्रह्माधीयीत चाहूतः
verses.transliteration: dvitīyaṃ prāpyānupūrvyāj janmopanayanaṃ dvijaḥ / vasan guru-kule dānto brahmādhīyīta cāhūtaḥ
verses.translation: A twice-born soul having undergone a second birth with purificatory rites [with samskras receiving a sacred thread] and [initiation into] the Gyatr mantra, should reside in the residence [the school] of the guru and must, with his senses under control, on his request study the scriptures [see also B.G. 16: 24].

verses.sanskrit: मेखलाजिन-दण्डाक्ष- ब्रह्म-सूत्र-कमण्डलून् । जटिलो ऽधौत-दद्-वासो ऽरक्त-पीठः कुशान् दधत्
verses.transliteration: mekhalājina-daṇḍākṣa- brahma-sūtra-kamaṇḍalūn / jaṭilo 'dhauta-dad-vāso 'rakta-pīṭhaḥ kuśān dadhat
verses.translation: Carrying a belt, a deerskin, a staff, prayer beads, a brahmin thread, a water pot and matted hair, he [the brahmacr] without neglecting [*] his teeth and clothes, uses kus'a grass [to sit upon] and [does] not [desire] a pleasant seat.

verses.sanskrit: स्नान-भोजन-होमेषु जपोच्चारे च वाग्-यतः । न च्छिन्द्यान् नख-रोमाणि कक्षोपस्थ-गतान्य् अपि
verses.transliteration: snāna-bhojana-homeṣu japoccāre ca vāg-yataḥ / na cchindyān nakha-romāṇi kakṣopastha-gatāny api
verses.translation: Bathing and eating, attending sacrifices and doing japa, passing stool and urine, he does in silence [Vaishnavas actually murmur doing japa]; he should not [fully] trim his nails or hair, including the hair under his arms and in the pubic area [see also s'ikh].

verses.sanskrit: रेतो नावकिरेज् जातु ब्रह्म-व्रत-धरः स्वयम् । अवकीर्णे ऽवगाह्याप्सु यतासुस् त्रि-पदां जपेत्
verses.transliteration: reto nāvakirej jātu brahma-vrata-dharaḥ svayam / avakīrṇe 'vagāhyāpsu yatāsus tri-padāṃ japet
verses.translation: Someone under the vow of celibacy should never spill his semen and, when it flowed of its own, he should take a bath, control his breath and chant the Gyatr [see also rdhva-retah].

verses.sanskrit: अग्न्य्-अर्काचार्य-गो-विप्र- गुरु-वृद्ध-सुराञ् शुचिः । समाहित उपासीत सन्ध्ये द्वे यत-वाग् जपन्
verses.transliteration: agny-arkācārya-go-vipra- guru-vṛddha-surāñ śuciḥ / samāhita upāsīta sandhye dve yata-vāg japan
verses.translation: Purified with fixed attention, at the beginning and the end of the day performing his japa silently, he should serve the fire-god, the sun [see cakra], the crya, the cow, the brahmin, the teacher, the elder and the God-fearing soul [compare: 11.14: 35].

verses.sanskrit: आचार्यं मां विजानीयान् नावन्मन्येत कर्हिचित् । न मर्त्य-बुद्ध्यासूयेत सर्व-देव-मयो गुरुः
verses.transliteration: ācāryaṃ māṃ vijānīyān nāvanmanyeta karhicit / na martya-buddhyāsūyeta sarva-deva-mayo guruḥ
verses.translation: He has to recognize Me in the teacher of example [the crya]. The crya he should never at any time enviously deny respect considering him a mortal being, for the spiritual master represents all the gods [see also rule of thumb and compare e.g. 7.14: 17, 10.81: 39, 10.45: 32 and 11.15: 27].

verses.sanskrit: सायं प्रातर् उपानीय भैक्ष्यं तस्मै निवेदयेत् । यच् चान्यद् अप्य् अनुज्ञातम् उपयुञ्जीत संयतः
verses.transliteration: sāyaṃ prātar upānīya bhaikṣyaṃ tasmai nivedayet / yac cānyad apy anujñātam upayuñjīta saṃyataḥ
verses.translation: In the morning and evening he should bring him the food that was collected and offer it to him together with other articles. In self-restraint he should accept what he allows [to be his share].

verses.sanskrit: शुश्रूषमाण आचार्यं सदोपासीत नीच-वत् । यान-शय्यासन-स्थानैर् नाति-दूरे कृताञ्जलिः
verses.transliteration: śuśrūṣamāṇa ācāryaṃ sadopāsīta nīca-vat / yāna-śayyāsana-sthānair nāti-dūre kṛtāñjaliḥ
verses.translation: He should always humbly be engaged in serving the crya, following him closely, proving his respect with folded hands wherever he goes, rests, sits or stands.

verses.sanskrit: एवं-वृत्तो गुरु-कुले वसेद् भोग-विवर्जितः । विद्या समाप्यते यावद् बिभ्रद् व्रतम् अखण्डितम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ-vṛtto guru-kule vased bhoga-vivarjitaḥ / vidyā samāpyate yāvad bibhrad vratam akhaṇḍitam
verses.translation: Thus being engaged he [the upakurvna brahmacr], free from [unregulated] sense gratification and without breaking the vow [of celibacy], should live in the school of the guru until the education is completed [see also Kumras].

verses.sanskrit: यद्य् असौ छन्दसां लोकम् आरोक्ष्यन् ब्रह्म-विष्टपम् । गुरवे विन्यसेद् देहं स्वाध्यायार्थं बृहद्-व्रतः
verses.transliteration: yady asau chandasāṃ lokam ārokṣyan brahma-viṣṭapam / gurave vinyased dehaṃ svādhyāyārthaṃ bṛhad-vrataḥ
verses.translation: If he [naishthhika, faithful for the rest of his life] desires to climb up to the world of the verses [Maharloka], the heaven of Brahm, he should, for the sake of his Vedic studies, offer his body to the guru with the great vow [of permanent celibacy, see yama].

verses.sanskrit: अग्नौ गुराव् आत्मनि च सर्व-भूतेषु मां परम् । अपृथग्-धीर् उपसीत ब्रह्म-वर्चस्व्य् अकल्मषः
verses.transliteration: agnau gurāv ātmani ca sarva-bhūteṣu māṃ param / apṛthag-dhīr upasīta brahma-varcasvy akalmaṣaḥ
verses.translation: Actively engaged in the spirit and sinless, he should worship Me as being the Supreme Undivided Intelligence present in the fire, in the spiritual master, in himself and in all living beings [see also B.G. 5: 18, siddhnta and advaita].

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीणां निरीक्षण-स्पर्श- संलाप-क्ष्वेलनादिकम् । प्राणिनो मिथुनी-भूतान् अगृहस्थो ऽग्रतस् त्यजेत्
verses.transliteration: strīṇāṃ nirīkṣaṇa-sparśa- saṃlāpa-kṣvelanādikam / prāṇino mithunī-bhūtān agṛhastho 'gratas tyajet
verses.translation: The first thing to be forsaken by someone [sexually ripe but spiritually directed who is] not running his own household, is to glance at, touch, converse and joke and such with [sexually receptive] women or other sexually active living beings [see 11.14: 29 and 6.1: 56-68].

verses.sanskrit: शौचम् आचमनं स्नानं सन्ध्योपास्तिर् ममार्चनम् । तीर्थ-सेवा जपो ऽस्पृश्या- भक्ष्यासम्भाष्य-वर्जनम्
verses.transliteration: śaucam ācamanaṃ snānaṃ sandhyopāstir mamārcanam / tīrtha-sevā japo 'spṛśyā- bhakṣyāsambhāṣya-varjanam
verses.translation: Cleanliness, washing one's hands, bathing, in the morning in the afternoon and evening, being of religious service, worshiping Me, visiting holy places, handling the prayer beads, avoiding things untouchable, things not fit for consumption and things not to be discussed - this all, oh Uddhava, constitutes the voluntary penance that, in order to restrain the mind, the words and the body with Me, I who reside within all beings, is enjoined for all spiritual departments [s'ramas].

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाश्रम-प्रयुक्तो ऽयं नियमः कुल-नन्दन । मद्-भावः सर्व-भूतेषु मनो-वाक्-काय-संयमः
verses.transliteration: sarvāśrama-prayukto 'yaṃ niyamaḥ kula-nandana / mad-bhāvaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu mano-vāk-kāya-saṃyamaḥ
verses.translation: Cleanliness, washing one's hands, bathing, in the morning in the afternoon and evening, being of religious service, worshiping Me, visiting holy places, handling the prayer beads, avoiding things untouchable, things not fit for consumption and things not to be discussed - this all, oh Uddhava, constitutes the voluntary penance that, in order to restrain the mind, the words and the body with Me, I who reside within all beings, is enjoined for all spiritual departments [s'ramas].

verses.sanskrit: एवं बृहद्-व्रत-धरो ब्राह्मणो ऽग्निर् इव ज्वलन् । मद्-भक्तस् तीव्र-तपसा दग्ध-कर्माशयो ऽमलः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ bṛhad-vrata-dharo brāhmaṇo 'gnir iva jvalan / mad-bhaktas tīvra-tapasā dagdha-karmāśayo 'malaḥ
verses.translation: A brahmin observing the great vow becomes as bright as a fire. Thus he burns his karmic mentality by the intensity of his penance and becomes a spotless devotee of Mine.

verses.sanskrit: अथानन्तरम् आवेक्ष्यन् यथा-जिज्ञासितागमः । गुरवे दक्षिणां दत्त्वा स्नायाद् गुर्व्-अनुमोदितः
verses.transliteration: athānantaram āvekṣyan yathā-jijñāsitāgamaḥ / gurave dakṣiṇāṃ dattvā snāyād gurv-anumoditaḥ
verses.translation: When after thus properly having studied the Vedic knowledge he [the brahmacr] desires [to enter family life], he must offer the spiritual master remuneration and with his permission bathe himself, [put on nice clothes and leave **].

verses.sanskrit: गृहं वनं वोपविशेत् प्रव्रजेद् वा द्विजोत्तमः । आश्रमाद् आश्रमं गच्छेन् नान्यथामत्-परश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: gṛhaṃ vanaṃ vopaviśet pravrajed vā dvijottamaḥ / āśramād āśramaṃ gacchen nānyathāmat-paraś caret
verses.translation: Except for when he is My devotee, a true dvija [a person from the three higher classes] should either enter family life [grihastha], enter the forest [vanaprastha], or else become a monk, a mendicant ['samniysin' or sannysi]; he should move from one authorized societal position [s'rama] to the other and not act otherwise.

verses.sanskrit: गृहार्थी सदृशीं भार्याम् उद्वहेद् अजुगुप्सिताम् । यवीयसीं तु वयसा यं स-वर्णाम् अनु क्रमात्
verses.transliteration: gṛhārthī sadṛśīṃ bhāryām udvahed ajugupsitām / yavīyasīṃ tu vayasā yaṃ sa-varṇām anu kramāt
verses.translation: Desiring a household one should marry a wife with similar qualities who is beyond reproach and younger in age. When the first wife is of the same vocation another one may follow [of a lower class - varna - or subdivision of it, the caste - jati].

verses.sanskrit: इज्याध्ययन-दानानि सर्वेषां च द्वि-जन्मनाम् । प्रतिग्रहो ऽध्यापनं च ब्राह्मणस्यैव याजनम्
verses.transliteration: ijyādhyayana-dānāni sarveṣāṃ ca dvi-janmanām / pratigraho 'dhyāpanaṃ ca brāhmaṇasyaiva yājanam
verses.translation: Sacrifice, Vedic study and charity are the activities of all twice-born souls, but only the brahmins practice the acceptance of charity, giving [Vedic] instructions and performing sacrifices for others [compare 7.11: 14].

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिग्रहं मन्यमानस् तपस्-तेजो-यशो-नुदम् । अन्याभ्याम् एव जीवेत शिलैर् वा दोष-दृक् तयोः
verses.transliteration: pratigrahaṃ manyamānas tapas-tejo-yaśo-nudam / anyābhyām eva jīveta śilair vā doṣa-dṛk tayoḥ
verses.translation: When a brahmin considers the acceptance of charity as detrimental to his penance, spiritual power and glory, he must subsist on the other two [of teaching and sacrifice], and when he considers also these two as incompatible with his spirituality, he must subsist on gathering ears of corn left behind in the field [live 'of the stones', see also 6.7: 36, 7.15: 30 and B.G. 9: 22].

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणस्य हि देहो ऽयं क्षुद्र-कामाय नेष्यते । कृच्छ्राय तपसे चेह प्रेत्यानन्त-सुखाय च
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇasya hi deho 'yaṃ kṣudra-kāmāya neṣyate / kṛcchrāya tapase ceha pretyānanta-sukhāya ca
verses.translation: The body of a brahmin is not meant for futile sense gratification [and the consequent involuntary penances of war, disease and incarceration], it is meant for [voluntary] penances in this world and an unlimited happiness in the world hereafter [see also 11.6: 9 and B.G. 17: 14-19].

verses.sanskrit: शिलोञ्छ-वृत्त्या परितुष्ट-चित्तो धर्मं महान्तं विरजं जुषाणः । मय्य् अर्पितात्मा गृह एव तिष्ठन् नाति-प्रसक्तः समुपैति शान्तिम्
verses.transliteration: śiloñcha-vṛttyā parituṣṭa-citto dharmaṃ mahāntaṃ virajaṃ juṣāṇaḥ / mayy arpitātmā gṛha eva tiṣṭhan nāti-prasaktaḥ samupaiti śāntim
verses.translation: With his consciousness perfectly contented in his occupation of gleaning grains and magnanimously, free from passion cultivating dharma, someone who has fixed his mind upon Me - and who is thus not that attached - can achieve liberation even when he lives a householder's life [compare B.G. 3: 22 and 10.69].

verses.sanskrit: समुद्धरन्ति ये विप्रं सीदन्तं मत्-परायणम् । तान् उद्धरिष्ये न चिराद् आपद्भ्यो नौर् इवार्णवात्
verses.transliteration: samuddharanti ye vipraṃ sīdantaṃ mat-parāyaṇam / tān uddhariṣye na cirād āpadbhyo naur ivārṇavāt
verses.translation: He who delivers a brahmin surrendered to Me [or an experienced devotee] from a life of suffering [poverty], I before long, like a boat in the ocean, will deliver from all misery.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाः समुद्धरेद् राजा पितेव व्यसनात् प्रजाः । आत्मानम् आत्मना धीरो यथा गज-पतिर् गजान्
verses.transliteration: sarvāḥ samuddhared rājā piteva vyasanāt prajāḥ / ātmānam ātmanā dhīro yathā gaja-patir gajān
verses.translation: A king saves himself by delivering, like a father, his people from difficulties, just as a bull elephant without any fear for himself protects himself and other elephants [see also 4.20: 14].

verses.sanskrit: एवं-विधो नर-पतिर् विमानेनार्क-वर्चसा । विधूयेहाशुभं कृत्स्नम् इन्द्रेण सह मोदते
verses.transliteration: evaṃ-vidho nara-patir vimānenārka-varcasā / vidhūyehāśubhaṃ kṛtsnam indreṇa saha modate
verses.translation: The human ruler who thus proceeding on earth removes all sins, will therefore enjoy heaven, together with the king of heaven [Indra] riding a heavenly vehicle as brilliant as the sun.

verses.sanskrit: सीदन् विप्रो वणिग्-वृत्त्या पण्यैर् एवापदं तरेत् । खड्गेन वापदाक्रान्तो न श्व-वृत्त्या कथञ्चन
verses.transliteration: sīdan vipro vaṇig-vṛttyā paṇyair evāpadaṃ taret / khaḍgena vāpadākrānto na śva-vṛttyā kathañcana
verses.translation: When a [Vedic] scholar suffers want, he can engage in doing business like a merchant, or suffering misfortune he must take up the sword [engage in politics]. In no case of misfortune he can behave like a dog [follow a lower master].

verses.sanskrit: वैश्य-वृत्त्या तु राजन्यो जीवेन् मृगययापदि । चरेद् वा विप्र-रूपेण न श्व-वृत्त्या कथञ्चन
verses.transliteration: vaiśya-vṛttyā tu rājanyo jīven mṛgayayāpadi / cared vā vipra-rūpeṇa na śva-vṛttyā kathañcana
verses.translation: A king in case of emergency may maintain himself by acting like a merchant, by hunting or by acting like a man of knowledge. But he can never follow the course of a[n obedient] dog.

verses.sanskrit: शूद्र-वृत्तिं भजेद् वैश्यः शूद्रः कारु-कट-क्रियाम् । कृच्छ्रान् मुक्तो न गर्ह्येण वृत्तिं लिप्सेत कर्मणा
verses.transliteration: śūdra-vṛttiṃ bhajed vaiśyaḥ śūdraḥ kāru-kaṭa-kriyām / kṛcchrān mukto na garhyeṇa vṛttiṃ lipseta karmaṇā
verses.translation: A vais'ya may adopt the business of a s'dra and a s'dra may adopt the way of an artisan and make baskets and mats, but once he is freed from misery he must no longer desire a livelihood by engaging in a lower type of work [see also 7.11: 17].

verses.sanskrit: वेदाध्याय-स्वधा-स्वाहा- बल्य्-अन्नाद्यैर् यथोदयम् । देवर्षि-पितृ-भूतानि मद्-रूपाण्य् अन्व्-अहं यजेत्
verses.transliteration: vedādhyāya-svadhā-svāhā- baly-annādyair yathodayam / devarṣi-pitṛ-bhūtāni mad-rūpāṇy anv-ahaṃ yajet
verses.translation: [Being a householder] one should perform worship on a daily basis for the demigods, the sages, the forefathers and all living beings as being My potencies, by studying Vedic knowledge, saying the mantras svadh ['blessed be'] and svh ['hail to'], and by offering food and such, depending the prosperity one enjoys [see also 11.5: 41].

verses.sanskrit: यदृच्छयोपपन्नेन शुक्लेनोपार्जितेन वा । धनेनापीडयन् भृत्यान् न्यायेनैवाहरेत् क्रतून्
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayopapannena śuklenopārjitena vā / dhanenāpīḍayan bhṛtyān nyāyenaivāharet kratūn
verses.translation: Whether one's money was acquired without endeavor or acquired by performing one's straight duty, one should without burdening one's dependents, be of proper respect with the help of Vedic rituals.

verses.sanskrit: कुटुम्बेषु न सज्जेत न प्रमाद्येत् कुटुम्ब्य् अपि । विपश्चिन् नश्वरं पश्येद् अदृष्टम् अपि दृष्ट-वत्
verses.transliteration: kuṭumbeṣu na sajjeta na pramādyet kuṭumby api / vipaścin naśvaraṃ paśyed adṛṣṭam api dṛṣṭa-vat
verses.translation: One should not be fixed on one's family members nor get bewildered by them [trying to control them]; being wise one sees that things ahead are just as temporary as matters of the past.

verses.sanskrit: पुत्र-दाराप्त-बन्धूनां सङ्गमः पान्थ-सङ्गमः । अनु-देहं वियन्त्य् एते स्वप्नो निद्रानुगो यथा
verses.transliteration: putra-dārāpta-bandhūnāṃ saṅgamaḥ pāntha-saṅgamaḥ / anu-dehaṃ viyanty ete svapno nidrānugo yathā
verses.translation: The association with one's children, wife, relatives and friends is like an association with travelers; one is separated from all of them in a next body, just like a dream disappears that occurs in one's sleep [see also 7.2: 21, 9.19: 27-28].

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं परिमृशन् मुक्तो गृहेष्व् अतिथि-वद् वसन् । न गृहैर् अनुबध्येत निर्ममो निरहङ्कृतः
verses.transliteration: itthaṃ parimṛśan mukto gṛheṣv atithi-vad vasan / na gṛhair anubadhyeta nirmamo nirahaṅkṛtaḥ
verses.translation: A liberated soul thus convinced who does not identify with the body and selflessly lives at home like a guest, will not get entangled in the domestic situation.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मभिर् गृह-मेधीयैर् इष्ट्वा माम् एव भक्तिमान् । तिष्ठेद् वनं वोपविशेत् प्रजावान् वा परिव्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: karmabhir gṛha-medhīyair iṣṭvā mām eva bhaktimān / tiṣṭhed vanaṃ vopaviśet prajāvān vā parivrajet
verses.translation: When one with the activities of a family life worships Me, one may as a devotee remain at home or enter the forest, or also, given responsible offspring, take to the renounced order.

verses.sanskrit: यस् त्व् आसक्त-मतिर् गेहे पुत्र-वित्तैषणातुरः । स्त्रैणः कृपण-धीर् मूढो ममाहम् इति बध्यते
verses.transliteration: yas tv āsakta-matir gehe putra-vittaiṣaṇāturaḥ / straiṇaḥ kṛpaṇa-dhīr mūḍho mamāham iti badhyate
verses.translation: Someone though whose consciousness is disturbed by his desire for a home, children and money, is in a state of bondage and thinks - henpecked - being of a miserly mentality, unintelligently in terms of I and mine.

verses.sanskrit: अहो मे पितरौ वृद्धौ भार्या बालात्मजात्मजाः । अनाथा माम् ऋते दीनाः कथं जीवन्ति दुःखिताः
verses.transliteration: aho me pitarau vṛddhau bhāryā bālātmajātmajāḥ / anāthā mām ṛte dīnāḥ kathaṃ jīvanti duḥkhitāḥ
verses.translation: 'Oh my poor old mom and dad. Oh my wife with a baby in her arms and, oh my young, unprotected children! How in the world can they live when they, wretched because of missing me, have to suffer greatly' [see e.g. also 11.7: 52-57]?

verses.sanskrit: एवं गृहाशयाक्षिप्त- हृदयो मूढ-धीर् अयम् । अतृप्तस् तान् अनुध्यायन् मृतो ऽन्धं विशते तमः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ gṛhāśayākṣipta- hṛdayo mūḍha-dhīr ayam / atṛptas tān anudhyāyan mṛto 'ndhaṃ viśate tamaḥ
verses.translation: Such a person whose mind in his domestic situation thus is overwhelmed by thoughts and emotions, will because of his bewildered intelligence of constant concern about his family, find no satisfaction and land in darkness when he dies.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच वनं विविक्षुः पुत्रेषु भार्यां न्यस्य सहैव वा । वन एव वसेच् छान्तस् तृतीयं भागम् आयुषः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca vanaṃ vivikṣuḥ putreṣu bhāryāṃ nyasya sahaiva vā / vana eva vasec chāntas tṛtīyaṃ bhāgam āyuṣaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When one in the third phase of one's life wants to retreat into the forest, one should, in order to reside there peacefully, entrust the wife to one's sons or else together with her dwell in the forest.

verses.sanskrit: कन्द-मूल-फलैर् वन्यैर् मेध्यैर् वृत्तिं प्रकल्पयेत् । वसीत वल्कलं वासस् तृण-पर्णाजिनानि वा
verses.transliteration: kanda-mūla-phalair vanyair medhyair vṛttiṃ prakalpayet / vasīta valkalaṃ vāsas tṛṇa-parṇājināni vā
verses.translation: One should arrange for the pure [*] sustenance of the bulbs, roots and fruits of the forest, and dress oneself with tree bark, grass, leaves or animal skins.

verses.sanskrit: केश-रोम-नख-श्मश्रु- मलानि बिभृयाद् दतः । न धावेद् अप्सु मज्जेत त्रि कालं स्थण्डिले-शयः
verses.transliteration: keśa-roma-nakha-śmaśru- malāni bibhṛyād dataḥ / na dhāved apsu majjeta tri kālaṃ sthaṇḍile-śayaḥ
verses.translation: [In the forest] one should allow the hair on one's head and body, one's facial hair and nails [to grow], as also the filth of one's body, not [extensively] clean one's teeth, [but] bathe three times a day and [at night] sleep on the ground.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रीष्मे तप्येत पञ्चाग्नीन् वर्षास्व् आसार-षाड् जले । आकण्थ-मग्नः शिशिर एवं वृत्तस् तपश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: grīṣme tapyeta pañcāgnīn varṣāsv āsāra-ṣāḍ jale / ākaṇtha-magnaḥ śiśira evaṃ vṛttas tapaś caret
verses.translation: Thus proceeding one must ascetically tolerate the five fires during the summer [the sacrificial fires in four directions and the sun above], the torrents of rain during the rainy season, and the cold of winter when one is submerged in water up to one's neck [see also 4.23: 6].

verses.sanskrit: अग्नि-पक्वं समश्नीयात् काल-पक्वम् अथापि वा । उलूखलाश्म-कुट्टो वा दन्तोलूखल एव वा
verses.transliteration: agni-pakvaṃ samaśnīyāt kāla-pakvam athāpi vā / ulūkhalāśma-kuṭṭo vā dantolūkhala eva vā
verses.translation: One eats what is either prepared on a fire, what has ripened by time or what was pulverized with a mortar, with a stone or ground with one's teeth.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं सञ्चिनुयात् सर्वम् आत्मनो वृत्ति-कारणम् । देश-काल-बलाभिज्ञो नाददीतान्यदाहृतम्
verses.transliteration: svayaṃ sañcinuyāt sarvam ātmano vṛtti-kāraṇam / deśa-kāla-balābhijño nādadītānyadāhṛtam
verses.translation: One should personally collect whatever that is needed for one's sustenance depending the place, the time and one's energy, and understand that [living in the forest] one must not store anything for another time [see also 7.12: 19].

verses.sanskrit: वन्यैश् चरु-पुरोडाशैर् निर्वपेत् काल-चोदितान् । न तु श्रौतेन पशुना मां यजेत वनाश्रमी
verses.transliteration: vanyaiś caru-puroḍāśair nirvapet kāla-coditān / na tu śrautena paśunā māṃ yajeta vanāśramī
verses.translation: A vnaprastha may choose for oblations [of rice, barley and dl], may offer rice cakes or offer fruits according to the season, but he may never worship Me with sacrificing animals, [even though it is] mentioned in the scriptures.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रं च दर्शश् च पौर्णमासश् च पूर्व-वत् । चातुर्मास्यानि च मुनेर् आम्नातानि च नैगमैः
verses.transliteration: agnihotraṃ ca darśaś ca paurṇamāsaś ca pūrva-vat / cāturmāsyāni ca muner āmnātāni ca naigamaiḥ
verses.translation: As he did before [when he was a grihastha] he should perform the fire sacrifice, the sacrifice on a new moon day and a full moon day, as also the four months' sacrifice [of cturmsya], that are enjoined by the Vedic experts.

verses.sanskrit: एवं चीर्णेन तपसा मुनिर् धमनि-सन्ततः । मां तपो-मयम् आराध्य ऋषि-लोकाद् उपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ cīrṇena tapasā munir dhamani-santataḥ / māṃ tapo-mayam ārādhya ṛṣi-lokād upaiti mām
verses.translation: When he with that ascetic practice has worshiped Me, the sage [the vnaprastha] all skin and bones, from the world of the seers will achieve Me, the Goal of All Penance [see also maharloka].

verses.sanskrit: यस् त्व् एतत् कृच्छ्रतश् चीर्णं तपो निःश्रेयसं महत् । कामायाल्पीयसे युञ्ज्याद् बालिशः को ऽपरस् ततः
verses.transliteration: yas tv etat kṛcchrataś cīrṇaṃ tapo niḥśreyasaṃ mahat / kāmāyālpīyase yuñjyād bāliśaḥ ko 'paras tataḥ
verses.translation: Is there a greater fool than someone who, for a long time being of this difficult but glorious penance which awards liberation, practices this penance for the purpose of trivial sense gratification [see also vnts']?

verses.sanskrit: यदासौ नियमे ऽकल्पो जरया जात-वेपथुः । आत्मन्य् अग्नीन् समारोप्य मच्-चित्तो ऽग्निं समाविशेत्
verses.transliteration: yadāsau niyame 'kalpo jarayā jāta-vepathuḥ / ātmany agnīn samāropya mac-citto 'gniṃ samāviśet
verses.translation: When he in his regulated activities due to old age with his body trembling is no longer able to carry on, he should place the sacrificial fire in his heart, concentrate on Me and enter the fire [see also 7.12: 23].

verses.sanskrit: यदा कर्म-विपाकेषु लोकेषु निरयात्मसु । विरागो जायते सम्यङ् न्यस्ताग्निः प्रव्रजेत् ततः
verses.transliteration: yadā karma-vipākeṣu lokeṣu nirayātmasu / virāgo jāyate samyaṅ nyastāgniḥ pravrajet tataḥ
verses.translation: But in case full detachment from all result-minded action has developed in him and [the achievement of] a higher world means nothing more to him than hell, he may give up the [vnaprastha] sacrificial fire and take to the renounced order [see also B.G. 18: 2 and **].

verses.sanskrit: इष्ट्वा यथोपदेशं मां दत्त्वा सर्व-स्वम् ऋत्विजे । अग्नीन् स्व-प्राण आवेश्य निरपेक्षः परिव्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: iṣṭvā yathopadeśaṃ māṃ dattvā sarva-svam ṛtvije / agnīn sva-prāṇa āveśya nirapekṣaḥ parivrajet
verses.translation: After having worshiped Me according to the injunctions and having given all he has to the priest, he [the vnaprastha] must place the sacrificial fire within his life breath and free from desires and worries wander about [as a sannys, see also 9.6*].

verses.sanskrit: विप्रस्य वै सन्न्यसतो देवा दारादि-रूपिणः । विघ्नान् कुर्वन्त्य् अयं ह्य् अस्मान् आक्रम्य समियात् परम्
verses.transliteration: viprasya vai sannyasato devā dārādi-rūpiṇaḥ / vighnān kurvanty ayaṃ hy asmān ākramya samiyāt param
verses.translation: The demigods to begin with create obstacles for him in the form of his wife [and other allurements later on]; these he should surpass and transcend [see also B.G. 6: 25, 1.19: 2-3, 5.6: 4, 11.4: 7].

verses.sanskrit: बिभृयाच् चेन् मुनिर् वासः कौपीनाच्छादनं परम् । त्यक्तं न दण्ड-पात्राभ्याम् अन्यत् किञ्चिद् अनापदि
verses.transliteration: bibhṛyāc cen munir vāsaḥ kaupīnācchādanaṃ param / tyaktaṃ na daṇḍa-pātrābhyām anyat kiñcid anāpadi
verses.translation: If a sage wishes to wear clothes he uses another cloth to cover his loincloth [or kaupna]. All the rest he gives up, besides a water pot and a staff [accepting] nothing else without necessity.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्टि-पूतं न्यसेत् पादं वस्त्र-पूतं पिबेज् जलम् । सत्य-पूतां वदेद् वाचं मनः-पूतं समाचरेत्
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭi-pūtaṃ nyaset pādaṃ vastra-pūtaṃ pibej jalam / satya-pūtāṃ vaded vācaṃ manaḥ-pūtaṃ samācaret
verses.translation: He should place his foot on a clear place [free from living beings], clear the water he wants to drink with his cloth, speak the truth in clear terms and engage in action with a clear mind.

verses.sanskrit: मौनानीहानिलायामा दण्डा वाग्-देह-चेतसाम् । न ह्य् एते यस्य सन्त्य् अङ्ग वेणुभिर् न भवेद् यतिः
verses.transliteration: maunānīhānilāyāmā daṇḍā vāg-deha-cetasām / na hy ete yasya santy aṅga veṇubhir na bhaved yatiḥ
verses.translation: Saying little, reserve and breath control constitute the strict disciplines for the voice, the body and the mind. He with whom these are not found My dearest, can, despite his bamboo rods, never be considered a real sannys [see also tridanda].

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षां चतुर्षु वर्णेषु विगर्ह्यान् वर्जयंश् चरेत् । सप्तागारान् असङ्कॢप्तांस् तुष्येल् लब्धेन तावता
verses.transliteration: bhikṣāṃ caturṣu varṇeṣu vigarhyān varjayaṃś caret / saptāgārān asaṅkḷptāṃs tuṣyel labdhena tāvatā
verses.translation: When he goes begging with the four varnas he should at random approach seven different houses, avoid impure [sinful, polluted] households and be satisfied with whatever he acquired [see also cakra, compare 1.4: 8].

verses.sanskrit: बहिर् जलाशयं गत्वा तत्रोपस्पृश्य वाग्-यतः । विभज्य पावितं शेषं भुञ्जीताशेषम् आहृतम्
verses.transliteration: bahir jalāśayaṃ gatvā tatropaspṛśya vāg-yataḥ / vibhajya pāvitaṃ śeṣaṃ bhuñjītāśeṣam āhṛtam
verses.translation: He next somewhere outside has to go to a reservoir of water, wash himself and then without saying a word distribute the food that was begged and purified. Thereafter he should eat the food that remained in its entirety.

verses.sanskrit: एकश् चरेन् महीम् एतां निःसङ्गः संयतेन्द्रियः । आत्म-क्रीड आत्म-रत आत्म-वान् सम-दर्शनः
verses.transliteration: ekaś caren mahīm etāṃ niḥsaṅgaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / ātma-krīḍa ātma-rata ātma-vān sama-darśanaḥ
verses.translation: He should move about alone on this earth free from attachment, with his senses fully under control, and, satisfied and amused with the True Self, steady on the spiritual platform, be of an equal vision [B.G. 5: 18, see bhajan Ohe! Vaishnava Thhkura].

verses.sanskrit: विविक्त-क्षेम-शरणो मद्-भाव-विमलाशयः । आत्मानं चिन्तयेद् एकम् अभेदेन मया मुनिः
verses.transliteration: vivikta-kṣema-śaraṇo mad-bhāva-vimalāśayaḥ / ātmānaṃ cintayed ekam abhedena mayā muniḥ
verses.translation: In a remote and safe place the sage, with his consciousness purified by his love for Me, should concentrate on the soul only as being non-different from Me.

verses.sanskrit: अन्वीक्षेतात्मनो बन्धं मोक्षं च ज्ञान-निष्ठया । बन्ध इन्द्रिय-विक्षेपो मोक्ष एषां च संयमः
verses.transliteration: anvīkṣetātmano bandhaṃ mokṣaṃ ca jñāna-niṣṭhayā / bandha indriya-vikṣepo mokṣa eṣāṃ ca saṃyamaḥ
verses.translation: By focussing in knowledge he must gain insight in the bound and liberated state of the self. He is bound when the senses are distracted and he is liberated when he has them under control [when he - devoted to Me - concentrates, meditates and gets absorbed, see also 11.10].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान् नियम्य षड्-वर्गं मद्-भावेन चरेन् मुनिः । विरक्तः क्षुद्र-कामेभ्यो लब्ध्वात्मनि सुखं महत्
verses.transliteration: tasmān niyamya ṣaḍ-vargaṃ mad-bhāvena caren muniḥ / viraktaḥ kṣudra-kāmebhyo labdhvātmani sukhaṃ mahat
verses.translation: The sage with his six senses [the five physical senses and the mind] fully under control being conscious of Me, having experienced the greater happiness of the soul, therefore should live detached from futile material desires.

verses.sanskrit: पुर-ग्राम-व्रजान् सार्थान् भिक्षार्थं प्रविशंश् चरेत् । पुण्य-देश-सरिच्-छैल- वनाश्रम-वतीं महीम्
verses.transliteration: pura-grāma-vrajān sārthān bhikṣārthaṃ praviśaṃś caret / puṇya-deśa-saric-chaila- vanāśrama-vatīṃ mahīm
verses.translation: He should travel to the pure places on earth and travel to the pure places on earth with rivers, mountains and forests. The cities, towns and pasturing grounds he should enter only to beg for alms from those working for material purposes.

verses.sanskrit: वानप्रस्थाश्रम-पदेष्व् अभीक्ष्णं भैक्ष्यम् आचरेत् । संसिध्यत्य् आश्व् असम्मोहः शुद्ध-सत्त्वः शिलान्धसा
verses.transliteration: vānaprasthāśrama-padeṣv abhīkṣṇaṃ bhaikṣyam ācaret / saṃsidhyaty āśv asammohaḥ śuddha-sattvaḥ śilāndhasā
verses.translation: He in the withdrawn position of life should always go for alms, for by food [thus] obtained by begging [and not by performing fruitfu l labor, karma], he is freed from illusion and quickly achieves spiritual perfection.

verses.sanskrit: नैतद् वस्तुतया पश्येद् दृश्यमानं विनश्यति । असक्त-चित्तो विरमेद् इहामुत्र-चिकीर्षितात्
verses.transliteration: naitad vastutayā paśyed dṛśyamānaṃ vinaśyati / asakta-citto viramed ihāmutra-cikīrṣitāt
verses.translation: He should never take the perishable things he sees with his eyes for the ultimate reality; with a consciousness free from attachment he should turn away from all designs for [material progress in] this life or a future existence.

verses.sanskrit: यद् एतद् आत्मनि जगन् मनो-वाक्-प्राण-संहतम् । सर्वं मायेति तर्केण स्व-स्थस् त्यक्त्वा न तत् स्मरेत्
verses.transliteration: yad etad ātmani jagan mano-vāk-prāṇa-saṃhatam / sarvaṃ māyeti tarkeṇa sva-sthas tyaktvā na tat smaret
verses.translation: Fixed in his [true] self he should no longer keep the universe in mind and thus arguing [as previously] forsake all that illusory energy that in the self binds together mind, speech and vital air [see ahankra].

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञान-निष्ठो विरक्तो वा मद्-भक्तो वानपेक्षकः । स-लिङ्गान् आश्रमांस् त्यक्त्वा चरेद् अविधि-गोचरः
verses.transliteration: jñāna-niṣṭho virakto vā mad-bhakto vānapekṣakaḥ / sa-liṅgān āśramāṃs tyaktvā cared avidhi-gocaraḥ
verses.translation: Whether he in detachment is devoted to knowledge or as My devotee not even desires liberation, he [ultimately] should abandon the s'rama duties and their rituals and move beyond the range of rules and regulations [see also 10.78: 31-32, 3.29: 25 and 5.1*].

verses.sanskrit: बुधो बालक-वत् क्रीडेत् कुशलो जड-वच् चरेत् । वदेद् उन्मत्त-वद् विद्वान् गो-चर्यां नैगमश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: budho bālaka-vat krīḍet kuśalo jaḍa-vac caret / vaded unmatta-vad vidvān go-caryāṃ naigamaś caret
verses.translation: Despite his intelligence he [the detached soul, a sannys] should play like a child, despite his expertise he should act as if incompetent, despite his learning he should speak like being absent-minded and despite his knowing the injunctions he should behave in an unrestricted manner ['wander as a cow'].

verses.sanskrit: वेद-वाद-रतो न स्यान् न पाषण्डी न हैतुकः । शुष्क-वाद-विवादे न कञ्चित् पक्षं समाश्रयेत्
verses.transliteration: veda-vāda-rato na syān na pāṣaṇḍī na haitukaḥ / śuṣka-vāda-vivāde na kañcit pakṣaṃ samāśrayet
verses.translation: He should never attach to the [karma-kanda] Vedic philosophy [of sacrificing for the sake of return], nor should he heretically oppose it; he should refrain from scepticism and empty talk, nor take sides in [political] arguments.

verses.sanskrit: नोद्विजेत जनाद् धीरो जनं चोद्वेजयेन् न तु । अति-वादांस् तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कञ्चन । देहम् उद्दिश्य पशु-वद् वैरं कुर्यान् न केनचित्
verses.transliteration: nodvijeta janād dhīro janaṃ codvejayen na tu / ati-vādāṃs titikṣeta nāvamanyeta kañcana / deham uddiśya paśu-vad vairaṃ kuryān na kenacit
verses.translation: Someone wise should never feel disturbed by other people nor should he disturb others. He should tolerate harsh words, never treat anyone with disrespect and never - like an animal to the interest of the body - with anyone create hostility [see also B.G. 12: 15].

verses.sanskrit: एक एव परो ह्य् आत्मा भूतेष्व् आत्मन्य् अवस्थितः । यथेन्दुर् उद-पात्रेषु भूतान्य् एकात्मकानि च
verses.transliteration: eka eva paro hy ātmā bhūteṣv ātmany avasthitaḥ / yathendur uda-pātreṣu bhūtāny ekātmakāni ca
verses.translation: The One Supreme Soul is situated within all living beings as also within one's own body. Just like the one moon is reflected in different reservoirs of water, all material bodies are composed of the energy of the One [Lord, see also B.G. 6: 29 & 13: 34].

verses.sanskrit: अलब्ध्वा न विषीदेत काले काले ऽशनं क्वचित् । लब्ध्वा न हृष्येद् धृतिमान् उभयं दैव-तन्त्रितम्
verses.transliteration: alabdhvā na viṣīdeta kāle kāle 'śanaṃ kvacit / labdhvā na hṛṣyed dhṛtimān ubhayaṃ daiva-tantritam
verses.translation: He [the sannys] should not feel dejected when there is no [or no proper] food, nor should he rejoice at times when there is plenty of food; he should be firmly fixed in the notion that both matters [of plenty and scarcity] are determined by fate [by the Lord].

verses.sanskrit: आहारार्थं समीहेत युक्तं तत्-प्राण-धारणम् । तत्त्वं विमृश्यते तेन तद् विज्ञाय विमुच्यते
verses.transliteration: āhārārthaṃ samīheta yuktaṃ tat-prāṇa-dhāraṇam / tattvaṃ vimṛśyate tena tad vijñāya vimucyate
verses.translation: He should endeavor to eat and sustain his personal life force properly, for by that strength he contemplates the spiritual truth that, being understood, leads to liberation [see B.G. 6: 16].

verses.sanskrit: यदृच्छयोपपन्नान्नम् अद्याच् छ्रेष्ठम् उतापरम् । तथा वासस् तथा शय्यां प्राप्तं प्राप्तं भजेन् मुनिः
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayopapannānnam adyāc chreṣṭham utāparam / tathā vāsas tathā śayyāṃ prāptaṃ prāptaṃ bhajen muniḥ
verses.translation: The food he happened to obtain he must eat, whether it is first class or of a low quality. Similarly the clothes and place to sleep must by a sage be accepted the way they came of their own accord [see also 7.13].

verses.sanskrit: शौचम् आचमनं स्नानं न तु चोदनया चरेत् । अन्यांश् च नियमाञ् ज्ञानी यथाहं लीलयेश्वरः
verses.transliteration: śaucam ācamanaṃ snānaṃ na tu codanayā caret / anyāṃś ca niyamāñ jñānī yathāhaṃ līlayeśvaraḥ
verses.translation: General cleanliness, washing the hands, taking a bath and other regular duties are to be performed without any compulsion by a person of spiritual realization, just as [free as] I, the Controller, act according to the game I play.

verses.sanskrit: न हि तस्य विकल्पाख्या या च मद्-वीक्षया हता । आ-देहान्तात् क्वचित् ख्यातिस् ततः सम्पद्यते मया
verses.transliteration: na hi tasya vikalpākhyā yā ca mad-vīkṣayā hatā / ā-dehāntāt kvacit khyātis tataḥ sampadyate mayā
verses.translation: For him the perception of things [as existing] separate from Me is over, for having Me in view such a perception is destroyed. Sometimes such a notion lingers till the body dies, but he will attain Me then.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखोदर्केषु कामेषु जात-निर्वेद आत्मवान् । अज्ज्ञासित-मद्-धर्मो मुनिं गुरुम् उपव्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: duḥkhodarkeṣu kāmeṣu jāta-nirveda ātmavān / ajjñāsita-mad-dharmo muniṃ gurum upavrajet
verses.translation: He who has no notion of My dharma, but in his desire for spiritual perfection developed detachment from the lusty sense enjoyment that leads to unhappiness, should approach a wise [bonafide] spiritual master [of proper reference, a guru, see also B.G. 16: 23-24, 4: 34 & 17: 14].
