verses.sanskrit: तावत् परिचरेद् भक्तः श्रद्धावान् अनसूयकः । यावद् ब्रह्म विजानीयान् माम् एव गुरुम् आदृतः
verses.transliteration: tāvat paricared bhaktaḥ śraddhāvān anasūyakaḥ / yāvad brahma vijānīyān mām eva gurum ādṛtaḥ
verses.translation: The devotee should serve the spiritual master who embodies Me, with great faith without envy and offenses for as long as it takes to arrive at a clear realization of the Absolute Truth [see also 11.17: 27].

verses.sanskrit: यस् त्व् असंयत-षड्-वर्गः प्रचण्डेन्द्रिय-सारथिः । ज्ञान-वैराग्य-रहितस् त्रि-दण्डम् उपजीवति
verses.transliteration: yas tv asaṃyata-ṣaḍ-vargaḥ pracaṇḍendriya-sārathiḥ / jñāna-vairāgya-rahitas tri-daṇḍam upajīvati
verses.translation: He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of knowledge and detachment, and just wants to subsist on [and not learn from] the three-stick staff, denies Me, himself and the divine personalities within himself and harms the dharma; not having overcome the contamination of this world he will also be lost and abandoned in the world hereafter.

verses.sanskrit: सुरान् आत्मानम् आत्म-स्थं निह्नुते मां च धर्म-हा । अविपक्व-कषायो ऽस्माद् अमुष्माच् च विहीयते
verses.transliteration: surān ātmānam ātma-sthaṃ nihnute māṃ ca dharma-hā / avipakva-kaṣāyo 'smād amuṣmāc ca vihīyate
verses.translation: He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of knowledge and detachment, and just wants to subsist on [and not learn from] the three-stick staff, denies Me, himself and the divine personalities within himself and harms the dharma; not having overcome the contamination of this world he will also be lost and abandoned in the world hereafter.

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षोर् धर्मः शमो ऽहिंसा तप ईक्षा वनौकसः । गृहिणो भूत-रक्षेज्या द्विजस्याचार्य-सेवनम्
verses.transliteration: bhikṣor dharmaḥ śamo 'hiṃsā tapa īkṣā vanaukasaḥ / gṛhiṇo bhūta-rakṣejyā dvijasyācārya-sevanam
verses.translation: Nonviolence and equanimity constitutes the dharma of a mendicant [a sannys], discrimination [between body and soul] and penance constitutes the dharma of someone living in the forest [a vnaprastha], protecting all living beings and performing sacrifices constitutes the dharma of a householder [a grihastha], and serving the teacher of example [the crya] constitutes the dharma of a celibate student [a brahmacr].

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मचर्यं तपः शौचं सन्तोषो भूत-सौहृदम् । गृहस्थस्याप्य् ऋतौ गन्तुः सर्वेषां मद्-उपासनम्
verses.transliteration: brahmacaryaṃ tapaḥ śaucaṃ santoṣo bhūta-sauhṛdam / gṛhasthasyāpy ṛtau gantuḥ sarveṣāṃ mad-upāsanam
verses.translation: Of all those who worship Me with celibacy, austerity, cleanliness, contentment and being friendly towards all living entities, it is the householder who may approach his wife for intercourse at the proper time.

verses.sanskrit: इति मां यः स्व-धर्मेण भजेन् नित्यम् अनन्य-भाक् । सर्व-भूतेषु मद्-भावो मद्-भक्तिं विन्दते दृढाम्
verses.transliteration: iti māṃ yaḥ sva-dharmeṇa bhajen nityam ananya-bhāk / sarva-bhūteṣu mad-bhāvo mad-bhaktiṃ vindate dṛḍhām
verses.translation: Someone who thus according to his dharma worships Me, who is not devoted to anything else and understands Me to be present in all living entities, will obtain unflinching devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्योद्धवानपायिन्या सर्व-लोक-महेश्वरम् । सर्वोत्पत्त्य्-अप्ययं ब्रह्म कारणं मोपयाति सः
verses.transliteration: bhaktyoddhavānapāyinyā sarva-loka-maheśvaram / sarvotpatty-apyayaṃ brahma kāraṇaṃ mopayāti saḥ
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, he will come to Me by bhakti, by loving service unto Me, the Supreme Lord of All Worlds, the Absolute Truth and cause, the origin and dissolution of everything.

verses.sanskrit: इति स्व-धर्म-निर्णिक्त- सत्त्वो निर्ज्ञात-मद्-गतिः । ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सम्पन्नो न चिरात् समुपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: iti sva-dharma-nirṇikta- sattvo nirjñāta-mad-gatiḥ / jñāna-vijñāna-sampanno na cirāt samupaiti mām
verses.translation: When one thus by one's dharma has purified one's existence, fully understands My supreme position and is endowed with spiritual knowledge and wisdom, one will very soon reach Me.

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रमवतां धर्म एष आचार-लक्षणः । स एव मद्-भक्ति-युतो निःश्रेयस-करः परः
verses.transliteration: varṇāśramavatāṃ dharma eṣa ācāra-lakṣaṇaḥ / sa eva mad-bhakti-yuto niḥśreyasa-karaḥ paraḥ
verses.translation: The followers of the varns'rama dharma are characterized by this [traditional] code of conduct. This sense of duty combined with my bhakti awards the highest perfection of life.

verses.sanskrit: एतत् ते ऽभिहितं साधो भवान् पृच्छति यच् च माम् । यथा स्व-धर्म-संयुक्तो भक्तो मां समियात् परम्
verses.transliteration: etat te 'bhihitaṃ sādho bhavān pṛcchati yac ca mām / yathā sva-dharma-saṃyukto bhakto māṃ samiyāt param
verses.translation: Oh saintly soul, this what I described to you on your request, constitutes the means by which one as a devotee can be perfectly engaged in one's duty, and thus come to Me, the One Supreme.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच यो विद्या-श्रुत-सम्पन्नः आत्मवान् नानुमानिकः । मया-मात्रम् इदं ज्ञात्वा ज्ञानं च मयि सन्न्यसेत्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yo vidyā-śruta-sampannaḥ ātmavān nānumānikaḥ / mayā-mātram idaṃ jñātvā jñānaṃ ca mayi sannyaset
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Someone who, endowed with knowledge in accord with the oral tradition, is of self-realization and does not lose himself in speculations [about what would be true knowledge], must, known with the fact that this universe is a complete illusion, for both the sake of Me and spiritual knowledge renounce his worldly concerns.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानिनस् त्व् अहम् एवेष्टः स्वार्थो हेतुश् च सम्मतः । स्वर्गश् चैवापवर्गश् च नान्यो ऽर्थो मद्-ऋते प्रियः
verses.transliteration: jñāninas tv aham eveṣṭaḥ svārtho hetuś ca sammataḥ / svargaś caivāpavargaś ca nānyo 'rtho mad-ṛte priyaḥ
verses.translation: For a person of spiritual knowledge I am the object of worship, the goal, the motive and final conclusion. Besides Me as his favorite, his light and final beatitude, he has no other purpose.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञान-विज्ञान-संसिद्धाः पदं श्रेष्ठं विदुर् मम । ज्ञानी प्रियतमो ऽतो मे ज्ञानेनासौ बिभर्ति माम्
verses.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-saṃsiddhāḥ padaṃ śreṣṭhaṃ vidur mama / jñānī priyatamo 'to me jñānenāsau bibharti mām
verses.translation: They who have fully perfected their knowledge and wisdom know My lotus feet as most auspicious. For that reason the learned transcendentalist who by means of spiritual knowledge holds on to Me, is most dear to Me [see also B.G. 7: 17-18].

verses.sanskrit: तपस् तीर्थं जपो दानं पवित्राणीतराणि च । नालं कुर्वन्ति तां सिद्धिं या ज्ञान-कलया कृता
verses.transliteration: tapas tīrthaṃ japo dānaṃ pavitrāṇītarāṇi ca / nālaṃ kurvanti tāṃ siddhiṃ yā jñāna-kalayā kṛtā
verses.translation: Performing austerities, visiting holy places, doing japa, giving charity or other pious activities cannot provide the perfection that is created by but a fraction of this spiritual knowledge [compare 10.46: 32-33].

verses.sanskrit: तस्माज् ज्ञानेन सहितं ज्ञात्वा स्वात्मानम् उद्धव । ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सम्पन्नो भज मां भक्ति-भावतः
verses.transliteration: tasmāj jñānena sahitaṃ jñātvā svātmānam uddhava / jñāna-vijñāna-sampanno bhaja māṃ bhakti-bhāvataḥ
verses.translation: Therefore, oh Uddhava, knowing your true self in association with this spiritual discrimination that you accomplished by intelligence and consciousness, be of your personal way [svarpa] of serving Me with devotion.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञान-विज्ञान-यज्ञेन माम् इष्ट्वात्मानम् आत्मनि । सर्व-यज्ञ-पतिं मां वै संसिद्धिं मुनयो ऽगमन्
verses.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-yajñena mām iṣṭvātmānam ātmani / sarva-yajña-patiṃ māṃ vai saṃsiddhiṃ munayo 'gaman
verses.translation: With the sacrifice of Vedic knowledge and wisdom, the sages achieved the highest perfection by worshiping Me, the Lord of All Sacrifices who is the Supersoul inside of them.

verses.sanskrit: त्वय्य् उद्धवाश्रयति यस् त्रि-विधो विकारो मायान्तरापतति नाद्य्-अपवर्गयोर् यत् जन्मादयो ऽस्य यद् अमी तव तस्य किं स्युर् आद्य्-अन्तयोर् यद् असतो ऽस्ति तद् एव मध्ये
verses.transliteration: tvayy uddhavāśrayati yas tri-vidho vikāro $ māyāntarāpatati nādy-apavargayor yat &janmādayo 'sya yad amī tava tasya kiṃ syur % ādy-antayor yad asato 'sti tad eva madhye
verses.translation: The transformation of the three departments [of nature, the gunas] that surrounds you, oh Uddhava, constitutes an illusion that appears in the present [of the bewildering material energy] that is not there in the beginning nor in the end [of the universe]. In what sense can the being born and such of your material body, that at first did not exist, is not there afterwards, but only exists in between, have any relation to you[r essence, your true self, your soul]?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच ज्ञानं विशुद्धं विपुलं यथैतद् वैराग्य-विज्ञान-युतं पुराणम् । आख्याहि विश्वेश्वर विश्व-मूर्ते त्वद्-भक्ति-योगं च महद्-विमृग्यम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca jñānaṃ viśuddhaṃ vipulaṃ yathaitad vairāgya-vijñāna-yutaṃ purāṇam / ākhyāhi viśveśvara viśva-mūrte tvad-bhakti-yogaṃ ca mahad-vimṛgyam
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Controller of the Universe, oh You in the Form of the Universe, please explain the yoga of devotion unto You that is also sought by the greatest souls. This devotion encompasses the extensive, perfectly pure knowledge endowed with detachment and wisdom, that carries the approval of the established tradition [of storytelling].

verses.sanskrit: ताप-त्रयेणाभिहतस्य घोरे सन्तप्यमानस्य भवाध्वनीश । पश्यामि नान्यच् छरणं तवाङ्घ्रि- द्वन्द्वातपत्राद् अमृताभिवर्षात्
verses.transliteration: tāpa-trayeṇābhihatasya ghore santapyamānasya bhavādhvanīśa / paśyāmi nānyac charaṇaṃ tavāṅghri- dvandvātapatrād amṛtābhivarṣāt
verses.translation: Oh Lord, for the one who, tormented on the path of material existence, is overwhelmed by the threefold misery [see 1.17: 19], I see no other shelter but the canopy of Your two lotus feet that shower the nectar.

verses.sanskrit: दष्टं जनं सम्पतितं बिले ऽस्मिन् कालाहिना क्षुद्र-सुखोरु-तर्षम् । समुद्धरैनं कृपयापवर्ग्यैर् वचोभिर् आसिञ्च महानुभाव
verses.transliteration: daṣṭaṃ janaṃ sampatitaṃ bile 'smin kālāhinā kṣudra-sukhoru-tarṣam / samuddharainaṃ kṛpayāpavargyair vacobhir āsiñca mahānubhāva
verses.translation: Please uplift this person who so badly craving for some insignificant happiness, bitten by the snake of time, hopelessly fell down in this dark pit [of material existence]. Oh Greatest Authority, pour out Your words of mercy that lead to liberation!'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच इत्थम् एतत् पुरा राजा भीष्मं धर्म-भृतां वरम् । अजात-शत्रुः पप्रच्छ सर्वेषां नो ऽनुशृण्वताम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca ittham etat purā rājā bhīṣmaṃ dharma-bhṛtāṃ varam / ajāta-śatruḥ papraccha sarveṣāṃ no 'nuśṛṇvatām
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: '[Yudhishthhira,] the king who considered no one his enemy, in the past asked Bhshma, the best of the upholders of dharma, this question while we were all attentively listening [see 1.9: 25-42].

verses.sanskrit: निवृत्ते भारते युद्धे सुहृन्-निधन-विह्वलः । श्रुत्वा धर्मान् बहून् पश्चान् मोक्ष-धर्मान् अपृच्छत
verses.transliteration: nivṛtte bhārate yuddhe suhṛn-nidhana-vihvalaḥ / śrutvā dharmān bahūn paścān mokṣa-dharmān apṛcchata
verses.translation: When the war between the descendants of Bharata had ended, he, overwhelmed by the destruction of his beloved friends and relatives, after having heard about the many principles of religion, finally asked about the path of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तान् अहं ते ऽभिधास्यामि देव-व्रत-मखाच् छ्रुतान् । ज्ञान-वैराग्य-विज्ञान- श्रद्धा-भक्त्य्-उपबृंहितान्
verses.transliteration: tān ahaṃ te 'bhidhāsyāmi deva-vrata-makhāc chrutān / jñāna-vairāgya-vijñāna- śraddhā-bhakty-upabṛṃhitān
verses.translation: I shall describe to you what was heard from the mouth of that soul vowed to the divine [Bhshma] in support of higher knowledge, detachment, self-realization, faith and devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: नवैकादश पञ्च त्रीन् भावान् भूतेषु येन वै । ईक्षेताथाइकम् अप्य् एषु तज् ज्ञानं मम निश्चितम्
verses.transliteration: navaikādaśa pañca trīn bhāvān bhūteṣu yena vai / īkṣetāthāikam apy eṣu taj jñānaṃ mama niścitam
verses.translation: That knowledge by which one recognizes the one element [of the Absolute Spirit, the Supersoul, the Lord, see 1.2: 11] within the nine, eleven, five and three elements of all living beings, has been determined by Me to be higher knowledge [jna].

verses.sanskrit: एतद् एव हि विज्ञानं न तथैकेन येन यत् । स्थित्य्-उत्पत्त्य्-अप्ययान् पश्येद् भावानां त्रि-गुणात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: etad eva hi vijñānaṃ na tathaikena yena yat / sthity-utpatty-apyayān paśyed bhāvānāṃ tri-guṇātmanām
verses.translation: When one is not in that sense concerned with all the elements composed of the three basic qualities, but rather sees, as should, the One who brings about, maintains and annihilates this universe, one at that time speaks of self-realization [vijna].

verses.sanskrit: आदाव् अन्ते च मध्ये च सृज्यात् सृज्यं यद् अन्वियात् । पुनस् तत्-प्रतिसङ्क्रामे यच् छिष्येत तद् एव सत्
verses.transliteration: ādāv ante ca madhye ca sṛjyāt sṛjyaṃ yad anviyāt / punas tat-pratisaṅkrāme yac chiṣyeta tad eva sat
verses.translation: That what is there in the beginning, is halfway present from one creation to another, and still remains when there is the annihilation of everything, is what one considers true and eternal.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतिः प्रत्यक्षम् ऐतिह्यम् अनुमानं चतुष्टयम् । प्रमाणेष्व् अनवस्थानाद् विकल्पात् स विरज्यते
verses.transliteration: śrutiḥ pratyakṣam aitihyam anumānaṃ catuṣṭayam / pramāṇeṣv anavasthānād vikalpāt sa virajyate
verses.translation: Vedic knowledge [s'ruti], direct experience [pratyaksha], traditional instruction [aitihya or smriti] and logical inference [anumna] are the four forms of correct perception in facing the flickering nature by which a person masters [becomes detached from] the material diversity [see pramna].

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणां परिणामित्वाद् आ-विरिञ्च्याद् अमङ्गलम् । विपश्चिन् नश्वरं पश्येद् अदृष्टम् अपि दृष्ट-वत्
verses.transliteration: karmaṇāṃ pariṇāmitvād ā-viriñcyād amaṅgalam / vipaścin naśvaraṃ paśyed adṛṣṭam api dṛṣṭa-vat
verses.translation: Inauspiciousness is found up to the heaven of Virica [Brahm] because all material routines [including rituals] are subject to change. An intelligent person should understand that, just like the things he sees, everything he is about to see is transitory [see also shad-rmi, 11.3: 20 and B.G. 8: 16].

verses.sanskrit: भक्ति-योगः पुरैवोक्तः प्रीयमाणाय ते ऽनघ । पुनश् च कथयिष्यामि मद्-भक्तेः कारणं परं
verses.transliteration: bhakti-yogaḥ puraivoktaḥ prīyamāṇāya te 'nagha / punaś ca kathayiṣyāmi mad-bhakteḥ kāraṇaṃ paraṃ
verses.translation: Because of your love for Me I formerly talked to you about bhakti-yoga, oh sinless one. Let Me now also expound on the supreme process of devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धामृत-कथायां मे शश्वन् मद्-अनुकीर्तनम् । परिनिष्ठा च पूजायां स्तुतिभिः स्तवनं मम
verses.transliteration: śraddhāmṛta-kathāyāṃ me śaśvan mad-anukīrtanam / pariniṣṭhā ca pūjāyāṃ stutibhiḥ stavanaṃ mama
verses.translation: Faith in the nectar of the narrations about Me, always recite [sing] My glories, to be fixed on ceremonial worship, to relate to Me with hymns and prayers; to have great respect for My devotional service, to offer obeisances with all of one's limbs, to be of first-class worship for My devotees, to think of Me as being present in all living beings, to dedicate all one's normal activities to Me, to describe with words My qualities, to offer one's mind to Me and reject all material desires; to give up wealth for My sake, to renounce sensual pleasures and material happiness, to be of sacrifice with charity and offerings, to do japa and to keep to vows and austerities for My purpose, are for human beings of surrender, oh Uddhava, the different forms of religiosity from which arises loving service unto Me - what other purpose would remain for My devotee [to be accomplished]?

verses.sanskrit: आदरः परिचर्यायां सर्वाङ्गैर् अभिवन्दनम् । मद्-भक्त-पूजाभ्यधिका सर्व-भूतेषु मन्-मतिः
verses.transliteration: ādaraḥ paricaryāyāṃ sarvāṅgair abhivandanam / mad-bhakta-pūjābhyadhikā sarva-bhūteṣu man-matiḥ
verses.translation: Faith in the nectar of the narrations about Me, always recite [sing] My glories, to be fixed on ceremonial worship, to relate to Me with hymns and prayers; to have great respect for My devotional service, to offer obeisances with all of one's limbs, to be of first-class worship for My devotees, to think of Me as being present in all living beings, to dedicate all one's normal activities to Me, to describe with words My qualities, to offer one's mind to Me and reject all material desires; to give up wealth for My sake, to renounce sensual pleasures and material happiness, to be of sacrifice with charity and offerings, to do japa and to keep to vows and austerities for My purpose, are for human beings of surrender, oh Uddhava, the different forms of religiosity from which arises loving service unto Me - what other purpose would remain for My devotee [to be accomplished]?

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्थेष्व् अङ्ग-चेष्टा च वचसा मद्-गुणेरणम् । मय्य् अर्पणं च मनसः सर्व-काम-विवर्जनम्
verses.transliteration: mad-artheṣv aṅga-ceṣṭā ca vacasā mad-guṇeraṇam / mayy arpaṇaṃ ca manasaḥ sarva-kāma-vivarjanam
verses.translation: Faith in the nectar of the narrations about Me, always recite [sing] My glories, to be fixed on ceremonial worship, to relate to Me with hymns and prayers; to have great respect for My devotional service, to offer obeisances with all of one's limbs, to be of first-class worship for My devotees, to think of Me as being present in all living beings, to dedicate all one's normal activities to Me, to describe with words My qualities, to offer one's mind to Me and reject all material desires; to give up wealth for My sake, to renounce sensual pleasures and material happiness, to be of sacrifice with charity and offerings, to do japa and to keep to vows and austerities for My purpose, are for human beings of surrender, oh Uddhava, the different forms of religiosity from which arises loving service unto Me - what other purpose would remain for My devotee [to be accomplished]?

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्थे ऽर्थ-परित्यागो भोगस्य च सुखस्य च । इष्टं दत्तं हुतं जप्तं मद्-अर्थं यद् व्रतं तपः
verses.transliteration: mad-arthe 'rtha-parityāgo bhogasya ca sukhasya ca / iṣṭaṃ dattaṃ hutaṃ japtaṃ mad-arthaṃ yad vrataṃ tapaḥ
verses.translation: Faith in the nectar of the narrations about Me, always recite [sing] My glories, to be fixed on ceremonial worship, to relate to Me with hymns and prayers; to have great respect for My devotional service, to offer obeisances with all of one's limbs, to be of first-class worship for My devotees, to think of Me as being present in all living beings, to dedicate all one's normal activities to Me, to describe with words My qualities, to offer one's mind to Me and reject all material desires; to give up wealth for My sake, to renounce sensual pleasures and material happiness, to be of sacrifice with charity and offerings, to do japa and to keep to vows and austerities for My purpose, are for human beings of surrender, oh Uddhava, the different forms of religiosity from which arises loving service unto Me - what other purpose would remain for My devotee [to be accomplished]?

verses.sanskrit: एवं धर्मैर् मनुष्याणाम् उद्धवात्म-निवेदिनाम् । मयि सञ्जायते भक्तिः को ऽन्यो ऽर्थो ऽस्यावशिष्यते
verses.transliteration: evaṃ dharmair manuṣyāṇām uddhavātma-nivedinām / mayi sañjāyate bhaktiḥ ko 'nyo 'rtho 'syāvaśiṣyate
verses.translation: Faith in the nectar of the narrations about Me, always recite [sing] My glories, to be fixed on ceremonial worship, to relate to Me with hymns and prayers; to have great respect for My devotional service, to offer obeisances with all of one's limbs, to be of first-class worship for My devotees, to think of Me as being present in all living beings, to dedicate all one's normal activities to Me, to describe with words My qualities, to offer one's mind to Me and reject all material desires; to give up wealth for My sake, to renounce sensual pleasures and material happiness, to be of sacrifice with charity and offerings, to do japa and to keep to vows and austerities for My purpose, are for human beings of surrender, oh Uddhava, the different forms of religiosity from which arises loving service unto Me - what other purpose would remain for My devotee [to be accomplished]?

verses.sanskrit: यदात्मन्य् अर्पितं चित्तं शान्तं सत्त्वोपबृंहितम् । धर्मं ज्ञानं स वैराग्यम् ऐश्वर्यं चाभिपद्यते
verses.transliteration: yadātmany arpitaṃ cittaṃ śāntaṃ sattvopabṛṃhitam / dharmaṃ jñānaṃ sa vairāgyam aiśvaryaṃ cābhipadyate
verses.translation: When situated in peace one's consciousness is absorbed in the [supreme] soul, one achieves, being strengthened by the mode of goodness, religiosity, spiritual knowledge, detachment and opulence.

verses.sanskrit: यद् अर्पितं तद् विकल्पे इन्द्रियैः परिधावति । रजस्-वलं चासन्-निष्ठं चित्तं विद्धि विपर्ययम्
verses.transliteration: yad arpitaṃ tad vikalpe indriyaiḥ paridhāvati / rajas-valaṃ cāsan-niṣṭhaṃ cittaṃ viddhi viparyayam
verses.translation: But when one, being fixed on the material variety, with one's senses chases in every direction and thus becomes a prisoner of one's passion, you should understand that one, by that [materialistic] consciousness dedicated to the impermanent, will achieve the opposite.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मो मद्-भक्ति-कृत् प्रोक्तो ज्ञानं चैकात्म्य-दर्शनम् । गुणेस्व् असङ्गो वैराग्यम् ऐश्वर्यं चाणिमादयः
verses.transliteration: dharmo mad-bhakti-kṛt prokto jñānaṃ caikātmya-darśanam / guṇesv asaṅgo vairāgyam aiśvaryaṃ cāṇimādayaḥ
verses.translation: Dharma is said to lead to My devotional service and spiritual knowledge [jna] to witnessing the presence of the Supreme Soul. Detachment [vairagya] results in disinterestedness in the objects of sense gratification and opulence [ais'varya] culminates in the mystical perfections of anim and so on [perfections and powers, see 11.15 & 11.16 and bhaga].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यमः कति-विधः प्रोक्तो । नियमो वारि-कर्षण कः शमः को दमः कृष्ण
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yamaḥ kati-vidhaḥ prokto / niyamo vāri-karṣaṇa kaḥ śamaḥ ko damaḥ kṛṣṇa
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How many types of restrictions [yama] and regular duties [niyama] does one speak of, oh Subduer of the Enemy, what is equilibrium, what is self-control, dear Kṛṣṇa, what is tolerance and what is constancy, my Lord? What is charity and what is penance, what is heroism, what does one say about reality and truth, what is renunciation and wealth, what is desirable, what a sacrifice and what is religious remuneration? What do you think is the strength of a person, oh Fortunate One, what is opulence and gain, oh Kes'ava, what is education, what is modesty, what is superior, what is beauty and what is happiness as also unhappiness? Who is learned, who is a fool, what is the real path and what is the false path, what is heaven and what is hell, and who do You say is a friend and what is home? Who is wealthy, who is poor, who is a wretch and who is a controller; please speak to me about these matters as also about the opposite qualities, oh Lord of the Truthful Souls.'

verses.sanskrit: का तितिक्षा धृतिः प्रभो किं दानं किं तपः शौर्यं । किम् सत्यम् ऋतम् उच्यते कस् त्यागः किं धनं चेष्टं
verses.transliteration: kā titikṣā dhṛtiḥ prabho kiṃ dānaṃ kiṃ tapaḥ śauryaṃ / kim satyam ṛtam ucyate kas tyāgaḥ kiṃ dhanaṃ ceṣṭaṃ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How many types of restrictions [yama] and regular duties [niyama] does one speak of, oh Subduer of the Enemy, what is equilibrium, what is self-control, dear Kṛṣṇa, what is tolerance and what is constancy, my Lord? What is charity and what is penance, what is heroism, what does one say about reality and truth, what is renunciation and wealth, what is desirable, what a sacrifice and what is religious remuneration? What do you think is the strength of a person, oh Fortunate One, what is opulence and gain, oh Kes'ava, what is education, what is modesty, what is superior, what is beauty and what is happiness as also unhappiness? Who is learned, who is a fool, what is the real path and what is the false path, what is heaven and what is hell, and who do You say is a friend and what is home? Who is wealthy, who is poor, who is a wretch and who is a controller; please speak to me about these matters as also about the opposite qualities, oh Lord of the Truthful Souls.'

verses.sanskrit: को यज्ञः का च दक्षिणा पुंसः किं स्विद् बलं श्रीमन् । भगो लाभश् च केशव का विद्या ह्रीः परा का श्रीः
verses.transliteration: ko yajñaḥ kā ca dakṣiṇā puṃsaḥ kiṃ svid balaṃ śrīman / bhago lābhaś ca keśava kā vidyā hrīḥ parā kā śrīḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How many types of restrictions [yama] and regular duties [niyama] does one speak of, oh Subduer of the Enemy, what is equilibrium, what is self-control, dear Kṛṣṇa, what is tolerance and what is constancy, my Lord? What is charity and what is penance, what is heroism, what does one say about reality and truth, what is renunciation and wealth, what is desirable, what a sacrifice and what is religious remuneration? What do you think is the strength of a person, oh Fortunate One, what is opulence and gain, oh Kes'ava, what is education, what is modesty, what is superior, what is beauty and what is happiness as also unhappiness? Who is learned, who is a fool, what is the real path and what is the false path, what is heaven and what is hell, and who do You say is a friend and what is home? Who is wealthy, who is poor, who is a wretch and who is a controller; please speak to me about these matters as also about the opposite qualities, oh Lord of the Truthful Souls.'

verses.sanskrit: किं सुखं दुःखम् एव च कः पण्डितः कश् च मूर्खः । कः पन्था उत्पथश् च कः कः स्वर्गो नरकः कः स्वित्
verses.transliteration: kiṃ sukhaṃ duḥkham eva ca kaḥ paṇḍitaḥ kaś ca mūrkhaḥ / kaḥ panthā utpathaś ca kaḥ kaḥ svargo narakaḥ kaḥ svit
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How many types of restrictions [yama] and regular duties [niyama] does one speak of, oh Subduer of the Enemy, what is equilibrium, what is self-control, dear Kṛṣṇa, what is tolerance and what is constancy, my Lord? What is charity and what is penance, what is heroism, what does one say about reality and truth, what is renunciation and wealth, what is desirable, what a sacrifice and what is religious remuneration? What do you think is the strength of a person, oh Fortunate One, what is opulence and gain, oh Kes'ava, what is education, what is modesty, what is superior, what is beauty and what is happiness as also unhappiness? Who is learned, who is a fool, what is the real path and what is the false path, what is heaven and what is hell, and who do You say is a friend and what is home? Who is wealthy, who is poor, who is a wretch and who is a controller; please speak to me about these matters as also about the opposite qualities, oh Lord of the Truthful Souls.'

verses.sanskrit: को बन्धुर् उत किं गृहम् क आढ्यः को दरिद्रो वा । कृपणः कः क ईश्वरः एतान् प्रश्नान् मम ब्रूहि । विपरीतांश् च सत्-पते श्री-भगवान् उवाच
verses.transliteration: ko bandhur uta kiṃ gṛham ka āḍhyaḥ ko daridro vā / kṛpaṇaḥ kaḥ ka īśvaraḥ etān praśnān mama brūhi / viparītāṃś ca sat-pate śrī-bhagavān uvāca
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How many types of restrictions [yama] and regular duties [niyama] does one speak of, oh Subduer of the Enemy, what is equilibrium, what is self-control, dear Kṛṣṇa, what is tolerance and what is constancy, my Lord? What is charity and what is penance, what is heroism, what does one say about reality and truth, what is renunciation and wealth, what is desirable, what a sacrifice and what is religious remuneration? What do you think is the strength of a person, oh Fortunate One, what is opulence and gain, oh Kes'ava, what is education, what is modesty, what is superior, what is beauty and what is happiness as also unhappiness? Who is learned, who is a fool, what is the real path and what is the false path, what is heaven and what is hell, and who do You say is a friend and what is home? Who is wealthy, who is poor, who is a wretch and who is a controller; please speak to me about these matters as also about the opposite qualities, oh Lord of the Truthful Souls.'

verses.sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यम् अस्तेयम् असङ्गो ह्रीर् असञ्चयः । आस्तिक्यं ब्रह्मचर्यं च मौनं स्थैर्यं क्षमाभयम्
verses.transliteration: ahiṃsā satyam asteyam asaṅgo hrīr asañcayaḥ / āstikyaṃ brahmacaryaṃ ca maunaṃ sthairyaṃ kṣamābhayam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Nonviolence, truthfulness, not coveting or stealing the property of others, detachment, modesty, non-possessiveness, trustfulness, celibacy, silence, steadiness, forgiveness and fearlessness are the twelve types of restrictions [of yama]. [Inner and outer] cleanliness, doing japa, penance, sacrifice, faith, hospitality, worship of Me, visiting holy places, care for the welfare of others in this world, contentment and serving the spiritual master, are the twelve regular duties [of niyama]. These two times twelve types of principles and duties that human beings remember and honor, My dear one, yield results [like beatitude or prosperity] depending each his desire [*].

verses.sanskrit: शौचं जपस् तपो होमः श्रद्धातिथ्यं मद्-अर्चनम् । तीर्थाटनं परार्थेहा तुष्टिर् आचार्य-सेवनम्
verses.transliteration: śaucaṃ japas tapo homaḥ śraddhātithyaṃ mad-arcanam / tīrthāṭanaṃ parārthehā tuṣṭir ācārya-sevanam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Nonviolence, truthfulness, not coveting or stealing the property of others, detachment, modesty, non-possessiveness, trustfulness, celibacy, silence, steadiness, forgiveness and fearlessness are the twelve types of restrictions [of yama]. [Inner and outer] cleanliness, doing japa, penance, sacrifice, faith, hospitality, worship of Me, visiting holy places, care for the welfare of others in this world, contentment and serving the spiritual master, are the twelve regular duties [of niyama]. These two times twelve types of principles and duties that human beings remember and honor, My dear one, yield results [like beatitude or prosperity] depending each his desire [*].

verses.sanskrit: एते यमाः स-नियमा उभयोर् द्वादश स्मृताः । पुंसाम् उपासितास् तात यथा-कामं दुहन्ति हि
verses.transliteration: ete yamāḥ sa-niyamā ubhayor dvādaśa smṛtāḥ / puṃsām upāsitās tāta yathā-kāmaṃ duhanti hi
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Nonviolence, truthfulness, not coveting or stealing the property of others, detachment, modesty, non-possessiveness, trustfulness, celibacy, silence, steadiness, forgiveness and fearlessness are the twelve types of restrictions [of yama]. [Inner and outer] cleanliness, doing japa, penance, sacrifice, faith, hospitality, worship of Me, visiting holy places, care for the welfare of others in this world, contentment and serving the spiritual master, are the twelve regular duties [of niyama]. These two times twelve types of principles and duties that human beings remember and honor, My dear one, yield results [like beatitude or prosperity] depending each his desire [*].

verses.sanskrit: शमो मन्-निष्ठता बुद्धेर् दम इन्द्रिय-संयमः । तितिक्षा दुःख-सम्मर्षो जिह्वोपस्थ-जयो धृतिः
verses.transliteration: śamo man-niṣṭhatā buddher dama indriya-saṃyamaḥ / titikṣā duḥkha-sammarṣo jihvopastha-jayo dhṛtiḥ
verses.translation: Mental equilibrium means the absorption of one's intelligence in Me [see also 11.16: 10] and self-control entails the disciplining of the senses. Tolerance means that one has to endure unhappiness and constancy entails the conquering of the tongue and genitals. The highest charity is to forsake the rod [to punish others] and giving up one's lust one thinks of penance. One is a hero when one conquers one's propensity to enjoy materially and there is sense of reality when one sees [the One, the Lord] equally present. Next [the truth] is found in the veritable and pleasing words as proclaimed by the sages. One speaks of cleanliness when one is detached from performing productive labor [see also e.g. 1.1: 2 and B.G. 18: 6] and in case of renunciation one speaks of sannysa [the renounced order]. Religiousness constitutes for human beings the wealth to be desired, and I Myself, the Most Fortunate One, the Supreme Personality, am the sacrifice. The spiritual reward follows upon wise instruction and the control of one's breath constitutes supreme strength.

verses.sanskrit: दण्ड-न्यासः परं दानं काम-त्यागस् तपः स्मृतम् । स्वभाव-विजयः शौर्यं सत्यं च सम-दर्शनम्
verses.transliteration: daṇḍa-nyāsaḥ paraṃ dānaṃ kāma-tyāgas tapaḥ smṛtam / svabhāva-vijayaḥ śauryaṃ satyaṃ ca sama-darśanam
verses.translation: Mental equilibrium means the absorption of one's intelligence in Me [see also 11.16: 10] and self-control entails the disciplining of the senses. Tolerance means that one has to endure unhappiness and constancy entails the conquering of the tongue and genitals. The highest charity is to forsake the rod [to punish others] and giving up one's lust one thinks of penance. One is a hero when one conquers one's propensity to enjoy materially and there is sense of reality when one sees [the One, the Lord] equally present. Next [the truth] is found in the veritable and pleasing words as proclaimed by the sages. One speaks of cleanliness when one is detached from performing productive labor [see also e.g. 1.1: 2 and B.G. 18: 6] and in case of renunciation one speaks of sannysa [the renounced order]. Religiousness constitutes for human beings the wealth to be desired, and I Myself, the Most Fortunate One, the Supreme Personality, am the sacrifice. The spiritual reward follows upon wise instruction and the control of one's breath constitutes supreme strength.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यच् च सुनृता वाणी कविभिः परिकीर्तिता । कर्मस्व् असङ्गमः शौचं त्यागः सन्न्यास उच्यते
verses.transliteration: anyac ca sunṛtā vāṇī kavibhiḥ parikīrtitā / karmasv asaṅgamaḥ śaucaṃ tyāgaḥ sannyāsa ucyate
verses.translation: Mental equilibrium means the absorption of one's intelligence in Me [see also 11.16: 10] and self-control entails the disciplining of the senses. Tolerance means that one has to endure unhappiness and constancy entails the conquering of the tongue and genitals. The highest charity is to forsake the rod [to punish others] and giving up one's lust one thinks of penance. One is a hero when one conquers one's propensity to enjoy materially and there is sense of reality when one sees [the One, the Lord] equally present. Next [the truth] is found in the veritable and pleasing words as proclaimed by the sages. One speaks of cleanliness when one is detached from performing productive labor [see also e.g. 1.1: 2 and B.G. 18: 6] and in case of renunciation one speaks of sannysa [the renounced order]. Religiousness constitutes for human beings the wealth to be desired, and I Myself, the Most Fortunate One, the Supreme Personality, am the sacrifice. The spiritual reward follows upon wise instruction and the control of one's breath constitutes supreme strength.

verses.sanskrit: धर्म इष्टं धनं नॄणां यज्ञो ऽहं भगवत्तमः । दक्षिणा ज्ञान-सन्देशः प्राणायामः परं बलम्
verses.transliteration: dharma iṣṭaṃ dhanaṃ nṝṇāṃ yajño 'haṃ bhagavattamaḥ / dakṣiṇā jñāna-sandeśaḥ prāṇāyāmaḥ paraṃ balam
verses.translation: Mental equilibrium means the absorption of one's intelligence in Me [see also 11.16: 10] and self-control entails the disciplining of the senses. Tolerance means that one has to endure unhappiness and constancy entails the conquering of the tongue and genitals. The highest charity is to forsake the rod [to punish others] and giving up one's lust one thinks of penance. One is a hero when one conquers one's propensity to enjoy materially and there is sense of reality when one sees [the One, the Lord] equally present. Next [the truth] is found in the veritable and pleasing words as proclaimed by the sages. One speaks of cleanliness when one is detached from performing productive labor [see also e.g. 1.1: 2 and B.G. 18: 6] and in case of renunciation one speaks of sannysa [the renounced order]. Religiousness constitutes for human beings the wealth to be desired, and I Myself, the Most Fortunate One, the Supreme Personality, am the sacrifice. The spiritual reward follows upon wise instruction and the control of one's breath constitutes supreme strength.

verses.sanskrit: भगो म ऐश्वरो भावो लाभो मद्-भक्तिर् उत्तमः । विद्यात्मनि भिदा-बाधो जुगुप्सा ह्रीर् अकर्मसु
verses.transliteration: bhago ma aiśvaro bhāvo lābho mad-bhaktir uttamaḥ / vidyātmani bhidā-bādho jugupsā hrīr akarmasu
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: श्रीर् गुणा नैरपेक्ष्याद्याः सुखं दुःख-सुखात्ययः । दुःखं काम-सुखापेक्षा पण्डितो बन्ध-मोक्ष-वित्
verses.transliteration: śrīr guṇā nairapekṣyādyāḥ sukhaṃ duḥkha-sukhātyayaḥ / duḥkhaṃ kāma-sukhāpekṣā paṇḍito bandha-mokṣa-vit
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: मूर्खो देहाद्य्-अहं-बुद्धिः पन्था मन्-निगमः स्मृतः । उत्पथश् चित्त-विक्षेपः स्वर्गः सत्त्व-गुणोदयः
verses.transliteration: mūrkho dehādy-ahaṃ-buddhiḥ panthā man-nigamaḥ smṛtaḥ / utpathaś citta-vikṣepaḥ svargaḥ sattva-guṇodayaḥ
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: नरकस् तम-उन्नाहो बन्धुर् गुरुर् अहं सखे । गृहं शरीरं मानुष्यं गुणाढ्यो ह्य् आढ्य उच्यते
verses.transliteration: narakas tama-unnāho bandhur gurur ahaṃ sakhe / gṛhaṃ śarīraṃ mānuṣyaṃ guṇāḍhyo hy āḍhya ucyate
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: दरिद्रो यस् त्व् असन्तुष्टः कृपणो यो ऽजितेन्द्रियः । गुणेष्व् असक्त-धीर् ईशो गुण-सङ्गो विपर्ययः
verses.transliteration: daridro yas tv asantuṣṭaḥ kṛpaṇo yo 'jitendriyaḥ / guṇeṣv asakta-dhīr īśo guṇa-saṅgo viparyayaḥ
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: एत उद्धव ते प्रश्नाः सर्वे साधु निरूपिताः । किं वर्णितेन बहुना लक्षणं गुण-दोषयोः । गुण-दोष-दृशिर् दोषो गुणस् तूभय-वर्जितः
verses.transliteration: eta uddhava te praśnāḥ sarve sādhu nirūpitāḥ / kiṃ varṇitena bahunā lakṣaṇaṃ guṇa-doṣayoḥ / guṇa-doṣa-dṛśir doṣo guṇas tūbhaya-varjitaḥ
verses.translation: Opulence is My divine nature [see 11.16 and bhaga], gain is My bhakti, education is the nullifying of the division of the self [see siddhnta and advaita] and modesty [sense of shame] is the disgust against improper action [sin]. Beauty means to be of good qualities - like being detached from material desires and such, happiness means to transcend both [material] happiness and distress, unhappiness is to rely on the [physical] happiness of lust and a wise person is someone who can tell the difference between liberation and bondage. A fool is someone who identifies himself with the body and so on [the mind etc.], the right path is the one leading to Me, the wrong path is to be understood as the one leading to a bewildered mind, and heaven implies the predominance of the mode of goodness. Hell is the domination by the mode of ignorance, one's real friend is the spiritual master who is Me, My dear friend, and the human body is one's home. He who is enriched with good qualities is called a rich person while a poor person is someone who is discontented. A wretched person is someone who did not subdue his senses, a controller is someone whose intelligence is not attached to the material affair, and someone attached to sense gratification is of the opposite [kind of qualities, a slave]. These, oh Uddhava, are the subjects of your inquiry I all properly have elucidated. But why should one elaborately describe the characteristics of good and bad qualities when thinking about good and bad still means that one fails to see the [true] quality [compare with 3.10: 28-29, 6.16: 10-11, 11.7: 8, 11.11: 16 and B.G. 7: 5].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच विधिश् च प्रतिषेधश् च निगमो हीश्वरस्य ते । अवेक्षते ऽरविण्डाक्ष गुणं दोषं च कर्मणाम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vidhiś ca pratiṣedhaś ca nigamo hīśvarasya te / avekṣate 'raviṇḍākṣa guṇaṃ doṣaṃ ca karmaṇām
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'The Vedic literature of Your Lordship, oh Lotus-eyed One, that pays attention to the injunctions concerning actions and prohibitions, deals about the good and bad sides of karma [akarma and vikarma].

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रम-विकल्पं च प्रतिलोमानुलोमजम् । द्रव्य-देश-वयः-कालान् स्वर्गं नरकम् एव च
verses.transliteration: varṇāśrama-vikalpaṃ ca pratilomānulomajam / dravya-deśa-vayaḥ-kālān svargaṃ narakam eva ca
verses.translation: They also discuss the differences within the varns'rama system wherein the father may be of a higher [anuloma] or a lower [pratiloma] class than the mother, they are about heaven and hell and expound on the subjects of having possessions, one's age, place and time [see also 4.8: 54 and *].

verses.sanskrit: गुण-दोष-भिदा-दृष्टिम् अन्तरेण वचस् तव । निःश्रेयसं कथं नॄणां निषेध-विधि-लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: guṇa-doṣa-bhidā-dṛṣṭim antareṇa vacas tava / niḥśreyasaṃ kathaṃ nṝṇāṃ niṣedha-vidhi-lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: How can human beings without Your prohibitive and regulatory words concerning final beatitude, tell the difference between virtue and vice [compare 11.19: 40-45]?

verses.sanskrit: पितृ-देव-मनुष्यानां वेदश् चक्षुस् तवेश्वर । श्रेयस् त्व् अनुपलब्धे ऽर्थे साध्य-साधनयोर् अपि
verses.transliteration: pitṛ-deva-manuṣyānāṃ vedaś cakṣus taveśvara / śreyas tv anupalabdhe 'rthe sādhya-sādhanayor api
verses.translation: The Vedic knowledge emanating from You offers the forefathers, the gods as also the human beings a superior eye upon the - not for everyone that evident - meaning of life, what would be the goal, and how we may achieve.

verses.sanskrit: गुण-दोष-भिदा-दृष्टिर् निगमात् ते न हि स्वतः । निगमेनापवादश् च भिदाया इति ह भ्रमः
verses.transliteration: guṇa-doṣa-bhidā-dṛṣṭir nigamāt te na hi svataḥ / nigamenāpavādaś ca bhidāyā iti ha bhramaḥ
verses.translation: The difference between virtue and vice one can see with the help of Your Vedic knowledge and that insight does not arise by itself, but the Vedas also nullify such a difference and thus clearly confuse the issue....'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच योगास् त्रयो मया प्रोक्ता नॄणां श्रेयो-विधित्सया । ज्ञानं कर्म च भक्तिश् च नोपायो ऽन्यो ऽस्ति कुत्रचित्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yogās trayo mayā proktā nṝṇāṃ śreyo-vidhitsayā / jñānaṃ karma ca bhaktiś ca nopāyo 'nyo 'sti kutracit
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The three ways of yoga I described in My desire to grant human beings the perfection, are the path of philosophy [jna], the path of work [karma] and the path of devotion [bhakti]; no other means can be found [for one's emancipation. See also B.G. contents and triknda].

verses.sanskrit: निर्विण्णानां ज्ञान-योगो न्यासिनाम् इह कर्मसु । तेष्व् अनिर्विण्ण-चित्तानां कर्म-योगस् तु कामिनाम्
verses.transliteration: nirviṇṇānāṃ jñāna-yogo nyāsinām iha karmasu / teṣv anirviṇṇa-cittānāṃ karma-yogas tu kāminām
verses.translation: For those who disgusted with fruitful labor [or sacrifices] forsake such activities, there is the yoga of spiritual knowledge and for those who have not turned themselves consciously away and do feel for material happiness, there is the path of karma-yoga.

verses.sanskrit: यदृच्छया मत्-कथादौ जात-श्रद्धस् तु यः पुमान् । न निर्विण्णो नाति-सक्तो भक्ति-योगो ऽस्य सिद्धि-दः
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayā mat-kathādau jāta-śraddhas tu yaḥ pumān / na nirviṇṇo nāti-sakto bhakti-yogo 'sya siddhi-daḥ
verses.translation: When it happens that in a person, who is neither averse nor very attached, faith awakens in My narrations and such, the path of bhakti-yoga will bring perfection.

verses.sanskrit: तावत् कर्माणि कुर्वीत न निर्विद्येत यावता । मत्-कथा-श्रवणादौ वा श्रद्धा यावन् न जायते
verses.transliteration: tāvat karmāṇi kurvīta na nirvidyeta yāvatā / mat-kathā-śravaṇādau vā śraddhā yāvan na jāyate
verses.translation: For as long as one is not fed up [with one's material life] and no faith has awakened in My discourses and listening etc. [7.5: 23-24], one will have to continue with one's fruitful labor [see also 1.2: 7, 11.5: 41].

verses.sanskrit: स्व-धर्म-स्थो यजन् यज्ञैर् अनाशीः-काम उद्धव । न याति स्वर्ग-नरकौ यद्य् अन्यन् न समाचरेत्
verses.transliteration: sva-dharma-stho yajan yajñair anāśīḥ-kāma uddhava / na yāti svarga-narakau yady anyan na samācaret
verses.translation: Someone will not go to heaven nor to hell, oh Uddhava, if he holds on to his prescribed duties without deviating and, free from ulterior motives, is of worship with performing sacrifices [see also B.G. 8: 16].

verses.sanskrit: अस्मिंल् लोके वर्तमानः स्व-धर्म-स्थो ऽनघः शुचिः । ज्ञानं विशुद्धम् आप्नोति मद्-भक्तिं वा यदृच्छया
verses.transliteration: asmiṃl loke vartamānaḥ sva-dharma-stho 'naghaḥ śuciḥ / jñānaṃ viśuddham āpnoti mad-bhaktiṃ vā yadṛcchayā
verses.translation: When one, existing in this world, free from sin is situated in one's duties and is pure [in one's motives], one obtains transcendental knowledge and eventually My bhakti [compare 1.5: 23-31].

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गिणो ऽप्य् एतम् इच्छन्ति लोकं निरयिणस् तथा । साधकं ज्ञान-भक्तिभ्याम् उभयं तद्-असाधकम्
verses.transliteration: svargiṇo 'py etam icchanti lokaṃ nirayiṇas tathā / sādhakaṃ jñāna-bhaktibhyām ubhayaṃ tad-asādhakam
verses.translation: Just as the residents of hell, even the residents of heaven desire for this planet earth that is so conducive to the spiritual knowledge and devotional service, effective neither in hell nor in heaven.

verses.sanskrit: न नरः स्वर्-गतिं काङ्क्षेन् नारकीं वा विचक्षणः । नेमं लोकं च काङ्क्षेत देहावेशात् प्रमाद्यति
verses.transliteration: na naraḥ svar-gatiṃ kāṅkṣen nārakīṃ vā vicakṣaṇaḥ / nemaṃ lokaṃ ca kāṅkṣeta dehāveśāt pramādyati
verses.translation: A wise human being should not strive for hell or heaven, nor for this planet earth, because one gets bewildered when one enters a physical body.

verses.sanskrit: एतद् विद्वान् पुरा मृत्योर् अभवाय घटेत सः । अप्रमत्त इदं ज्ञात्वा मर्त्यम् अप्य् अर्थ-सिद्धि-दम्
verses.transliteration: etad vidvān purā mṛtyor abhavāya ghaṭeta saḥ / apramatta idaṃ jñātvā martyam apy artha-siddhi-dam
verses.translation: Even though [the body is] subject to death, it brings the perfection of one's life purpose within reach. Someone who knows this should [therefore], before he dies, attentively endeavor for transcendence.

verses.sanskrit: छिद्यमानं यमैर् एतैः कृत-नीडं वनस्पतिम् । खगः स्व-केतम् उत्सृज्य क्षेमं याति ह्य् अलम्पटः
verses.transliteration: chidyamānaṃ yamair etaiḥ kṛta-nīḍaṃ vanaspatim / khagaḥ sva-ketam utsṛjya kṣemaṃ yāti hy alampaṭaḥ
verses.translation: Not being attached a bird attains happiness after giving up the nest it made in a tree that was cut down by some messengers of death.

verses.sanskrit: अहो-रात्रैश् छिद्यमानं बुद्ध्वायुर् भय-वेपथुः । मुक्त-सङ्गः परं बुद्ध्वा निरीह उपशाम्यति
verses.transliteration: aho-rātraiś chidyamānaṃ buddhvāyur bhaya-vepathuḥ / mukta-saṅgaḥ paraṃ buddhvā nirīha upaśāmyati
verses.translation: Knowing that with every day and night one's life span is shortened [likewise], one is beset with fear; free from attachment [though] one, with the intelligence of transcendence being without desires, attains perfect peace.

verses.sanskrit: नृ-देहम् आद्यं सु-लभं सु-दुर्लभं प्लवं सु-कल्पं गुरु-कर्णधारम् मयानुकूलेन नभस्वतेरितं पुमान् भवाब्धिं न तरेत् स आत्म-हा
verses.transliteration: nṛ-deham ādyaṃ su-labhaṃ su-durlabhaṃ $ plavaṃ su-kalpaṃ guru-karṇadhāram &mayānukūlena nabhasvateritaṃ % pumān bhavābdhiṃ na taret sa ātma-hā
verses.translation: A person is the killer of his own soul when he, in this well suited boat of the so rarely attained but naturally acquired physical human body, does not cross over the ocean of material existence, being propelled by the favorable winds that I am, and with the spiritual master as the captain.

verses.sanskrit: यदारम्भेषु निर्विण्णो विरक्तः संयतेन्द्रियः । अभ्यासेनात्मनो योगी धारयेद् अचलं मनः
verses.transliteration: yadārambheṣu nirviṇṇo viraktaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / abhyāsenātmano yogī dhārayed acalaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: When a yogi, disgusted with material endeavoring, being detached is in full control of his senses, he has to concentrate his mind to stabilize it with self-discipline.

verses.sanskrit: धार्यमाणं मनो यर्हि भ्राम्यद् अश्व् अनवस्थितम् । अतन्द्रितो ऽनुरोधेन मार्गेणात्म-वशं नयेत्
verses.transliteration: dhāryamāṇaṃ mano yarhi bhrāmyad aśv anavasthitam / atandrito 'nurodhena mārgeṇātma-vaśaṃ nayet
verses.translation: The mind being narrowed down to the spiritual platform should, when suddenly being drawn away, carefully - according to the rules of the game - be brought under the control of the self [see also B.G. 6: 26].

verses.sanskrit: मनो-गतिं न विसृजेज् जित-प्राणो जितेन्द्रियः । सत्त्व-सम्पन्नया बुद्ध्या मन आत्म-वशं नयेत्
verses.transliteration: mano-gatiṃ na visṛjej jita-prāṇo jitendriyaḥ / sattva-sampannayā buddhyā mana ātma-vaśaṃ nayet
verses.translation: When one conquers one's breath and senses, one should not forget what the actual purpose of the mind is. With one's intelligence perfect in goodness, it should always be led back under the supervision of the soul [to be its servant, see B.G. 3: 42].

verses.sanskrit: एष वै परमो योगो मनसः सङ्ग्रहः स्मृतः । हृदय-ज्ञत्वम् अन्विच्छन् दम्यस्येवार्वतो मुहुः
verses.transliteration: eṣa vai paramo yogo manasaḥ saṅgrahaḥ smṛtaḥ / hṛdaya-jñatvam anvicchan damyasyevārvato muhuḥ
verses.translation: This restraining of the mind with the intimate confidentiality with which one also constantly observes a horse that one wants to tame, is what is known as the most supreme practice of yoga [see also B.G. 6: 33-34].

verses.sanskrit: साङ्ख्येन सर्व-भावानां प्रतिलोमानुलोमतः । भवाप्ययाव् अनुध्यायेन् मनो यावत् प्रसीदति
verses.transliteration: sāṅkhyena sarva-bhāvānāṃ pratilomānulomataḥ / bhavāpyayāv anudhyāyen mano yāvat prasīdati
verses.translation: By analyzing how all the different elements [of matter and the fields and principles of spiritual knowledge] cohere and are in conflict, how they originate and how they are lost, the mind should be kept attentive until [spiritually] satisfied.

verses.sanskrit: निर्विण्णस्य विरक्तस्य पुरुषस्योक्त-वेदिनः । मनस् त्यजति दौरात्म्यं चिन्तितस्यानुचिन्तया
verses.transliteration: nirviṇṇasya viraktasya puruṣasyokta-vedinaḥ / manas tyajati daurātmyaṃ cintitasyānucintayā
verses.translation: The mind of someone who, guided by the instructions [of the spiritual master], being disgusted got detached, will by analysis of and meditation on what is perceived, give up its false identification [with matter and the body].

verses.sanskrit: यमादिभिर् योग-पथैर् आन्वीक्षिक्या च विद्यया । ममार्चोपासनाभिर् वा नान्यैर् योग्यं स्मरेन् मनः
verses.transliteration: yamādibhir yoga-pathair ānvīkṣikyā ca vidyayā / mamārcopāsanābhir vā nānyair yogyaṃ smaren manaḥ
verses.translation: The mind must not be focussed on any other practice than on the austerities and procedures of the [eightfold] yoga method, on logical analyses and spiritual knowledge, or on the exercises of respect for My form [thus on triknda, threefold yoga: karma-, jna- or bhakti-yoga].

verses.sanskrit: यदि कुर्यात् प्रमादेन योगी कर्म विगर्हितम् । योगेनैव दहेद् अंहो नान्यत् तत्र कदाचन
verses.transliteration: yadi kuryāt pramādena yogī karma vigarhitam / yogenaiva dahed aṃho nānyat tatra kadācana
verses.translation: When a yogi out of negligence commits a reprehensible deed, he should burn away that sin by means of the yoga method only; never at any time he should try this a different way [compare B.G. 1.5: 17, 4: 19, 9: 30].

verses.sanskrit: स्वे स्वे ऽधिकारे या निष्ठा स गुणः परिकीर्तितः । कर्मणां जात्य्-अशुद्धानाम् अनेन नियमः कृतः । गुण-दोष-विधानेन सङ्गानां त्याजनेच्छया
verses.transliteration: sve sve 'dhikāre yā niṣṭhā sa guṇaḥ parikīrtitaḥ / karmaṇāṃ jāty-aśuddhānām anena niyamaḥ kṛtaḥ / guṇa-doṣa-vidhānena saṅgānāṃ tyājanecchayā
verses.translation: When each is firmly grounded in his own position one speaks of virtue, but because of the nature of karmic activities one is impure in one's engagement. Driven by desire to put an end to those [impurities or destabilizing] attachments, one consequently with this rule of virtue and vice [by Me, then] arrives at [the needed niyama] regulations.

verses.sanskrit: जात-श्रद्धो मत्-कथासु निर्विण्णः सर्व-कर्मसु । वेद दुःखात्मकान् कामान् परित्यागे ऽप्य् अनीश्वरः
verses.transliteration: jāta-śraddho mat-kathāsu nirviṇṇaḥ sarva-karmasu / veda duḥkhātmakān kāmān parityāge 'py anīśvaraḥ
verses.translation: When faith in my narrations and disgust with all karma has awakened in someone, he [the tmnandi bhakta], knows that his lusts are the root of his misery, despite his lack of ability to [fully] renounce them. Even though sometimes being engaged in them, he should remain happy and resolute in that faith worship Me, repenting the sense gratification that makes him unhappy.

verses.sanskrit: ततो भजेत मां प्रीतः श्रद्धालुर् दृढ-निश्चयः । जुषमाणश् च तान् कामान् दुःखोदर्कांश् च गर्हयन्
verses.transliteration: tato bhajeta māṃ prītaḥ śraddhālur dṛḍha-niścayaḥ / juṣamāṇaś ca tān kāmān duḥkhodarkāṃś ca garhayan
verses.translation: When faith in my narrations and disgust with all karma has awakened in someone, he [the tmnandi bhakta], knows that his lusts are the root of his misery, despite his lack of ability to [fully] renounce them. Even though sometimes being engaged in them, he should remain happy and resolute in that faith worship Me, repenting the sense gratification that makes him unhappy.

verses.sanskrit: प्रोक्तेन भक्ति-योगेन भजतो मासकृन् मुनेः । कामा हृदय्या नश्यन्ति सर्वे मयि हृदि स्थिते
verses.transliteration: proktena bhakti-yogena bhajato māsakṛn muneḥ / kāmā hṛdayyā naśyanti sarve mayi hṛdi sthite
verses.translation: All the lusts a devotee has in his heart, are destroyed when his heart is firmly established in Me by constantly worshiping Me in the bhakti-yoga as described [see sthita-praja].

verses.sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदय-ग्रन्थिश् छिद्यन्ते सर्व-संशयाः । क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि मयि दृष्टे ऽखिलात्मनि
verses.transliteration: bhidyate hṛdaya-granthiś chidyante sarva-saṃśayāḥ / kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi mayi dṛṣṭe 'khilātmani
verses.translation: The knots in the heart are cut, all misgivings are in shreds and the chain of fruitful actions ends when one sees Me as the Supreme Soul of All.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान् मद्-भक्ति-युक्तस्य योगिनो वै मद्-आत्मनः । न ज्ञानं न च वैराग्यं प्रायः श्रेयो भवेद् इह
verses.transliteration: tasmān mad-bhakti-yuktasya yogino vai mad-ātmanaḥ / na jñānaṃ na ca vairāgyaṃ prāyaḥ śreyo bhaved iha
verses.translation: For a yogi connected in My devotional service who fixed his mind on Me, for that reason generally neither the path of knowledge nor the path of detachment [from fruitful activities] is the way to become happy in this world.

verses.sanskrit: यत् कर्मभिर् यत् तपसा ज्ञान-वैराग्यतश् च यत् । योगेन दान-धर्मेण श्रेयोभिर् इतरैर् अपि
verses.transliteration: yat karmabhir yat tapasā jñāna-vairāgyataś ca yat / yogena dāna-dharmeṇa śreyobhir itarair api
verses.translation: When one happens to desire heaven, final beatitude or My abode, that what is obtained by fruitful action, penance, the cultivation of knowledge and detachment, that what is achieved by mystical yoga, charity, religious observances, auspicious actions or other methods, will by My devotee easily be achieved in being engaged in My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं मद्-भक्ति-योगेन मद्-भक्तो लभते ऽञ्जसा । स्वर्गापवर्गं मद्-धाम कथञ्चिद् यदि वाञ्छति
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ mad-bhakti-yogena mad-bhakto labhate 'ñjasā / svargāpavargaṃ mad-dhāma kathañcid yadi vāñchati
verses.translation: When one happens to desire heaven, final beatitude or My abode, that what is obtained by fruitful action, penance, the cultivation of knowledge and detachment, that what is achieved by mystical yoga, charity, religious observances, auspicious actions or other methods, will by My devotee easily be achieved in being engaged in My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: न किञ्चित् साधवो धीरा भक्ता ह्य् एकान्तिनो मम । वाञ्छन्त्य् अपि मया दत्तं कैवल्यम् अपुनर्-भवम्
verses.transliteration: na kiñcit sādhavo dhīrā bhaktā hy ekāntino mama / vāñchanty api mayā dattaṃ kaivalyam apunar-bhavam
verses.translation: Saintly persons well versed and skillful, devotees fully dedicated to Me, never desire anything but Me, I who grant them beatitude and freedom from birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: नैरपेक्ष्यं परं प्राहुर् निःश्रेयसम् अनल्पकम् । तस्मान् निराशिषो भक्तिर् निरपेक्षस्य मे भवेत्
verses.transliteration: nairapekṣyaṃ paraṃ prāhur niḥśreyasam analpakam / tasmān nirāśiṣo bhaktir nirapekṣasya me bhavet
verses.translation: It is said that it is best not to desire anything, so that with the one who does not seek any personal gain, that with someone who is fully independent, the bhakti unto Me may arise that grants the greatest happiness [see also 2.3: 10].

verses.sanskrit: न मय्य् एकान्त-भक्तानां गुण-दोषोद्भवा गुणाः । साधूनां सम-चित्तानां बुद्धेः परम् उपेयुषाम्
verses.transliteration: na mayy ekānta-bhaktānāṃ guṇa-doṣodbhavā guṇāḥ / sādhūnāṃ sama-cittānāṃ buddheḥ param upeyuṣām
verses.translation: With Me the unfavorable qualities of the modes of nature [like bewilderment, slowness and restlessness] cannot [again] manifest themselves in pure devotees, because they, free from desire, under all circumstances are stable in their consciousness. They now belong to those who moved beyond that what can be understood with a materially motivated intelligence [see also B.G. 9: 30].

verses.sanskrit: एवम् एतान् मया दिष्टान् अनुतिष्ठन्ति मे पथः । क्षेमं विन्दन्ति मत्-स्थानं यद् ब्रह्म परमं विदुः
verses.transliteration: evam etān mayā diṣṭān anutiṣṭhanti me pathaḥ / kṣemaṃ vindanti mat-sthānaṃ yad brahma paramaṃ viduḥ
verses.translation: Those who follow these methods I have now instructed, achieve the security of My abode in the direct perception of that what is the Absolute Truth.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच य एतान् मत्-पथो हित्वा भक्ति-ज्ञान-क्रियात्मकान् । क्षुद्रान् कामांश् चलैः प्राणैर् जुषन्तः संसरन्ति ते
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca ya etān mat-patho hitvā bhakti-jñāna-kriyātmakān / kṣudrān kāmāṃś calaiḥ prāṇair juṣantaḥ saṃsaranti te
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'They who give up My paths of jna, karma and bhakti, will, in the cultivation of their lusts and fickle senses, keep moving through the cycle of birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: स्वे स्वे ऽधिकारे या निष्ठा स गुणः परिकीर्तितः । विपर्ययस् तु दोषः स्याद् उभयोर् एष निश्चयः
verses.transliteration: sve sve 'dhikāre yā niṣṭhā sa guṇaḥ parikīrtitaḥ / viparyayas tu doṣaḥ syād ubhayor eṣa niścayaḥ
verses.translation: When one manages to be steady in one's position that is called virtue, while the opposite of it is considered vice; this is the conclusion about these two [see also B.G. 2: 16].

verses.sanskrit: शुद्ध्य्-अशुद्धी विधीयेते समानेष्व् अपि वस्तुषु । द्रव्यस्य विचिकित्सार्थं गुण-दोषौ शुभाशुभौ
verses.transliteration: śuddhy-aśuddhī vidhīyete samāneṣv api vastuṣu / dravyasya vicikitsārthaṃ guṇa-doṣau śubhāśubhau
verses.translation: What would be pure or impure concerning the religion, what would be vice or virtue in normal affairs and what would be favorable or unfavorable for one's physical survival, are matters [of good and bad] one must evaluate from the same category of elements, oh sinless one [what is good for the body e.g. is not necessarily good for the religion].

verses.sanskrit: धर्मार्थं व्यवहारार्थं यात्रार्थम् इति चानघ । दर्शितो ऽयं मयाचारो धर्मम् उद्वहतां धुरम्
verses.transliteration: dharmārthaṃ vyavahārārthaṃ yātrārtham iti cānagha / darśito 'yaṃ mayācāro dharmam udvahatāṃ dhuram
verses.translation: This approach of matters I put forward for the sake of those who bear the burden of religious principles.

verses.sanskrit: भूम्य्-अम्ब्व्-अग्न्य्-अनिलाकाशा भूतानां पञ्च-धातवः । आ-ब्रह्म-स्थावरादीनां शारीरा आत्म-संयुताः
verses.transliteration: bhūmy-ambv-agny-anilākāśā bhūtānāṃ pañca-dhātavaḥ / ā-brahma-sthāvarādīnāṃ śārīrā ātma-saṃyutāḥ
verses.translation: Earth, water, fire, air and ether are the five basic elements that, from Lord Brahm down to the nonmoving creatures, constitute the bodies of the living beings who are all connected in the Supreme Soul.

verses.sanskrit: वेदेन नाम-रूपाणि विषमाणि समेष्व् अपि । धातुषूद्धव कल्प्यन्त एतेषां स्वार्थ-सिद्धये
verses.transliteration: vedena nāma-rūpāṇi viṣamāṇi sameṣv api / dhātuṣūddhava kalpyanta eteṣāṃ svārtha-siddhaye
verses.translation: Even though they consist of the same elements and in that sense are equal, the Vedas assign different names and forms to them in service of their self-interest, Uddhava [see varns'rama].

verses.sanskrit: देश-कालादि-भावानां वस्तूनां मम सत्तम । गुण-दोषौ विधीयेते नियमार्थं हि कर्मणाम्
verses.transliteration: deśa-kālādi-bhāvānāṃ vastūnāṃ mama sattama / guṇa-doṣau vidhīyete niyamārthaṃ hi karmaṇām
verses.translation: What would be the right and wrong considerations concerning the time, place, the things and so on, is established by Me with the purpose of restricting materially motivated activities.

verses.sanskrit: अकृष्ण-सारो देशानाम् अब्रह्मण्यो ऽसुचिर् भवेत् । कृष्ण-सारो ऽप्य् असौवीर- कीकटासंस्कृतेरिणम्
verses.transliteration: akṛṣṇa-sāro deśānām abrahmaṇyo 'sucir bhavet / kṛṣṇa-sāro 'py asauvīra- kīkaṭāsaṃskṛteriṇam
verses.translation: Among all places, those places are impure where there is no respect for the brahminical culture and no spotted antelopes can be found, where there are no saintly, cultured men even when there are spotted antelopes, where it is unclean like Kkatha [a place of low-class men, see mleccha and *], places not consecrated and unprepared, and places where the earth is barren.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मण्यो गुणवान् कालो द्रव्यतः स्वत एव वा । यतो निवर्तते कर्म स दोषो ऽकर्मकः स्मृतः
verses.transliteration: karmaṇyo guṇavān kālo dravyataḥ svata eva vā / yato nivartate karma sa doṣo 'karmakaḥ smṛtaḥ
verses.translation: The time that by its nature [solar position, lunar phase] or by its objects [appointment by calendar and sundial] is suitable for performing one's prescribed duties is considered good and the time that impedes the performance of one's duties or is unsuitable [night time e.g. or times of different obligations] is considered bad [see also B.G. 7: 8, 11.20: 26, kla and klaktha **].

verses.sanskrit: द्रव्यस्य शुद्ध्य्-अशुद्धी च द्रव्येण वचनेन च । संस्कारेणाथ कालेन महत्वाल्पतयाथ वा
verses.transliteration: dravyasya śuddhy-aśuddhī ca dravyeṇa vacanena ca / saṃskāreṇātha kālena mahatvālpatayātha vā
verses.translation: The purity or impurity of a thing [or of a substance] is determined [validated] with the help of another thing, in respect of what one says about it, by means of a ritual performance [of purification], in respect of time or according to its relative magnitude [***].

verses.sanskrit: शक्त्याशक्त्याथ वा बुद्ध्या समृद्ध्या च यद् आत्मने । अघं कुर्वन्ति हि यथा देशावस्थानुसारतः
verses.transliteration: śaktyāśaktyātha vā buddhyā samṛddhyā ca yad ātmane / aghaṃ kurvanti hi yathā deśāvasthānusārataḥ
verses.translation: Whether it [- viz. the quality of a thing -] imposes a sinful [or pious] reaction upon a person, depends on that person's power or impotence, intelligence, wealth, condition and place.

verses.sanskrit: धान्य-दार्व्-अस्थि-तन्तूनां रस-तैजस-चर्मणाम् । काल-वाय्व्-अग्नि-मृत्-तोयैः पार्थिवानां युतायुतैः
verses.transliteration: dhānya-dārv-asthi-tantūnāṃ rasa-taijasa-carmaṇām / kāla-vāyv-agni-mṛt-toyaiḥ pārthivānāṃ yutāyutaiḥ
verses.translation: By a combination of time, air, fire, earth and water, or by each of them separately, [matters are purified, like] grains, things made of wood, clay and bone, thread, skins, liquids and things won from fire.

verses.sanskrit: अमेध्य-लिप्तं यद् येन गन्ध-लेपं व्यपोहति । भजते प्रकृतिं तस्य तच् छौचं तावद् इष्यते
verses.transliteration: amedhya-liptaṃ yad yena gandha-lepaṃ vyapohati / bhajate prakṛtiṃ tasya tac chaucaṃ tāvad iṣyate
verses.translation: When something in touch with that what is impure removes a bad smell or dirt and thus restores the original nature of an object, one speaks of purification.

verses.sanskrit: स्नान-दान-तपो-ऽवस्था- वीर्य-संस्कार-कर्मभिः । मत्-स्मृत्या चात्मनः शौचं शुद्धः कर्माचरेद् द्विजः
verses.transliteration: snāna-dāna-tapo-'vasthā- vīrya-saṃskāra-karmabhiḥ / mat-smṛtyā cātmanaḥ śaucaṃ śuddhaḥ karmācared dvijaḥ
verses.translation: A twice-born soul should be pure in his activities with cleansing himself, in remembrance of Me, by bathing, charity and austerity, as also by performing his duties and purificatory rituals according to his age and individual capacity.

verses.sanskrit: मन्त्रस्य च परिज्ञानं कर्म-शुद्धिर् मद्-अर्पणम् । धर्मः सम्पद्यते षड्भिर् अधर्मस् तु विपर्ययः
verses.transliteration: mantrasya ca parijñānaṃ karma-śuddhir mad-arpaṇam / dharmaḥ sampadyate ṣaḍbhir adharmas tu viparyayaḥ
verses.translation: The purification derived from a mantra is a consequence of the correct knowledge about it. The purification by a certain act is the consequence of one's dedication to Me. Dharma [religiosity] prospers by [the purity of] the six factors [as mentioned: the place, the time, the substance, the mantras, the doer and the devotional act], whereas godlessness [adharma] is produced by the contrary.

verses.sanskrit: क्वचिद् गुणो ऽपि दोषः स्याद् दोषो ऽपि विधिना गुणः । गुण-दोषार्थ-नियमस् तद्-भिदाम् एव बाधते
verses.transliteration: kvacid guṇo 'pi doṣaḥ syād doṣo 'pi vidhinā guṇaḥ / guṇa-doṣārtha-niyamas tad-bhidām eva bādhate
verses.translation: Sometimes though, a virtue turns out to be a vice and a vice - by providence [or Vedic instruction] - turns out to be a virtue. Respecting the regulative principles one is thus faced with the fact that the distinction between what is good and bad is factually effaced by them [4*].

verses.sanskrit: समान-कर्माचरणं पतितानां न पातकम् । औत्पत्तिको गुणः सङ्गो न शयानः पतत्य् अधः
verses.transliteration: samāna-karmācaraṇaṃ patitānāṃ na pātakam / autpattiko guṇaḥ saṅgo na śayānaḥ pataty adhaḥ
verses.translation: The same performance of karma because of which one person falls down is not the cause for another to fall down. Someone who fell [in love e.g.] does not fall any further; for such a one natural attachment changes into a virtue.

verses.sanskrit: यतो यतो निवर्तेत विमुच्येत ततस् ततः । एष धर्मो नृणां क्षेमः शोक-मोह-भयापहः
verses.transliteration: yato yato nivarteta vimucyeta tatas tataḥ / eṣa dharmo nṛṇāṃ kṣemaḥ śoka-moha-bhayāpahaḥ
verses.translation: Whatever one desists from one is freed from - this is for human beings the foundation of religious life [natural pious living] that takes away the suffering, fear and delusion.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेषु गुणाध्यासात् पुंसः सङ्गस् ततो भवेत् । सङ्गात् तत्र भवेत् कामः कामाद् एव कलिर् नृणाम्
verses.transliteration: viṣayeṣu guṇādhyāsāt puṃsaḥ saṅgas tato bhavet / saṅgāt tatra bhavet kāmaḥ kāmād eva kalir nṛṇām
verses.translation: When one presumes the objects stimulating the senses to be good, a person will develop attachment as a consequence, from that attachment lust originates and because of that lust [to enjoy at will] there is quarrel among people.

verses.sanskrit: कलेर् दुर्विषहः क्रोधस् तमस् तम् अनुवर्तते । तमसा ग्रस्यते पुंसश् चेतना व्यापिनी द्रुतम्
verses.transliteration: kaler durviṣahaḥ krodhas tamas tam anuvartate / tamasā grasyate puṃsaś cetanā vyāpinī drutam
verses.translation: Because of quarreling there is the anger that is difficult to handle and because of anger there is ignorance; and thus someone's broad consciousness is quickly overtaken by darkness [or narrowed consciousness].

verses.sanskrit: तया विरहितः साधो जन्तुः शून्याय कल्पते । ततो ऽस्य स्वार्थ-विभ्रंशो मूर्च्छितस्य मृतस्य च
verses.transliteration: tayā virahitaḥ sādho jantuḥ śūnyāya kalpate / tato 'sya svārtha-vibhraṃśo mūrcchitasya mṛtasya ca
verses.translation: Oh saintly soul, a living being bereft that way [of clear understanding] becomes empty-headed so that, as a consequence having fallen away from the goals in life, such a person - just like dull matter - is as good as dead [compare B.G. 2: 62-63].

verses.sanskrit: विषयाभिनिवेशेन नात्मानं वेद नापरम् । वृक्ष जीविकया जीवन् व्यर्थं भस्त्रेव यः श्वसन्
verses.transliteration: viṣayābhiniveśena nātmānaṃ veda nāparam / vṛkṣa jīvikayā jīvan vyarthaṃ bhastreva yaḥ śvasan
verses.translation: Adhering to the sensual affair, vainly living the life of a tree, one fails in knowing oneself and others, so that one's breathing is nothing more than pumping air.

verses.sanskrit: फल-श्रुतिर् इयं नॄणां न श्रेयो रोचनं परम् । श्रेयो-विवक्षया प्रोक्तं यथा भैषज्य-रोचनम्
verses.transliteration: phala-śrutir iyaṃ nṝṇāṃ na śreyo rocanaṃ param / śreyo-vivakṣayā proktaṃ yathā bhaiṣajya-rocanam
verses.translation: The awards promised in the [karma-knda part of the] scriptures are for man not the highest good; they are merely enticements to create a taste for the ultimate good [upsana-knda], similar to what one says to make someone take a medicine.

verses.sanskrit: उत्पत्त्यैव हि कामेषु प्राणेषु स्व-जनेषु च । आसक्त-मनसो मर्त्या आत्मनो ऽनर्थ-हेतुषु
verses.transliteration: utpattyaiva hi kāmeṣu prāṇeṣu sva-janeṣu ca / āsakta-manaso martyā ātmano 'nartha-hetuṣu
verses.translation: From the moment they are born, mortals develop a mind of attachment to their family, their vital functions and the objects of their desire, because of which they lose sight of the interest of their soul.

verses.sanskrit: नतान् अविदुषः स्वार्थं भ्राम्यतो वृजिनाध्वनि । कथं युञ्ज्यात् पुनस् तेषु तांस् तमो विशतो बुधः
verses.transliteration: natān aviduṣaḥ svārthaṃ bhrāmyato vṛjinādhvani / kathaṃ yuñjyāt punas teṣu tāṃs tamo viśato budhaḥ
verses.translation: Why would those of intelligence [the Vedic authorities] encourage souls to further engage in those [attachments], because of which they, on the path of danger blind to their real interest, in submission [to karmic actions] land in darkness [see also 5.5: 17]?

verses.sanskrit: एवं व्यवसितं केचिद् अविज्ञाय कुबुद्धयः । फल-श्रुतिं कुसुमितां न वेद-ज्ञा वदन्ति हि
verses.transliteration: evaṃ vyavasitaṃ kecid avijñāya kubuddhayaḥ / phala-śrutiṃ kusumitāṃ na veda-jñā vadanti hi
verses.translation: Some who thus with a perverted intelligence do not understand the purpose [of finding fulfillment in Krishna], speak in [karma-mmms] flowery language about [sacrificing for the sake of] material benefits; something about which he who really knows the Vedas does not speak [see also B.G. 2: 42-44].

verses.sanskrit: कामिनः कृपणा लुब्धाः पुष्पेषु फल-बुद्धयः । अग्नि-मुग्धा धूम-तान्ताः स्वं लोकं न विदन्ति ते
verses.transliteration: kāminaḥ kṛpaṇā lubdhāḥ puṣpeṣu phala-buddhayaḥ / agni-mugdhā dhūma-tāntāḥ svaṃ lokaṃ na vidanti te
verses.translation: Those who are lusty, miserly and greedy take the flowers [of karmic sacrifices] for the fruit [of realization]; bewildered by the fire they suffocate from the smoke and do not realize their position [their true identity of being an individual soul instead of a body].

verses.sanskrit: न ते माम् अङ्ग जानन्ति हृदि-स्थं य इदं यतः । उक्थ-शस्त्रा ह्य् असु-तृपो यथा नीहार-चक्षुषः
verses.transliteration: na te mām aṅga jānanti hṛdi-sthaṃ ya idaṃ yataḥ / uktha-śastrā hy asu-tṛpo yathā nīhāra-cakṣuṣaḥ
verses.translation: Armed with their expressions, My dear, they do not know Me who is seated within their heart and from whom this universe generated that is also Me. In their self-indulgence they are like people staring into fog.

verses.sanskrit: ते मे मतम् अविज्ञाय परोक्षं विषयात्मकाः । हिंसायां यदि रागः स्याद् यज्ञ एव न चोदना
