verses.transliteration: te me matam avijñāya parokṣaṃ viṣayātmakāḥ / hiṃsāyāṃ yadi rāgaḥ syād yajña eva na codanā
verses.translation: Not understanding My confidential conclusion [see also 10.87 and B.G. 9] they, absorbed in their sensuality, [as meat eaters] are attached to the violence [against animals] that may occur under conditions [in nature], but certainly never is encouraged for sacrifices. In reality they take pleasure in being violent against the animals that [without necessity] were slaughtered for their sense gratification. With their ritual worship of the gods, the forefathers and the leading spirits, they are mischievous people.

verses.sanskrit: हिंसा-विहारा ह्य् आलब्धैः पशुभिः स्व-सुखेच्छया । यजन्ते देवता यज्ञैः पितृ-भूत-पतीन् खलाः
verses.transliteration: hiṃsā-vihārā hy ālabdhaiḥ paśubhiḥ sva-sukhecchayā / yajante devatā yajñaiḥ pitṛ-bhūta-patīn khalāḥ
verses.translation: Not understanding My confidential conclusion [see also 10.87 and B.G. 9] they, absorbed in their sensuality, [as meat eaters] are attached to the violence [against animals] that may occur under conditions [in nature], but certainly never is encouraged for sacrifices. In reality they take pleasure in being violent against the animals that [without necessity] were slaughtered for their sense gratification. With their ritual worship of the gods, the forefathers and the leading spirits, they are mischievous people.

verses.sanskrit: स्वप्नोपमम् अमुं लोकम् असन्तं श्रवण-प्रियम् । आशिषो हृदि सङ्कल्प्य त्यजन्त्य् अर्थान् यथा वणिक्
verses.transliteration: svapnopamam amuṃ lokam asantaṃ śravaṇa-priyam / āśiṣo hṛdi saṅkalpya tyajanty arthān yathā vaṇik
verses.translation: In their hearts they all - like business men investing their money - imagine to achieve in a world that in words sounds nice, but which is as unreal as a dream.

verses.sanskrit: रजः-सत्त्व-तमो-निष्ठा रजः-सत्त्व-तमो-जुषः । उपासत इन्द्र-मुख्यान् देवादीन् न यथैव माम्
verses.transliteration: rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-niṣṭhā rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-juṣaḥ / upāsata indra-mukhyān devādīn na yathaiva mām
verses.translation: Established in the mode of passion, goodness or ignorance, they worship the gods and others headed by Indra who likewise delight in passion, goodness and ignorance; but Me they do not worship properly [thus, see also B.G. 9: 23 and 10: 24 & 25].

verses.sanskrit: इष्ट्वेह देवता यज्ञैर् गत्वा रंस्यामहे दिवि । तस्यान्त इह भूयास्म महा-शाला महा-कुलाः
verses.transliteration: iṣṭveha devatā yajñair gatvā raṃsyāmahe divi / tasyānta iha bhūyāsma mahā-śālā mahā-kulāḥ
verses.translation: [They think:] 'When we worship the demigods with sacrifices here, we will enjoy heaven, and when that has ended, we will turn back to earth in a nice home and a fine family.' With their minds thus bewildered by the flowery words [of the Vedas] they nevertheless, as proud and most greedy men, are not attracted to My topics.

verses.sanskrit: एवं पुष्पितया वाचा व्याक्षिप्त-मनसां नृणाम् । मानिनां चाति-लुब्धानां मद्-वार्तापि न रोचते
verses.transliteration: evaṃ puṣpitayā vācā vyākṣipta-manasāṃ nṛṇām / mānināṃ cāti-lubdhānāṃ mad-vārtāpi na rocate
verses.translation: [They think:] 'When we worship the demigods with sacrifices here, we will enjoy heaven, and when that has ended, we will turn back to earth in a nice home and a fine family.' With their minds thus bewildered by the flowery words [of the Vedas] they nevertheless, as proud and most greedy men, are not attracted to My topics.

verses.sanskrit: वेदा ब्रह्मात्म-विषयास् त्रि-काण्ड-विषया इमे । परोक्ष-वादा ऋषयः परोक्षं मम च प्रियम्
verses.transliteration: vedā brahmātma-viṣayās tri-kāṇḍa-viṣayā ime / parokṣa-vādā ṛṣayaḥ parokṣaṃ mama ca priyam
verses.translation: The triknda divided Vedas have the spiritual understanding of the true self, the soul, as their subject matter, but also the seers, who esoterically express themselves more indirectly [the 'other gurus'], are dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: शब्द-ब्रह्म सु-दुर्बोधं प्राणेन्द्रिय-मनो-मयम् । अनन्त-पारं गम्भीरं दुर्विगाह्यं समुद्र-वत्
verses.transliteration: śabda-brahma su-durbodhaṃ prāṇendriya-mano-mayam / ananta-pāraṃ gambhīraṃ durvigāhyaṃ samudra-vat
verses.translation: The transcendental [Vedic] sound [the s'abda-brahman] manifesting itself [at different levels] in the prna, the senses and the mind [of the pure, self-realized, enlightened person], is most difficult to understand; it is unlimited and as unfathomably deep as the ocean [see also 11.12: 17-18].

verses.sanskrit: मयोपबृंहितं भूम्ना ब्रह्मणानन्त-शक्तिना । भूतेषु घोष-रूपेण विसेषूर्णेव लक्ष्यते
verses.transliteration: mayopabṛṃhitaṃ bhūmnā brahmaṇānanta-śaktinā / bhūteṣu ghoṣa-rūpeṇa viseṣūrṇeva lakṣyate
verses.translation: The groundless, changeless Absolute of endless potencies that I promote [as My nature, see Omkra], is represented within the living beings in the form of sound vibrations, just as in a lotus stalk there is the appearance of a single strand of fiber [see also 11.18: 32 and 6.13: 15].

verses.sanskrit: यथोर्णनाभिर् हृदयाद् ऊर्णाम् उद्वमते मुखात् । आकाशाद् घोषवान् प्राणो मनसा स्पर्श-रूपिणा
verses.transliteration: yathorṇanābhir hṛdayād ūrṇām udvamate mukhāt / ākāśād ghoṣavān prāṇo manasā sparśa-rūpiṇā
verses.translation: The way a spider weaves its web from the heart by its orifice, the breath of the Lord [the prna] from the ether is manifesting the sound vibration through the mind in the form of the different phonemes. Full of nectar comprising all the shapes that branch out in thousands of directions, the Master, decorated with consonants, vowels, sibilants and semivowels, has expanded from the syllable om. By the elaborated diversity of expressions and metrical arrangements - that each have four more syllables -, He Himself creates and withdraws again the vast, unlimited expanse [of the Vedic manifestation of sound, see also B.G. 15: 15].

verses.sanskrit: छन्दो-मयो ऽमृत-मयः सहस्र-पदवीं प्रभुः । ॐकाराद् व्यञ्जित-स्पर्श- स्वरोष्मान्तस्थ-भूषिताम्
verses.transliteration: chando-mayo 'mṛta-mayaḥ sahasra-padavīṃ prabhuḥ / oṃkārād vyañjita-sparśa- svaroṣmāntastha-bhūṣitām
verses.translation: The way a spider weaves its web from the heart by its orifice, the breath of the Lord [the prna] from the ether is manifesting the sound vibration through the mind in the form of the different phonemes. Full of nectar comprising all the shapes that branch out in thousands of directions, the Master, decorated with consonants, vowels, sibilants and semivowels, has expanded from the syllable om. By the elaborated diversity of expressions and metrical arrangements - that each have four more syllables -, He Himself creates and withdraws again the vast, unlimited expanse [of the Vedic manifestation of sound, see also B.G. 15: 15].

verses.sanskrit: विचित्र-भाषा-विततां छन्दोभिश् चतुर्-उत्तरैः । अनन्त-पारां बृहतीं सृजत्य् आक्षिपते स्वयम्
verses.transliteration: vicitra-bhāṣā-vitatāṃ chandobhiś catur-uttaraiḥ / ananta-pārāṃ bṛhatīṃ sṛjaty ākṣipate svayam
verses.translation: The way a spider weaves its web from the heart by its orifice, the breath of the Lord [the prna] from the ether is manifesting the sound vibration through the mind in the form of the different phonemes. Full of nectar comprising all the shapes that branch out in thousands of directions, the Master, decorated with consonants, vowels, sibilants and semivowels, has expanded from the syllable om. By the elaborated diversity of expressions and metrical arrangements - that each have four more syllables -, He Himself creates and withdraws again the vast, unlimited expanse [of the Vedic manifestation of sound, see also B.G. 15: 15].

verses.sanskrit: गायत्र्य् उष्णिग् अनुष्टुप् च बृहती पङ्क्तिर् एव च । त्रिष्टुब् जगत्य् अतिच्छन्दो ह्य् अत्यष्ट्य्-अतिजगद्-विराट्
verses.transliteration: gāyatry uṣṇig anuṣṭup ca bṛhatī paṅktir eva ca / triṣṭub jagaty aticchando hy atyaṣṭy-atijagad-virāṭ
verses.translation: For instance the metres Gyatr, Ushnik and Anushthup; Brihat and Pankti as also Trishthup, Jagat, Aticchanda, Atyashthi, Atijagat and Ativirth [have each in this order four more syllables].

verses.sanskrit: किं विधत्ते किम् आचष्टे किम् अनूद्य विकल्पयेत् । इत्य् अस्या हृदयं लोके नान्यो मद् वेद कश्चन
verses.transliteration: kiṃ vidhatte kim ācaṣṭe kim anūdya vikalpayet / ity asyā hṛdayaṃ loke nānyo mad veda kaścana
verses.translation: The [confidential] heart of the matter of what these literatures [karma-knda] enjoin [to be done], what they [upsana-knda] indicate [as being the object of devotion], what aspects they describe or what alternatives they [jna-knda] thus offer [as philosophy], is in this world not known by anyone else but Me [compare 11.20, B.G. 4: 5, 7: 26, 10: 41].

verses.sanskrit: मां विधत्ते ऽभिधत्ते मां विकल्प्यापोह्यते त्व् अहम् । एतावान् सर्व-वेदार्थः शब्द आस्थाय मां भिदाम् । माया-मात्रम् अनूद्यान्ते प्रतिषिध्य प्रसीदति
verses.transliteration: māṃ vidhatte 'bhidhatte māṃ vikalpyāpohyate tv aham / etāvān sarva-vedārthaḥ śabda āsthāya māṃ bhidām / māyā-mātram anūdyānte pratiṣidhya prasīdati
verses.translation: I am the One enjoined, I am the object of worship, I am the alternative [the philosophical hypothesis] that is offered and the One who is explained away [5*]. The transcendental sound vibration of the Vedas establishes Me as being their meaning, and elaborately describes the material duality as the department of the bewildering energy one has to emasculate to ultimately become happy.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच कति तत्त्वानि विश्वेश सङ्ख्यातान्य् ऋषिभिः प्रभो । नवैकादश पञ्च त्रीण्य् आत्थ त्वम् इह शुश्रुम
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca kati tattvāni viśveśa saṅkhyātāny ṛṣibhiḥ prabho / navaikādaśa pañca trīṇy āttha tvam iha śuśruma
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lord of the Universe, how many basic elements of creation [tattvas] have been enumerated by the seers? Oh Master, I heard You speak about the twenty-eight basic elements of this world [see also 11.19: 14]. Some say there are twenty-six, others speak of twenty-five or twenty-seven, some speak of nine, some of four and others of eleven elements, while others speak of sixteen, seventeen or thirteen elements. Oh Eternal Supreme One, could You please explain to us what the sages, who so differently express themselves with the calculations of their divisions, have in mind with them?'

verses.sanskrit: केचित् षड्-विंशतिं प्राहुर् अपरे पञ्च-विंशतिं । सप्तैके नव षट् केचिच् चत्वार्य् एकादशापरे । केचित् सप्तदश प्राहुः षोडशैके त्रयोदश
verses.transliteration: kecit ṣaḍ-viṃśatiṃ prāhur apare pañca-viṃśatiṃ / saptaike nava ṣaṭ kecic catvāry ekādaśāpare / kecit saptadaśa prāhuḥ ṣoḍaśaike trayodaśa
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lord of the Universe, how many basic elements of creation [tattvas] have been enumerated by the seers? Oh Master, I heard You speak about the twenty-eight basic elements of this world [see also 11.19: 14]. Some say there are twenty-six, others speak of twenty-five or twenty-seven, some speak of nine, some of four and others of eleven elements, while others speak of sixteen, seventeen or thirteen elements. Oh Eternal Supreme One, could You please explain to us what the sages, who so differently express themselves with the calculations of their divisions, have in mind with them?'

verses.sanskrit: एतावत्त्वं हि सङ्ख्यानाम् ऋषयो यद्-विवक्षया । गायन्ति पृथग् आयुष्मन्न् इदं नो वक्तुम् अर्हसि
verses.transliteration: etāvattvaṃ hi saṅkhyānām ṛṣayo yad-vivakṣayā / gāyanti pṛthag āyuṣmann idaṃ no vaktum arhasi
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lord of the Universe, how many basic elements of creation [tattvas] have been enumerated by the seers? Oh Master, I heard You speak about the twenty-eight basic elements of this world [see also 11.19: 14]. Some say there are twenty-six, others speak of twenty-five or twenty-seven, some speak of nine, some of four and others of eleven elements, while others speak of sixteen, seventeen or thirteen elements. Oh Eternal Supreme One, could You please explain to us what the sages, who so differently express themselves with the calculations of their divisions, have in mind with them?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच युक्तं च सन्ति सर्वत्र भाषन्ते ब्राह्मणा यथा । मायां मदीयाम् उद्गृह्य वदतां किं नु दुर्घटम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yuktaṃ ca santi sarvatra bhāṣante brāhmaṇā yathā / māyāṃ madīyām udgṛhya vadatāṃ kiṃ nu durghaṭam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'With them [those elements] present everywhere, the brahmins speak the way it suits them, after all, what would there for those who lifted up [the veil of] My my, be difficult to say?

verses.sanskrit: नैतद् एवं यथात्थ त्वं यद् अहं वच्मि तत् तथा । एवं विवदतां हेतुं शक्तयो मे दुरत्ययाः
verses.transliteration: naitad evaṃ yathāttha tvaṃ yad ahaṃ vacmi tat tathā / evaṃ vivadatāṃ hetuṃ śaktayo me duratyayāḥ
verses.translation: 'It is not the way you say it, it is the way I say it': this is what My unsurpassable [bewildering] energies do to those who argue about causes [see darshanas and 6.4: 31].

verses.sanskrit: यासां व्यतिकराद् आसीद् विकल्पो वदतां पदम् । प्राप्ते शम-दमे ऽप्येति वादस् तम् अनु शाम्यति
verses.transliteration: yāsāṃ vyatikarād āsīd vikalpo vadatāṃ padam / prāpte śama-dame 'pyeti vādas tam anu śāmyati
verses.translation: Because My energies are interacting, differences of opinion arise among those who talk about this subject [of causation], but when one finds peace in the control over one's senses the controversy subsides and the arguing stops [one attains the true nature of the Supreme Spirit, tmatattva].

verses.sanskrit: परस्परानुप्रवेशात् तत्त्वानां पुरुषर्षभ । पौर्वापर्य-प्रसङ्ख्यानं यथा वक्तुर् विवक्षितम्
verses.transliteration: parasparānupraveśāt tattvānāṃ puruṣarṣabha / paurvāparya-prasaṅkhyānaṃ yathā vaktur vivakṣitam
verses.translation: Because the various [subtle and gross] elements mutually pervade one another, oh best among men, a speaker wants to give a fitting description with an enumeration of causes and consequences.

verses.sanskrit: एकस्मिन्न् अपि दृश्यन्ते प्रविष्टानीतराणि च । पूर्वस्मिन् वा परस्मिन् वा तत्त्वे तत्त्वानि सर्वशः
verses.transliteration: ekasminn api dṛśyante praviṣṭānītarāṇi ca / pūrvasmin vā parasmin vā tattve tattvāni sarvaśaḥ
verses.translation: With each of those divisions any single element refers to the other elements; whether it is there as a cause or an effect, when you see one element [like the ether] you also see all the other elements that element is part of [*].

verses.sanskrit: पौर्वापर्यम् अतो ऽमीषां प्रसङ्ख्यानम् अभीप्सताम् । यथा विविक्तं यद्-वक्त्रं गृह्णीमो युक्ति-सम्भवात्
verses.transliteration: paurvāparyam ato 'mīṣāṃ prasaṅkhyānam abhīpsatām / yathā viviktaṃ yad-vaktraṃ gṛhṇīmo yukti-sambhavāt
verses.translation: Discrimination as heard from the mouth of anyone who wants to reflect upon cause and effect, we accept [as authoritative], when that distinction originated from consistent reasoning.

verses.sanskrit: अनाद्य्-अविद्या-युक्तस्य पुरुषस्यात्म-वेदनम् । स्वतो न सम्भवाद् अन्यस् तत्त्व-ज्ञो ज्ञान-दो भवेत्
verses.transliteration: anādy-avidyā-yuktasya puruṣasyātma-vedanam / svato na sambhavād anyas tattva-jño jñāna-do bhavet
verses.translation: A person is stuck to eternal ignorance and cannot all by himself figure out what the process of self-realization entails. That knowledge is derived from someone else familiar with the Absolute Truth [compare 11.21: 10].

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषेश्वरयोर् अत्र न वैलक्षण्यम् अण्व् अपि । तद्-अन्य-कल्पनापार्था ज्ञानं च प्रकृतेर् गुणः
verses.transliteration: puruṣeśvarayor atra na vailakṣaṇyam aṇv api / tad-anya-kalpanāpārthā jñānaṃ ca prakṛter guṇaḥ
verses.translation: In this knowledge, belonging to the quality of goodnes of material nature, there is not the slightest difference between the purusha - the Supreme Being, the Soul, the actual person - and s'vara the Lord. To suppose that it would be otherwise is a useless figment [see B.G. 18: 20 and 9: 15 and **].

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिर् गुण-साम्यं वै प्रकृतेर् नात्मनो गुणाः । सत्त्वं रजस् तम इति स्थित्य्-उत्पत्त्य्-अन्त-हेतवः
verses.transliteration: prakṛtir guṇa-sāmyaṃ vai prakṛter nātmano guṇāḥ / sattvaṃ rajas tama iti sthity-utpatty-anta-hetavaḥ
verses.translation: The basic qualities of goodness, passion and ignorance, as the causes of [respectively] maintenance, creation and destruction, constitute an equilibrium in material nature [prakriti] but [do] not [control] the soul [see also B.G. 3: 27].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं ज्ञानं रजः कर्म तमो ऽज्ञानम् इहोच्यते । गुण-व्यतिकरः कालः स्वभावः सूत्रम् एव च
verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ jñānaṃ rajaḥ karma tamo 'jñānam ihocyate / guṇa-vyatikaraḥ kālaḥ svabhāvaḥ sūtram eva ca
verses.translation: In this world the mode of goodness is of knowledge [light], the mode of passion is of fruitive labor [karma] and the mode of ignorance is of a lack of wisdom. The interaction of the modes is called Time and [the combination of] their natural disposition [svabhva] constitutes the thread [or lead, symbolically worn by the first three varnas, see also 11.12: 19-21].

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषः प्रकृतिर् व्यक्तम् अहङ्कारो नभो ऽनिलः । ज्योतिर् आपः क्षितिर् इति तत्त्वान्य् उक्तानि मे नव
verses.transliteration: puruṣaḥ prakṛtir vyaktam ahaṅkāro nabho 'nilaḥ / jyotir āpaḥ kṣitir iti tattvāny uktāni me nava
verses.translation: The original person [purusha], the primary substance [prakriti, elementary nature], the visible manifestation [mahat-tattva, the 'great principle'], the identification with the form [ahankra, false ego], ether, air, fire, water and earth are thus the nine elements of creation I referred to [in verse 1].

verses.sanskrit: श्रोत्रं त्वग् दर्शनं घ्राणो जिह्वेति ज्ञान-शक्तयः । वाक्-पाण्य्-उपस्थ-पाय्व्-अङ्घ्रिः कर्माण्य् अङ्गोभयं मनः
verses.transliteration: śrotraṃ tvag darśanaṃ ghrāṇo jihveti jñāna-śaktayaḥ / vāk-pāṇy-upastha-pāyv-aṅghriḥ karmāṇy aṅgobhayaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: Hearing, touching, seeing, smelling and tasting are the five [senses] by which one acquires knowledge; the speech organ, the hands, the genitals, the anus and the legs constitute their operation, oh dear one, and the mind is there for both of them.

verses.sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शो रसो गन्धो रूपं चेत्य् अर्थ-जातयः । गत्य्-उक्त्य्-उत्सर्ग-शिल्पानि कर्मायतन-सिद्धयः
verses.transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśo raso gandho rūpaṃ cety artha-jātayaḥ / gaty-ukty-utsarga-śilpāni karmāyatana-siddhayaḥ
verses.translation: Sounds, tactile qualities, tastes, fragrances, and forms [or colors] are the categories of the sense objects [see vishaya] and speech, manufacturing, excretion [by anus and genitals] and locomotion are the functions covered by them.

verses.sanskrit: सर्गादौ प्रकृतिर् ह्य् अस्य कार्य-कारण-रूपिणी । सत्त्वादिभिर् गुणैर् धत्ते पुरुषो ऽव्यक्त ईक्षते
verses.transliteration: sargādau prakṛtir hy asya kārya-kāraṇa-rūpiṇī / sattvādibhir guṇair dhatte puruṣo 'vyakta īkṣate
verses.translation: In the beginning of creation the purusha uninvolved witnesses the material nature of this universe, the universe that by the operation of sattva and the other modes assumes the forms of the gross manifestations and subtler causes [see also 2.10: 10].

verses.sanskrit: व्यक्तादायो विकुर्वाणा धातवः पुरुषेक्षया । लब्ध-वीर्याः सृजन्त्य् अण्डं संहताः प्रकृतेर् बलात्
verses.transliteration: vyaktādāyo vikurvāṇā dhātavaḥ puruṣekṣayā / labdha-vīryāḥ sṛjanty aṇḍaṃ saṃhatāḥ prakṛter balāt
verses.translation: All the elements of the visible manifestation [the mahat-tattva] and what belongs to it, received their potencies from the glance of the Lord [the Original Person], undergo transformation and create, amalgamated by the power of nature, the egg of the universe [see also 2.5: 35, 3.20: 14-15, 3.26: 51-53, 3.32: 29, 5.26: 38, 11.6: 16].

verses.sanskrit: सप्तैव धातव इति तत्रार्थाः पञ्च खादयः । ज्ञानम् आत्मोभयाधारस् ततो देहेन्द्रियासवः
verses.transliteration: saptaiva dhātava iti tatrārthāḥ pañca khādayaḥ / jñānam ātmobhayādhāras tato dehendriyāsavaḥ
verses.translation: With the five physical elements beginning with the ether on the one hand and the individual knower [the jva] with the Supreme Soul [the Paramtma] on the other hand, we speak of seven constituent elements as the foundation from which the body, the senses and the life air [are produced].

verses.sanskrit: षड् इत्य् अत्रापि भूतानि पञ्च षष्ठः परः पुमान् । तैर् युइत आत्म-सम्भूतैः सृष्ट्वेदं समपाविशत्
verses.transliteration: ṣaḍ ity atrāpi bhūtāni pañca ṣaṣṭhaḥ paraḥ pumān / tair yuita ātma-sambhūtaiḥ sṛṣṭvedaṃ samapāviśat
verses.translation: Departing from six elements one speaks of the Transcendental Person as the sixth element connected to the five material elements, the elements He first projected as His creation and thereupon entered.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वार्य् एवेति तत्रापि तेज आपो ऽन्नम् आत्मनः । जातानि तैर् इदं जातं जन्मावयविनः खलु
verses.transliteration: catvāry eveti tatrāpi teja āpo 'nnam ātmanaḥ / jātāni tair idaṃ jātaṃ janmāvayavinaḥ khalu
verses.translation: When one speaks of four elements, fire, water and earth arise from the Original Self; from these elements this cosmos originated, the birth place of all material products.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्ख्याने सप्तदशके भूत-मात्रेन्द्रियाणि च । पञ्च पञ्चैक-मनसा आत्मा सप्तदशः स्मृतः
verses.transliteration: saṅkhyāne saptadaśake bhūta-mātrendriyāṇi ca / pañca pañcaika-manasā ātmā saptadaśaḥ smṛtaḥ
verses.translation: Counting seventeen there is the consideration of the five gross elements, the five senses and their five objects together with the one mind and the soul as the seventeenth element.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वत् षोडश-सङ्ख्याने आत्मैव मन उच्यते । भूतेन्द्रियाणि पञ्चैव मन आत्मा त्रयोदश
verses.transliteration: tadvat ṣoḍaśa-saṅkhyāne ātmaiva mana ucyate / bhūtendriyāṇi pañcaiva mana ātmā trayodaśa
verses.translation: The same way counting sixteen elements the soul is identified with the mind. With thirteen elements one has the five gross elements, the five senses, the mind and the [individual and supreme] soul.

verses.sanskrit: एकादशत्व आत्मासौ महा-भूतेन्द्रियाणि च । अष्टौ प्रकृतयश् चैव पुरुषश् च नवेत्य् अथ
verses.transliteration: ekādaśatva ātmāsau mahā-bhūtendriyāṇi ca / aṣṭau prakṛtayaś caiva puruṣaś ca navety atha
verses.translation: Counting eleven elements one speaks of the soul, the gross elements and the senses. With the eight natural elements [the five gross ones, mind, intelligence and false ego] and the purusha, the Original Person, one thereupon has nine.

verses.sanskrit: इति नाना-प्रसङ्ख्यानं तत्त्वानाम् ऋषिभिः कृतम् । सर्वं न्याय्यं युक्तिमत्त्वाद् विदुषां किम् अशोभनम्
verses.transliteration: iti nānā-prasaṅkhyānaṃ tattvānām ṛṣibhiḥ kṛtam / sarvaṃ nyāyyaṃ yuktimattvād viduṣāṃ kim aśobhanam
verses.translation: In this way the various divisions of the tattva elements have been contrived by the seers, all logically being supported by rational arguments; with the sages there is no lack of clarity.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच प्रकृतिः पुरुषश् चोभौ यद्य् अप्य् आत्म-विलक्षणौ । अन्योन्यापाश्रयात् कृष्ण दृश्यते न भिदा तयोः । प्रकृतौ लक्ष्यते ह्य् आत्मा प्रकृतिश् च तथात्मनि
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca prakṛtiḥ puruṣaś cobhau yady apy ātma-vilakṣaṇau / anyonyāpāśrayāt kṛṣṇa dṛśyate na bhidā tayoḥ / prakṛtau lakṣyate hy ātmā prakṛtiś ca tathātmani
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Because both nature and the enjoyer [prakriti and purusha], despite being constitutionally different, cover one another, oh Krishna, there seems to be no difference between the two: one sees the soul within nature and nature within the soul [see also B.G. 18: 16].

verses.sanskrit: एवं मे पुण्डरीकाक्ष महान्तं संशयं हृदि । छेत्तुम् अर्हसि सर्व-ज्ञ वचोभिर् नय-नैपुणैः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ me puṇḍarīkākṣa mahāntaṃ saṃśayaṃ hṛdi / chettum arhasi sarva-jña vacobhir naya-naipuṇaiḥ
verses.translation: Please, oh Lotus-eyed One, All-knowing and Very Expert in Reasoning, cut down with Your words the great doubt in my heart.

verses.sanskrit: त्वत्तो ज्ञानं हि जीवानां प्रमोषस् ते ऽत्र शक्तितः । त्वम् एव ह्य् आत्म-मायाया गतिं वेत्थ न चापरः
verses.transliteration: tvatto jñānaṃ hi jīvānāṃ pramoṣas te 'tra śaktitaḥ / tvam eva hy ātma-māyāyā gatiṃ vettha na cāparaḥ
verses.translation: The living beings receive from You the knowledge that by the potency of Your outer illusion is stolen away [again]. Only You understand the real nature of Your illusory power and no one else [see also B.G. 15: 15].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच प्रकृतिः पुरुषश् चेति विकल्पः पुरुषर्षभ । एष वैकारिकः सर्गो गुण-व्यतिकरात्मकः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca prakṛtiḥ puruṣaś ceti vikalpaḥ puruṣarṣabha / eṣa vaikārikaḥ sargo guṇa-vyatikarātmakaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Prakriti and purusha [nature and the enjoyer] are completely different, oh best of all persons. This creation [prakriti] is subjected to transformation because of the interaction of the gunas.

verses.sanskrit: ममाङ्ग माया गुण-मय्य् अनेकधा विकल्प-बुद्धीश् च गुणैर् विधत्ते । वैकारिकस् त्रि-विधो ऽध्यात्मम् एकम् अथाधिदैवम् अधिभूतम् अन्यत्
verses.transliteration: mamāṅga māyā guṇa-mayy anekadhā vikalpa-buddhīś ca guṇair vidhatte / vaikārikas tri-vidho 'dhyātmam ekam athādhidaivam adhibhūtam anyat
verses.translation: My dear, the deluding energy consisting of the three modes establishes by those basic qualities a diversity of combinations and mentalities. This changeable nature based upon the gunas is of three kinds, one is called adhytma, the next adhidaiva and another adhibhta [see also kles'as and 1.17: 19].

verses.sanskrit: दृग् रूपम् आर्कं वपुर् अत्र रन्ध्रे परस्परं सिध्यति यः स्वतः खे । आत्मा यद् एषाम् अपरो य आद्यः स्वयानुभूत्याखिल-सिद्ध-सिद्धिः
verses.transliteration: dṛg rūpam ārkaṃ vapur atra randhre parasparaṃ sidhyati yaḥ svataḥ khe / ātmā yad eṣām aparo ya ādyaḥ svayānubhūtyākhila-siddha-siddhiḥ
verses.translation: In this world one's sight [adhytma, as caused by oneself], that what one sees [adhibhta, as caused by others] and the light upon it [adhidaiva, as caused by nature], create each other's perfection with the sun independently in the sky. [So too] the [Super]soul, the original cause separate from these three aspects, by its own conscious experience acts as the perfection of all that was achieved.

verses.sanskrit: एवं त्वग्-आदि श्रवणादि चक्षुर् जिह्वादि नासादि च चित्त-युक्तम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ tvag-ādi śravaṇādi cakṣur jihvādi nāsādi ca citta-yuktam
verses.translation: Next to the eyes the same [trinity] applies to the sense of touch and what one feels with it, to the ear and what one hears, to the tongue and its occupation, to the nose with what is smelled and to one's consciousness together with its attributes.

verses.sanskrit: यो ऽसौ गुण-क्षोभ-कृतो विकारः प्रधान-मूलान् महतः प्रसूतः । अहं त्रि-वृन् मोह-विकल्प-हेतुर् वैकारिकस् तामस ऐन्द्रियश् च
verses.transliteration: yo 'sau guṇa-kṣobha-kṛto vikāraḥ pradhāna-mūlān mahataḥ prasūtaḥ / ahaṃ tri-vṛn moha-vikalpa-hetur vaikārikas tāmasa aindriyaś ca
verses.translation: The agitation of the modes takes place on the basis of the primal ether and leads to changes [or pradhna constitutes the cause of the time phenomena]. The principle of the intellect [the mahat-tattva, see also ***] therefrom gives rise to a false I-awareness that is the cause of three different types of bewilderment: emotion [vaikrika], ignorance [tmasa] and sensual pleasure [aindriya].

verses.sanskrit: आत्मापरिज्ञान-मयो विवादो ह्य् अस्तीति नास्तीति भिदार्थ-निष्ठः । व्यर्थो ऽपि नैवोपरमेत पुंसां मत्तः परावृत्त-धियां स्व-लोकात्
verses.transliteration: ātmāparijñāna-mayo vivādo hy astīti nāstīti bhidārtha-niṣṭhaḥ / vyartho 'pi naivoparameta puṃsāṃ mattaḥ parāvṛtta-dhiyāṃ sva-lokāt
verses.translation: Lacking the full knowledge of the Supersoul one says things like 'this is real and that is not real' with the focus of discussion on material dualities. Although useless, such [speculations] will not cease for as long as persons have turned their attention away from Me, their true abode.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच त्वत्तः परावृत्त-धियः स्व-कृतैः कर्मभिः प्रभो । उच्चावचान् यथा देहान् गृह्णन्ति विसृजन्ति च
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca tvattaḥ parāvṛtta-dhiyaḥ sva-kṛtaiḥ karmabhiḥ prabho / uccāvacān yathā dehān gṛhṇanti visṛjanti ca
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How do those souls whose minds are diverted from You by the fruitful activities they perform, oh Master, accept and give up higher and lower material bodies? Please Govinda, explain to me what by those, who are not that spiritual, is not understood because they, predominantly with the knowledge of this world, were cheated.'

verses.sanskrit: तन् ममाख्याहि गोविन्द दुर्विभाव्यम् अनात्मभिः । न ह्य् एतत् प्रायशो लोके विद्वांसः सन्ति वञ्चिताः
verses.transliteration: tan mamākhyāhi govinda durvibhāvyam anātmabhiḥ / na hy etat prāyaśo loke vidvāṃsaḥ santi vañcitāḥ
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How do those souls whose minds are diverted from You by the fruitful activities they perform, oh Master, accept and give up higher and lower material bodies? Please Govinda, explain to me what by those, who are not that spiritual, is not understood because they, predominantly with the knowledge of this world, were cheated.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच मनः कर्म-मयं णॄणाम् इन्द्रियैः पञ्चभिर् युतम् । लोकाल् लोकं प्रयात्य् अन्य आत्मा तद् अनुवर्तते
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca manaḥ karma-mayaṃ ṇṝṇām indriyaiḥ pañcabhir yutam / lokāl lokaṃ prayāty anya ātmā tad anuvartate
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The mind of the people ruled by their fruitive labor, is bound to the five senses. Traveling from one world to the next, the soul, which has a separate existence, follows that mind [see also linga, vsan and B.G. 2: 22].

verses.sanskrit: ध्यायन् मनो ऽनु विषयान् दृष्टान् वानुश्रुतान् अथ । उद्यत् सीदत् कर्म-तन्त्रं स्मृतिस् तद् अनु शाम्यति
verses.transliteration: dhyāyan mano 'nu viṣayān dṛṣṭān vānuśrutān atha / udyat sīdat karma-tantraṃ smṛtis tad anu śāmyati
verses.translation: The mind that depending its karma always contemplates, rises because of what is seen or heard through the senses, but inert [when dying away from the sense objects] the remembrance [of that life] is then lost.

verses.sanskrit: विषयाभिनिवेशेन नात्मानं यत् स्मरेत् पुनः । जन्तोर् वै कस्यचिद् धेतोर् मृत्युर् अत्यन्त-विस्मृतिः
verses.transliteration: viṣayābhiniveśena nātmānaṃ yat smaret punaḥ / jantor vai kasyacid dhetor mṛtyur atyanta-vismṛtiḥ
verses.translation: This total forgetfulness of the living entity in which it does not remember a self that for this or that reason was absorbed in the objects of the senses, is what one calls death.

verses.sanskrit: जन्म त्व् आत्मतया पुंसः सर्व-भावेन भूरि-द । विषय-स्वीकृतिं प्राहुर् यथा स्वप्न-मनोरथः
verses.transliteration: janma tv ātmatayā puṃsaḥ sarva-bhāvena bhūri-da / viṣaya-svīkṛtiṃ prāhur yathā svapna-manorathaḥ
verses.translation: Oh man of charity, what one calls birth is when a person completely identifies himself with the body he assumed, just like what one does in a dream or when one has a fantasy.

verses.sanskrit: स्वप्नं मनोरथं चेत्थं प्राक्तनं न स्मरत्य् असौ । तत्र पूर्वम् इवात्मानम् अपूर्वम् चानुपश्यति
verses.transliteration: svapnaṃ manorathaṃ cetthaṃ prāktanaṃ na smaraty asau / tatra pūrvam ivātmānam apūrvam cānupaśyati
verses.translation: And just as one in a dream or fantasy has no remembrance of a previous dream or fantasy, one neither thinks of having had a previous existence [*4 en B.G. 4: 5].

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियायन-सृष्ट्येदं त्रै-विध्यं भाति वस्तुनि । बहिर्-अन्तर्-भिदा-हेतुर् जनो ऽसज्-जन-कृद् यथा
verses.transliteration: indriyāyana-sṛṣṭyedaṃ trai-vidhyaṃ bhāti vastuni / bahir-antar-bhidā-hetur jano 'saj-jana-kṛd yathā
verses.translation: Because of the creation of this sense refuge, this body, a threefold notion [of being of a high, middle or low class birth] appears concerning the form assumed. This leads the person to [believe in] an outer duality also found inside, like giving birth to bad offspring.

verses.sanskrit: नित्यदा ह्य् अङ्ग भूतानि भवन्ति न भवन्ति च । कालेनालक्ष्य-वेगेन सूक्ष्मत्वात् तन् न दृश्यते
verses.transliteration: nityadā hy aṅga bhūtāni bhavanti na bhavanti ca / kālenālakṣya-vegena sūkṣmatvāt tan na dṛśyate
verses.translation: My best one, created bodies constantly find and lose their existence as a consequence of Time, the imperceptible stream not seen for being that subtle.

verses.sanskrit: यथार्चिषां स्रोतसां च फलानां वा वनस्पतेः । तथैव सर्व-भूतानां वयो-ऽवस्थादयः कृताः
verses.transliteration: yathārciṣāṃ srotasāṃ ca phalānāṃ vā vanaspateḥ / tathaiva sarva-bhūtānāṃ vayo-'vasthādayaḥ kṛtāḥ
verses.translation: The lifespan, the circumstances and such of all created beings are determined by it, just like the flame of a candle, the stream of a river and the fruits of a tree are.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽयं दीपो ऽर्चिषां यद्वत् स्रोतसां तद् इदं जलम् । सो ऽयं पुमान् इति नृणां मृषा गीर् धीर् मृषायुषाम्
verses.transliteration: so 'yaṃ dīpo 'rciṣāṃ yadvat srotasāṃ tad idaṃ jalam / so 'yaṃ pumān iti nṛṇāṃ mṛṣā gīr dhīr mṛṣāyuṣām
verses.translation: One has it wrong when one says 'this light is the same as this lamp' and 'this flow of water is the same as this river'. The same way it is wrong to say that 'this human [body] is the same as this person'. It is a way of reasoning by which men are wasting their lives [see also 6.16: 58, 7.6: 1-2]!

verses.sanskrit: मा स्वस्य कर्म-बीजेन जायते सो ऽप्य् अयं पुमान् । म्रियते वामरो भ्रान्त्या यथाग्निर् दारु-संयुतः
verses.transliteration: mā svasya karma-bījena jāyate so 'py ayaṃ pumān / mriyate vāmaro bhrāntyā yathāgnir dāru-saṃyutaḥ
verses.translation: Actually this person does not take birth from the seed of his own activities, nor does he die. He is immortal and is only by illusion joined [with this body], just like fire with firewood [see B.G. 2: 24].

verses.sanskrit: निषेक-गर्भ-जन्मानि बाल्य-कौमार-यौवनम् । वयो-मध्यं जरा मृत्युर् इत्य् अवस्थास् तनोर् नव
verses.transliteration: niṣeka-garbha-janmāni bālya-kaumāra-yauvanam / vayo-madhyaṃ jarā mṛtyur ity avasthās tanor nava
verses.translation: Impregnation, gestation, birth, infancy, childhood, youth, middle age, old age and death are the nine stages of the body.

verses.sanskrit: एता मनोरथ-मयीर् हान्यस्योच्चावचास् तनूः । गुण-सङ्गाद् उपादत्ते क्वचित् कश्चिज् जहाति च
verses.transliteration: etā manoratha-mayīr hānyasyoccāvacās tanūḥ / guṇa-saṅgād upādatte kvacit kaścij jahāti ca
verses.translation: These superior or inferior physical conditions - that one owes to one's own motives [of karmic rebirth] -, a soul accepts as his own because of being bound to the modes, but sometimes he [by the grace of the Lord with due effort in yoga] manages to distance himself from them.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मनः पितृ-पुत्राभ्याम् अनुमेयौ भवाप्ययौ । न भवाप्यय-वस्तूनाम् अभिज्ञो द्वय-लक्षणः
verses.transliteration: ātmanaḥ pitṛ-putrābhyām anumeyau bhavāpyayau / na bhavāpyaya-vastūnām abhijño dvaya-lakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: From the birth of one's offspring and death of one's forefathers one may conclude to the truth [of one's own life]. He who properly understands the characteristics of this duality [and thus knows he is the continuing soul] is no longer subject to this generation and destruction of things.

verses.sanskrit: तरोर् बीज-विपाकाभ्यां यो विद्वाञ् जन्म-संयमौ । तरोर् विलक्षणो द्रष्टा एवं द्रष्टा तनोः पृथक्
verses.transliteration: taror bīja-vipākābhyāṃ yo vidvāñ janma-saṃyamau / taror vilakṣaṇo draṣṭā evaṃ draṣṭā tanoḥ pṛthak
verses.translation: Someone knowing about the seed and maturity of a tree, is the witness distinct from the birth and death of that tree. One is the same way the witness separate from the [birth and death of] the physical body.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतेर् एवम् आत्मानम् अविविच्याबुधः पुमान् । तत्त्वेन स्पर्श-सम्मूढः संसारं प्रतिपद्यते
verses.transliteration: prakṛter evam ātmānam avivicyābudhaḥ pumān / tattvena sparśa-sammūḍhaḥ saṃsāraṃ pratipadyate
verses.translation: An unintelligent person who fails to distinguish between soul and matter and in touch with matter takes the external world for the real thing, lands completely bewildered in the cycle of birth and death [see also B.G. 9: 21-22 and 1.7: 5].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्व-सङ्गाद् ऋषीन् देवान् रजसासुर-मानुषान् । तमसा भूत-तिर्यक्त्वं भ्रामितो याति कर्मभिः
verses.transliteration: sattva-saṅgād ṛṣīn devān rajasāsura-mānuṣān / tamasā bhūta-tiryaktvaṃ bhrāmito yāti karmabhiḥ
verses.translation: Wandering around because of his karma he, when he follows the mode of goodness, will go to the sages and the gods. Following the lead of passion he will move among the common people or among the demons, and by the mode of ignorance he will find himself among the ghosts and spirits or reach the animal kingdom [see also B.G. 6: 41-42, 9: 25; 17: 4].

verses.sanskrit: नृत्यतो गायतः पश्यन् यथैवानुकरोति तान् । एवं बुद्धि-गुणान् पश्यन्न् अनीहो ऽप्य् अनुकार्यते
verses.transliteration: nṛtyato gāyataḥ paśyan yathaivānukaroti tān / evaṃ buddhi-guṇān paśyann anīho 'py anukāryate
verses.translation: Observing dancing and singing persons one tends to imitate them. The same way one is, despite [as a silent witness] not being engaged, inclined towards a material intelligence when one is faced with the qualities of matter [see also 11.21: 19-21].

verses.sanskrit: यथाम्भसा प्रचलता तरवो ऽपि चला इव । चक्षुसा भ्राम्यमाणेन दृश्यते भ्रमतीव भूः
verses.transliteration: yathāmbhasā pracalatā taravo 'pi calā iva / cakṣusā bhrāmyamāṇena dṛśyate bhramatīva bhūḥ
verses.translation: Just as trees seem to move when seen in water that moves and the world seems to spin when one's eyes are spinning around, one's mental impressions of experienced sense objects are neither real. Just like the things one sees in a dream are but figments of one's imagination, also the soul's image of a life of birth and death is but a phantom.

verses.sanskrit: यथा मनोरथ-धियो विषय्षानुभवो मृषा । स्वप्न-दृष्टाश् च दाशार्ह तथा संसार आत्मनः
verses.transliteration: yathā manoratha-dhiyo viṣayṣānubhavo mṛṣā / svapna-dṛṣṭāś ca dāśārha tathā saṃsāra ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Just as trees seem to move when seen in water that moves and the world seems to spin when one's eyes are spinning around, one's mental impressions of experienced sense objects are neither real. Just like the things one sees in a dream are but figments of one's imagination, also the soul's image of a life of birth and death is but a phantom.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थे ह्य् अविद्यमाने ऽपि संसृतिर् न निवर्तते । ध्यायतो विषयान् अस्य स्वप्ने ऽनर्थागमो यथा
verses.transliteration: arthe hy avidyamāne 'pi saṃsṛtir na nivartate / dhyāyato viṣayān asya svapne 'narthāgamo yathā
verses.translation: For someone meditating the objects of the senses, material life will not stop despite being an illusory affair, just like the occurrence of unpleasant things in a dream [may repeat itself *5].

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् उद्धव मा भुङ्क्ष्व विषयान् असद्-इन्द्रियैः । आत्माग्रहण-निर्भातं पश्य वैकल्पिकं भ्रमम्
verses.transliteration: tasmād uddhava mā bhuṅkṣva viṣayān asad-indriyaiḥ / ātmāgrahaṇa-nirbhātaṃ paśya vaikalpikaṃ bhramam
verses.translation: Therefore Uddhava, do not delight in the sense objects that play games with the senses. Just see how one, based upon the illusion of the material duality risen within the self, fails to realize the soul.

verses.sanskrit: क्षिप्तो ऽवमानितो ऽसद्भिः प्रलब्धो ऽसूयितो ऽथ वा । ताडितः सन्निरुद्धो वा वृत्त्या वा परिहापितः
verses.transliteration: kṣipto 'vamānito 'sadbhiḥ pralabdho 'sūyito 'tha vā / tāḍitaḥ sanniruddho vā vṛttyā vā parihāpitaḥ
verses.translation: When one is insulted, neglected, ridiculed or envied by bad people, or else chastised, held captive or deprived of one's means of livelihood, or when one is repeatedly spat or urinated upon by ignorant people, someone desiring the Supreme who thus being shaken is having difficulties, should save himself by resorting to his essence [see also 5.5: 30].'

verses.sanskrit: निष्ठ्युतो मूत्रितो वाज्ञैर् बहुधैवं प्रकम्पितः । श्रेयस्-कामः कृच्छ्र-गत आत्मनात्मानम् उद्धरेत्
verses.transliteration: niṣṭhyuto mūtrito vājñair bahudhaivaṃ prakampitaḥ / śreyas-kāmaḥ kṛcchra-gata ātmanātmānam uddharet
verses.translation: When one is insulted, neglected, ridiculed or envied by bad people, or else chastised, held captive or deprived of one's means of livelihood, or when one is repeatedly spat or urinated upon by ignorant people, someone desiring the Supreme who thus being shaken is having difficulties, should save himself by resorting to his essence [see also 5.5: 30].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यथैवम् अनुबुध्येयं वद नो वदतां वर । सु-दुःषहम् इमं मन्य आत्मन्य् असद्-अतिक्रमम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yathaivam anubudhyeyaṃ vada no vadatāṃ vara / su-duḥṣaham imaṃ manya ātmany asad-atikramam
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'How do I learn this? Please, oh Best of All Speakers, tell us that.

verses.sanskrit: विदुषाम् अपि विश्वात्मन् प्रकृतिर् हि बलीयसी । ऋते त्वद्-धर्म-निरतान् शान्तांस् ते चरणालयान्
verses.transliteration: viduṣām api viśvātman prakṛtir hi balīyasī / ṛte tvad-dharma-niratān śāntāṃs te caraṇālayān
verses.translation: The offenses of ignorant people against oneself, is what I find most difficult to tolerate. Even for scholars it is difficult, oh Soul of the Universe. Except for those who, fixed in Your dharma, in peace reside at Your lotus feet, material nature no doubt constitutes the greatest burden.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-बादरायणिर् उवाच स एवम् आशंसित उद्धवेन भागवत-मुख्येन दाशार्ह-मुख्यः । सभाजयन् भृत्य-वचो मुकुन्दस् तम् आबभाषे श्रवणीय-वीर्यः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvāca sa evam āśaṃsita uddhavena bhāgavata-mukhyena dāśārha-mukhyaḥ / sabhājayan bhṛtya-vaco mukundas tam ābabhāṣe śravaṇīya-vīryaḥ
verses.translation: The son of Vysa said: 'After Uddhava, the greatest of the devotees, had said this to Him, the chief of the Ds'rhas whose heroism is so worth mentioning, He praised His servant for his words and replied.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच बार्हस्पत्य स नास्त्य् अत्र साधुर् वै दुर्जनेरितैः । दुरक्तैर् भिन्नम् आत्मानं यः समाधातुम् ईश्वरः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca bārhaspatya sa nāsty atra sādhur vai durjaneritaiḥ / duraktair bhinnam ātmānaṃ yaḥ samādhātum īśvaraḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Oh disciple of Brihaspati, in this world there is virtually no pious soul capable of keeping his mind in check after being disturbed by the insulting words of an uncivilized person.

verses.sanskrit: न तथा तप्यते विद्धः पुमान् बाणैस् तु मर्म-गैः । यथा तुदन्ति मर्म-स्था ह्य् असतां परुषेषवः
verses.transliteration: na tathā tapyate viddhaḥ pumān bāṇais tu marma-gaiḥ / yathā tudanti marma-sthā hy asatāṃ paruṣeṣavaḥ
verses.translation: A person is not as much hurt when pierced by arrows through a sensitive part of his body, as by the painful arrows of the harsh words of uncivilized people getting lodged in his heart.

verses.sanskrit: कथयन्ति महत् पुण्यम् इतिहासम् इहोद्धव । तम् अहं वर्णयिष्यामि निबोध सु-समाहितः
verses.transliteration: kathayanti mahat puṇyam itihāsam ihoddhava / tam ahaṃ varṇayiṣyāmi nibodha su-samāhitaḥ
verses.translation: In this regard, Uddhava, a most pious story is told. Please listen carefully, I shall describe it to you.

verses.sanskrit: केनचिद् भिक्षुणा गीतं परिभूतेन दुर्जनैः । स्मरता धृति-युक्तेन विपाकं निज-कर्मणाम्
verses.transliteration: kenacid bhikṣuṇā gītaṃ paribhūtena durjanaiḥ / smaratā dhṛti-yuktena vipākaṃ nija-karmaṇām
verses.translation: It was related by a mendicant who, upon being insulted by bad people, kept his composure reminding himself that it happened as a consequence of his past deeds.

verses.sanskrit: अवन्तिषु द्विजः कश्चिद् आसीद् आढ्यतमः श्रिया । वार्ता-वृत्तिः कदर्यस् तु कामी लुब्धो ऽति-कोपनः
verses.transliteration: avantiṣu dvijaḥ kaścid āsīd āḍhyatamaḥ śriyā / vārtā-vṛttiḥ kadaryas tu kāmī lubdho 'ti-kopanaḥ
verses.translation: In Avant [in the district of Malwa] there once lived a certain brahmin, very rich with many opulences, who earned his livelihood doing business; but he was a miserly person, full of lust, greed and very prone to anger [see also B.G. 2: 49].

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञातयो ऽतिथयस् तस्य वाङ्-मात्रेणापि नार्चिताः । शून्यावसथ आत्मापि काले कामैर् अनर्चितः
verses.transliteration: jñātayo 'tithayas tasya vāṅ-mātreṇāpi nārcitāḥ / śūnyāvasatha ātmāpi kāle kāmair anarcitaḥ
verses.translation: He had no respect for his relatives and guests, not even in words, nor catered he, devoid of religiosity, timely to his own needs.

verses.sanskrit: दुह्शीलस्य कदर्यस्य द्रुह्यन्ते पुत्र-बान्धवाः । दारा दुहितरो भृत्या विषण्णा नाचरन् प्रियम्
verses.transliteration: duhśīlasya kadaryasya druhyante putra-bāndhavāḥ / dārā duhitaro bhṛtyā viṣaṇṇā nācaran priyam
verses.translation: His sons, in-laws, his wife, daughters and servants turned against the miser with his bad character and in disgust withheld their affection.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैवं यक्ष-वित्तस्य च्युतस्योभय-लोकतः । धर्म-काम-विहीनस्य चुक्रुधुः पञ्च-भागिनः
verses.transliteration: tasyaivaṃ yakṣa-vittasya cyutasyobhaya-lokataḥ / dharma-kāma-vihīnasya cukrudhuḥ pañca-bhāginaḥ
verses.translation: Thus lacking in dharma as also in pleasure, the five claimants of sacrifice [the deities, see paca-bhga] became angry with that obsessive treasurer who failed for both the worlds [this and the next].

verses.sanskrit: तद्-अवध्यान-विस्रस्त- पुण्य-स्कन्धस्य भूरि-द । अर्थो ऽप्य् अगच्छन् निधनं बह्व्-आयास-परिश्रमः
verses.transliteration: tad-avadhyāna-visrasta- puṇya-skandhasya bhūri-da / artho 'py agacchan nidhanaṃ bahv-āyāsa-pariśramaḥ
verses.translation: By his neglect of them he lost all his credit oh magnanimous one, and all the wealth he so painstakingly had troubled himself for was lost.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञात्यो जगृहुः किञ्चित् किञ्चिद् दस्यव उद्धव । दैवतः कालतः किञ्चिद् ब्रह्म-बन्धोर् नृ-पार्थिवात्
verses.transliteration: jñātyo jagṛhuḥ kiñcit kiñcid dasyava uddhava / daivataḥ kālataḥ kiñcid brahma-bandhor nṛ-pārthivāt
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, a part of the wealth of this so-called brahmin was seized by his relatives, some by thieves, some by providence, some by time, some by common people and some by higher authorities [see also 10.49: 22].

verses.sanskrit: स एवं द्रविणे नष्टे धर्म-काम-विवर्जितः । उपेक्षितश् च स्व-जनैश् चिन्ताम् आप दुरत्ययाम्
verses.transliteration: sa evaṃ draviṇe naṣṭe dharma-kāma-vivarjitaḥ / upekṣitaś ca sva-janaiś cintām āpa duratyayām
verses.translation: When he had lost his property a hard to endure anxiety arose in him who, devoid of religiosity and pleasure, was neglected by his kin.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैवं ध्यायतो दीर्घं नष्ट-रायस् तपस्विनः । खिद्यतो बाष्प-कण्ठस्य निर्वेदः सु-महान् अभूत्
verses.transliteration: tasyaivaṃ dhyāyato dīrghaṃ naṣṭa-rāyas tapasvinaḥ / khidyato bāṣpa-kaṇṭhasya nirvedaḥ su-mahān abhūt
verses.translation: Thus ruminating he, choked with tears, for a long time lamented in pain over his lost riches, whereupon a great feeling of disgust for worldly affairs came over him.

verses.sanskrit: स चाहेदम् अहो कष्टं वृथात्मा मे ऽनुतापितः । न धर्माय न कामाय यस्यार्थायास ईदृशः
verses.transliteration: sa cāhedam aho kaṣṭaṃ vṛthātmā me 'nutāpitaḥ / na dharmāya na kāmāya yasyārthāyāsa īdṛśaḥ
verses.translation: He then said to himself: 'Alas, how painful to trouble myself that much with all this toiling for money that brought me neither pleasure, nor served the dharmic purpose.

verses.sanskrit: प्रायेणाथाः कदर्याणां न सुखाय कदाचन । इह चात्मोपतापाय मृतस्य नरकाय च
verses.transliteration: prāyeṇāthāḥ kadaryāṇāṃ na sukhāya kadācana / iha cātmopatāpāya mṛtasya narakāya ca
verses.translation: In general the wealth of misers never ever results in any happiness: in this life it leads to self-torment and when they die they end up in hell with it.

verses.sanskrit: यशो यशस्विनां शुद्धं श्लाघ्या ये गुणिनां गुणाः । लोभः स्व्-अल्पो ऽपि तान् हन्ति श्वित्रो रूपम् इवेप्सितम्
verses.transliteration: yaśo yaśasvināṃ śuddhaṃ ślāghyā ye guṇināṃ guṇāḥ / lobhaḥ sv-alpo 'pi tān hanti śvitro rūpam ivepsitam
verses.translation: However pure the reputation of the famous may be or however praiseworthy the qualities of the virtuous are, it is all destroyed with a little greed, just like what white leprosy [vitiligo] does with an enchanting, physical beauty.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थस्य साधने सिद्धे उत्कर्षे रक्षणे व्यये । नाशोपभोग आयासस् त्रासश् चिन्ता भ्रमो नृणाम्
verses.transliteration: arthasya sādhane siddhe utkarṣe rakṣaṇe vyaye / nāśopabhoga āyāsas trāsaś cintā bhramo nṛṇām
verses.translation: In the building up, realizing, increasing, protecting, spending, losing of and rejoicing with capital, man must toil, fear, worry and live with uncertainty.

verses.sanskrit: स्तेयं हिंसानृतं दम्भः कामः क्रोधः स्मयो मदः । भेदो वैरम् अविश्वासः संस्पर्धा व्यसनानि च
verses.transliteration: steyaṃ hiṃsānṛtaṃ dambhaḥ kāmaḥ krodhaḥ smayo madaḥ / bhedo vairam aviśvāsaḥ saṃspardhā vyasanāni ca
verses.translation: Theft, violence, lies, duplicity, lust, anger, perplexity, pride, discord, enmity, lack of faith, competition and [the three] dangers [of intoxication, promiscuity and gambling, see also 1.17: 24], are the fifteen unwanted things known by man as the consequence of fostering riches. He who desires the ultimate benefit in life should therefore keep the undesirable, which poses as wealth, at a great distance.

verses.sanskrit: एते पञ्चदशानर्था ह्य् अर्थ-मूला मता नृणाम् । तस्माद् अनर्थम् अर्थाख्यं श्रेयो-ऽर्थी दूरतस् त्यजेत्
verses.transliteration: ete pañcadaśānarthā hy artha-mūlā matā nṛṇām / tasmād anartham arthākhyaṃ śreyo-'rthī dūratas tyajet
verses.translation: Theft, violence, lies, duplicity, lust, anger, perplexity, pride, discord, enmity, lack of faith, competition and [the three] dangers [of intoxication, promiscuity and gambling, see also 1.17: 24], are the fifteen unwanted things known by man as the consequence of fostering riches. He who desires the ultimate benefit in life should therefore keep the undesirable, which poses as wealth, at a great distance.

verses.sanskrit: भिद्यन्ते भ्रातरो दाराः पितरः सुहृदस् तथा । एकास्निग्धाः काकिणिना सद्यः सर्वे ऽरयः कृताः
verses.transliteration: bhidyante bhrātaro dārāḥ pitaraḥ suhṛdas tathā / ekāsnigdhāḥ kākiṇinā sadyaḥ sarve 'rayaḥ kṛtāḥ
verses.translation: One's brothers, wife, parents and friends who are unified in love, all, from one moment to the next, turn into enemies over a single penny.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थेनाल्पीयसा ह्य् एते संरब्धा दीप्त-मन्यवः । त्यजन्त्य् आशु स्पृधो घ्नन्ति सहसोत्सृज्य सौहृदम्
verses.transliteration: arthenālpīyasā hy ete saṃrabdhā dīpta-manyavaḥ / tyajanty āśu spṛdho ghnanti sahasotsṛjya sauhṛdam
verses.translation: For the smallest amount of money they agitated give in to anger, and very quickly, as an adversary out for destruction, forget their goodwill and turn you down in the wink of an eye.

verses.sanskrit: लब्ध्वा जन्मामर-प्रार्थ्यं मानुष्यं तद् द्विजाग्र्यताम् । तद् अनादृत्य ये स्वार्थं घ्नन्ति यान्त्य् अशुभां गतिम्
verses.transliteration: labdhvā janmāmara-prārthyaṃ mānuṣyaṃ tad dvijāgryatām / tad anādṛtya ye svārthaṃ ghnanti yānty aśubhāṃ gatim
verses.translation: They who do not appreciate it as a human being to have achieved a birth the immortals pray for, with next to that [having the option of even] a superior second birth, destroy their self-interest and head for an unfavorable destination [see also B.G. 16: 19-20].

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गापवर्गयोर् द्वारं प्राप्य लोकम् इमं पुमान् । द्रविणे को ऽनुषज्जेत मर्त्यो ऽनर्थस्य धामनि
verses.transliteration: svargāpavargayor dvāraṃ prāpya lokam imaṃ pumān / draviṇe ko 'nuṣajjeta martyo 'narthasya dhāmani
verses.translation: What person who achieved this human life, this gateway to heaven and liberation, would attach to property, a realm of meaninglessness where one is subject to death?

verses.sanskrit: देवर्षि-पितृ-भूतानि ज्ञातीन् बन्धूंश् च भागिनः । असंविभज्य चात्मानं यक्ष-वित्तः पतत्य् अधः
verses.transliteration: devarṣi-pitṛ-bhūtāni jñātīn bandhūṃś ca bhāginaḥ / asaṃvibhajya cātmānaṃ yakṣa-vittaḥ pataty adhaḥ
verses.translation: When one does not share with those who deserve a share - the greater family of the gods, the seers, the forefathers, one's relatives, the living entities and oneself - one falls down like a money minded Yaksha.

verses.sanskrit: व्यर्थयार्थेहया वित्तं प्रमत्तस्य वयो बलम् । कुशला येन सिध्यन्ति जरठः किं नु साधये
verses.transliteration: vyarthayārthehayā vittaṃ pramattasya vayo balam / kuśalā yena sidhyanti jaraṭhaḥ kiṃ nu sādhaye
verses.translation: What can one do as an old man when one, maddened by one's youth, strength and wealth - the means by which a smart man settles for his perfection - has wasted one's life endeavoring for money [and status etc., see B.G. 3: 35]?

verses.sanskrit: कस्मात् सङ्क्लिश्यते विद्वान् व्यर्थयार्थेहयासकृत् । कस्यचिन् मायया नूनं लोको ऽयं सु-विमोहितः
verses.transliteration: kasmāt saṅkliśyate vidvān vyarthayārthehayāsakṛt / kasyacin māyayā nūnaṃ loko 'yaṃ su-vimohitaḥ
verses.translation: How does [even] a man of intelligence fall victim to a never ending, vain pursuit of wealth? All the world is most bewildered enchanted by some kind of inescapable illusory power!

verses.sanskrit: किं धनैर् धन-दैर् वा किं कामैर् वा काम-दैर् उत । मृत्युना ग्रस्यमानस्य कर्मभिर् वोत जन्म-दैः
verses.transliteration: kiṃ dhanair dhana-dair vā kiṃ kāmair vā kāma-dair uta / mṛtyunā grasyamānasya karmabhir vota janma-daiḥ
verses.translation: What is the use of the goods or those who provide them, or what would be the use of the objects of desire or the people who try to satisfy you? Or, differently stated, of what use is it for someone in the grip of death to be engaged in fruitful activities that only lead to yet another birth?

verses.sanskrit: नूनं मे भगवांस् तुष्टः सर्व-देव-मयो हरिः । येन नीतो दशाम् एतां निर्वेदश् चात्मनः प्लवः
verses.transliteration: nūnaṃ me bhagavāṃs tuṣṭaḥ sarva-deva-mayo hariḥ / yena nīto daśām etāṃ nirvedaś cātmanaḥ plavaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord, the Supreme Personality who comprises all the gods and who, satisfied with me, led me to this condition of detachment, constitutes the boat for the soul [to cross the material ocean. See also 11.17: 44].

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं कालावशेषेण शोषयिष्ये ऽङ्गम् आत्मनः । अप्रमत्तो ऽखिल-स्वार्थे यदि स्यात् सिद्ध आत्मनि
verses.transliteration: so 'haṃ kālāvaśeṣeṇa śoṣayiṣye 'ṅgam ātmanaḥ / apramatto 'khila-svārthe yadi syāt siddha ātmani
verses.translation: With the time remaining [in my life] I will, free from confusion about my true self-interest, restrict my body to the minimum and find peace within my self [see also 2.2: 3, 7.12: 6].

verses.sanskrit: तत्र माम् अनुमोदेरन् देवास् त्रि-भुवनेश्वराः । मुहूर्तेन ब्रह्म-लोकं खट्वाङ्गः समसाधयत्
verses.transliteration: tatra mām anumoderan devās tri-bhuvaneśvarāḥ / muhūrtena brahma-lokaṃ khaṭvāṅgaḥ samasādhayat
verses.translation: May the gods, the controllers of the three worlds, with this be pleased with me. Was it not Khathvnga [see 9.9: 41-47] who achieved the spiritual abode in a single moment?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच इत्य् अभिप्रेत्य मनसा ह्य् आवन्त्यो द्विज-सत्तमः । उन्मुच्य हृदय-ग्रन्थीन् शान्तो भिक्षुर् अभून् मुनिः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca ity abhipretya manasā hy āvantyo dvija-sattamaḥ / unmucya hṛdaya-granthīn śānto bhikṣur abhūn muniḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Thus making up his mind, the most pious brahmin from Avant untied the knots [of desire] in his heart and became a peaceful, silent mendicant.

verses.sanskrit: स चचार महीम् एतां संयतात्मेन्द्रियानिलः । भिक्षार्थं नगर-ग्रामान् असङ्गो ऽलक्षितो ऽविशत्
verses.transliteration: sa cacāra mahīm etāṃ saṃyatātmendriyānilaḥ / bhikṣārthaṃ nagara-grāmān asaṅgo 'lakṣito 'viśat
verses.translation: He wandered this world alone and inconspicuous, and entered, with his self, senses and vital air under control [see tridanda], its cities and villages to subsist on charity.

verses.sanskrit: तं वै प्रवयसं भिक्षुम् अवधूतम् असज्-जनाः । दृष्ट्वा पर्यभवन् भद्र बह्वीभिः परिभूतिभिः
verses.transliteration: taṃ vai pravayasaṃ bhikṣum avadhūtam asaj-janāḥ / dṛṣṭvā paryabhavan bhadra bahvībhiḥ paribhūtibhiḥ
verses.translation: Seeing him as an old, dirty beggar, low-class people dishonored him with many an insult, oh blessed soul.

verses.sanskrit: केचित् त्रि-वेणुं जगृहुर् एके पात्रं कमण्डलुम् । पीठं चैके ऽक्ष-सूत्रं च कन्थां चीराणि केचन । प्रदाय च पुनस् तानि दर्शितान्य् आददुर् मुनेः
verses.transliteration: kecit tri-veṇuṃ jagṛhur eke pātraṃ kamaṇḍalum / pīṭhaṃ caike 'kṣa-sūtraṃ ca kanthāṃ cīrāṇi kecana / pradāya ca punas tāni darśitāny ādadur muneḥ
verses.translation: Some stole away his triple staff, his begging bowl, his water pot and his seat, while others took his prayer beads and his torn rags. Showing them to him they offered them back and then took them again away from the sage.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं च भैक्ष्य-सम्पन्नं भुञ्जानस्य सरित्-तटे । मूत्रयन्ति च पापिष्ठाः ष्ठीवन्त्य् अस्य च मूर्धनि
verses.transliteration: annaṃ ca bhaikṣya-sampannaṃ bhuñjānasya sarit-taṭe / mūtrayanti ca pāpiṣṭhāḥ ṣṭhīvanty asya ca mūrdhani
verses.translation: When he at the river shore wanted to enjoy his share of the food he had acquired by begging, the grave sinners urinated upon it and spat on his head.

verses.sanskrit: यत-वाचं वाचयन्ति ताडयन्ति न वक्ति चेत् । तर्जयन्त्य् अपरे वाग्भिः स्तेनो ऽयम् इति वादिनः । बध्नन्ति रज्ज्वा तं केचिद् बध्यतां बध्यताम् इति
verses.transliteration: yata-vācaṃ vācayanti tāḍayanti na vakti cet / tarjayanty apare vāgbhiḥ steno 'yam iti vādinaḥ / badhnanti rajjvā taṃ kecid badhyatāṃ badhyatām iti
verses.translation: He who after his vow of silence did not speak, they would challenge to speak, beating him when he kept silent. Some shouted: 'This one is a thief' while others said: 'Tie him up, bind him!' and bound him in ropes.

verses.sanskrit: क्षिपन्त्य् एके ऽवजानन्त एष धर्म-ध्वजः शठः । क्षीण-वित्त इमां वृत्तिम् अग्रहीत् स्व-जनोज्झितः
verses.transliteration: kṣipanty eke 'vajānanta eṣa dharma-dhvajaḥ śaṭhaḥ / kṣīṇa-vitta imāṃ vṛttim agrahīt sva-janojjhitaḥ
verses.translation: Some taunted him with disrespect like: 'This one is a religious hypocrite, a cheater who lost his wealth, was thrown out by his family and has now taken to this profession.'

verses.sanskrit: अहो एष महा-सारो धृतिमान् गिरि-राड् इव । मौनेन साधयत्य् अर्थं बक-वद् दृढ-निश्चयः
verses.transliteration: aho eṣa mahā-sāro dhṛtimān giri-rāḍ iva / maunena sādhayaty arthaṃ baka-vad dṛḍha-niścayaḥ
verses.translation: 'See how this person, who in his silence pursues his goal as powerful and steadfast as the king of the mountains, is as firmly determined as a [deceptive] heron.' Some ridiculed him speaking thus, while others passed foul air and, binding him in chains, kept the brahmin captive like a pet animal.

verses.sanskrit: इत्य् एके विहसन्त्य् एनम् एके दुर्वातयन्ति च । तं बबन्धुर् निरुरुधुर् यथा क्रीडनकं द्विजम्
verses.transliteration: ity eke vihasanty enam eke durvātayanti ca / taṃ babandhur nirurudhur yathā krīḍanakaṃ dvijam
verses.translation: 'See how this person, who in his silence pursues his goal as powerful and steadfast as the king of the mountains, is as firmly determined as a [deceptive] heron.' Some ridiculed him speaking thus, while others passed foul air and, binding him in chains, kept the brahmin captive like a pet animal.

verses.sanskrit: एवं स भौतिकं दुःखं दैविकं दैहिकं च यत् । भोक्तव्यम् आत्मनो दिष्टं प्राप्तं प्राप्तम् अबुध्यत
verses.transliteration: evaṃ sa bhautikaṃ duḥkhaṃ daivikaṃ daihikaṃ ca yat / bhoktavyam ātmano diṣṭaṃ prāptaṃ prāptam abudhyata
verses.translation: Thus subjected to [the three types of] impositions, as caused by other living beings, by higher powers and by his own nature [see kles'a], he understood that whatever came his way, befell him because of fate.

verses.sanskrit: परिभूत इमां गाथाम् अगायत नराधमैः । पातयद्भिः स्व धर्म-स्थो धृतिम् आस्थाय सात्त्विकीम्
verses.transliteration: paribhūta imāṃ gāthām agāyata narādhamaiḥ / pātayadbhiḥ sva dharma-stho dhṛtim āsthāya sāttvikīm
verses.translation: Being insulted by lowly people trying to get the better of him he, fixed in goodness keeping firm to his duty, sang the following song [see also B.G. 18: 33].

verses.sanskrit: द्विज उवाच नायं जनो मे सुख-दुःख-हेतुर् न देवतात्मा ग्रह-कर्म-कालाः । मनः परं कारणम् आमनन्ति संसार-चक्रं परिवर्तयेद् यत्
verses.transliteration: dvija uvāca nāyaṃ jano me sukha-duḥkha-hetur na devatātmā graha-karma-kālāḥ / manaḥ paraṃ kāraṇam āmananti saṃsāra-cakraṃ parivartayed yat
verses.translation: The brahmin said: 'These people are not the cause of my happiness or distress, nor can I blame the demigods, my body, the planets, my karma or the time. It is, according to the standard authorities [the s'ruti], nothing but the mind that causes someone to rotate in the cycle of material life.

verses.sanskrit: मनो गुणान् वै सृजते बलीयस् ततश् च कर्माणि विलक्षणानि । शुक्लानि कृष्णान्य् अथ लोहितानि तेभ्यः स-वर्णाः सृतयो भवन्ति
verses.transliteration: mano guṇān vai sṛjate balīyas tataś ca karmāṇi vilakṣaṇāni / śuklāni kṛṣṇāny atha lohitāni tebhyaḥ sa-varṇāḥ sṛtayo bhavanti
verses.translation: The mind acquiring the qualities of the modes, becomes very strong because of them and thus gives rise to the different sorts of white [good], red [passionate] and black [ignorant] activities, that lead to the conditions [the societal classes] corresponding to those colors.

verses.sanskrit: अनीह आत्मा मनसा समीहता हिरण्-मयो मत्-सख उद्विचष्टे । मनः स्व-लिङ्गं परिगृह्य कामान् जुषन् निबद्धो गुण-सङ्गतो ऽसौ
verses.transliteration: anīha ātmā manasā samīhatā hiraṇ-mayo mat-sakha udvicaṣṭe / manaḥ sva-liṅgaṃ parigṛhya kāmān juṣan nibaddho guṇa-saṅgato 'sau
verses.translation: The uninvolved Supersoul of transcendental enlightenment as a friend exists along with - and perceives - the struggling mind, that, with the image of the world it carries, embraces the objects of desire. It is in the engagement with the modes of nature that the individual soul [bewildered by that mind] gets entangled in attachment [see also B.G. 3.42-43].

verses.sanskrit: दानं स्व-धर्मो नियमो यमश् च श्रुतं च कर्माणि च सद्-व्रतानि । सर्वे मनो-निग्रह-लक्षणान्ताः परो हि योगो मनसः समाधिः
verses.transliteration: dānaṃ sva-dharmo niyamo yamaś ca śrutaṃ ca karmāṇi ca sad-vratāni / sarve mano-nigraha-lakṣaṇāntāḥ paro hi yogo manasaḥ samādhiḥ
verses.translation: Charity, one's prescribed duty, niyama, yama, and listening [to the scripture], pious works and the purification by vows, all entail the subduing of the mind and have as their aim the absorption of the mind [samdhi] that constitutes the supreme [self-realization] of yoga.

verses.sanskrit: समाहितं यस्य मनः प्रशान्तं दानादिभिः किं वद तस्य कृत्यम् । असंयतं यस्य मनो विनश्यद् दानादिभिश् चेद् अपरं किम् एभिः
verses.transliteration: samāhitaṃ yasya manaḥ praśāntaṃ dānādibhiḥ kiṃ vada tasya kṛtyam / asaṃyataṃ yasya mano vinaśyad dānādibhiś ced aparaṃ kim ebhiḥ
verses.translation: What would be the use of charitative rituals and such for someone whose mind has been pacified by perfectly being absorbed [in Him]? Or, why would one in addition, occupy oneself with these processes of distribution and such when one has lost one's way with a mind not under control?

verses.sanskrit: मनो-वशे ऽन्ये ह्य् अभवन् स्म देवा मनश् च नान्यस्य वशं समेति । भीष्मो हि देवः सहसः सहीयान् युञ्ज्याद् वशे तं स हि देव-देवः
verses.transliteration: mano-vaśe 'nye hy abhavan sma devā manaś ca nānyasya vaśaṃ sameti / bhīṣmo hi devaḥ sahasaḥ sahīyān yuñjyād vaśe taṃ sa hi deva-devaḥ
verses.translation: Other gods [and the senses they represent] have always fallen under the control of the mind that itself never allows the control of anything [or anyone] else. It constitutes a fearsome god stronger than the strongest and the One who [in the form of His mantras] can bring it under control, is therefore the God of gods [see also B.G. 6: 35-36, *].

verses.sanskrit: तम् दुर्जयं शत्रुम् असह्य-वेगम् अरुन्-तुदं तन् न विजित्य केचित् । कुर्वन्त्य् असद्-विग्रहम् अत्र मर्त्यैर् मित्राण्य् उदासीन-रिपून् विमूढाः
verses.transliteration: tam durjayaṃ śatrum asahya-vegam arun-tudaṃ tan na vijitya kecit / kurvanty asad-vigraham atra martyair mitrāṇy udāsīna-ripūn vimūḍhāḥ
verses.translation: When one [being worldly engaged] fails to subdue that difficult to conquer enemy [see B.G. 6: 6] tormenting and attacking because of its unmanageable urges, some therefore being utterly bewildered create useless quarrels and are thus, with the mortals in this world, friends, neuters and rivals.

verses.sanskrit: देहं मनो-मात्रम् इमं गृहीत्वा ममाहम् इत्य् अन्ध-धियो मनुष्याः । एषो ऽहम् अन्यो ऽयम् इति भ्रमेण दुरन्त-पारे तमसि भ्रमन्ति
verses.transliteration: dehaṃ mano-mātram imaṃ gṛhītvā mamāham ity andha-dhiyo manuṣyāḥ / eṣo 'ham anyo 'yam iti bhrameṇa duranta-pāre tamasi bhramanti
verses.translation: People whose entire mind is seized by their body, think in terms of 'I' and 'mine' and are thus blinded in their intelligence. Because of this difficult to defeat illusion of 'this I am' and 'that is someone else', they wander around in darkness.

verses.sanskrit: जनस् तु हेतुः सुख-दुःखयोश् चेत् किम् आत्मनश् चात्र हि भौमयोस् तत् । जिह्वां क्वचित् सन्दशति स्व-दद्भिस् तद्-वेदनायां कतमाय कुप्येत्
verses.transliteration: janas tu hetuḥ sukha-duḥkhayoś cet kim ātmanaś cātra hi bhaumayos tat / jihvāṃ kvacit sandaśati sva-dadbhis tad-vedanāyāṃ katamāya kupyet
verses.translation: When you say that [adhibhautika] another human being is the cause of your happiness or distress, you may wonder what this means for the soul; happiness and distress [thus seen] belong to the earth [and not to the soul who finds happiness by self-realization]. With whom can you be angry about the pain when your tongue happens to be bitten by your own teeth?

verses.sanskrit: दुःखस्य हेतुर् यदि देवतास् तु किम् आत्मनस् तत्र विकारयोस् तत् । यद् अङ्गम् अङ्गेन निहन्यते क्वचित् क्रुध्येत कस्मै पुरुषः स्व-देहे
verses.transliteration: duḥkhasya hetur yadi devatās tu kim ātmanas tatra vikārayos tat / yad aṅgam aṅgena nihanyate kvacit krudhyeta kasmai puruṣaḥ sva-dehe
verses.translation: When you [adhidaivika] say that the gods are responsible for your suffering, then how would that relate to your soul? That sufferig pertains to the changeable nature [of the senses and their rulers, the soul stands apart from]. With whom should you be angry when one limb of your body hurts another limb?

verses.sanskrit: आत्मा यदि स्यात् सुख-दुःख-हेतुः किम् अन्यतस् तत्र निज-स्वभावः । न ह्य् आत्मनो ऽन्यद् यदि तन् मृषा स्यात् क्रुध्येत कस्मान् न सुखं न दुःखम्
verses.transliteration: ātmā yadi syāt sukha-duḥkha-hetuḥ kim anyatas tatra nija-svabhāvaḥ / na hy ātmano 'nyad yadi tan mṛṣā syāt krudhyeta kasmān na sukhaṃ na duḥkham
verses.translation: When you say that the soul itself [adhytmika] would be the cause of your happiness and distress, such a difference would be part of your own nature. But how can one blame anyone when there is only the soul and nothing outside - neither happiness nor distress? That difference after all would be unreal then [see B.G. 2: 14].

verses.sanskrit: ग्रहा निमित्तं सुख-दुःखयोश् चेत् किम् आत्मनो ऽजस्य जनस्य ते वै । ग्रहैर् ग्रहस्यैव वदन्ति पीडां क्रुध्येत कस्मै पुरुषस् ततो ऽन्यः
verses.transliteration: grahā nimittaṃ sukha-duḥkhayoś cet kim ātmano 'jasya janasya te vai / grahair grahasyaiva vadanti pīḍāṃ krudhyeta kasmai puruṣas tato 'nyaḥ
verses.translation: If the planets would be the cause of one's happiness and distress, how would that relate to the soul who is unborn? The heavenly bodies relate to that what is born. A planet is only troubled by other planets, so they [the astrologers] say, so with whom should the living being distinguished from his body [and his planetary positions] be angry then?

verses.sanskrit: कर्मास्तु हेतुः सुख-दुःखयोश् चेत् किम् आत्मनस् तद् धि जडाजडत्वे । देहस् त्व् अचित् पुरुषो ऽयं सुपर्णः क्रुध्येत कस्मै न हि कर्म मूलम्
verses.transliteration: karmāstu hetuḥ sukha-duḥkhayoś cet kim ātmanas tad dhi jaḍājaḍatve / dehas tv acit puruṣo 'yaṃ suparṇaḥ krudhyeta kasmai na hi karma mūlam
verses.translation: If you assume karma to be the cause of your happiness and distress, what does that karma then mean to your soul? Certain is that with the animating person on the one hand and this animated body on the other hand endowed with consciousness [that on itself is] not alive, neither of both constitute the root cause of your karma. What is there left to be upset about then?

verses.sanskrit: कालस् तु हेतुः सुख-दुःखयोश् चेत् किम् आत्मनस् तत्र तद्-आत्मको ऽसौ । नाग्नेर् हि तापो न हिमस्य तत् स्यात् क्रुध्येत कस्मै न परस्य द्वन्द्वम्
verses.transliteration: kālas tu hetuḥ sukha-duḥkhayoś cet kim ātmanas tatra tad-ātmako 'sau / nāgner hi tāpo na himasya tat syāt krudhyeta kasmai na parasya dvandvam
verses.translation: And if we say that time would be the cause of our happiness and distress, where do we find the soul in that notion? The soul is not equal to the time [phenomena], the way fire is not equal to its heat and snow is not equal to [cold]. With whom must one be angry when there is no duality in the transcendental position [see also B.G. 18: 16 and time quotes]?

verses.sanskrit: न केनचित् क्वापि कथञ्चनास्य द्वन्द्वोपरागः परतः परस्य । यथाहमः संसृति-रूपिणः स्याद् एवं प्रबुद्धो न बिभेति भूतैः
verses.transliteration: na kenacit kvāpi kathañcanāsya dvandvoparāgaḥ parataḥ parasya / yathāhamaḥ saṃsṛti-rūpiṇaḥ syād evaṃ prabuddho na bibheti bhūtaiḥ
verses.translation: For him, [the spiritual soul] superior in transcendence, there is not from anyone, from whatever side or in any way the influence of the duality [of happiness and distress], the influence of the world of opposites, as can be seen with the arising false ego [of the mind being seized] that shapes one's material existence. He who awakens to this intelligence has nothing to fear from the material creation [with all her living beings].

verses.sanskrit: एतां स आस्थाय परात्म-निष्ठाम् अध्यासितां पूर्वतमैर् महर्षिभिः । अहं तरिष्यामि दुरन्त-पारं तमो मुकुन्दाङ्घ्रि-निषेवयैव
verses.transliteration: etāṃ sa āsthāya parātma-niṣṭhām adhyāsitāṃ pūrvatamair maharṣibhiḥ / ahaṃ tariṣyāmi duranta-pāraṃ tamo mukundāṅghri-niṣevayaiva
verses.translation: By the worship of Mukunda's feet I will cross over the difficult to defeat ocean of material nescience. I am certain of this because of the foregoing great seers [or cryas] who were firmly fixed in the worship of the Supreme Soul [see also B.G. 6: 1-2].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच निर्विद्य नष्ट-द्रविणे गत-क्लमः प्रव्रज्य गां पर्यटमान इत्थम् । निराकृतो ऽसद्भिर् अपि स्व-धर्माद् अकम्पितो ऽमूं मुनिर् आह गाथाम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca nirvidya naṣṭa-draviṇe gata-klamaḥ pravrajya gāṃ paryaṭamāna ittham / nirākṛto 'sadbhir api sva-dharmād akampito 'mūṃ munir āha gāthām
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'While he had lost his wealth and gotten detached, while he had left his home and free from moroseness traveled the earth, the sage, despite being insulted by rascals, did not forsake his duties and spoke this song.

verses.sanskrit: सुख-दुःख-प्रदो नान्यः पुरुषस्यात्म-विभ्रमः । मित्रोदासीन-रिपवः संसारस् तमसः कृतः
verses.transliteration: sukha-duḥkha-prado nānyaḥ puruṣasyātma-vibhramaḥ / mitrodāsīna-ripavaḥ saṃsāras tamasaḥ kṛtaḥ
verses.translation: There is no other cause of happiness and grief than the bewilderment of someone's mind that in material life out of ignorance created its friends, neuters and enemies [see also 10.32: 17-22, B.G. 9: 29].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् सर्वात्मना तात निगृहाण मनो धिया । मय्य् आवेशितया युक्त एतावान् योग-सङ्ग्रहः
verses.transliteration: tasmāt sarvātmanā tāta nigṛhāṇa mano dhiyā / mayy āveśitayā yukta etāvān yoga-saṅgrahaḥ
verses.translation: Therefore, My best, bring in every respect with an intelligence absorbed in Me, the mind under control and [attain] thus being connected the essence of the science of yoga [see also S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka verse 1].

verses.sanskrit: य एतां भिक्षुणा गीतां ब्रह्म-निष्ठां समाहितः । धारयञ् छ्रावयञ् छृण्वन् द्वन्द्वैर् नैवाभिभूयते
verses.transliteration: ya etāṃ bhikṣuṇā gītāṃ brahma-niṣṭhāṃ samāhitaḥ / dhārayañ chrāvayañ chṛṇvan dvandvair naivābhibhūyate
verses.translation: Whoever with full attention meditates on, makes others listen or listens himself to this [song] based upon the knowledge of the Absolute as sung by the mendicant, will for certain never [again] be overwhelmed by the dualities [of happiness and grief].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच अथ ते सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि साङ्ख्यं पूर्वैर् विनिश्चितम् । यद् विज्ञाय पुमान् सद्यो जह्याद् वैकल्पिकं भ्रमम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca atha te sampravakṣyāmi sāṅkhyaṃ pūrvair viniścitam / yad vijñāya pumān sadyo jahyād vaikalpikaṃ bhramam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'I shall now discuss with you the analytic knowledge as established by the classical authorities. Knowing this a person can immediately give up the bewilderment based upon the material duality.

verses.sanskrit: आसीज् ज्ञानम् अथो अर्थ एकम् एवाविकल्पितम् । यदा विवेक-निपुणा आदौ कृत-युगे ऽयुगे
verses.transliteration: āsīj jñānam atho artha ekam evāvikalpitam / yadā viveka-nipuṇā ādau kṛta-yuge 'yuge
verses.translation: In the first age of dutifulness [Krita], in the beginning when there were [only] persons expert in [spiritual] discrimination, as also before that time [during the period of annihilation], the Knower was simply one and the same as the [universe] known [see also 11.22: 29].

verses.sanskrit: तन् माया-फल-रूपेण केवलं निर्विकल्पितम् । वाङ्-मनो-ऽगोचरं सत्यं द्विधा समभवद् बृहत्
verses.transliteration: tan māyā-phala-rūpeṇa kevalaṃ nirvikalpitam / vāṅ-mano-'gocaraṃ satyaṃ dvidhā samabhavad bṛhat
verses.translation: That One Great Undifferentiated Truth inaccessible to speech and mind [Brahman], turned into the two forms of material nature on the one hand and the enjoyment [of the enjoyer of that result] on the other hand [see 11.22].

verses.sanskrit: तयोर् एकतरो ह्य् अर्थः प्रकृतिः सोभयात्मिका । ज्ञानं त्व् अन्यतमो भावः पुरुषः सो ऽभिधीयते
verses.transliteration: tayor ekataro hy arthaḥ prakṛtiḥ sobhayātmikā / jñānaṃ tv anyatamo bhāvaḥ puruṣaḥ so 'bhidhīyate
verses.translation: One essential half, material nature [prakriti], is she [the 'mother'] who is of a dualistic nature, while he, the other entity, the knower, is called the purusha [the enjoying person or male principle].

verses.sanskrit: तमो रजः सत्त्वम् इति प्रकृतेर् अभवन् गुणाः । मया प्रक्षोभ्यमाणायाः पुरुषानुमतेन च
verses.transliteration: tamo rajaḥ sattvam iti prakṛter abhavan guṇāḥ / mayā prakṣobhyamāṇāyāḥ puruṣānumatena ca
verses.translation: By My agitation of the elementary nature [in the form of time, of kla], the modes of tamas, rajas and sattva [the gunas] have manifested in order to fulfill the desires of the living entity.

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्यः समभवत् सूत्रं महान् सूत्रेण संयुतः । ततो विकुर्वतो जातो यो ऽहङ्कारो विमोहनः
verses.transliteration: tebhyaḥ samabhavat sūtraṃ mahān sūtreṇa saṃyutaḥ / tato vikurvato jāto yo 'haṅkāro vimohanaḥ
verses.translation: From them the thread [the activating principle of the stra] arose, together with the principle of intelligence [mahat]. From the transformation of mahat came about the false ego [the ahankra of the purusha who identifies with the object of perception] that is the cause of bewilderment.

verses.sanskrit: वैकारिकस् तैजसश् च तामसश् चेत्य् अहं त्रि-वृत् । तन्-मात्रेन्द्रिय-मनसां कारणं चिद्-अचिन्-मयः
verses.transliteration: vaikārikas taijasaś ca tāmasaś cety ahaṃ tri-vṛt / tan-mātrendriya-manasāṃ kāraṇaṃ cid-acin-mayaḥ
verses.translation: That I-awareness is thus of the three [guna] categories and [accordingly makes] with clarity, emotion and ignorance [alternately use of] the sense objects [tanmtra], the senses [indriyas] and the mind [manas]. Thus it [the identified self] constitutes the cause of understanding and not understanding [the so-called conscious and unconscious].

verses.sanskrit: अर्थस् तन्-मात्रिकाज् जज्ञे तामसाद् इन्द्रियाणि च । तैजसाद् देवता आसन्न् एकादश च वैकृतात्
verses.transliteration: arthas tan-mātrikāj jajñe tāmasād indriyāṇi ca / taijasād devatā āsann ekādaśa ca vaikṛtāt
verses.translation: The darkness of the false ego gave rise to the subtle sensations [or perceptions] of gross matter, its emotion awakened the senses and the clarity of the identified self called for the eleven gods [see deva].

verses.sanskrit: मया सञ्चोदिता भावाः सर्वे संहत्य-कारिणः । अण्डम् उत्पादयाम् आसुर् ममायतनम् उत्तमम्
verses.transliteration: mayā sañcoditā bhāvāḥ sarve saṃhatya-kāriṇaḥ / aṇḍam utpādayām āsur mamāyatanam uttamam
verses.translation: Because all the elements combined to function under My influence, they brought about the egg of the universe that serves as My supreme residence [see from 11.22: 18].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्न् अहं समभवम् अण्डे सलिल-संस्थितौ । मम नाभ्याम् अभूत् पद्मं विश्वाख्यं तत्र चात्म-भूः
verses.transliteration: tasminn ahaṃ samabhavam aṇḍe salila-saṃsthitau / mama nābhyām abhūt padmaṃ viśvākhyaṃ tatra cātma-bhūḥ
verses.translation: I appeared [as Nryana] in the egg that was situated in the water of the causal ocean, and from My navel a lotus arose that is known as the universe. On that lotus the self-born one found his existence [Brahm, see 3.8].

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽसृजत् तपसा युक्तो रजसा मद्-अनुग्रहात् । लोकान् स-पालान् विश्वात्मा भूर् भुवः स्वर् इति त्रिधा
verses.transliteration: so 'sṛjat tapasā yukto rajasā mad-anugrahāt / lokān sa-pālān viśvātmā bhūr bhuvaḥ svar iti tridhā
verses.translation: He, the soul of the universe endowed with passion, created from his penance by My mercy the three different worlds called earth, the atmosphere [or ether] and heaven [bhh, bhuvah and svaha], as also its rulers [see Gyatr and loka].

verses.sanskrit: देवानाम् ओक आसीत् स्वर् भूतानां च भुवः पदम् । मर्त्यादीनां च भूर् लोकः सिद्धानां त्रितयात् परम्
verses.transliteration: devānām oka āsīt svar bhūtānāṃ ca bhuvaḥ padam / martyādīnāṃ ca bhūr lokaḥ siddhānāṃ tritayāt param
verses.translation: Heaven became the residence of the demigods, the atmosphere the home of the ghostly spirits, the earthly places offered the humans and other living beings shelter and the place beyond these three is there for the Siddhas, the souls of perfection [Siddhaloka].

verses.sanskrit: अधो ऽसुराणां नागानां भूमेर् ओको ऽसृजत् प्रभुः । त्रि-लोक्यां गतयः सर्वाः कर्मणां त्रि-गुणात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: adho 'surāṇāṃ nāgānāṃ bhūmer oko 'sṛjat prabhuḥ / tri-lokyāṃ gatayaḥ sarvāḥ karmaṇāṃ tri-guṇātmanām
verses.translation: The places of the underworld were by the master created as the residence for all asuras ['unenlightened souls' or demons] and those perfect in their ego [the 'snakes', the Ngas]. All the destinations of the three worlds thus owe their existence to the fruitful activities proper to the modes [see B.G. 4: 17, 10.1: 42-43].

verses.sanskrit: योगस्य तपसश् चैव न्यासस्य गतयो ऽमलाः । महर् जनस् तपः सत्यं भक्ति-योगस्य मद्-गतिः
verses.transliteration: yogasya tapasaś caiva nyāsasya gatayo 'malāḥ / mahar janas tapaḥ satyaṃ bhakti-yogasya mad-gatiḥ
verses.translation: By penance, yoga and by forsaking [in sannysa] one is of the spotless destinations of mahar, janas, tapas and satya, but My destination [Vaikunthha] is reached by performing devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: मया कालात्मना धात्रा कर्म-युक्तम् इदं जगत् । गुण-प्रवाह एतस्मिन्न् उन्मज्जति निमज्जति
verses.transliteration: mayā kālātmanā dhātrā karma-yuktam idaṃ jagat / guṇa-pravāha etasminn unmajjati nimajjati
verses.translation: As arranged by Me, the Supporter, the Soul [the energy] of Time, one rises up from or drowns in the mighty stream of the modes of this world in which one is bound to performing fruitive labor.

verses.sanskrit: अणुर् बृहत् कृशः स्थूलो यो यो भावः प्रसिध्यति । सर्वो ऽप्य् उभय-संयुक्तः प्रकृत्या पुरुषेण च
