verses.transliteration: aṇur bṛhat kṛśaḥ sthūlo yo yo bhāvaḥ prasidhyati / sarvo 'py ubhaya-saṃyuktaḥ prakṛtyā puruṣeṇa ca
verses.translation: Whatever the small, the big, the thin and the massive of manifestation, is all brought about by the combination of material nature and its enjoyer [see also B.G. 18: 16].

verses.sanskrit: यस् तु यस्यादिर् अन्तश् च स वै मध्यं च तस्य सन् । विकारो व्यवहारार्थो यथा तैजस-पार्थिवाः
verses.transliteration: yas tu yasyādir antaś ca sa vai madhyaṃ ca tasya san / vikāro vyavahārārtho yathā taijasa-pārthivāḥ
verses.translation: That what constitutes the cause of something - common matters like things of gold and things of clay - is there in the beginning, during the life as also in the end of that what was produced and is subject to transformation [and is thus illusory as for its form, compare 6.16: 22, 10.87: 15, 11.22: 8].

verses.sanskrit: यद् उपादाय पूर्वस् तु भावो विकुरुते ऽपरम् । आदिर् अन्तो यदा यस्य तत् सत्यम् अभिधीयते
verses.transliteration: yad upādāya pūrvas tu bhāvo vikurute 'param / ādir anto yadā yasya tat satyam abhidhīyate
verses.translation: Something that serves as a previous ingredient of a thing that - as something different - constitutes a change of form of that ingredient, is called the true of something provided it is present from the beginning to the end [compare B.G. 2: 13, 2: 16].

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिर् यस्योपादानम् आधारः पुरुषः परः । सतो ऽभिव्यञ्जकः कालो ब्रह्म तत् त्रितयं त्व् अहम्
verses.transliteration: prakṛtir yasyopādānam ādhāraḥ puruṣaḥ paraḥ / sato 'bhivyañjakaḥ kālo brahma tat tritayaṃ tv aham
verses.translation: Material nature [prakriti] the foundation of which is constituted by the causal [transformed] ingredient of the Supreme Person [the purusha], together with that what is the agitating agent, viz. Time [kla], makes up the threefold of the Absolute Truth [Brahman] that I am.

verses.sanskrit: सर्गः प्रवर्तते तावत् पौर्वापर्येण नित्यशः । महान् गुण-विसर्गार्थः स्थित्य्-अन्तो यावद् ईक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: sargaḥ pravartate tāvat paurvāparyeṇa nityaśaḥ / mahān guṇa-visargārthaḥ sthity-anto yāvad īkṣaṇam
verses.translation: For as long as I look after it [or cast My glance], the grand creation will perpetually, for the sake of the variegation of its qualities, generation after generation continue to exist until its dissolution [see also B.G. 3: 24].

verses.sanskrit: विराण् मयासाद्यमानो लोक-कल्प-विकल्पकः । पञ्चत्वाय विशेषाय कल्पते भुवनैः सह
verses.transliteration: virāṇ mayāsādyamāno loka-kalpa-vikalpakaḥ / pañcatvāya viśeṣāya kalpate bhuvanaiḥ saha
verses.translation: When the form of the universe that is pervaded by Me has manifested the planetary variety of its time periods [of creation, maintenance and decay], this variety with its different worlds [losing its synergy] arrives at [a dissolution into] its five composing gross elements [see yugas, manvantaras, and B.G. 11: 13].

verses.sanskrit: अन्ने प्रलीयते मर्त्यम् अन्नं धानासु लीयते । धाना भूमौ प्रलीयन्ते भूमिर् गन्धे प्रलीयते
verses.transliteration: anne pralīyate martyam annaṃ dhānāsu līyate / dhānā bhūmau pralīyante bhūmir gandhe pralīyate
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: अप्सु प्रलीयते गन्ध आपश् च स्व-गुणे रसे । लीयते ज्योतिषि रसो ज्योती रूपे प्रलीयते
verses.transliteration: apsu pralīyate gandha āpaś ca sva-guṇe rase / līyate jyotiṣi raso jyotī rūpe pralīyate
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: रूपं वायौ स च स्पर्शे लीयते सो ऽपि चाम्बरे । अम्बरं शब्द-तन्-मात्र इन्द्रियाणि स्व-योनिषु
verses.transliteration: rūpaṃ vāyau sa ca sparśe līyate so 'pi cāmbare / ambaraṃ śabda-tan-mātra indriyāṇi sva-yoniṣu
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: योनिर् वैकारिके सौम्य लीयते मनसीश्वरे । शब्दो भूतादिम् अप्येति भूतादिर् महति प्रभुः
verses.transliteration: yonir vaikārike saumya līyate manasīśvare / śabdo bhūtādim apyeti bhūtādir mahati prabhuḥ
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: स लीयते महान् स्वेषु गुणेसु गुण-वत्तमः । ते ऽव्यक्ते सम्प्रलीयन्ते तत् काले लीयते ऽव्यये
verses.transliteration: sa līyate mahān sveṣu guṇesu guṇa-vattamaḥ / te 'vyakte sampralīyante tat kāle līyate 'vyaye
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: कालो माया-मये जीवे जीव आत्मनि मय्य् अजे । आत्मा केवल आत्म-स्थो विकल्पापाय-लक्षणः
verses.transliteration: kālo māyā-maye jīve jīva ātmani mayy aje / ātmā kevala ātma-stho vikalpāpāya-lakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: एवम् अन्वीक्षमाणस्य कथं वैकल्पिको भ्रमः । मनसो हृदि तिष्ठेत व्योम्नीवार्कोदये तमः
verses.transliteration: evam anvīkṣamāṇasya kathaṃ vaikalpiko bhramaḥ / manaso hṛdi tiṣṭheta vyomnīvārkodaye tamaḥ
verses.translation: Just as with the darkness when the sun rises in the sky, how can the bewilderment of the dual mind remain in the heart of the one who seriously studies this?

verses.sanskrit: एष साङ्ख्य-विधिः प्रोक्तः संशय-ग्रन्थि-भेदनः । प्रतिलोमानुलोमाभ्यां परावर-दृश मया
verses.transliteration: eṣa sāṅkhya-vidhiḥ proktaḥ saṃśaya-granthi-bhedanaḥ / pratilomānulomābhyāṃ parāvara-dṛśa mayā
verses.translation: This is what I, the Supervisor of the Spiritual and Material world, had to say concerning this Snkhya instruction of analysis [see also 3.25 - 3.33] that breaks through the bondage of doubts of the people who both go along with and go against nature.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच गुणानाम् असम्मिश्राणां पुमान् येन यथा भवेत् । तन् मे पुरुष-वर्येदम् उपधारय शंसतः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca guṇānām asammiśrāṇāṃ pumān yena yathā bhavet / tan me puruṣa-varyedam upadhāraya śaṃsataḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Oh best of persons, try to understand what I am about to say concerning the way someone is influenced by a certain mode of My material nature [*].

verses.sanskrit: शमो दमस् तितिक्षेक्षा तपः सत्यं दया स्मृतिः । तुष्टिस् त्यागो ऽस्पृहा श्रद्धा ह्रीर् दयादिः स्व-निर्वृतिः
verses.transliteration: śamo damas titikṣekṣā tapaḥ satyaṃ dayā smṛtiḥ / tuṣṭis tyāgo 'spṛhā śraddhā hrīr dayādiḥ sva-nirvṛtiḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: काम ईहा मदस् तृष्णा स्तम्भ आशीर् भिदा सुखम् । मदोत्साहो यशः-प्रीतिर् हास्यं वीर्यं बलोद्यमः
verses.transliteration: kāma īhā madas tṛṣṇā stambha āśīr bhidā sukham / madotsāho yaśaḥ-prītir hāsyaṃ vīryaṃ balodyamaḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: क्रोधो लोभो ऽनृतं हिंसा याच्ञा दम्भः क्लमः कलिः । शोक-मोहौ विषादार्ती निद्राशा भीर् अनुद्यमः
verses.transliteration: krodho lobho 'nṛtaṃ hiṃsā yācñā dambhaḥ klamaḥ kaliḥ / śoka-mohau viṣādārtī nidrāśā bhīr anudyamaḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वस्य रजसश् चैतास् तमसश् चानुपूर्वशः । वृत्तयो वर्णित-प्रायाः सन्निपातम् अथो शृणु
verses.transliteration: sattvasya rajasaś caitās tamasaś cānupūrvaśaḥ / vṛttayo varṇita-prāyāḥ sannipātam atho śṛṇu
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: सन्निपातस् त्व् अहम् इति ममेत्य् उद्धव या मतिः । व्यवहारः सन्निपातो मनो-मात्रेन्द्रियासुभिः
verses.transliteration: sannipātas tv aham iti mamety uddhava yā matiḥ / vyavahāraḥ sannipāto mano-mātrendriyāsubhiḥ
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, the notion of 'I am this way' and 'that is a trait of mine' that people have [in relation to these qualities] when they are engaged with their mind, senses, sense objects and life breath, reflects a combination of the modes [see also 11.23: 49, 11.24: 7, 11.24: 13].

verses.sanskrit: धर्मे चार्थे च कामे च यदासौ परिनिष्ठितः । गुणानां सन्निकर्षो ऽयं श्रद्धा-रति-धनावहः
verses.transliteration: dharme cārthe ca kāme ca yadāsau pariniṣṭhitaḥ / guṇānāṃ sannikarṣo 'yaṃ śraddhā-rati-dhanāvahaḥ
verses.translation: In case a person is fixed in his religiosity, economic development and sense gratification, [also] the resultant faith, wealth and enjoyment is an expression of the interaction of the different modes.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्ति-लक्षणे निष्ठा पुमान् यर्हि गृहाश्रमे । स्व-धर्मे चानु तिष्ठेत गुणानां समितिर् हि सा
verses.transliteration: pravṛtti-lakṣaṇe niṣṭhā pumān yarhi gṛhāśrame / sva-dharme cānu tiṣṭheta guṇānāṃ samitir hi sā
verses.translation: When a person in family life is of a dedication characterized by sense gratification and thereafter performs his religious duties, a combination of the modes is a fact.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषं सत्त्व-संयुक्तम् अनुमीयाच् छमादिभिः । कामादिभी रजो-युक्तं क्रोधाद्यैस् तमसा युतम्
verses.transliteration: puruṣaṃ sattva-saṃyuktam anumīyāc chamādibhiḥ / kāmādibhī rajo-yuktaṃ krodhādyais tamasā yutam
verses.translation: From someone's self-control can be deduced that he is endowed with goodness and so on, his lust is indicative of the mode of passion and such, and from his anger etcetera one may conclude that he is caught in ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: यदा भजति मां भक्त्या निरपेक्षः स्व-कर्मभिः । तं सत्त्व-प्रकृतिं विद्यात् पुरुषं स्त्रियम् एव वा
verses.transliteration: yadā bhajati māṃ bhaktyā nirapekṣaḥ sva-karmabhiḥ / taṃ sattva-prakṛtiṃ vidyāt puruṣaṃ striyam eva vā
verses.translation: When someone worships Me with devotion and indifference about the results of his labor, such a person should be understood to be of a practice of goodness, whether he is a man or a woman.

verses.sanskrit: यदा आशिष आशास्य मां भजेत स्व-कर्मभिः । तं रजः-प्रकृतिं विद्यात् हिंसाम् आशास्य तामसम्
verses.transliteration: yadā āśiṣa āśāsya māṃ bhajeta sva-karmabhiḥ / taṃ rajaḥ-prakṛtiṃ vidyāt hiṃsām āśāsya tāmasam
verses.translation: When one in the fulfillment of one's duties worships Me hoping for benedictions, such devotion must be understood as being of the nature of passion, and when one does so with violent intentions one is of ignorance [see also B.G. 17: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस् तम इति गुणा जीवस्य नैव मे । चित्त-जा यैस् तु भूतानां सज्जमानो निबध्यते
verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ rajas tama iti guṇā jīvasya naiva me / citta-jā yais tu bhūtānāṃ sajjamāno nibadhyate
verses.translation: The modes of sattva, tamas and rajas influence the [conditioned] individual but not Me; one is bound to them because they manifest in the mind and lead to attachment to life-forms and sense-objects [see also B.G. 4: 14].

verses.sanskrit: यदेतरौ जयेत् सत्त्वं भास्वरं विशदं शिवम् । तदा सुखेन युज्येत धर्म-ज्ञानादिभिः पुमान्
verses.transliteration: yadetarau jayet sattvaṃ bhāsvaraṃ viśadaṃ śivam / tadā sukhena yujyeta dharma-jñānādibhiḥ pumān
verses.translation: When the mode of goodness - which is pure, luminous and auspicious - predominates over the other two [of passion and ignorance], a person will be blessed with happiness, religiosity, knowledge and other good qualities [see also B.G. 14: 11, 18: 37].

verses.sanskrit: यदा जयेत् तमः सत्त्वं रजः सङ्गं भिदा चलम् । तदा दुःखेन युज्येत कर्मणा यशसा श्रिया
verses.transliteration: yadā jayet tamaḥ sattvaṃ rajaḥ saṅgaṃ bhidā calam / tadā duḥkhena yujyeta karmaṇā yaśasā śriyā
verses.translation: When passion defeats goodness and ignorance one gets attached, wants to make a difference and tends to impermanence, because of which one becomes unhappy by profit minded actions and striving for a good name and wealth [see also B.G. 14: 12, 18: 38].

verses.sanskrit: यदा जयेद् रजः सत्त्वं तमो मूढं लयं जडम् । युज्येत शोक-मोहाभ्यां निद्रया हिंसयाशया
verses.transliteration: yadā jayed rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamo mūḍhaṃ layaṃ jaḍam / yujyeta śoka-mohābhyāṃ nidrayā hiṃsayāśayā
verses.translation: When ignorance dominates passion and goodness one's discrimination is defeated, one's consciousness is covered, one's initiative is lost and one becomes endowed with bewilderment, complaints, sleeping too much, violence and false hopes [see also B.G. 14: 13, 18: 39].

verses.sanskrit: यदा चित्तं प्रसीदेत इन्द्रियाणां च निर्वृतिः । देहे ऽभयं मनो-ऽसङ्गं तत् सत्त्वं विद्धि मत्-पदम्
verses.transliteration: yadā cittaṃ prasīdeta indriyāṇāṃ ca nirvṛtiḥ / dehe 'bhayaṃ mano-'saṅgaṃ tat sattvaṃ viddhi mat-padam
verses.translation: When one's consciousness clears up and the senses are not distracted, one achieves physical self-confidence and a detached mind; know that to be the goodness of My refuge.

verses.sanskrit: विकुर्वन् क्रियया चा-धीर् अनिवृत्तिश् च चेतसाम् । गात्रास्वास्थ्यं मनो भ्रान्तं रज एतैर् निशामय
verses.transliteration: vikurvan kriyayā cā-dhīr anivṛttiś ca cetasām / gātrāsvāsthyaṃ mano bhrāntaṃ raja etair niśāmaya
verses.translation: When the intelligence is disturbed by too much activity, when one fails to disengage from one's senses, when one is not at ease with one's body and when the mind is unsteady, you should understand that to be the symptoms of passion.

verses.sanskrit: सीदच् चित्तं विलीयेत चेतसो ग्रहणे ऽक्षमम् । मनो नष्टं तमो ग्लानिस् तमस् तद् उपधारय
verses.transliteration: sīdac cittaṃ vilīyeta cetaso grahaṇe 'kṣamam / mano naṣṭaṃ tamo glānis tamas tad upadhāraya
verses.translation: Failing in the higher functions of consciousness, getting dull, being unable to focus, not being mindful, not understanding things and being gloomy, you should recognize as the mode of ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: एधमाने गुणे सत्त्वे देवानां बलम् एधते । असुराणां च रजसि तमस्य् उद्धव रक्षसाम्
verses.transliteration: edhamāne guṇe sattve devānāṃ balam edhate / asurāṇāṃ ca rajasi tamasy uddhava rakṣasām
verses.translation: When goodness increases the strength of the gods increases, when passion increases the demons grow stronger, and when ignorance increases, Uddhava, the wild men will get on top.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वाज् जागरणं विद्याद् रजसा स्वप्नम् आदिशेत् । प्रस्वापं तमसा जन्तोस् तुरीयं त्रिषु सन्ततम्
verses.transliteration: sattvāj jāgaraṇaṃ vidyād rajasā svapnam ādiśet / prasvāpaṃ tamasā jantos turīyaṃ triṣu santatam
verses.translation: Know that one is wakeful in the mode of goodness, that one is sleepy in passion, that one is not aware in the ignorant mode of the living entity, and that the fourth [transcendental] state [of consciousness turya] pervades the three [see also 7.7: 25 and B.G. 6: 16].

verses.sanskrit: उपर्य् उपरि गच्छन्ति सत्त्वेन ब्राह्मणा जनाः । तमसाधो ऽध आ-मुख्याद् रजसान्तर-चारिणः
verses.transliteration: upary upari gacchanti sattvena brāhmaṇā janāḥ / tamasādho 'dha ā-mukhyād rajasāntara-cāriṇaḥ
verses.translation: In the mode of goodness spiritual [Vedic] persons reach higher and higher, in the mode of ignorance one reaches head first lower and lower [births], and in the mode of passion one is stuck in between [in attachments, see also B.G. 6: 45, 16: 19].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वे प्रलीनाः स्वर् यान्ति नर-लोकं रजो-लयाः । तमो-लयास् तु निरयं यान्ति माम् एव निर्गुणाः
verses.transliteration: sattve pralīnāḥ svar yānti nara-lokaṃ rajo-layāḥ / tamo-layās tu nirayaṃ yānti mām eva nirguṇāḥ
verses.translation: Those who die in goodness go to heaven, those who die in passion go to the human world and those who die in ignorance go to hell. They however who are free from the modes come to Me [see also B.G. 9: 25, 14: 18].

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्पणं निष्फलं वा सात्त्विकं निज-कर्म तत् । राजसं फल-सङ्कल्पं हिंसा-प्रायादि तामसम्
verses.transliteration: mad-arpaṇaṃ niṣphalaṃ vā sāttvikaṃ nija-karma tat / rājasaṃ phala-saṅkalpaṃ hiṃsā-prāyādi tāmasam
verses.translation: Work dutifully done as a sacrifice unto Me without desiring the fruits is in the mode of goodness, work done with a profit motive is of the mode of passion and work performed with violence and pressure and such, is of the mode of ignorance [B.G. 17: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: कैवल्यं सात्त्विकं ज्ञानं रजो वैकल्पिकं च यत् । प्राकृतं तामसं ज्ञानं मन्-निष्ठं निर्गुणं स्मृतम्
verses.transliteration: kaivalyaṃ sāttvikaṃ jñānaṃ rajo vaikalpikaṃ ca yat / prākṛtaṃ tāmasaṃ jñānaṃ man-niṣṭhaṃ nirguṇaṃ smṛtam
verses.translation: Spiritual knowledge of detachment is of the mode of goodness, fostering opinions one is of the mode of passion, and a materialistic conviction belongs to the mode of ignorance. Spiritual knowledge focussed on Me [however] is considered to be free from the modes [see also 6.14: 2].

verses.sanskrit: वनं तु सात्त्विको वासो ग्रामो राजस उच्यते । तामसं द्यूत-सदनं मन्-निकेतं तु निर्गुणम्
verses.transliteration: vanaṃ tu sāttviko vāso grāmo rājasa ucyate / tāmasaṃ dyūta-sadanaṃ man-niketaṃ tu nirguṇam
verses.translation: To have one's residence in the forest is of the mode of goodness, to reside in a town is said to be of the mode of passion, to reside in a gambling house is of the mode of ignorance, but My residence is elevated above the modes [see also 7.12: 22, 11.18: 25].

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विकः कारको ऽसङ्गी रागान्धो राजसः स्मृतः । तामसः स्मृति-विभ्रष्टो निर्गुणो मद्-अपाश्रयः
verses.transliteration: sāttvikaḥ kārako 'saṅgī rāgāndho rājasaḥ smṛtaḥ / tāmasaḥ smṛti-vibhraṣṭo nirguṇo mad-apāśrayaḥ
verses.translation: A worker free from attachment is of the mode of goodness, blinded by personal desire one is of the mode of passion, having lost one's memory one speaks of the mode of ignorance [see 11.22: 38-39], [but] the one who has taken shelter of Me is free from the modes.

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विक्य् आध्यात्मिकी श्रद्धा कर्म-श्रद्धा तु राजसी । तामस्य् अधर्मे या श्रद्धा मत्-सेवायां तु निर्गुणा
verses.transliteration: sāttviky ādhyātmikī śraddhā karma-śraddhā tu rājasī / tāmasy adharme yā śraddhā mat-sevāyāṃ tu nirguṇā
verses.translation: In the mode of goodness one believes in spiritual matters, in the mode of passion one believes in fruitful activities, in the mode of ignorance one is irreligious, but one is transcendental to the modes with faith in My devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: पथ्यं पूतम् अनायस्तम् आहार्यं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् । राजसं चेन्द्रिय-प्रेष्ठं तामसं चार्ति-दाशुचि
verses.transliteration: pathyaṃ pūtam anāyastam āhāryaṃ sāttvikaṃ smṛtam / rājasaṃ cendriya-preṣṭhaṃ tāmasaṃ cārti-dāśuci
verses.translation: Food that is wholesome, pure and attained effortlessly is considered to be of the mode of goodness, [strongly] catering to the senses it is of the mode of passion, and impure food that makes one suffer is of ignorance [see also B.G. 17: 7-10].

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विकं सुखम् आत्मोत्थं विषयोत्थं तु राजसम् । तामसं मोह-दैन्योत्थं निर्गुणं मद्-अपाश्रयम्
verses.transliteration: sāttvikaṃ sukham ātmotthaṃ viṣayotthaṃ tu rājasam / tāmasaṃ moha-dainyotthaṃ nirguṇaṃ mad-apāśrayam
verses.translation: Happiness derived from the soul is of the mode of goodness, generated by sense objects it is of passion, happiness derived from delusion and depravity is of the mode of ignorance, but free from the modes happiness is found in Me [see 11.15: 17 & B.G. 5: 21, but also 6: 7].

verses.sanskrit: द्रव्यं देशः फलं कालो ज्ञानं कर्म च कारकः । श्रद्धावस्थाकृतिर् निष्ठा त्रै-गुण्यः सर्व एव हि
verses.transliteration: dravyaṃ deśaḥ phalaṃ kālo jñānaṃ karma ca kārakaḥ / śraddhāvasthākṛtir niṣṭhā trai-guṇyaḥ sarva eva hi
verses.translation: Material substance, the place, the fruit of action, time, knowledge, activity, the performer, faith, the state of consciousness, and the species and destinations of life thus all belong to the three gunas.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे गुण-मया भावाः पुरुषाव्यक्त-धिष्ठिताः । दृष्टं श्रुतं अनुध्यातं बुद्ध्या वा पुरुषर्षभ
verses.transliteration: sarve guṇa-mayā bhāvāḥ puruṣāvyakta-dhiṣṭhitāḥ / dṛṣṭaṃ śrutaṃ anudhyātaṃ buddhyā vā puruṣarṣabha
verses.translation: Oh best among men, all that exists, being seen, heard or pictured in one's mind, is a composition of the three basic qualities that was established by the unseen [Original] Enjoyer.

verses.sanskrit: एताः संसृतयः पुंसो गुण-कर्म-निबन्धनाः । येनेमे निर्जिताः सौम्य गुणा जीवेन चित्त-जाः । भक्ति-योगेन मन्-निष्ठो मद्-भावाय प्रपद्यते
verses.transliteration: etāḥ saṃsṛtayaḥ puṃso guṇa-karma-nibandhanāḥ / yeneme nirjitāḥ saumya guṇā jīvena citta-jāḥ / bhakti-yogena man-niṣṭho mad-bhāvāya prapadyate
verses.translation: These forms of existence [and stages of life] of the [repeatedly incarnating] living being, are bound to the operation of the modes. Oh gentle one, the individual soul who, dedicated to Me in bhakti-yoga, conquers these natural qualities that manifest themselves in the mind, qualifies for My transcendental love.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् देहम् इमं लब्ध्वा ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सम्भवम् । गुण-सङ्गं विनिर्धूय मां भजन्तु विचक्षणाः
verses.transliteration: tasmād deham imaṃ labdhvā jñāna-vijñāna-sambhavam / guṇa-saṅgaṃ vinirdhūya māṃ bhajantu vicakṣaṇāḥ
verses.translation: They who obtained this human body by which one acquires knowledge and develops wisdom, should therefore be as smart to shake off their attachment to the natural modes and worship Me.

verses.sanskrit: निःसङ्गो मां भजेद् विद्वान् अप्रमत्तो जितेन्द्रियः । रजस् तमश् चाभिजयेत् सत्त्व-संसेवया मुनिः
verses.transliteration: niḥsaṅgo māṃ bhajed vidvān apramatto jitendriyaḥ / rajas tamaś cābhijayet sattva-saṃsevayā muniḥ
verses.translation: A learned man should worship Me free from attachment; attentively having subdued his senses a sage should take to the mode of goodness and conquer the modes of passion and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं चाभिजयेद् युक्तो नैरपेक्ष्येण शान्त-धीः । सम्पद्यते गुणैर् मुक्तो जीवो जीवं विहाय माम्
verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ cābhijayed yukto nairapekṣyeṇa śānta-dhīḥ / sampadyate guṇair mukto jīvo jīvaṃ vihāya mām
verses.translation: With his intelligence pacified he, being connected [in bhakti] without any [other] dependency, should also conquer the mode of goodness. The embodied soul who [thus] freed from the modes gives up the cause of his conditioning, reaches Me.

verses.sanskrit: जीवो जीव-विनिर्मुक्तो गुणैश् चाशय-सम्भवैः । मयैव ब्रह्मणा पूर्णो न बहिर् नान्तरश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: jīvo jīva-vinirmukto guṇaiś cāśaya-sambhavaiḥ / mayaiva brahmaṇā pūrṇo na bahir nāntaraś caret
verses.translation: The living entity, who as an individual soul by Me thus was liberated from the basic qualities of nature that nestled in his mind, achieves thus, by dint of the Absolute Truth, complete fulfillment and will neither internally nor externally, any longer wander around.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच मल्-लक्षणम् इमं कायं लब्ध्वा मद्-धर्म आस्थितः । आनन्दं परमात्मानम् आत्म-स्थं समुपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca mal-lakṣaṇam imaṃ kāyaṃ labdhvā mad-dharma āsthitaḥ / ānandaṃ paramātmānam ātma-sthaṃ samupaiti mām
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Having acquired this human body that is My characteristic, one achieves, being situated in My dharma, Me, the Supreme Soul of Spiritual Happiness situated in the heart.

verses.sanskrit: गुण-मय्या जीव-योन्या विमुक्तो ज्ञान-निष्ठया । गुणेषु माया-मात्रेषु दृश्यमानेष्व् अवस्तुतः । वर्तमानो ऽपि न पुमान् युज्यते ऽवस्तुभिर् गुणैः
verses.transliteration: guṇa-mayyā jīva-yonyā vimukto jñāna-niṣṭhayā / guṇeṣu māyā-mātreṣu dṛśyamāneṣv avastutaḥ / vartamāno 'pi na pumān yujyate 'vastubhir guṇaiḥ
verses.translation: Someone who, fixed in spiritual knowledge, has become free from the cause of material life based on the products of the modes, does not get entangled in their illusory qualities despite of being surrounded by them; although present before his eyes they are insubstantial and nothing but illusion to him.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्गं न कुर्याद् असतां शिश्नोदर-तृपां क्वचित् । तस्यानुगस् तमस्य् अन्धे पतत्य् अन्धानुगान्ध-वत्
verses.transliteration: saṅgaṃ na kuryād asatāṃ śiśnodara-tṛpāṃ kvacit / tasyānugas tamasy andhe pataty andhānugāndha-vat
verses.translation: One should never at any time seek the company of materialists devoted to the gratification of their genitals and bellies, for they who follow such people will fall into the darkest pit, like a blind man following another one.

verses.sanskrit: ऐलः सम्राड् इमां गाथाम् अगायत बृहच्-छ्रवाः । उर्वशी-विरहान् मुह्यन् निर्विण्णः शोक-संयमे
verses.transliteration: ailaḥ samrāḍ imāṃ gāthām agāyata bṛhac-chravāḥ / urvaśī-virahān muhyan nirviṇṇaḥ śoka-saṃyame
verses.translation: The descendant of Il [called Aila or Purrav, see also 9.14: 15-16], the well-known great emperor, sang the following mighty song when he, bewildered being separated from Urvas', in resignation managed to restrain his grief.

verses.sanskrit: त्यक्त्वात्मानं व्रयन्तीं तां नग्न उन्मत्त-वन् नृपः । विलपन्न् अन्वगाज् जाये घोरे तिष्ठेति विक्लवः
verses.transliteration: tyaktvātmānaṃ vrayantīṃ tāṃ nagna unmatta-van nṛpaḥ / vilapann anvagāj jāye ghore tiṣṭheti viklavaḥ
verses.translation: The moment she abandoned him and left, he like a madman naked and crying ran after her calling out: 'Oh my wife, oh you terrible woman, please stop!'

verses.sanskrit: कामान् अतृप्तो ऽनुजुषन् क्षुल्लकान् वर्ष-यामिनीः । न वेद यान्तीर् नायान्तीर् उर्वश्य्-आकृष्ट-चेतनः
verses.transliteration: kāmān atṛpto 'nujuṣan kṣullakān varṣa-yāminīḥ / na veda yāntīr nāyāntīr urvaśy-ākṛṣṭa-cetanaḥ
verses.translation: With his mind possessed by Urvas' he, after years of insignificantly gratifying his lusts, was not satisfied and did not notice the nights coming and going.'

verses.sanskrit: ऐल उवाच अहो मे मोह-विस्तारः काम-कश्मल-चेतसः । देव्या गृहीत-कण्ठस्य नायुः-खण्डा इमे स्मृताः
verses.transliteration: aila uvāca aho me moha-vistāraḥ kāma-kaśmala-cetasaḥ / devyā gṛhīta-kaṇṭhasya nāyuḥ-khaṇḍā ime smṛtāḥ
verses.translation: Purrav said: 'Just see how bewildered I got! With my consciousness contaminated by lust I, in the embrace of this goddess, did not notice my life time passing.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं वेदाभिनिर्मुक्तः सूर्यो वाभ्युदितो ऽमुया । मूषितो वर्ष-पूगानां बताहानि गतान्य् उत
verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ vedābhinirmuktaḥ sūryo vābhyudito 'muyā / mūṣito varṣa-pūgānāṃ batāhāni gatāny uta
verses.translation: Captivated by her I had no idea whether the sun was rising or setting and have thus alas, for many years, spent my days.

verses.sanskrit: अहो मे आत्म-सम्मोहो येनात्मा योषितां कृतः । क्रीडा-मृगश् चक्रवर्ती नरदेव-शिखामणिः
verses.transliteration: aho me ātma-sammoho yenātmā yoṣitāṃ kṛtaḥ / krīḍā-mṛgaś cakravartī naradeva-śikhāmaṇiḥ
verses.translation: Oh what a pity this total bewilderment of mine because of which the body of this mighty emperor, this crown jewel of kings, became a toy animal for women!

verses.sanskrit: स-परिच्छदम् आत्मानं हित्वा तृणम् इवेश्वरम् । यान्तीं स्त्रियं चान्वगमं नग्न उन्मत्त-वद् रुदन्
verses.transliteration: sa-paricchadam ātmānaṃ hitvā tṛṇam iveśvaram / yāntīṃ striyaṃ cānvagamaṃ nagna unmatta-vad rudan
verses.translation: When she abandoned me, the mighty controller, together with all of my kingdom as if I were a blade of grass, I ran crying naked like a madman after the woman.

verses.sanskrit: कुतस् तस्यानुभावः स्यात् तेज ईशत्वम् एव वा । यो ऽन्वगच्छं स्त्रियं यान्तीं खर-वत् पाद-ताडितः
verses.transliteration: kutas tasyānubhāvaḥ syāt teja īśatvam eva vā / yo 'nvagacchaṃ striyaṃ yāntīṃ khara-vat pāda-tāḍitaḥ
verses.translation: Where is now the influence, strength and sovereignty of the person I am? I ran after this woman leaving me, just like an ass being kicked with the hoof for punishment!

verses.sanskrit: किं विद्यया किं तपसा किं त्यागेन श्रुतेन वा । किं विविक्तेन मौनेन स्त्रीभिर् यस्य मनो हृतम्
verses.transliteration: kiṃ vidyayā kiṃ tapasā kiṃ tyāgena śrutena vā / kiṃ viviktena maunena strībhir yasya mano hṛtam
verses.translation: What is the use of knowledge, austerities, renunciation, the scriptures, or of solitude and silence, for the one whose mind is stolen by women?

verses.sanskrit: स्वार्थस्याकोविदं धिङ् मां मूर्खं पण्डित-मानिनम् । यो ऽहम् ईश्वरतां प्राप्य स्त्रीभिर् गो-खर-वज् जितः
verses.transliteration: svārthasyākovidaṃ dhiṅ māṃ mūrkhaṃ paṇḍita-māninam / yo 'ham īśvaratāṃ prāpya strībhir go-khara-vaj jitaḥ
verses.translation: To hell with the fool I am unaware of what is good for him; I who thought to be a scholar in achieving the position of a lord and controller but who, just like a bullock or ass, was conquered by women!

verses.sanskrit: सेवतो वर्ष-पूगान् मे उर्वश्या अधरासवम् । न तृप्यत्य् आत्म-भूः कामो वह्निर् आहुतिभिर् यथा
verses.transliteration: sevato varṣa-pūgān me urvaśyā adharāsavam / na tṛpyaty ātma-bhūḥ kāmo vahnir āhutibhir yathā
verses.translation: For so many years serving Urvas''s lips, I, with the lust born from my mind, never got enough of the nectar, just like a fire one can never satisfy with oblations.

verses.sanskrit: पुंश्चल्यापहृतं चित्तं को न्व् अन्यो मोचितुं प्रभुः । आत्मारामेश्वरम् ऋते भगवन्तम् अधोक्षजम्
verses.transliteration: puṃścalyāpahṛtaṃ cittaṃ ko nv anyo mocituṃ prabhuḥ / ātmārāmeśvaram ṛte bhagavantam adhokṣajam
verses.translation: Who else but the Innerly Satisfied Lord of the Sages, the Supreme Lord Beyond the Senses, can free someone who lost his mind with a courtesan?

verses.sanskrit: बोधितस्यापि देव्या मे सूक्त-वाक्येन दुर्मतेः । मनो-गतो महा-मोहो नापयात्य् अजितात्मनः
verses.transliteration: bodhitasyāpi devyā me sūkta-vākyena durmateḥ / mano-gato mahā-moho nāpayāty ajitātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Out of control with myself being dull-minded, I saw no end to my confusion, even though the goddess [Urvas'] eloquently gave me advice [see 9.14: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: किम् एतया नो ऽपकृतं रज्ज्वा वा सर्प-चेतसः । द्रष्टुः स्वरूपाविदुषो यो ऽहं यद् अजितेन्द्रियः
verses.transliteration: kim etayā no 'pakṛtaṃ rajjvā vā sarpa-cetasaḥ / draṣṭuḥ svarūpāviduṣo yo 'haṃ yad ajitendriyaḥ
verses.translation: What would she have done wrong to a 'seer' like me who, taking a rope for a snake, has no notion of his real nature [his svarpa]? I am the one out of control with his senses is it not?

verses.sanskrit: क्वायं मलीमसः कायो दौर्गन्ध्याद्य्-आत्मको ऽशुचिः । क्व गुणाः सौमनस्याद्या ह्य् अध्यासो ऽविद्यया कृतः
verses.transliteration: kvāyaṃ malīmasaḥ kāyo daurgandhyādy-ātmako 'śuciḥ / kva guṇāḥ saumanasyādyā hy adhyāso 'vidyayā kṛtaḥ
verses.translation: What does this filthy body have to offer, unclean and full of bad odors; what are those 'pleasing [feminine] qualities' and so on anyway? They constitute an influence rooting in ignorance!

verses.sanskrit: पित्रोः किं स्वं नु भार्यायाः स्वामिनो ऽग्नेः श्व-गृध्रयोः । किम् आत्मनः किं सुहृदाम् इति यो नावसीयते
verses.transliteration: pitroḥ kiṃ svaṃ nu bhāryāyāḥ svāmino 'gneḥ śva-gṛdhrayoḥ / kim ātmanaḥ kiṃ suhṛdām iti yo nāvasīyate
verses.translation: One can never tell whether this body belongs to one's parents, wife or employer, to [the funeral] fire, the dogs and jackals or to the [indwelling] soul or one's fr iends. One gets attached to this unholy matter and praises it, in case of a woman, for having such a cute nose, beautiful smile and face, but one heads with it for the lowest destination [of decay].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् कलेवरे ऽमेध्ये तुच्छ-निष्ठे विषज्जते । अहो सु-भद्रं सु-नसं सु-स्मितं च मुखं स्त्रियः
verses.transliteration: tasmin kalevare 'medhye tuccha-niṣṭhe viṣajjate / aho su-bhadraṃ su-nasaṃ su-smitaṃ ca mukhaṃ striyaḥ
verses.translation: One can never tell whether this body belongs to one's parents, wife or employer, to [the funeral] fire, the dogs and jackals or to the [indwelling] soul or one's fr iends. One gets attached to this unholy matter and praises it, in case of a woman, for having such a cute nose, beautiful smile and face, but one heads with it for the lowest destination [of decay].

verses.sanskrit: त्वङ्-मांस-रुधिर-स्नायु- मेदो-मज्जास्थि-संहतौ । विण्-मूत्र-पूये रमतां कृमीणां कियद् अन्तरम्
verses.transliteration: tvaṅ-māṃsa-rudhira-snāyu- medo-majjāsthi-saṃhatau / viṇ-mūtra-pūye ramatāṃ kṛmīṇāṃ kiyad antaram
verses.translation: In what sense would one differ from worms when one enjoys that what is composed of skin, flesh, blood, muscle, fat, marrow and bone, urine, stool and pus?

verses.sanskrit: अथापि नोपसज्जेत स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चार्थ-वित् । विषयेन्द्रिय-संयोगान् मनः क्षुभ्यति नान्यथा
verses.transliteration: athāpi nopasajjeta strīṣu straiṇeṣu cārtha-vit / viṣayendriya-saṃyogān manaḥ kṣubhyati nānyathā
verses.translation: A man understanding what is best for him, should never run after women or associate with men thus engaged, for the sole reason that the mind, united with the senses, reaches for sense objects and thus gets agitated [compare 5.5: 2, 7.12: 9, 9.19: 17, 9.14: 36].

verses.sanskrit: अदृष्टाद् अश्रुताद् भावान् न भाव उपजायते । असम्प्रयुञ्जतः प्राणान् शाम्यति स्तिमितं मनः
verses.transliteration: adṛṣṭād aśrutād bhāvān na bhāva upajāyate / asamprayuñjataḥ prāṇān śāmyati stimitaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: [Because] a thing not heard or seen gives no rise to mental agitation, the mind of someone not engaging his senses calms down and finds peace.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् सङ्गो न कर्तव्यः स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चेन्द्रियैः । विदुषां चाप्य् अविस्रब्धः षड्-वर्गः किम् उ मादृशाम्
verses.transliteration: tasmāt saṅgo na kartavyaḥ strīṣu straiṇeṣu cendriyaiḥ / viduṣāṃ cāpy avisrabdhaḥ ṣaḍ-vargaḥ kim u mādṛśām
verses.translation: When not even wise men can rely on the six enemies [lust, anger, greed, bewilderment, intoxication and envy; the shath-varga], then what about persons like me? One therefore should not get sensorily attached to women or to men attached to women [see also yoshita].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच एवं प्रगायन् नृप-देव-देवः स उर्वशी-लोकम् अथो विहाय । आत्मानम् आत्मन्य् अवगम्य मां वै उपारमज् ज्ञाअन-विधूत-मोहः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca evaṃ pragāyan nṛpa-deva-devaḥ sa urvaśī-lokam atho vihāya / ātmānam ātmany avagamya māṃ vai upāramaj jñāana-vidhūta-mohaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'He, the worshipable lord of gods and men, who thus sang his song [of lamentation], then gave up the world of Urvas'. Realizing Me, the Supersoul in his heart, he by the transcendental knowledge found peace within himself and ended his illusion.

verses.sanskrit: ततो दुःसङ्गम् उत्सृज्य सत्सु सज्जेत बुद्धिमान् । सन्त एवास्य छिन्दन्ति मनो-व्यासङ्गम् उक्तिभिः
verses.transliteration: tato duḥsaṅgam utsṛjya satsu sajjeta buddhimān / santa evāsya chindanti mano-vyāsaṅgam uktibhiḥ
verses.translation: An intelligent person having abandoned bad association therefore should be fixed on devotees, for only by their words he can cut with the deep attachment of his mind.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तो ऽनपेक्षा मच्-चित्ताः प्रशान्ताः सम-दर्शिनः । निर्ममा निरहङ्कारा निर्द्वन्द्वा निष्परिग्रहाः
verses.transliteration: santo 'napekṣā mac-cittāḥ praśāntāḥ sama-darśinaḥ / nirmamā nirahaṅkārā nirdvandvā niṣparigrahāḥ
verses.translation: Devotees with their minds fixed on Me do not depend [on lusts] and are, with an equal minded vision, completely peaceful and free from possessiveness, false ego, dualities and greed.

verses.sanskrit: तेषु नित्यं महा-भाग महा-भागेषु मत्-कथाः । सम्भवन्ति हि ता नॄणां जुषतां प्रपुनन्त्य् अघम्
verses.transliteration: teṣu nityaṃ mahā-bhāga mahā-bhāgeṣu mat-kathāḥ / sambhavanti hi tā nṝṇāṃ juṣatāṃ prapunanty agham
verses.translation: Oh most fortunate one, these greatly fortunate souls are constantly discussing My stories that have the power to completely eradicate the sins of anyone choosing for them.

verses.sanskrit: ता ये शृण्वन्ति गायन्ति ह्य् अनुमोदन्ति चादृताः । मत्-पराः श्रद्दधानाश् च भक्तिं विन्दन्ति ते मयि
verses.transliteration: tā ye śṛṇvanti gāyanti hy anumodanti cādṛtāḥ / mat-parāḥ śraddadhānāś ca bhaktiṃ vindanti te mayi
verses.translation: They who, faithfully dedicated to Me, hear, chant and respectfully take them [My stories] to heart, will attain My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: भक्तिं लब्धवतः साधोः किम् अन्यद् अवशिष्यते । मय्य् अनन्त-गुणे ब्रह्मण्य् आनन्दानुभवात्मनि
verses.transliteration: bhaktiṃ labdhavataḥ sādhoḥ kim anyad avaśiṣyate / mayy ananta-guṇe brahmaṇy ānandānubhavātmani
verses.translation: What else would there remain [to be accomplished] for a devotee once he has achieved devotional service unto Me, the Absolute Truth of Countless Qualities, comprising the experience of spiritual happiness?

verses.sanskrit: यथोपश्रयमाणस्य भगवन्तं विभावसुम् । शीतं भयं तमो ऽप्येति साधून् संसेवतस् तथा
verses.transliteration: yathopaśrayamāṇasya bhagavantaṃ vibhāvasum / śītaṃ bhayaṃ tamo 'pyeti sādhūn saṃsevatas tathā
verses.translation: Just as cold, fear and darkness will dissipate for the one who resorts to the supreme grace of fire [Agni], similarly dullness, apprehension and ignorance will dissolve for someone who serves the devotees.

verses.sanskrit: निमज्ज्योन्मज्जतां घोरे भवाब्धौ परमायणम् । सन्तो ब्रह्म-विदः शान्ता नौर् दृढेवाप्सु मज्जताम्
verses.transliteration: nimajjyonmajjatāṃ ghore bhavābdhau paramāyaṇam / santo brahma-vidaḥ śāntā naur dṛḍhevāpsu majjatām
verses.translation: For those who submerge and again rise in the fearful ocean of material life, the saintly devotees, peacefu l in understanding the Absolute, constitute a supreme shelter as good as a life boat for people drowning in the water [compare 11.23: 28 and 11.17: 44].

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं हि प्राणिनां प्राण आर्तानां शरणं त्व् अहम् । धर्मो वित्तं नृणां प्रेत्य सन्तो ऽर्वाग् बिभ्यतो ऽरणम्
verses.transliteration: annaṃ hi prāṇināṃ prāṇa ārtānāṃ śaraṇaṃ tv aham / dharmo vittaṃ nṛṇāṃ pretya santo 'rvāg bibhyato 'raṇam
verses.translation: Just as food is there to grant the living beings their life, just as I exist as the shelter for the distressed and dharma is there as the wealth of the deceased, so my devotees constitute the refuge of those afraid to fall down.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तो दिशन्ति चक्षूंसि बहिर् अर्कः समुत्थितः । देवता बान्धवाः सन्तः सन्त आत्माहम् एव च
verses.transliteration: santo diśanti cakṣūṃsi bahir arkaḥ samutthitaḥ / devatā bāndhavāḥ santaḥ santa ātmāham eva ca
verses.translation: The devotees grant you the [divine] eyes while the sun [only] shows the external world after having risen; they are the worshipable ones, one's [true] relatives, they are one's actual self and Me as well [see also e.g. 1.1: 15, 3.5: 47, 3.6: 28, 11.2: 6].

verses.sanskrit: वैतसेनस् ततो ऽप्य् एवम् उर्वश्या लोक-निष्पृहः । मुक्त-सङ्गो महीम् एताम् आत्मारामश् चचार ह
verses.transliteration: vaitasenas tato 'py evam urvaśyā loka-niṣpṛhaḥ / mukta-saṅgo mahīm etām ātmārāmaś cacāra ha
verses.translation: He [Purrav] who for that reason no longer desired the world of Urvas', thus liberated from all attachment, innerly satisfied wandered this earth.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच क्रिया-योगं समाचक्ष्व भवद्-आराधनं प्रभो । यस्मात् त्वां ये यथार्चन्ति सात्वताः सात्वतर्षभ
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca kriyā-yogaṃ samācakṣva bhavad-ārādhanaṃ prabho / yasmāt tvāṃ ye yathārcanti sātvatāḥ sātvatarṣabha
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Can You please explain the yoga of rites [kriy-yoga], the yoga of the service unto You as a deity, oh Master? Who is of that worship, in respect of what form is one of worship and in what manner are You worshiped then, oh Master of the Stvatas [see also mrti and 11.3: 48-55]?

verses.sanskrit: एतद् वदन्ति मुनयो मुहुर् निःश्रेयसं नृणाम् । नारदो भगवान् व्यास आचार्यो ऽङ्गिरसः सुतः
verses.transliteration: etad vadanti munayo muhur niḥśreyasaṃ nṛṇām / nārado bhagavān vyāsa ācāryo 'ṅgirasaḥ sutaḥ
verses.translation: The sages Nrada, Bhagavn Vysa and my preceptor the son of Angir [Brihaspati], repeatedly say that for the welfare of men there is nothing as conducive.

verses.sanskrit: निःसृतं ते मुखाम्भोजाद् यद् आह भगवान् अजः । पुत्रेभ्यो भृगु-मुख्येभ्यो देव्यै च भगवान् भवः
verses.transliteration: niḥsṛtaṃ te mukhāmbhojād yad āha bhagavān ajaḥ / putrebhyo bhṛgu-mukhyebhyo devyai ca bhagavān bhavaḥ
verses.translation: The words about this that emanated from Your lotus mouth were spoken by the great unborn Lord [Brahm] unto his sons headed by Bhrigu, as also by the great Lord S'iva speaking to the goddess [Prvat, see B.G. 3: 9-10]. This [service to Your deity form] is approved by all classes and spiritual orders of society and is, I think, most beneficial for women and the working class, oh Magnanimous One.

verses.sanskrit: एतद् वै सर्व-वर्णानाम् आश्रमाणां च सम्मतम् । श्रेयसाम् उत्तमं मन्ये स्त्री-शूद्राणां च मान-द
verses.transliteration: etad vai sarva-varṇānām āśramāṇāṃ ca sammatam / śreyasām uttamaṃ manye strī-śūdrāṇāṃ ca māna-da
verses.translation: The words about this that emanated from Your lotus mouth were spoken by the great unborn Lord [Brahm] unto his sons headed by Bhrigu, as also by the great Lord S'iva speaking to the goddess [Prvat, see B.G. 3: 9-10]. This [service to Your deity form] is approved by all classes and spiritual orders of society and is, I think, most beneficial for women and the working class, oh Magnanimous One.

verses.sanskrit: एतत् कमल-पत्राक्ष कर्म-बन्ध-विमोचनम् । भक्ताय चानुरक्ताय ब्रूहि विश्वेश्वरेश्वर
verses.transliteration: etat kamala-patrākṣa karma-bandha-vimocanam / bhaktāya cānuraktāya brūhi viśveśvareśvara
verses.translation: Oh Lotus-eyed Lord, please, oh Controller of All Controllers in the Universe, speak to Your devoted bhakta about this means of liberation from the bondage of karma.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच न ह्य् अन्तो ऽनन्त-पारस्य कर्म-काण्डस्य चोद्धव । सङ्क्षिप्तं वर्णयिष्यामि यथावद् अनुपूर्वशः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca na hy anto 'nanta-pārasya karma-kāṇḍasya coddhava / saṅkṣiptaṃ varṇayiṣyāmi yathāvad anupūrvaśaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The number of [karma-knda] prescriptions for worshiping deities [see e.g. B.G. 1-6] is endless, Uddhava, let Me in brief explain it nicely one step at a time.

verses.sanskrit: वैदिकस् तान्त्रिको मिश्र इति मे त्रि-विधो मखः । त्रयाणाम् ईप्सितेनैव विधिना मां समर्चरेत्
verses.transliteration: vaidikas tāntriko miśra iti me tri-vidho makhaḥ / trayāṇām īpsitenaiva vidhinā māṃ samarcaret
verses.translation: One should worship Me as one desires following one of the three kinds of sacrifice according to the Vedas, the explanatory literatures [tantras like the Pacartra] or a combination of them.

verses.sanskrit: यदा स्व-निगमेनोक्तं द्विजत्वं प्राप्य पूरुषः । यथा यजेत मां भक्त्या श्रद्धया तन् निबोध मे
verses.transliteration: yadā sva-nigamenoktaṃ dvijatvaṃ prāpya pūruṣaḥ / yathā yajeta māṃ bhaktyā śraddhayā tan nibodha me
verses.translation: Now hear from Me how a person, who according to the for him specific Vedic precepts [*] achieved the status of a second birth, should worship Me with faith and devotion.

verses.sanskrit: अर्चायां स्थण्डिले ऽग्नौ वा सूर्ये वाप्सु हृदि द्विजः । द्रव्येण भक्ति-युक्तो ऽर्चेत् स्व-गुरुं माम् अमायया
verses.transliteration: arcāyāṃ sthaṇḍile 'gnau vā sūrye vāpsu hṛdi dvijaḥ / dravyeṇa bhakti-yukto 'rcet sva-guruṃ mām amāyayā
verses.translation: He must, connected in bhakti, free from ulterior motives ['honestly'] with the necessary paraphernalia worship Me, his worshipable guru, as being present in a deity, in a sacrificial area, a fire, in the [position of the] sun, in water or in the twice-born heart itself [**].

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वं स्नानं प्रकुर्वीत धौत-दन्तो ऽङ्ग-शुद्धये । उभयैर् अपि च स्नानं मन्त्रैर् मृद्-ग्रहणादिना
verses.transliteration: pūrvaṃ snānaṃ prakurvīta dhauta-danto 'ṅga-śuddhaye / ubhayair api ca snānaṃ mantrair mṛd-grahaṇādinā
verses.translation: For purification he should first bathe and brush his teeth, and next purify himself with both types of [Vedic and tantric] mantras while applying clay and such [see tilaka, kavaca and 6.8: 3-10].

verses.sanskrit: सन्ध्योपास्त्यादि-कर्माणि वेदेनाचोदितानि मे । पूजां तैः कल्पयेत् सम्यक्- सङ्कल्पः कर्म-पावनीम्
verses.transliteration: sandhyopāstyādi-karmāṇi vedenācoditāni me / pūjāṃ taiḥ kalpayet samyak- saṅkalpaḥ karma-pāvanīm
verses.translation: To be freed from his karma, he perfectly convinced should engage in My ritual worship [pj] and thereto perform duties, as prescribed in the Vedas [see also 11.14: 35], with worship and such [like expressing the Gyatr mantra] at the three junctures of the day [dawn, noon and sunset].

verses.sanskrit: शैली दारु-मयी लौही लेप्या लेख्या च सैकती । मनो-मयी मणि-मयी प्रतिमाष्ट-विधा स्मृता
verses.transliteration: śailī dāru-mayī lauhī lepyā lekhyā ca saikatī / mano-mayī maṇi-mayī pratimāṣṭa-vidhā smṛtā
verses.translation: There are eight types of forms with which one remembers Me: in stone, wood, metal, smearable substances [like clay], being painted, in sand, in jewels and as an image kept in mind.

verses.sanskrit: चलाचलेति द्वि-विधा प्रतिष्ठा जीव-मन्दिरम् । उद्वासावाहने न स्तः स्थिरायाम् उद्धवार्चने
verses.transliteration: calācaleti dvi-vidhā pratiṣṭhā jīva-mandiram / udvāsāvāhane na staḥ sthirāyām uddhavārcane
verses.translation: Of the two kinds of individual temple deities that are moved and not moved, the installed deity, oh Uddhava, is for His worship not taken away (udvsa) or again brought forward (vdane).

verses.sanskrit: अस्थिरायां विकल्पः स्यात् स्थण्डिले तु भवेद् द्वयम् । स्नपनं त्व् अविलेप्यायाम् अन्यत्र परिमार्जनम्
verses.transliteration: asthirāyāṃ vikalpaḥ syāt sthaṇḍile tu bhaved dvayam / snapanaṃ tv avilepyāyām anyatra parimārjanam
verses.translation: Not being installed one has these options, but when assigned a fixed place the following two possibilities are found: not being of a smearable substance [or being painted or made of wood] He is washed, in all other cases He is cleansed without water.

verses.sanskrit: द्रव्यैः प्रसिद्धैर् मद्-यागः प्रतिमादिष्व् अमायिनः । भक्तस्य च यथा-लब्धैर् हृदि भावेन चैव हि
verses.transliteration: dravyaiḥ prasiddhair mad-yāgaḥ pratimādiṣv amāyinaḥ / bhaktasya ca yathā-labdhair hṛdi bhāvena caiva hi
verses.translation: There is the worship of My different deities with excellent paraphernalia, there is the worship of a devotee free from material desire using whatever that is readily available, and there is the worship in the heart in a mental respect [by love and meditation only].

verses.sanskrit: स्नानालङ्करणं प्रेष्ठम् अर्चायाम् एव तूद्धव । स्थण्डिले तत्त्व-विन्यासो वह्नाव् आज्य-प्लुतं हविः
verses.transliteration: snānālaṅkaraṇaṃ preṣṭham arcāyām eva tūddhava / sthaṇḍile tattva-vinyāso vahnāv ājya-plutaṃ haviḥ
verses.translation: With a deity [in the temple] customary bathing and decorating is most appreciated, Uddhava, for a holy place that is an exercise of respect in mantras [tattva-vinysa] and for fire oblations [of sesame, barley etc.] drenched in ghee are considered best. With the sun that is an exercise of respect [with a sundial, see Srya-namaskar] and with water offerings of water and such are most suitable. Offerings presented with faith by a devotee of Mine are most dear to Me, even if it is just a bit of water.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्ये चाभ्यर्हणं प्रेष्ठं सलिले सलिलादिभिः । श्रद्धयोपाहृतं प्रेष्ठं भक्तेन मम वार्य् अपि
verses.transliteration: sūrye cābhyarhaṇaṃ preṣṭhaṃ salile salilādibhiḥ / śraddhayopāhṛtaṃ preṣṭhaṃ bhaktena mama vāry api
verses.translation: With a deity [in the temple] customary bathing and decorating is most appreciated, Uddhava, for a holy place that is an exercise of respect in mantras [tattva-vinysa] and for fire oblations [of sesame, barley etc.] drenched in ghee are considered best. With the sun that is an exercise of respect [with a sundial, see Srya-namaskar] and with water offerings of water and such are most suitable. Offerings presented with faith by a devotee of Mine are most dear to Me, even if it is just a bit of water.

verses.sanskrit: भूर्य् अप्य् अभक्तोपाहृतं न मे तोषाय कल्पते । गन्धो धूपः सुमनसो दीपो ऽन्नाद्यं च किं पुनः
verses.transliteration: bhūry apy abhaktopāhṛtaṃ na me toṣāya kalpate / gandho dhūpaḥ sumanaso dīpo 'nnādyaṃ ca kiṃ punaḥ
verses.translation: And what to say of an offering [by devotees] of foodstuffs, flowers, lamps, fragrances and incense [see also B.G. 9: 26]? An offer [by contrast] that, even if it is very rich, is made by a non-devotee, will not satisfy Me [see also B.G. 16].

verses.sanskrit: शुचिः सम्भृत-सम्भारः प्राग्-दर्भैः कल्पितासनः । आसीनः प्राग् उदग् वार्चेद् अर्चायां त्व् अथ सम्मुखः
verses.transliteration: śuciḥ sambhṛta-sambhāraḥ prāg-darbhaiḥ kalpitāsanaḥ / āsīnaḥ prāg udag vārced arcāyāṃ tv atha sammukhaḥ
verses.translation: Being cleansed he should then be of worship, having collected the necessary items, having arranged the seat with blades [of kus'a grass] pointing to the east and sitting down facing the east or the north, or else directly facing the deity [compare 1.19: 17, 4.24: 10, 8.9: 14-15].

verses.sanskrit: कृत-न्यासः कृत-न्यासां मद्-अर्चां पाणिनामृजेत् । कलशं प्रोक्षणीयं च यथावद् उपसाधयेत्
verses.transliteration: kṛta-nyāsaḥ kṛta-nyāsāṃ mad-arcāṃ pāṇināmṛjet / kalaśaṃ prokṣaṇīyaṃ ca yathāvad upasādhayet
verses.translation: After having assigned mantras to his own body and also having done this to My form, he should clean My Deity with his hand and properly prepare the sprinkling vessel and the sacred pot.

verses.sanskrit: तद्-अद्भिर् देव-यजनं द्रव्याण्य् आत्मानम् एव च । प्रोक्ष्य पात्राणि त्रीण्य् अद्भिस् तैस् तैर् द्रव्यैश् च साधयेत्
verses.transliteration: tad-adbhir deva-yajanaṃ dravyāṇy ātmānam eva ca / prokṣya pātrāṇi trīṇy adbhis tais tair dravyaiś ca sādhayet
verses.translation: With the water of the vessel sprinkling the area of the deity, the utensils and his own body, he next should prepare three vessels with water and arrange for the necessary auspicious items as far as available [like flowers, grains, blades of grass, sesame seeds etc., see ***].

verses.sanskrit: पाद्यार्घ्याचमनीयार्थं त्रीणि पात्राणि देशिकः । हृदा शीर्ष्णाथ शिखया गायत्र्या चाभिमन्त्रयेत्
verses.transliteration: pādyārghyācamanīyārthaṃ trīṇi pātrāṇi deśikaḥ / hṛdā śīrṣṇātha śikhayā gāyatryā cābhimantrayet
verses.translation: With the mantras for the heart [hridayya namah], the head [s'rase svh] and the tuft of hair [s'ikhyai vashath] the worshiper should purify the three vessels of water for His feet [pdya], His hands [arghya], and His mouth [camana], and do the same with the Gyatr.

verses.sanskrit: पिण्डे वाय्व्-अग्नि-संशुद्धे हृत्-पद्म-स्थां परां मम । अण्वीं जीव-कलां ध्यायेन् नादान्ते सिद्ध-भाविताम्
verses.transliteration: piṇḍe vāyv-agni-saṃśuddhe hṛt-padma-sthāṃ parāṃ mama / aṇvīṃ jīva-kalāṃ dhyāyen nādānte siddha-bhāvitām
verses.translation: He should meditate on the Original Individuality of all Expansions, the very subtle transcendental form of Mine that, within his body that was fully purified by air and fire, is situated on the lotus of the heart and by perfected souls is experienced in the end vibration of the Pranava [see also 2.2].

verses.sanskrit: तयात्म-भूतया पिण्डे व्याप्ते सम्पूज्य तन्-मयः । आवाह्यार्चादिषु स्थाप्य न्यस्ताङ्गं मां प्रपूजयेत्
verses.transliteration: tayātma-bhūtayā piṇḍe vyāpte sampūjya tan-mayaḥ / āvāhyārcādiṣu sthāpya nyastāṅgaṃ māṃ prapūjayet
verses.translation: With that [meditated form] by his own realization conceived as pervaded within his body, he, of worship fully being absorbed in thought of Me, should invite Me within the deity - and all that is respected along with it - by touching My limbs with mantras [nyasta] and thereupon honor Me [externally by performing pūjā].

verses.sanskrit: पाद्योपस्पर्शार्हणादीन् उपचारान् प्रकल्पयेत् । धर्मादिभिश् च नवभिः कल्पयित्वासनं मम
verses.transliteration: pādyopasparśārhaṇādīn upacārān prakalpayet / dharmādibhiś ca navabhiḥ kalpayitvāsanaṃ mama
verses.translation: After first having imagined My seat, decorated with the nine s'aktis and the [deities of] dharma etc. [*4] as an effulgent eight-petaled lotus with saffron filaments in its whorl, he should offer to Me the pdya, arghya and camana water and other items of worship to be perfect with the two [of enjoyment and liberation] in respect of both the Vedas and the tantras.

verses.sanskrit: पद्मम् अष्ट-दलं तत्र कर्णिका-केसरोज्ज्वलम् । उभाभ्यां वेद-तन्त्राभ्यां मह्यं तूभय-सिद्धये
verses.transliteration: padmam aṣṭa-dalaṃ tatra karṇikā-kesarojjvalam / ubhābhyāṃ veda-tantrābhyāṃ mahyaṃ tūbhaya-siddhaye
verses.translation: After first having imagined My seat, decorated with the nine s'aktis and the [deities of] dharma etc. [*4] as an effulgent eight-petaled lotus with saffron filaments in its whorl, he should offer to Me the pdya, arghya and camana water and other items of worship to be perfect with the two [of enjoyment and liberation] in respect of both the Vedas and the tantras.

verses.sanskrit: सुदर्शनं पाञ्चजन्यं गदासीषु-धनुर्-हलान् । मुषलं कौस्तुभं मालां श्रीवत्सं चानुपूजयेत्
verses.transliteration: sudarśanaṃ pāñcajanyaṃ gadāsīṣu-dhanur-halān / muṣalaṃ kaustubhaṃ mālāṃ śrīvatsaṃ cānupūjayet
verses.translation: One after the other he next must honor My disc-weapon [the Sudars'ana cakra], conch [the Pcajanya], club [the Kaumodak] and arrows and bow [the S'arnga], My [Balarma items of the] plow and pestle [hala and mushala], My gem [the Kaustubha], garland [the Vaijayant] and chest mark curl of white hairs [the S'rvatsa].

verses.sanskrit: नन्दं सुनन्दं गरुडं प्रचण्डं चण्डं एव च । महाबलं बलं चैव कुमुदं कमुदेक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: nandaṃ sunandaṃ garuḍaṃ pracaṇḍaṃ caṇḍaṃ eva ca / mahābalaṃ balaṃ caiva kumudaṃ kamudekṣaṇam
verses.translation: [He also honors] Garuda, Nanda, Sunanda, Pracanda and Canda, Mahbala, Bala, Kumuda and Kumudekshana [My carrier bird and eight associates].

verses.sanskrit: दुर्गां विनायकं व्यासं विष्वक्षेनं गुरून् सुरान् । स्वे स्वे स्थाने त्व् अभिमुखान् पूजयेत् प्रोक्षणादिभिः
verses.transliteration: durgāṃ vināyakaṃ vyāsaṃ viṣvakṣenaṃ gurūn surān / sve sve sthāne tv abhimukhān pūjayet prokṣaṇādibhiḥ
verses.translation: Durg, Vinyaka [Ganes'a], Vysa, Vishvaksena [see 6.8: 29, 9.21: 25-26], the spiritual masters and the demigods - each in their own place in front of the deity - should be worshiped with the sprinkling of water and other rituals [*5].

verses.sanskrit: चन्दनोशीर-कर्पूर- कुङ्कुमागुरु-वासितैः । सलिलैः स्नापयेन् मन्त्रैर् नित्यदा विभवे सति
verses.transliteration: candanośīra-karpūra- kuṅkumāguru-vāsitaiḥ / salilaiḥ snāpayen mantrair nityadā vibhave sati
verses.translation: Every day [the deity] should be bathed, as far as the means permit, using different kinds of water scented with sandalwood, us'ra root, camphor, kunkuma and aguru. Also hymns should be chanted, such as those from a section of the Vedas known as Svarna-gharma, the incantation called Mahpurusha, the Purusha-skta [from the Rig Veda] and songs from the Sma Veda, like the Rjana and others.

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्ण-घर्मानुवाकेन महापुरुष-विद्यया । पौरुषेणापि सूक्तेन सामभी राजनादिभिः
verses.transliteration: svarṇa-gharmānuvākena mahāpuruṣa-vidyayā / pauruṣeṇāpi sūktena sāmabhī rājanādibhiḥ
verses.translation: Every day [the deity] should be bathed, as far as the means permit, using different kinds of water scented with sandalwood, us'ra root, camphor, kunkuma and aguru. Also hymns should be chanted, such as those from a section of the Vedas known as Svarna-gharma, the incantation called Mahpurusha, the Purusha-skta [from the Rig Veda] and songs from the Sma Veda, like the Rjana and others.

verses.sanskrit: वस्त्रोपवीताभरण- पत्र-स्रग्-गन्ध-लेपनैः । अलङ्कुर्वीत स-प्रेम मद्-भक्तो मां यथोचितम्
verses.transliteration: vastropavītābharaṇa- patra-srag-gandha-lepanaiḥ / alaṅkurvīta sa-prema mad-bhakto māṃ yathocitam
verses.translation: My devotee should lovingly decorate Me with clothing, a sacred thread, ornaments, marks of tilaka, garlands and [apply] fragrant oils, the way it is enjoined.

verses.sanskrit: पाद्यम् आचमनीयं च गन्धं सुमनसो ऽक्षतान् । धूप-दीपोपहार्याणि दद्यान् मे श्रद्धयार्चकः
verses.transliteration: pādyam ācamanīyaṃ ca gandhaṃ sumanaso 'kṣatān / dhūpa-dīpopahāryāṇi dadyān me śraddhayārcakaḥ
verses.translation: The worshiper should with faith present to Me pdya and camana water, fragrances and flowers, whole grains, incense, lamps and other items.

verses.sanskrit: गुड-पायस-सर्पींषि शष्कुल्य्-आपूप-मोदकान् । संयाव-दधि-सूपांश् च नैवेद्यं सति कल्पयेत्
verses.transliteration: guḍa-pāyasa-sarpīṃṣi śaṣkuly-āpūpa-modakān / saṃyāva-dadhi-sūpāṃś ca naivedyaṃ sati kalpayet
verses.translation: According to his means he should make offerings of foodstuffs, like candy, sweet rice, ghee, rice flour cake [s'ashkul], sweet cakes [ppa], sweet rice flour dumplings with coconut [modaka], spicy sweet wheat cake of ghee and milk [samyva], yogurt and vegetable soups.

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यङ्गोन्मर्दनादर्श- दन्त-धावाभिषेचनम् । अन्नाद्य-गीत-नृत्यानि पर्वणि स्युर् उतान्व्-अहम्
verses.transliteration: abhyaṅgonmardanādarśa- danta-dhāvābhiṣecanam / annādya-gīta-nṛtyāni parvaṇi syur utānv-aham
verses.translation: On special days, or else every day, [the deity] should be offered a massage with ointment, a mirror, an eucalyptus stick for cleaning the teeth, a bath, food to be chewed and not to be chewed, as also song and dance.

verses.sanskrit: विधिना विहिते कुण्डे मेखला-गर्त-वेदिभिः । अग्निम् आधाय परितः समूहेत् पाणिनोदितम्
verses.transliteration: vidhinā vihite kuṇḍe mekhalā-garta-vedibhiḥ / agnim ādhāya paritaḥ samūhet pāṇinoditam
verses.translation: In a sacrificial area set up as prescribed he should, wearing a girdle, using a fire pit and an elevation for sacrificing, by hand build and bring to a blaze a fire that is equally piled up.

verses.sanskrit: परिस्तीर्याथ पर्युक्षेद् अन्वाधाय यथा-विधि । प्रोक्षण्यासाद्य द्रव्याणि प्रोक्ष्याग्नौ भावयेत माम्
verses.transliteration: paristīryātha paryukṣed anvādhāya yathā-vidhi / prokṣaṇyāsādya dravyāṇi prokṣyāgnau bhāvayeta mām
verses.translation: Spreading [kus'a grass mats] and then sprinkling and ceremonially [anvdhna] placing wood in the fire according to the rules, he should, having arranged for the camana water, sprinkle the items to offer and meditate on Me as residing in the fire.

verses.sanskrit: तप्त-जाम्बूनद-प्रख्यं शङ्ख-चक्र-गदाम्बुजैः । लसच्-चतुर्-भुजं शान्तं पद्म-किञ्जल्क-वाससम्
verses.transliteration: tapta-jāmbūnada-prakhyaṃ śaṅkha-cakra-gadāmbujaiḥ / lasac-catur-bhujaṃ śāntaṃ padma-kiñjalka-vāsasam
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: स्फुरत्-किरीट-कटक कटि-सूत्र-वराङ्गदम् । श्रीवत्स-वक्षसं भ्राजत्- कौस्तुभं वन-मालिनम्
verses.transliteration: sphurat-kirīṭa-kaṭaka kaṭi-sūtra-varāṅgadam / śrīvatsa-vakṣasaṃ bhrājat- kaustubhaṃ vana-mālinam
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: ध्यायन्न् अभ्यर्च्य दारूणि हविषाभिघृतानि च । प्रास्याज्य-भागाव् आघारौ दत्त्वा चाज्य-प्लुतं हविः
verses.transliteration: dhyāyann abhyarcya dārūṇi haviṣābhighṛtāni ca / prāsyājya-bhāgāv āghārau dattvā cājya-plutaṃ haviḥ
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: जुहुयान् मूल-मन्त्रेण षोडशर्चावदानतः । धर्मादिभ्यो यथा-न्यायं मन्त्रैः स्विष्टि-कृतं बुधः
verses.transliteration: juhuyān mūla-mantreṇa ṣoḍaśarcāvadānataḥ / dharmādibhyo yathā-nyāyaṃ mantraiḥ sviṣṭi-kṛtaṃ budhaḥ
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यर्च्याथ नमस्कृत्य पार्षदेभ्यो बलिं हरेत् । मूल-मन्त्रं जपेद् ब्रह्म स्मरन् नारायणात्मकम्
verses.transliteration: abhyarcyātha namaskṛtya pārṣadebhyo baliṃ haret / mūla-mantraṃ japed brahma smaran nārāyaṇātmakam
verses.translation: Thus having been of worship he should bow to offer obeisances unto My associates and next present offerings chanting the basic mantra for the deity in question, thereby remembering Nryana as the Original Self of the Absolute Truth.

verses.sanskrit: दत्त्वाचमनम् उच्छेषं विष्वक्षेनाय कल्पयेत् । मुख-वासं सुरभिमत् ताम्बूलाद्यम् अथार्हयेत्
verses.transliteration: dattvācamanam uccheṣaṃ viṣvakṣenāya kalpayet / mukha-vāsaṃ surabhimat tāmbūlādyam athārhayet
verses.translation: After having offered camana water and giving the remnants of the food to Vishvaksena, he should present to Me prepared betel nut with fragrant substances for the mouth [see also 11.3: 48-53, 11.25: 28].

verses.sanskrit: उपगायन् गृणन् नृत्यन् कर्माण्य् अभिनयन् मम । मत्-कथाः श्रावयन् शृण्वन् मुहूर्तं क्षणिको भवेत्
verses.transliteration: upagāyan gṛṇan nṛtyan karmāṇy abhinayan mama / mat-kathāḥ śrāvayan śṛṇvan muhūrtaṃ kṣaṇiko bhavet
verses.translation: He should [next] for some time [see kla, 11.21: 9] become absorbed in My celebration, by listening himself - and make others listen - to My stories, by acting out My transcendental activities, by dancing, by chanting loudly and by singing along with others [see also e.g. 11.5: 36-37, 11.14: 23-24].

verses.sanskrit: स्तवैर् उच्चावचैः स्तोत्रैः पौराणैः प्राकृतैर् अपि । स्तुत्वा प्रसीद भगवन्न् इति वन्देत दण्ड-वत्
verses.transliteration: stavair uccāvacaiḥ stotraiḥ paurāṇaiḥ prākṛtair api / stutvā prasīda bhagavann iti vandeta daṇḍa-vat
verses.translation: With prayers from the Purnas, with large or small prayers from other ancient scriptures, with prayers written by others [see bhajans] and prayers from more common sources, he should prostrate himself, pay his obeisances and say: 'Oh Lord, please show Your mercy [prasda bhagavan].'

verses.sanskrit: शिरो मत्-पादयोः कृत्वा बाहुभ्यां च परस्परम् । प्रपन्नं पाहि माम् ईश भीतं मृत्यु-ग्रहार्णवात्
verses.transliteration: śiro mat-pādayoḥ kṛtvā bāhubhyāṃ ca parasparam / prapannaṃ pāhi mām īśa bhītaṃ mṛtyu-grahārṇavāt
verses.translation: Placing his head at My feet with his palms brought together [he may say a prayer like:] 'Oh Lord, please protect this surrendered soul who in this material ocean is afraid of being devoured by death [prapannam phi mm s'a, bhtam mrityu-grahrnavat, compare B.G. 11: 19].'

verses.sanskrit: इति शेषां मया दत्तां शिरस्य् आधाय सादरम् । उद्वासयेच् चेद् उद्वास्यं ज्योतिर् ज्योतिषि तत् पुनः
verses.transliteration: iti śeṣāṃ mayā dattāṃ śirasy ādhāya sādaram / udvāsayec ced udvāsyaṃ jyotir jyotiṣi tat punaḥ
verses.translation: Praying thus he should put the remnants granted by Me to his head and do this prayer once more - when the deity respectfully is to be bidden farewell - to give the light [of the deity] a place within the light [of his heart *6].

verses.sanskrit: अर्चादिषु यदा यत्र श्रद्धा मां तत्र चार्चयेत् । सर्व-भूतेष्व् आत्मनि च सर्वात्माहम् अवस्थितः
verses.transliteration: arcādiṣu yadā yatra śraddhā māṃ tatra cārcayet / sarva-bhūteṣv ātmani ca sarvātmāham avasthitaḥ
verses.translation: Whenever one develops faith in Me, in whatever deity form or other manifestation, one should for that form be of worship, since I, the Original Soul of All, am situated within My own form as also in all living beings [see also B.G. 6: 31 and *7].

verses.sanskrit: एवं क्रिया-योग-पथैः पुमान् वैदिक-तान्त्रिकैः । अर्चन्न् उभयतः सिद्धिं मत्तो विन्दत्य् अभीप्सिताम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ kriyā-yoga-pathaiḥ pumān vaidika-tāntrikaiḥ / arcann ubhayataḥ siddhiṃ matto vindaty abhīpsitām
verses.translation: By thus being of worship with the [ritual] processes of acting in yoga as described in the Vedas and in more specialized texts, a person will, in both this life and the next, by My grace achieve the perfection he desired.

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्चां सम्प्रतिष्ठाप्य मन्दिरं कारयेद् दृढम् । पुष्पोद्यानानि रम्याणि पूजा-यात्रोत्सवाश्रितान्
verses.transliteration: mad-arcāṃ sampratiṣṭhāpya mandiraṃ kārayed dṛḍham / puṣpodyānāni ramyāṇi pūjā-yātrotsavāśritān
verses.translation: In order to properly establish My deity, the devotee should build a strong temple and maintain beautiful flower gardens [that provide flowers] for daily pj, festivals and yearly occasions.

verses.sanskrit: पूजादीनां प्रवाहार्थं महा-पर्वस्व् अथान्व्-अहम् । क्षेत्रापण-पुर-ग्रामान् दत्त्वा मत्-सार्ष्टिताम् इयात्
verses.transliteration: pūjādīnāṃ pravāhārthaṃ mahā-parvasv athānv-aham / kṣetrāpaṇa-pura-grāmān dattvā mat-sārṣṭitām iyāt
verses.translation: He who donates land, shops, cities and villages, so as to assure the continuance of the daily worship and the special occasions, will achieve an opulence equal to Mine.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिष्ठया सार्वभौमं सद्मना भुवन-त्रयम् । पूजादिना ब्रह्म-लोकं त्रिभिर् मत्-साम्यताम् इयात्
verses.transliteration: pratiṣṭhayā sārvabhaumaṃ sadmanā bhuvana-trayam / pūjādinā brahma-lokaṃ tribhir mat-sāmyatām iyāt
verses.translation: Installing a deity one attains the entire earth, building a temple one attains the three worlds, and performing pj and likewise services one attains the realm of Brahm, but when one does all of these three, one will attain a quality [a transcendental integrity] equal to Mine.

verses.sanskrit: माम् एव नैरपेक्ष्येण भक्ति-योगेन विन्दति । भक्ति-योगं स लभत एवं यः पूजयेत माम्
verses.transliteration: mām eva nairapekṣyeṇa bhakti-yogena vindati / bhakti-yogaṃ sa labhata evaṃ yaḥ pūjayeta mām
verses.translation: He who free from ulterior motives worships Me thus, will by bhakti-yoga unite his consciousness in devotion and attain Me [see also 5.5: 14, 11.12: 24 and B.G. 6: 44].

verses.sanskrit: यः स्व-दत्तां परैर् दत्तां हरेत सुर-विप्रयोः । वृत्तिं स जायते विड्-भुग् वर्षाणाम् अयुतायुतम्
verses.transliteration: yaḥ sva-dattāṃ parair dattāṃ hareta sura-viprayoḥ / vṛttiṃ sa jāyate viḍ-bhug varṣāṇām ayutāyutam
verses.translation: The one who steals [or destroys] the property [and/or the service] delivered to the gods and the brahmins, whether given by himself or by others, is bound to take birth as a stool-eating worm for a hundred million years [compare 10.64: 39].

verses.sanskrit: कर्तुश् च सारथेर् हेतोर् अनुमोदितुर् एव च । कर्मणां भागिनः प्रेत्य भूयो भूयसि तत्-फलम्
verses.transliteration: kartuś ca sārather hetor anumoditur eva ca / karmaṇāṃ bhāginaḥ pretya bhūyo bhūyasi tat-phalam
verses.translation: Both the perpetrator and his accomplice, the one who instigated it and the one who approved it, all will have to share, over and over, the karmic consequences in the life that follows [depending the degree of the damage done].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच पर-स्वभाव-कर्माणि न प्रशंसेन् न गर्हयेत् । विश्वम् एकामकं पश्यन् प्रकृत्या पुरुषेण च
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca para-svabhāva-karmāṇi na praśaṃsen na garhayet / viśvam ekāmakaṃ paśyan prakṛtyā puruṣeṇa ca
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When one understands that the world, this combination of matter and person, is based upon one and the same reality, one should refrain from praising and criticizing someone else's nature and activities.

verses.sanskrit: पर-स्वभाव-कर्माणि यः प्रशंसति निन्दति । स आशु भ्रश्यते स्वार्थाद् असत्य् अभिनिवेशतः
verses.transliteration: para-svabhāva-karmāṇi yaḥ praśaṃsati nindati / sa āśu bhraśyate svārthād asaty abhiniveśataḥ
